# Changelog

All notable changes to Phantom are documented here.

The format is loosely based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/), and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html). Pre-1.0 releases bump the patch on every tagged release; minor bumps will resume after the v1.0.0 cut.

Each release section is written in operator language, not git-shortlog language. For commit-level granularity, run `git log vPREV..vNEW`.

---

## [v0.17.146] — 2026-06-08 — *Data sources: two-tier validation label (Mapping Validated vs Raw Validated)*

The single "Validated" pill is now **two tiers**, so a Browse card tells you exactly how far a source was proven against a live Cortex XSIAM tenant:

- **Mapping Validated** (green) — the modeling rule populates `xdm.*` on the live tenant (or, when the vendor has no modeling rule, the parsing rule extracts the columns). The pack is installed, the rule is bound, the synthetic event maps. The existing **40** validated sources carry this tier.
- **Raw Validated** (amber, new) — the vendor's content pack is **not installed** on the tenant, so parsing/modeling can't be exercised, but a raw-dataset query (`dataset = X | fields <rule columns> | limit 10`) confirmed our synthetic data lands the **exact field names the rule would read**. Proven-correct shape, ready-to-map the moment the operator installs the pack.

This validates content correctness **independent of what's installed** on the test tenant — following the syslog/CEF-only doctrine, the broker + parsing + modeling rules map on data *shape*, so confirming the wire columns confirms the source. Three named-column sources earn the first Raw Validated pills: **Microsoft Defender for Cloud**, **Proofpoint CASB**, **Azure App Service**.

Raw validation deliberately applies only to **named-column / JSON-composite** rules (where the rule reads the columns we land). `_raw_log`-regex rules are excluded — their columns can land yet the rule reads `_raw_log` content, so columns-present isn't proof of mapping.

Two canonical manifests (`validated_data_sources.txt`, `raw_validated_data_sources.txt`) plus the new `check_raw_validated_data_sources_manifest` validator enforce the tiers and forbid a source claiming both.

Review `/help/architecture#data-sources` (new **Validation tiers** subsection) and `/help/user#data-sources` (validation-pills paragraph).

Scenario 1 upgrade (bundled content + agent UI, installer unchanged) — re-run your existing installer.

---

## [v0.17.145] — 2026-06-08 — *Data sources: 3 newly validated (Cisco ASA, Microsoft Entra ID, Zscaler NSS Weblog)*

Three more bundled data sources earned the green **Validated** pill after a live Cortex XSIAM tenant run confirmed they map a rich set of `xdm.*` fields through the simulate → broker → XQL loop:

- **Cisco ASA** (`cisco_asa_raw`) — **65** distinct `xdm.*` fields (firewall connection/threat/VPN telemetry; JSON-native `_json` composite).
- **Microsoft Entra ID** (`msft_azure_raw`) — **42** `xdm.*` fields (sign-in + audit identity events; gated on `category=SignInLogs`).
- **Zscaler NSS Weblog** (`zscaler_nssweblog_raw`) — **52** `xdm.*` fields (web-proxy CEF telemetry).

The bundle now has **40 validated sources** (up from 37). Validation followed the syslog/CEF-only doctrine — synthetic CEF crafted to each modeling rule's expected shape, sent to the broker, verified by counting distinct non-null `xdm.*` across 20 rows on the tenant.

Scenario 1 upgrade (bundled content, installer unchanged) — re-run your existing installer.

---

## [v0.17.144] — 2026-06-08 — *Data sources: remove 12 action-integration vendors (not real data sources)*

Validating against the live Cortex XSIAM tenant established a sharper definition of what belongs in the data-source catalog: **a data source must be a real XSIAM log-ingestion + parsing path** — the vendor has a parsing and/or modeling rule (in a content pack or built into the platform) so its logs land in a dataset and get *structured*. A vendor whose only Cortex presence is an XSOAR/Marketplace integration with no parsing/modeling rules is an **action integration** (it fetches alerts or runs response actions via API; it does not ingest + parse logs), not a data source. SIEMs that are log *destinations* (IBM QRadar, Rapid7 InsightIDR) are likewise not sources.

Twelve of the vendors added in v0.17.142 failed this test — confirmed on the live tenant, they land raw over syslog/CEF but have **no Cortex rule to structure them** (e.g. PingFederate landed 45,229 raw rows with `xdm=0`; CrowdStrike 38,256 with `xdm=0`), so they can never map to XDM and aren't useful as structured SOC-simulation sources. They are removed:

CrowdStrike Falcon, Sophos Central, Sophos Firewall, Ping Identity PingOne, Wiz, Rapid7 InsightVM, Rapid7 InsightIDR, SAP Security Audit Log, ESET PROTECT, Snowflake, Veeam Backup & Replication, IBM QRadar.

**Kept: Ping Identity PingFederate** — the one of the thirteen with a real *native* Cortex parser and a matching dataset (`ping_identity_pingfederate_raw`, documented by Palo Alto Networks).

The catalog is back to a healthy shape (295 sources, all backed by a real ingest + parse path). The admission rule is codified in the project's data-source doctrine so the catalog stays honest going forward.

Scenario 1 upgrade (bundled content, installer unchanged) — re-run your existing installer.

## [v0.17.143] — 2026-06-07 — *Data sources: brand logos for the 13 new vendors*

The 13 vendors added in v0.17.142 shipped with placeholder tiles. This release gives them real brand marks in the Browse Data Sources catalog: CrowdStrike, Sophos (Central + Firewall), Ping Identity (PingFederate + PingOne), Wiz, Rapid7 (InsightVM + InsightIDR), SAP, Snowflake, Veeam, and ESET. IBM QRadar inherits the existing IBM mark.

Each logo is the vendor's official brand mark from a license-clean source (Simple Icons, worldvectorlogo, or Wikimedia Commons), embedded inline so the catalog renders offline with no outbound network. Every mark was contrast-verified against the catalog's near-white (`#F7F8FA`) logo chip in both light and dark themes.

Scenario 1 upgrade (bundled content, installer unchanged) — re-run your existing installer.

## [v0.17.142] — 2026-06-07 — *Data sources: 13 new high-value vendors (coverage expansion)*

The data-source audit flagged the highest-value vendors missing from the bundle. This release adds 13 of them — each authored from the vendor's real Cortex XSIAM modeling rule (or documented native-collector field names where no modeling rule exists) and adversarially reviewed to catch fabricated fields before shipping:

- **Endpoint / EDR** — CrowdStrike Falcon (FDR event stream), Sophos Central, ESET PROTECT
- **Network / firewall** — Sophos Firewall (XG/XGS)
- **Identity / SSO** — Ping Identity (PingFederate, PingOne)
- **Cloud security** — Wiz (CNAPP issues), Snowflake (account_usage login/query history)
- **Vuln management / SIEM** — Rapid7 InsightVM, Rapid7 InsightIDR, IBM QRadar (offenses)
- **ERP audit** — SAP Security Audit Log
- **Backup / ransomware-recovery** — Veeam Backup & Replication

Each ships with 23–49 documented fields, accurate descriptions + examples, and a `how_to_use` that names the routing literal and the XSIAM content pack to install. They are marked **not yet validated** (the green pill stays off) because XDM saturation has not been proven on a live tenant — each `how_to_use` states the prerequisite.

The adversarial review earned its keep: it caught and corrected fabricated fields before they shipped (PingFederate's invented CEF keys, ESET's non-existent `object_uri_url`, Veeam's eight phantom fields with wrong numeric-vs-string types, Rapid7's bogus status value). A 14th candidate — Cisco AnyConnect/Secure Client — was **dropped** because its VPN events route into the existing `cisco_asa_raw` dataset, not a new one.

Also in this release: fixed a typo in the Apple macOS source description and refreshed the catalog-size figure in the user guide. Brand logos for the nine brand-new vendors land in a follow-up (six aren't on the Simple Icons source and need careful per-vendor sourcing).

Scenario 1 upgrade (bundled content, installer unchanged) — re-run your existing installer.

## [v0.17.141] — 2026-06-07 — *Data sources: audit cleanup (vendor casing, Salesforce descriptions, MacOS flag)*

An in-depth audit of all 294 bundled data sources surfaced three clear-cut quality issues, now fixed:

- **Vendor-casing duplicates** — three sources carried a lowercase `vendor` (`microsoft`, `salesforce`, `zscaler`) that split them into their own group in the Browse Data Sources catalog, separate from their canonical-cased siblings. Normalized to `Microsoft` / `Salesforce` / `Zscaler` so each vendor groups as one tile.
- **Salesforce descriptions** — five Salesforce sources used the category label `"CRM Services"` as their description. Replaced with accurate per-dataset descriptions: Setup Audit Trail, Event Log Files, Login History, Real-Time Event Monitoring, and the combined audit/event stream.
- **MacOS raw-log flag** — the Apple macOS source carries a single `_raw_log` field but had `is_rawlog_only: false`; set to `true` so the catalog correctly marks it a raw-log passthrough.

The rest of the bundle audited clean: all 294 sources have a `how_to_use`, valid field types, unique field names, and a healthy median of 29 fields.

Scenario 1 upgrade (bundled content, installer unchanged) — re-run your existing installer.
