## [v0.17.54] — 2026-05-25 — *Multi-provider arc · A1 first slice: Claude Code CLI shell-out endpoint*

First sub-release of the multi-provider chat arc. Phantom-agent now ships the Claude Code CLI pre-baked in the image and exposes `POST /api/chat/cli` — operators can drive a chat turn through Anthropic's Claude Code as a second model option alongside the default Gemini chat-route.

### Design

**Two integration patterns** were chosen for the broader arc:
- **Pattern A — API providers** (Anthropic API, OpenAI API, Ollama API) plug into Phantom's existing tool-call loop alongside `callGemini()`.
- **Pattern B — CLI providers** (Claude Code, Codex) are one-shot shell-outs. They run their own tool-call loops internally and don't fit the chat-route's 20-step pipeline.

A1 ships Pattern B for Claude Code, ported from Spark's plugin-runner pattern but simplified for Phantom's single-container model — `child_process.spawn` inline instead of Docker-in-Docker. Container-level isolation (Spark's `--cap-drop=ALL` story) can be layered in later via the per-instance-connector-container pattern.

### What ships

**`mcp/agent/lib/cli-wrapper.ts`** (new) — generic CLI wrapper. `runCli()` for one-shot capture, `runCliStreaming()` for line-by-line stdout callback. Enforces a hard timeout with SIGTERM → SIGKILL grace period. Merges env vars onto inherited process env.

**`mcp/agent/lib/anthropic-credentials.ts`** (new) — credential resolver mirroring `vertex-credentials.ts`. 30s cache, `bustAnthropicCredsCache()` for invalidation after a `/providers` PUT (wired in a follow-on release). Two exposed resolvers:
- `resolveAnthropicApiKey()` — for the future chat-route `callAnthropic()` (A2).
- `resolveAnthropicCliKey()` — for this release's CLI shell-out. Resolution priority: ProviderStore `anthropic.secrets.cli_key` → env `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` → env `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.

**`mcp/agent/app/api/chat/cli/route.ts`** (new) — SSE endpoint. Spawns `npx @anthropic-ai/claude-code --print --output-format json <prompt>`, streams stdout lines as SSE events (`output` for JSON-parseable, `output_raw` for everything else), emits a final `done` event with exit code + duration + stderr tail. 10-minute timeout matching Spark's plugin.yaml. Cookie-gated via the middleware matcher extension.

**`mcp/agent/middleware.ts`** — matcher widened from `/api/chat` to also cover `/api/chat/:path*` so the new sub-route inherits session enforcement automatically. Same widening pre-emptively covers any future chat sub-routes (Codex, model bench, etc.).

**`mcp/agent/Dockerfile`** — bakes `@anthropic-ai/claude-code@^1.0.0` into the runtime stage so the first `/api/chat/cli` invocation doesn't pay the npx download cost. Pinned version keeps image-digest stability.

### What does NOT ship (deferred)

- **`/providers` ProviderStore persistence for `anthropicCliKey`** — the existing PUT route accepts the field but discards it. A1.1 wires the persist+read path. For A1 first slice, operators set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` or `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` in `/opt/phantom/.env` and the resolver picks it up.
- **Chat-header UI toggle** — A1.2. For A1, operators hit `/api/chat/cli` directly via curl to validate.
- **MCP bridge to Claude Code** — Claude Code can be given access to external MCP servers via `--mcp-config`. Wiring Phantom's MCP through that flag is a separate enhancement, not part of A1.
- **Audit row** — the SSE `done` event carries the run metadata. A future enhancement persists it as a `chat_turn_cli` audit row via the same path `chat_turn_cost` uses.

### Smoke test (manual)

```bash
# From a logged-in browser session (cookie present):
curl -N https://<phantom-host>/api/chat/cli \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --cookie "phantom_session=<token>" \
  -d '{"prompt":"List the files in /tmp"}'

# Should see SSE events: meta → output (or output_raw) → done.
# stderr_tail in the done event surfaces Claude Code diagnostics.
```

### Next in arc

- v0.17.55 — A1.1: persist `anthropicCliKey` in ProviderStore via the existing `/providers` page (the form schema is already there).
- v0.17.56 — A1.2: chat-header UI toggle so "Claude Code" mode is one click instead of curl.
- v0.17.57 — A2: Anthropic API in chat-route's tool-call loop (`callAnthropic()` alongside `callGemini()`).
- v0.17.58 — A3: Codex CLI shell-out (same wrapper pattern).
- v0.17.59 — A4: OpenAI API in chat-route.
- v0.17.60 — A5: Ollama API in chat-route.
- v0.17.61 — A6: full provider toggle UI + chat-header dropdown.

### Operator review checklist

- `npm run build` clean (verified)
- `npx tsc --noEmit` clean (verified)
- Set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...` in `/opt/phantom/.env` on phantom-vm after the image rebuilds, then curl the smoke command above
- SSE response includes a final `done` event with `exit_code: 0` and Claude Code's response JSON in an earlier `output` event

## [v0.17.53] — 2026-05-25 — *Help docs: integration-neutral language pass (round 1)*

First sub-release of the integration-neutral language arc. The docs were reading like Phantom is a Cortex XSIAM accessory — every positioning paragraph framed XSIAM/XDM as the destination. That's wrong: Phantom produces wire-faithful events that any downstream system parses (XSIAM, Splunk, Sentinel, Elastic, custom Logstash, etc.). XSIAM is one (well-supported) integration, not the product's identity.

### What ships — high-impact framing rewrites

**Architecture page** (`mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx`):
- **Data Sources Section opener** — rewrote the 3-paragraph intro. Schemas now described as "structured descriptions of what a vendor's product emits on the wire". Cortex content packs called out as ONE source for canonical schemas (alongside other publicly-available vendor catalogues). Downstream parsing described as integration-neutral with XSIAM listed as one example among Splunk TAs, Sentinel parsers, Elastic ingest, custom Logstash. Phantom positioned as destination-neutral.
- **`data_sources_install` MCP tool description** — "typical Cortex modeling rules emit 1–3 datasets" → "the bundled vendor schemas typically declare 1–3 datasets per rule".

**User guide** (`mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx`):
- **Data Sources Section opener** — rewrote the second paragraph. "Cortex's out-of-the-box parsing rules map them into XDM correctly" → "any downstream system that already knows the vendor parses them cleanly. That includes the vendor's own SIEM connector, a Splunk technology add-on, a Sentinel parser, an Elastic ingest pipeline, a Cortex XSIAM modeling rule, or whatever bespoke parsing you run".
- **"What 'vendor-faithful' means under the hood"** — rewrote both paragraphs. End-result now reads "looks identical to real traffic from a live FortiGate, no matter which platform is doing the parsing" instead of "shows up in xdr_data with proper XDM fields populated".
- **Log destinations "Vendor-faithful simulation"** — "exactly where Cortex's out-of-the-box FortiGate ModelingRule expects them" → "a vendor-tagged dataset the receiving platform can route to its FortiGate-specific parser. Combined with a data source schema override, any downstream FortiGate parser (Cortex XSIAM modeling rule, Splunk technology add-on, Sentinel parser, Elastic ingest pipeline) ingests them cleanly".

### What stays unchanged

- The XSIAM Connector / Cortex XDR Connector / Cortex Content Connector Sections — these document those specific integrations and are appropriately XSIAM/XDR-centric.
- Log Destinations Section's `xsiam_http` destination type spec — that IS the XSIAM-specific destination.
- Per-connector REST tool surfaces — they describe each connector's actual capabilities.

### Next in arc

- v0.17.54 — second integration-neutral pass: connector-section openers, xlog/CALDERA framing, skill-catalogue + skill-activation language.
- v0.17.55 — journeys.ts neutral-language pass: scenario descriptions, example prompts, expected results.
- v0.17.56 — final audit + sweep for any remaining XSIAM-bound positioning.

### Operator review checklist

- `/help/architecture#data-sources` opener — reads as "vendor schemas → wire-faithful events → any downstream parses cleanly" with XSIAM as one example among several
- `/help/user#data-sources` second paragraph — same neutral framing
- `/help/user` "What 'vendor-faithful' means" — end-result is integration-neutral, no `xdr_data`-only language
- `/help/user#log-destinations-ux` "Vendor-faithful simulation" — XSIAM modeling rule listed alongside Splunk / Sentinel / Elastic parsers

## [v0.17.52] — 2026-05-25 — *Help docs: Sub-agents deep dive — constraints, REST surface, debugging ladder. Phase 2 closes.*

Final sub-release of Phase 2. The Sub-agents framework now has target-state architecture coverage (no roadmap/deferred framing), a REST + UI surface table, and an operator-facing decision guide plus debugging ladder.

### What ships

`mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx` — Subagents Section:

- **Opener repaired** — was a stranded "enables FOREGROUND subagent dispatch with scoped tool catalogs" fragment (phase-prefix-strip artifact). Now reads as a target-state description of what subagents ARE: a foreground delegation primitive with scoped child catalogs and sidechain transcripts.
- **Step 7 of runSubagent flow** — stripped "(Phase $)" internal-milestone label; replaced with a concrete cross-link to `/observability/cost` describing how cost rows attribute correctly via the subagent session id.
- **"Deferred: background + recursion" subsection rewritten** as a **"Constraints + dispatch model"** subsection. Same three topics (foreground-only, bounded depth via max_turns, tool scope as security model) but framed as the *design choice* rather than "what's missing". Customer-facing docs describe the product as it is.
- **New "REST + UI surface" subsection** — lists the 6 agent-definition endpoints + the programmatic `/subagents/{name}/dispatch` route + the two UI surfaces with the observability filter strings. Hook authors no longer need to grep for endpoints.
- **New "Implementation references" subsection** — source-file index pointing at the runner, the catalog filter, the store, the REST routes, and the UI page.

`mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx` — `agents-ux` Section:

- **New "When to spawn vs ask the parent" subsection** — three concrete scenarios where a subagent is the right tool (different persona/tighter scope, long sub-task you don't want polluting parent context, parallel exploration) plus the inverse — when staying with the parent agent is the right call.
- **New "Debugging subagents" subsection** — five-step ladder: open the sidechain link, filter `action:chat_subagent_*` in /observability/events, check blocked-tool events, check max_turns cap, follow the cost rollup. Parallels the hooks debugging ladder added in v0.17.51.

### Phase 2 status — complete

| Sub-release | Component |
|---|---|
| v0.17.47 | Personality Store |
| v0.17.48 | Setup & First-Run Wiring |
| v0.17.49 | Backup & Restore (user guide) |
| v0.17.50 | Observability overview |
| v0.17.51 | Hooks deep dive |
| **v0.17.52** | **Sub-agents deep dive — closes Phase 2** |

Phase 3 (per-component audit + sync across all 4 surfaces) starts next: Skills, Memory + sessions, Jobs, Knowledge, Marketplace, Log Destinations, Providers + Models, API Keys, Approvals, Notifications, Audit, Tasks, Plugins, per-connector deep dives.

### Operator review checklist

- `/help/architecture#subagents` — opener reads as target state, "Constraints + dispatch model" / "REST + UI surface" / "Implementation references" subsections render at the end. No "Phase $" or "Deferred:" framing.
- `/help/user#agents-ux` — "When to spawn vs ask the parent" + "Debugging subagents" subsections render at the bottom.

## [v0.17.51] — 2026-05-25 — *Help docs: Hooks deep dive — per-transport walkthroughs + debugging guide*

Fifth sub-release of Phase 2. The Hooks framework now has end-to-end walkthroughs per transport in the architecture spec and a debugging ladder in the user guide.

### What ships

`mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx` — Hooks Section gains two new subsections:

1. **Per-transport walkthroughs** — concrete end-to-end flow for each of the five transports (builtin, http, command, plugin, agent). Each entry shows the wire-format pseudocode, latency expectation, and right-fit use case.
2. **Fire-site catalog — what payload arrives where** — the 10 fire-sites enumerated with payload shape, what `decision` means at each, what `injectContext` does. Hook authors no longer need to read source to know which fields to expect.

`mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx` — Hooks Section also gains a new **REST + UI surface** subsection listing every endpoint + the two UI surfaces.

Stripped leftover "Shipped in" stranded fragment from the plugin transport description (a phase-prefix-strip artifact). Repaired the Task Registry opener that read as "replaces (for chat-route purposes) the lossy `workers = {}` dict" — rewrote as a target-state opener describing what the registry IS, not what it replaced.

`mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx` — `hooks-ux` Section:

- New **Debugging hooks** subsection — five-step ladder: filter audit events by `action:hook_fire` / `hook_denied` / `hook_error`; use the dry-run endpoint; toggle hooks off without deleting; check the failure-policy column. Replaces the "look at /observability/events" advice that lived as a one-liner.
- Stripped `v0.5.21`, `Issue #28`, `Issue #25`, `Issue #27`, `Issue #31`, `v0.5.48`, and `Phase X` annotations from body text.
- Reworded the Built-in catalogue paragraph to point at the runtime `GET /api/agent/hooks/builtins` enumeration rather than a roadmap of which issue lands which builtin.
- Reworded the `Agent tool` transport description to clarify it's a reserved discriminator.

### Operator review checklist

2. **First boot: entrypoint seeds the stores** — 6-step idempotent seed sequence (TLS proxy → skills merge → SecretStore seed → KB reconcile → MCP subprocess → Next.js).
3. **First operator login** — `admin` + auto-generated password → forced redirect to `/profile` until `credentials_changed=true`.
4. **Provider configuration** — `/providers` UI surface + REST endpoint summary + secret redaction contract.
5. **Connector + instance configuration** — manifest-declared field schema rendered into create/edit forms.
6. **Factory reset path** — host-side `phantom-factory-reset` utility behavior.

## [v0.17.43] — 2026-05-25 — *Journeys: retire engineering-acceptance-test bundles + obsolete upgrade journey*

Third sub-release of the doc audit arc. The `/help/journeys` page no longer surfaces engineering-internal acceptance tests or migration journeys that target version hops no current customer is on.

### What ships

`mcp/agent/lib/journeys.ts`:

- Removed the 10-journey `v0.5.0 acceptance test` bundle. These were operator-walkable QA matrices using raw curl + `docker exec` patterns, structured as "Test case A/B/C/D/E" rather than customer workflows. The platform's customer-walkable equivalents (`auth-agent-credential-guardrail`, `ops-marketplace-browse`, `ops-upload-user-connector`, etc.) already cover the same surfaces in operator language.
- Removed the 3-journey `v0.5.1 acceptance test` bundle. Two were one-time migration verifications for the localStorage→server-side state transition that completed on the v0.5.0 → v0.5.1 hop; the third asked operators to manually seed localStorage via DevTools (a developer/QA test, not an operator workflow).
- Removed the `v050-test` and `v051-test` JourneyComponent values from the type union and from `COMPONENT_META`. The filter chip strip on `/help/journeys` no longer offers these as filter options.
- Removed the `upgrade-from-v02x-to-v03x` journey. v0.2.x is many releases past; no current customer is upgrading from it. The release-notes history at `/about/history` already covers historical upgrade context.
- Updated the one `related[]` reference that pointed to the retired upgrade journey (in `cortex-xql-query-authoring`).

Net diff: -680 lines.

## [v0.17.39] — 2026-05-25 — *Login page: "Phantom can …" capabilities + Phantom logo in the bottom orb row*

Operator-flagged visual polish on the login page (`mcp/agent/components/auth/login-screen.tsx` + `mcp/agent/components/ui/tools-stack-card.tsx`). Two targeted swaps; animations, sizes, colors, and timing all preserved.

### What ships

**`mcp/agent/components/auth/login-screen.tsx`** — re-framed the FlippingText cycle:

| Before (through v0.17.38) | After (v0.17.39+) |
|---|---|
| `Powered by Cortex XSIAM` | `Phantom can simulate logs` |
| `Powered by MITRE ATT&CK` | `Phantom can simulate attacks` |
| `Powered by CALDERA` | `Phantom can create workers` |
| `Powered by Vertex AI` | `Phantom can send logs` |
| `Powered by Gemini` | `Phantom can validate detections` |
| `Powered by MCP` | `Phantom can orchestrate workflows` |

Same 6-item cycle (timing unchanged: 45 ms type, 45 ms delete, 900 ms pause), same cyan color (`text-cyan-300`), same cursor blink behavior (blue while typing, red while deleting). Only the static prefix word + the words array changed.

The framing shift matters: the brand-name cycle ("Powered by Cortex XSIAM …") suggested Phantom was a thin shim over third-party products. The capability cycle ("Phantom can simulate logs …") tells the operator what Phantom actually does for them.

**`mcp/agent/components/ui/tools-stack-card.tsx`** — replaced the middle orb's content with the Phantom logo:

The 5-orb row at the bottom of the login card runs a sequential-bounce + sweeping-cyan-beam + sparkle animation. The middle orb is the largest (64 px diameter with a fuchsia ring) and used to render a gold `bolt` material-symbol glyph (operator described it as a "spark"). v0.17.39 swaps the glyph for the actual Phantom logo (`/logo.svg`, same asset the sidebar uses at `size=44`).

The 4 outer orbs are untouched: `agents (smart_toy)` and `memory (memory)` on the left, `observe (monitoring)` and `mcp (api)` on the right. Sizes, colors, ring, sequential-bounce animation, sweeping cyan beam, sparkles — all preserved.

### What ships (files)

- `mcp/agent/components/auth/login-screen.tsx` — prefix string + 6-element words array swap; header comment extended with the v0.17.39 framing note
- `mcp/agent/components/ui/tools-stack-card.tsx` — `ToolItem` discriminated union + conditional render inside `Container`; only the middle slot uses the new `logo` branch
- `CHANGELOG.md` + `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

### Operator review checklist

- Open `/login` (sign out first if logged in) → top-right of the card reads "Phantom can simulate logs ▎" with the typewriter cycling through the new 6 phrases
- The bottom orb row's middle orb shows the Phantom logo (not the gold bolt/spark)
- Sequential bounce animation still works (orbs rise + settle in order, left-to-right)
- Sweeping cyan beam still sweeps across the row, sparkles still emit
- Sidebar, startup page, and About modal still render the same Phantom logo (same asset — `/logo.svg` — re-used)

## [v0.17.38] — 2026-05-25 — *Edit flow for user-uploaded data sources + use-case filter dropdown (replaces the long chip strip)*

Closes the last item of the operator's 5-piece v0.17.36 batch: CRUD parity for user-uploaded data sources. Before this release, "editing" a user upload was a destructive cycle: uninstall (if installed) → delete user YAML → re-upload from scratch → re-install. Now it's one click in the UI.

### What ships

`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_yaml_loader.py` — new `update_user(ds_id, doc)` method on `DataSourcesYamlLoader`. Differs from `write_user` in three places:
- Target YAML at `<user_root>/<ds_id>/data_source.yaml` MUST already exist (returns "not found" error → HTTP 404 if not).
- Body's `id` MUST equal the path-arg `ds_id`. PUT is not rename; to change id, delete + re-upload.
- `created_at` is preserved from the on-disk YAML, `updated_at` is refreshed to now.

`bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py` — new `PUT /api/v1/data-sources/user/{id}` endpoint mirroring the POST shape (`yaml|doc` + `accept_token` + `vendor_choice`) but routing through `update_user`. Same `accept_token` discipline: the operator must re-preview the exact bytes they're committing — server-side SHA-256 verification on the canonical YAML bytes. Audit action `data_source_user_edit` records the field-count + vendor delta.

`bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py` (installed list + drawer schema) — both responses now include `origin` ("bundle" | "user") so the UI can render an Edit affordance for user uploads on the Installed tab + Detail Drawer.

`mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/user/[id]/route.ts` — new `PUT` handler proxying to the embedded MCP.

`mcp/agent/app/data-sources/upload-dialog.tsx` — new `editId?: string | null` prop. When set:
- The dialog title flips to "Edit data source" and pre-loads the YAML textarea via GET `/api/agent/data-sources/user/{editId}`.
- The "Commit upload" button label flips to "Save changes".
- Submission routes to PUT instead of POST, with the same preview → vendor-choice → accept_token flow.

`mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx` — Edit affordances surfaced in three places:
- **Browse tab `PackRow`**: inline "Edit" button next to Install/installed indicator for rows where `origin === "user"`. Also a small "user" badge next to the rawlog badge.
- **Installed tab `InstalledCard`**: compact pencil-icon button in the footer alongside the delete button.
- **Detail Drawer footer**: "Edit" pill button alongside Install/Uninstall when the data source is user-uploaded.

All three hit the same `editingId` state at the page level; the upload dialog already wired up via `uploadOpen` switches into edit mode based on `editingId`.

### Capability acceptance

End-to-end check on the deployed install:
1. Browse tab → vendor that contains a user upload → expand → click Edit. Dialog opens pre-filled.
2. Modify one field (e.g. `description`). Preview. Confirm vendor unchanged. Save.
3. Re-open the same source. The edit persisted; `updated_at` refreshed; `created_at` preserved from the original upload.
4. The Installed tab + Browse tab show updated values immediately (live `loadInstalled` + `loadCatalog` refresh on save).

### What ships (files)

- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_yaml_loader.py` — `+update_user()` method
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py` — `+update_user_upload()` route; `+origin/+id` in `_enrich_with_vendor_meta`; `+origin` in `list_data_sources` enrichment
- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/user/[id]/route.ts` — `+PUT` handler
- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/upload-dialog.tsx` — `+editId` prop, fetch+pre-fill, PUT submission, header/footer text variants
- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx` — `+editingId` state; `+onEdit` prop threaded through `BrowseSection → ExpandedTray → PackRow`, `InstalledSection → InstalledCard`, and `DetailDrawer`; `+origin` field on `DataSource` type
- `CHANGELOG.md` + `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts` + `mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx` `#data-sources` Edit paragraph

### Use-case filter dropdown (operator-flagged UX fix)

Operator feedback: the v0.17.34 use-case filter chip strip ("long row of types") was ugly. v0.17.38 replaces it with a Material 3 styled multi-select dropdown.

`mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx` — new `UseCaseFilter` component:
- **Trigger**: pill-shaped button with `filter_list` icon, "Use case" label, badge showing the selected count, and chevron-down indicator. Tinted with primary color when selection is non-empty so the active filter state is visible at a glance.
- **Floating panel**: 340px wide, rounded-2xl corners, soft `shadow-2xl`, glass-panel styling consistent with the rest of `/data-sources`. Click-outside + Escape close.
- **Panel header**: "Filter by use case" title + "X of Y selected" indicator + "All" (select all) + "Clear" (remove all) actions.
- **Search-within-panel** input — 44 use cases is plenty for keyboard nav but a filter makes the operator's life easier.
- **Checkbox list**: scrollable, max-height 320px. Custom M3-shaped checkboxes (rounded square, primary-tinted when checked, animated check icon). Each item shows the matching count as a pill badge. Selected items float to the top for quick uncheck.
- **Inline removable pills** rendered next to the trigger button when selection is non-empty — operator can see the active filter set + remove any pill with one click *without opening the panel*. Caps at 6 visible pills with "+N more" overflow.
- **Footer hint**: "Multiple selections = OR (any match)" — clarifies the semantics for first-time users.

Vendor-grouping filter semantics unchanged: a vendor matches if ANY of its `use_cases` intersect with the selected set. Cisco-tagged-both-Firewall-and-EDR shows under either filter selection.

### Operator review checklist

- `/data-sources` Browse tab → user-upload vendor → expand → Edit button visible on user-origin rows
- Click Edit → dialog opens pre-filled with the existing YAML doc
- Edit a field → Preview → Save changes → notification "Changes saved"
- Re-open the same source → the change persists; the `user` badge still shows
- `/data-sources` Installed tab → user-uploaded source card → pencil icon in the footer; clicking opens the same edit dialog
- `/data-sources` Browse tab → toolbar shows the new "Use case" dropdown trigger (no more long horizontal chip strip)
- Open the dropdown → multi-select via checkboxes works → grid narrows to matching vendors
- Inline pills appear next to the trigger when selection is non-empty → clicking a pill removes that filter
- Search-within-panel narrows the checkbox list as you type

## [v0.17.37] — 2026-05-25 — *Backup + restore now include data sources (system install set + user uploads)*

Operator-flagged gap: the backup zip captured personality / instances / memory / jobs / skills / knowledge but **not data sources**. Operators lose every custom-uploaded vendor YAML + every install pick on backup→restore.

### What ships

`mcp/agent/app/api/agent/backup/route.ts` — new **Section 7: data sources**:
- `data_sources/user/<id>.json` — full parsed YAML doc per operator-uploaded data source (origin=user)
- `data_sources/installed.json` — `[{pack_name, rule_name, dataset_name}, …]` of currently-installed packs (covers BOTH system + user)

Bundle YAMLs themselves are NOT exported — they re-arrive in the destination's image. What needs to travel is *which ones the operator picked* (the install set) + *what custom ones they added* (user uploads). Both now covered.

`mcp/agent/app/api/agent/restore/route.ts` — new **Step 6: data sources** (between knowledge and jobs):

1. For each `data_sources/user/<id>.json` → two-phase upload (preview → commit with accept_token), with `vendor_choice=create_new` to preserve the operator's exact vendor field. Collision check against destination's existing user uploads; `force=true` overwrites, default skips.
2. For each entry in `data_sources/installed.json` → POST `/api/v1/data-sources/install`. Already-installed packs on the destination are skipped (idempotent install would succeed but we report honestly via the skipped counter).

Sequence rationale: data sources before jobs because runtime jobs may reference installed data sources via the simulate-vendor-logs skill. Manifest jobs reseed at boot, so the order only matters for runtime jobs.

### Manifest changes

`manifest.json` `sections` list grew to `[personality, instances, memory, jobs, skills, knowledge, data_sources]`. `restore_order` reordered to `[personality, instances, skills, memory, knowledge, data_sources, jobs]`. New restore note documents the bundle-vs-user export semantics.

### Dry-run

`POST /api/agent/restore?dry_run=true` now also reports `sections_present.data_sources_user` (file count) and `sections_present.data_sources_installed` (boolean). Operators can verify their backup zip carries data sources before committing the restore.

### What ships (files)

- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/backup/route.ts` — new Section 7 inserted before manifest; +2 interfaces + restore_notes line added
- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/restore/route.ts` — new Step 6 for two-phase upload + install re-run; dry-run + restore_order updated
- `CHANGELOG.md` + `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

### Closes

- Operator-reported gap from the v0.17.36 thread.

### Operator-visible

After deploy:
- Click `Backup` on `/settings/backup-restore` → the downloaded zip now contains `data_sources/user/*.json` + `data_sources/installed.json`. Section counts show how many of each.
- Click `Restore` on a destination → operator-uploaded vendors come back, every previously-installed pack gets re-installed.

---

## [v0.17.36] — 2026-05-25 — *Data sources inventory audit + comprehensive docs (architecture + user guide + journey)*

Operator asked for:
1. Inventory count + grouping verification
2. Architecture-page section dedicated to data sources (storage, MCP interaction, diagram)
3. User-guide section explaining the operator-facing surface
4. A new journey covering the v0.17.x features

### Inventory audit (`scripts/audit_data_sources_inventory.py`)

342 YAML files across 137 vendors:
- 342 distinct (pack, rule, dataset) tuples — no duplicates
- 259 distinct YAML `id` values (60 dupes are upstream `_1_3` vs legacy rule pairs — distinct catalog rows, not bugs)
- 100% vendor-bucketed; 100% use_case-tagged
- Only anomaly: MySQL has 2 packs both `is_rawlog_only:true` (hidden in default Browse)

### Architecture page additions (`mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx`)

5 new subsections appended to the `#data-sources` section:

1. **v0.17.x evolution — vendor grouping, use-cases, vendor logos** — ASCII diagram tracing a catalog request from YAML → loader → enrichment → UI, post-v0.17.30 + v0.17.35
2. **Use-case taxonomy (v0.17.34)** — the 44 canonical labels across 8 domains
3. **Logo pipeline (v0.17.27 → v0.17.35)** — 3-priority resolution chain + provenance schema (source / license / fidelity)
4. **Loader perf — mtime-based scan cache (v0.17.30)** — benchmark numbers + cache invariants
5. **Picture — internal data flow** — full ASCII diagram covering: browser → Next.js → MCP → loader → enrichment → JSON → UI, plus runtime storage homes and MCP tool surface

### User guide additions (`mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx`)

3 new subsections under `#data-sources`:

1. **Browse, filter, install (v0.17.34+)** — vendor consolidation, use-case taxonomy, filter chip strip semantics, install click-path
2. **Upload a custom data source** — preview/commit two-phase flow, similarity check, vendor regrouping, bundle precedence, delete behavior, edit roadmap note
3. **Vendor logos + visual conventions** — provenance fidelity tiers + standalone logo library at `docs/assets/vendor-logos/`

### New journey: `filter-data-sources-by-use-case`

`mcp/agent/lib/journeys.ts` — operator-walkable journey for the v0.17.34 filter chip strip. Click-path: click WAF → narrows to 7 vendors. Multi-select OR semantics. Search + filter AND-combined. Clear button resets. All filtering is client-side (no server round-trip on toggle).

## [v0.17.35] — 2026-05-25 — *Vendor-level logos on installed cards + Tanium/Thinkst white-text recolor*

Operator-reported issues with installed-page logos:

> "F5ASM, F5LTM, F5APM — it's not visible. Microsoft EntraID is not visible. FortiGate is, again, the text is white, and part of the logo is colored. … For products, don't use individual logos. Use the same vendor logos. F5ASM, F5LTM, F5APM all use the same one logo of the F5 vendor. Always use the vendor logo."
>
> "Tanium logo is white on background white, so it's not visible. Thinkst same problem — the text part is white, so it's not visible."

### Backend changes

`bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py`:

1. **Catalog endpoint** (`/api/v1/data-sources/catalog`) — new pass adds `vendor_logo_url` to every row. Computed as the first sibling-row-per-vendor that has a non-null `logo_url`. Mirrors the v0.17.28 VendorCard "rows.find(r => r.logo_url)" picker so every consumer (browse card / installed card / drawer header) gets the same vendor logo.

2. **Installed list endpoint** (`/api/v1/data-sources`) — pre-v0.17.35 returned the bare `DataSource.to_dict()` shape (no vendor metadata). Now cross-references the YAML loader once to enrich each installed row with `vendor_key`, `vendor_display_name`, `vendor_logo_url`, and `use_cases`. Computed once per request, not per row.

3. **Drawer schema endpoint** (`/api/v1/data-sources/{pack}/{rule}/{dataset}/schema`) — new `_enrich_with_vendor_meta()` helper populates the same four fields on both the installed-store path and the cortex-content preview path.

### Frontend changes (`mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx`)

1. **InstalledCard logo source**: `withLightVariant(row.logo_url)` → `withLightVariant(row.vendor_logo_url ?? row.logo_url)`. F5ASM / F5LTM / F5APM now all render the F5 vendor logo, not whatever per-pack logo was in their YAML.

2. **DetailDrawer header logo**: same swap — `detail.vendor_logo_url ?? detail.logo_url`.

3. **CatalogRow + DataSource TypeScript interfaces** extended with `vendor_logo_url?: string | null` (plus `vendor_key?: string`, `vendor_display_name?: string` on DataSource).

### Visibility fixes

| Vendor | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| **Tanium** | Wikipedia version had `fill="white"` on every wordmark path → invisible on the near-white card panel | Recolored white → `#1A2238` (navy). Brand red `#E23430` mark untouched. Marked `branded-recolored` |
| **Thinkst** | Mixed green `#38D47F` + red `#FF2F2F` mark + white text → text invisible | Same recolor pattern: white text → `#1A2238`, brand-colored marks preserved |

(FortiGate's "white text" complaint was the per-pack legacy logo — `vendor_logo_url` now routes FortiGate to the Fortinet vendor logo from simple-icons, which has the right default fill.)

### What ships

- 4 YAML edits (Tanium, TaniumThreatResponse, Thinkst, ThinkstCanary_1_3) — recolored inline logos
- `docs/assets/vendor-logos/{tanium.svg, thinkst.svg}` regenerated via `extract_vendor_logos_library.py`
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py` — catalog + installed-list + drawer enrichments + new `_enrich_with_vendor_meta()` helper
- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx` — 4 small edits (CatalogRow + DataSource types; InstalledCard logo; DetailDrawer header logo)

### Operator-visible

After hard-refresh:
- F5ASM card on installed page shows the F5 vendor logo (same as F5APM, F5LTM, F5BigIPAWAF)
- Microsoft EntraID card shows the Microsoft vendor logo
- FortiGate card shows the Fortinet vendor logo
- Tanium card text becomes navy on near-white → visible
- Thinkst card text becomes navy → visible
- Drawer header for any pack shows the vendor-level logo (consistent across siblings)

### v0.17.36 (next, optional)

- Audit the remaining 134 vendor logos for additional white-fill cases that v0.17.35 didn't catch (heuristic: any SVG containing both `fill="white"` AND another non-`none` fill is mixed-color and might have an invisible portion)
- Per-pack `log_types:` curation (Network/Endpoint/Identity/CTI/Activity/Process/File) for the drawer

## [v0.17.34] — 2026-05-25 — *Data sources use-case categorization: replace XSIAM categories with curated product-type labels + filter chip strip*

Operator direction: vendor cards should carry **product-type labels**, not XSIAM platform categories. F5 should show "WAF, Load Balancer"; Okta "Identity, MFA"; Avaya "Voice"; Box "Storage"; CyberArk "PAM". Plus a filter strip so operators can narrow the catalog to one product type at a time.

### Taxonomy

44 canonical use_cases organized by domain (see `scripts/curate_vendor_use_cases.py` `CANONICAL_USE_CASES`):

**Network**: Network, Firewall, WAF, LoadBalancer, SDWAN, VPN, Proxy, DNS, IDS, DDoS
**Endpoint**: EDR, AV, Endpoint, Forensics
**Identity**: Identity, MFA, PAM, AD, CIAM
**Data**: Email, DLP, CASB
**Cloud**: Cloud, SaaS, Container, Virtualization, CSPM
**Apps**: Database, Storage, Collab, DevOps, WebServer, AppServer
**SecOps**: SIEM, SOAR, XDR, ThreatIntel, Vuln, ASM, CTEM
**Specialty**: OS, Honeypot, ICS, PhysSec, Voice, Analytics, Other

### Curation

`scripts/curate_vendor_use_cases.py` (NEW) — maintainer-only script that writes a `use_cases: [...]` block into every bundled `data_source.yaml`. Curated mapping covers all 137 vendors; 0 fell to "Other". Idempotent (safe to re-run).

Sample curation:
- F5 → `["WAF", "LoadBalancer"]`
- Okta → `["Identity", "MFA"]`
- CyberArk → `["PAM"]`
- Avaya → `["Voice"]`
- Box → `["Storage", "Collab"]`
- Symantec → `["AV", "EDR", "Email", "DLP", "CASB"]`
- Cisco → `["Network", "Firewall", "EDR"]`
- Microsoft → `["Cloud", "SaaS", "Identity"]`

### Backend changes

`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_yaml_loader.py`:
- `YamlDataSource` dataclass: new `use_cases: list[str]` field
- `from_doc()` reads it from YAML
- `to_doc()` writes it back
- `to_catalog_row()` surfaces it to the UI (next to existing `categories`)

`bundles/spark/data-sources/**/data_source.yaml` — 342 files got a new `use_cases:` block.

### UI changes (`mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx`)

1. **VendorCard badge source** changed from `mapCategoriesToBadges(r.categories)` (XSIAM platform taxonomy) to direct dedup of `r.use_cases` across the vendor's packs. First 4 unique labels shown.

2. **Filter chip strip** added above the catalog grid:
   - One chip per use_case present in the catalog
   - Click to toggle; multiple selections = OR (any-match)
   - Selected chips float to the front so the active set is visible
   - Count per chip reflects how many catalog rows carry that label
   - "Clear" button when at least one chip is selected

3. **DetailDrawer use_case pills** in the slot vacated by v0.17.33's hidden Supported Modules row.

4. **CatalogRow + DataSource TypeScript interfaces** extended with `use_cases?: string[]`.

### What ships

- `scripts/curate_vendor_use_cases.py` (NEW)
- `bundles/spark/data-sources/**/data_source.yaml` — 342 file edits
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_yaml_loader.py` — 3 small touches
- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx` — 5 edits in one file (type + filter state + filter chip strip + badge source swap + drawer pills)
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

### Closes

- (direct response to operator feedback; no separate issue)

### Operator-visible

After deploy + hard-refresh:
- Vendor cards show product-type badges (F5 → WAF + LoadBalancer, Okta → Identity + MFA, etc.) instead of XSIAM platform categories.
- A filter strip appears above the catalog. Click "WAF" → grid narrows to F5, Cloudflare, Imperva, Barracuda, Radware, Akamai, Fortinet. Click "MFA" → Okta, Duo, RSA, 1Password, SecureAuth, Silverfort, Thales, Keeper.
- Drawer header carries a "Use case: WAF · LoadBalancer" pill row above the description.
- Old `categories` field stays on the YAML as backend metadata; no operator-visible drift.

### v0.17.35 (next, optional)

`log_types:` per-pack curation (Network / Endpoint / Identity / CTI / Activity / Process / File) for the drawer. The operator mentioned this as a wish; not yet curated. v0.17.34 ships without it.

## [v0.17.33] — 2026-05-25 — *Data sources drawer UX pass: drop tag column, hide supported modules, fix dark-mode contrast, restyle Install buttons*

Operator-reported issues with the data-sources drawer + install buttons:

> "Now when I open a data source, I see Supported Modules and then XSIAM. We need to change that. … I see the name of the field, the type, description, the tag. I don't want the tag. … the column headers, the name, the type, the description — these are bluish blue color on a dark blue background, so it's not visible. … Install data source. Same. The text is blue and dark blue, so it's not quite visible from the dark theme. … the button itself is not stylish. It's not round. It's like square box."

### Changes

1. **Dropped "Tag" column from the fields table.** The vendor/meta distinction was already conveyed by the two surrounding section containers; the per-row `vendor`/`meta` badge was redundant + added blue-on-blue contrast noise. Remaining columns redistributed: Name 3→4, Type stays at 2, Description 5→6, summing to 12.

2. **Hid "Supported Modules" row in the drawer.** The backend hardcoded `["xsiam"]` for every YAML-loader row (XSIAM is the only deployment target Phantom ships against today), so the pill carried no operator-visible meaning. The slot is reserved for v0.17.34's use-case-tag pills.

3. **Fixed table column headers blue-on-blue in dark theme.** Header text switched from `text-primary` to `text-on-surface` (theme-aware: white-ish in dark, near-black in light). Header border switched from `border-primary/20` to `border-outline-variant/30` to soften the divider.

4. **Fixed StatTile count values blue-on-blue.** "Total Fields" and "Vendor Fields" tiles' value text was `text-primary` (blue) on a `bg-surface-container-low/60` background that picks up a blue tint in dark mode. Default `toneClass` switched to `text-on-surface`; secondary/tertiary tones (used for Version + Style tiles) are deliberate accent colors and stay.

5. **Restyled Install buttons — both render sites.** Two buttons fixed in lockstep:
   - `PackRow` Install (inside expanded VendorCard's dataset list)
   - `DetailDrawer` footer Install + Uninstall

   Before: `rounded` (4 px radius) + `bg-primary/10 text-primary border border-primary/20` (low-opacity tinted box, blue-on-blue in dark).
   After: `rounded-full` (pill shape, matches M3 button conventions used elsewhere) + `bg-primary text-on-primary hover:bg-primary-dim shadow-sm` (solid filled, WCAG AAA contrast). Uninstall stays low-opacity + bordered to keep visual weight asymmetric (destructive ≠ primary action).

### Files

- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx` — all 5 edits in one component
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

### Closes

- (direct response to operator feedback; no separate issue)

### Operator-visible

After deploy + hard-refresh:
- Drawer "Schema Fields" table shows Name / Type / Description (no Tag column). All header text readable in both themes.
- "Supported Modules: xsiam" row is gone.
- "Total Fields" + "Vendor Fields" tiles show clear numbers in both themes.
- Both Install buttons are pill-shaped, solid-filled, high-contrast.

### v0.17.34 preview

Use-case categorization arc (separate release):
- New `use_cases:` field in `data_source.yaml` schema
- Curated map: 137 vendors → list of one-word use-case labels (Avaya → Network, Box → Storage, F5 → WAF, Load Balancer, Okta → Identity, MFA, CyberArk → PAM, …)
- Replace category badges with use-case badges on vendor cards
- Add filter strip to the catalog so operators can filter to e.g. "WAF" or "Identity"
- New `log_types:` field at the pack level for the drawer (Network / Endpoint / Identity / Activity / Process / File / CTI)

## [v0.17.32] — 2026-05-25 — *Fill the last 3 vendor-logo gaps (Clearswift, SecureAuth, Semperis) — 100% coverage*

Operator direction: *"If you can't find SVG, create one. So check the image logo online of these vendors, and then let's create an SVG for them."*

After v0.17.31, three vendors still showed the placeholder. Sourced each via a different path:

| Vendor | Approach | Result |
|---|---|---|
| **Clearswift** | Wikipedia article PNG (`Clearswift_-_A_HelpSystems_Logo.png`, 11.6 KB) | Inlined as PNG. Real brand mark + HelpSystems sub-brand text. |
| **SecureAuth** | Their site's `/assets/secureauth-logo-white-JQccCguH.svg` (5 KB) had `fill: #fff;` — invisible on our near-white card panel. Recolored `#fff` → `#1A2238` (navy) for WCAG AA contrast on `#F7F8FA`. | Inlined as SVG. Genuine SecureAuth brand mark + wordmark, just dark fill instead of white. |
| **Semperis** | Site uses an empty inline data-URI for the logo; nothing fetchable. **Hand-crafted SVG** wordmark in Semperis brand navy `#0F1934` per operator direction. | Inlined as SVG. Clean typographic wordmark + stylized S-mark. Approximation, not the official mark. |

### Net visual result

| Metric | v0.17.31 | v0.17.32 |
|---|---|---|
| Vendor cards with logos | 133/136 | **136/136 — 100%** |
| Standalone library count | 134 files | **137 files** |

Every vendor card on `/data-sources` now shows a logo. The standalone library at `docs/assets/vendor-logos/` (added in the previous docs commit) also picked up the three new logos automatically when re-running `scripts/extract_vendor_logos_library.py`.

### Fidelity tagging

Each YAML's `logo:` block records a `fidelity:` field to flag the provenance:

| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `branded` | Logo is the vendor's actual brand mark, unmodified (e.g. Apache, Cisco, Microsoft via simple-icons; Arista, Avaya, etc. via Wikipedia Commons; Clearswift via Wikipedia PNG) |
| `branded-recolored` | Vendor's actual SVG, fills swapped for visibility on the near-white panel (SecureAuth) |
| `approximation` | Hand-crafted by the maintainer; resembles the vendor's branding but isn't their official artwork (Semperis) |
| `monochrome-brand` | simple-icons-style single-color mark in the vendor's brand color (most of v0.17.29's batch) |

This lets future maintainers/operators upgrade lower-fidelity entries to the vendor's official artwork when it becomes available.

### What ships

- `scripts/source_handcrafted_vendor_svgs.py` (NEW) — maintainer-only orchestration: embeds the three prepared assets (PNG / recolored SVG / hand-crafted SVG) into matching YAMLs.
- `bundles/spark/data-sources/ClearswiftDLP__ClearswiftDLP__clearswift_dlp_raw/data_source.yaml` — inline PNG logo
- `bundles/spark/data-sources/SecureAuthIdentityPlatform__SecureAuthIdentityPlatform__secureauth_idp_raw/data_source.yaml` — inline recolored SVG
- `bundles/spark/data-sources/SemperisDSP__SemperisDSP__semperis_dsp_raw/data_source.yaml` — inline hand-crafted SVG
- `docs/assets/vendor-logos/clearswift.png`, `secureauth.svg`, `semperis.svg` — standalone library entries
- `docs/assets/vendor-logos/manifest.yaml` + `README.md` — regenerated

### Files

- 3 × `bundles/spark/data-sources/**/data_source.yaml`
- 3 new files in `docs/assets/vendor-logos/`
- `docs/assets/vendor-logos/manifest.yaml` + `README.md` (regenerated)
- `scripts/source_handcrafted_vendor_svgs.py` (NEW)
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

## [v0.17.30] — 2026-05-25 — *Data sources catalog/logo cache: 3,400 ms → <1 ms per request (operator-reported slowness fix)*

**Operator reported the /data-sources page is "very slow"** to load after v0.17.27-29. Server-side timing on phantom-vm confirmed it's backend, not network:

```
=== Catalog API timing (v0.17.29) ===
  /api/v1/data-sources/catalog?xsiam_only=true
  Response time: 3403.2 ms
  Response size: 209368 bytes
  Row count: 330

=== Inline-logo route per pack (v0.17.29) ===
  AWS-CloudTrail    → 200  8235 B in 3397.1 ms
  AWS_ELB           → 200  3156 B in 3353.3 ms
  MacOS             → 200   650 B in 3295.7 ms
  Siemens_SiPass    → 200  1427 B in 3357.0 ms
  …
  Avg per logo: 3335.7 ms; 2376 B
```

Every request — even tiny 650-byte SVG fetches — took **3.3 seconds**. With 125 vendor card logos to fetch per page, that's ~135 s of backend work (50-70 s wall clock with browser's 6-way parallelism). Matches operator's "very slow" report exactly.

### Root cause

`DataSourcesYamlLoader.list_all()` walks both `bundle_root` and `user_root` and parses **all 342 bundled YAMLs from scratch on every call**. The loader's class docstring explicitly noted "re-read on every catalog request" but estimated the cost at ~1 MB read — that was true at the v0.13.0 baseline with smaller YAMLs and no inline base64 logos. Post-v0.17.28's logo-embedding pass, YAMLs grew much larger (some hold KB-sized base64 SVGs) and the cumulative parse load became 1 s+ per `list_all()` call.

Worse, `get_by_id()` was a linear scan — it called `list_all()` then iterated. The inline-logo route calls `get_by_id()` per request, so every logo fetch triggered a full directory scan + 342 YAML parses.

### Fix

`DataSourcesYamlLoader` now caches per-root scans by directory mtime:

- `_scan_cache: dict[str, tuple[mtime_ns, list[YamlDataSource]]]` — one entry per root.
- `_scan_root()` short-circuits when the root's `st_mtime_ns` matches the cached value. Modifying an entry inside the dir doesn't bump dir mtime (most cache hits land here), but **adding or removing** an entry does — automatic invalidation for upload/delete operations.
- `_id_index: dict[str, YamlDataSource]` — O(1) lookup for `get_by_id()`. Built lazily from `list_all()`; cleared whenever the scan cache invalidates.
- `write_user()` and `delete_user()` explicitly call `invalidate()` after writes, in case the filesystem quantizes mtime to 1 s and a same-second upload + read would otherwise miss the change.

### Benchmark (in-process, dev machine with 342 bundled YAMLs)

| Operation | Pre-v0.17.30 | Post-v0.17.30 |
|---|---|---|
| `list_all()` first call (cold cache) | 1,076 ms | 1,076 ms |
| `list_all()` second call | 1,076 ms | **0.0 ms** |
| 100 `get_by_id()` calls | ~107 s (107 ms × 100 full scans) | **0.03 ms total** |
| 125 vendor-card page load (backend time) | ~135 s | **< 10 ms** |

phantom-vm cold call is slower (3.3 s, due to container CPU + disk profile) but the WARM cost is the same — sub-millisecond. First /data-sources load: ~3 s. Every subsequent load: instant.

### Cache invariants

- **Bundle root never changes at runtime** — it's baked into the agent image. Cache effectively eternal until container restart.
- **User root changes only via upload/delete endpoints** — both call `invalidate()` after writing. Subsequent `list_all()` re-scans only user_root (bundle stays cached).
- **Container restart re-scans** by design — YAMLs are the source of truth; the cache is purely an in-memory accelerator.

### What ships

- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_yaml_loader.py` — `_scan_cache` + `_id_index` + `invalidate()`; `_scan_root()` uses mtime; `get_by_id()` uses dict lookup; `write_user()` + `delete_user()` invalidate explicitly.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_data_sources_yaml_loader.py` — 2 new cache tests (cold/warm + invalidation-on-write).

### Closes

- (no separate issue — direct response to operator's "slowness" complaint after v0.17.28 deploy)

### Operator-visible

- `/data-sources` first load goes from ~30-60 s to ~3-5 s. Subsequent loads (panel re-render, theme toggle, filter change) are effectively instant.
- Catalog endpoint and inline-logo route both share the same speedup — first hit warms the cache, all subsequent requests are sub-millisecond.

### Files

- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_yaml_loader.py`
- `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_data_sources_yaml_loader.py`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

---

## [v0.17.29] — 2026-05-25 — *Source 40 missing vendor logos from simple-icons (Apache, Apple, AWS, Cisco, Citrix, Dell, Fortinet, IBM, Kubernetes, Microsoft, MySQL, NGINX, Okta, VMware, …)*

After v0.17.28's structural pipeline fix, 22 vendor cards still showed the placeholder icon because those vendors genuinely had no baked SVG anywhere. v0.17.29 closes most of that gap by sourcing from simple-icons.

### What ships

- **`scripts/source_missing_vendor_svgs.py` (NEW, maintainer-only)** — pulls SVGs from the simple-icons CDN (https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/simple-icons@latest/icons/<slug>.svg) using a curated pack-name → slug mapping. Idempotent; skips YAMLs that already have inline logos.
- **40 `data_source.yaml` files** got inline base64 SVGs from simple-icons:
  - **Apache family**: ApacheTomcat (both rules), ApacheWebServer (both rules)
  - **Apple**: MacOS
  - **AWS**: AWS_ELB
  - **Cisco family**: CiscoASR, CiscoISR, CiscoNexus, Cisco_Wireless_LAN_Controller
  - **Citrix**: CitrixADC
  - **Dell**: DellEMCUnity
  - **F5**: F5APM, F5BigIPAWAF
  - **Fortinet**: FortiManager
  - **HashiCorp**: HashiCorp-Vault
  - **Huawei**: HuaweiNetworkDevices
  - **IBM**: IBM_AIX
  - **Juniper**: JuniperSRX (both rules)
  - **Kubernetes**: Kubernetes
  - **Linux**: Auditd
  - **Microsoft**: MicrosoftIISWebServer, MicrosoftNPS (both rules), MicrosoftWSUS (both rules)
  - **MySQL**: MySQLEnterprise (both rules)
  - **NGINX**: NGINXWebServer (both rules)
  - **Okta**: OktaOAG (both rules)
  - **Siemens**: Siemens_SiPass
  - **SonicWall**: SonicWallNSv
  - **Tableau**: Tableau (both rules)
  - **TrendMicro**: TrendMicroInterScanWebSecurity
  - **VMware**: VMwareESXi, VMwareVCenter (both rules)
  - **Zoho (for ManageEngine)**: ManageEngine-ADManager (both rules)

License: SVGs from simple-icons are CC0-1.0. Each embedded blob's `source:` field records `simpleicons:<slug>` for provenance.

### Still missing (13 packs / ~10 vendor cards)

The simple-icons collection doesn't cover every brand. These packs still have `logo: null` and the placeholder icon:

- AristaSwitch (slug `arista` not on simple-icons)
- AvayaAuraCommunicationManager
- BarracudaEmailProtection / BarracudaWAF (slug `barracuda` not on simple-icons)
- BeyondTrustPrivilegedRemoteAccess / BeyondTrustRemoteSupport (Barracuda_Cloudgen_Firewall + BeyondTrust_Password_Safe still carry their baked logos from v0.17.28's inline-embed pass, so the Barracuda / BeyondTrust VENDOR cards now show their logos — just not these specific data sources)
- BrocadeSwitch (both rules)
- HPESwitch
- Kiteworks
- RSASecureID
- Squid
- TigeraCalico

These need hand-curated SVGs (probably from each vendor's official press kit). Tracked for future content work.

### Net visual result

- **Pre-v0.17.29**: 22 vendor cards showed placeholder icon
- **Post-v0.17.29**: ~10 vendor cards still show placeholder (the unfilled list above)
- **Individual data-source cards**: 40 more cards now show real logos

### Closes

- (no separate issue — rides with #93's "still need external SVG sourcing" follow-up note)

- `scripts/source_missing_vendor_svgs.py` (NEW)
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

## [v0.17.28] — 2026-05-25 — *Data sources logo pipeline: inline-embed baked logos, fix VendorCard row picking, drop 404-prone legacy URLs from catalog*

**Operator-reported regressions on `/data-sources` after v0.17.27**: many vendor cards showed the placeholder icon instead of their logo. Audit identified two distinct root causes + a perf side-effect.

### Root cause 1 — VendorCard picks the wrong row

After v0.17.27's vendor-rebucketing, vendor groups contain multiple packs. `VendorCard` used `rows[0].logo_url` — whichever pack happened to be first in the catalog response. If that pack had no baked SVG, the whole card showed the placeholder, **even when OTHER packs in the same vendor group carried valid logos**.

Audit found 4 affected vendor groups:

| Vendor | First row (404'd) | Available alternative |
|---|---|---|
| barracuda | BarracudaEmailProtection | Barracuda_Cloudgen_Firewall (323 B) |
| beyondtrust | BeyondTrustPrivilegedRemoteAccess | BeyondTrust_Password_Safe (3,705 B) |
| f5 | F5APM | F5ASM (2,576 B — the inline base64 from v0.13.0) |
| linux | Auditd | LinuxEventsCollection (5,463 B) |

**Fix**: `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx` builds the vendor card with `rows.find((r) => r.logo_url) ?? rows[0]` for the logo source. Vendor card now uses whichever pack in the group has a working logo; falls back to first row's null (= placeholder icon) only when no pack in the group has any.

### Root cause 2 — Catalog always returned a logo URL, even for packs guaranteed to 404

`_compute_logo_url()` always returned `/api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack>` for bundle packs without inline logo. The UI then fetched that URL, the MCP walked the baked tree, found nothing for the 22 vendors with no baked asset (Apache, Apple, Amazon, Arista, Brocade, Citrix, Dell EMC, Ivanti, Juniper, Kubernetes, NGINX, RSA, Squid, Tableau, etc.), and returned 404. Per-card visible cost: a wasted HTTPS round-trip + bearer-auth check + filesystem scan + 404 response. Multiplied by 22 cards on first page load = noticeable slow first paint, AND those 22 cards eventually showed the placeholder icon when `<LogoOrFallback>`'s `onError` fired.

**Fix**: `_compute_logo_url()` returns `None` when the YAML has no inline logo. UI renders the placeholder icon **directly** without making a request. Saves 22+ wasted round-trips per page load.

The legacy `/api/v1/data-sources/logo/<pack>` route remains live on the MCP for backward compat with older agent images, but new catalogs don't reference it.

### Root cause 3 — 21 packs WITH baked logos didn't have them in their YAMLs yet

After the v0.17.25 refresh added 82 new packs, `migrate_missing_packs.py` set `logo: null` for all of them. Some of those packs (BeyondTrust_Password_Safe, BluecatAddressManager, CiscoSMA, F5LTM, FortiManager, HashiCorp-Vault, Infoblox, IronPort, McAfee*, MicrosoftECM, Symantec*, etc.) ACTUALLY had baked legacy `_dark.svg` or `_image.png` files — but the YAMLs didn't know, so post-v0.17.28's null-catalog fix they'd lose their logos.

**Fix**: `scripts/inline_embed_baked_logos.py` (NEW, maintainer-only per `scripts/CLAUDE.md`) walks the baked tree for every YAML with `logo: null`, picks the best available asset (priority `_image_light.svg` > `_dark.svg` > `_image.png` > `Author_image.png`), and base64-embeds it into the YAML's `logo:` block. **21 YAMLs got inline logos this way.**

### Net result

- **Pre-v0.17.28**: 110 vendor cards had logos, 26 showed placeholder (22 truly missing + 4 wrong-row-picked).
- **Post-v0.17.28**: estimated **131 vendor cards have logos** (110 + 21 newly inline-embedded), **5 still show placeholder** (operator-installed CiscoSMA/BeyondTrust etc. now find a real logo; the 22 truly-missing vendors stay placeholder until external SVG sourcing).

The 22 vendors with no baked asset anywhere — Apache, Apple, Amazon, Arista, AWS_ELB, Avaya, Barracuda*, Brocade, Cisco-ASR/ISR/Nexus/WLC, Citrix, Clearswift, Dell EMC, F5APM/BigIPAWAF, HPE, Huawei, IBM, Ivanti, Juniper, Kiteworks, Kubernetes, Linux/Auditd, MacOS, ManageEngine, MySQL, NGINX, RSA, SecureAuth, Semperis, Siemens, SonicWall, Squid, Tableau, Tigera, TrendMicro — remain `logo: null` until an external SVG-sourcing pass (separate research task, simple-icons / vendor websites).

### What ships

- `scripts/inline_embed_baked_logos.py` (NEW) — maintainer one-shot logo-embedding tool. Idempotent.
- `bundles/spark/data-sources/<21 directories>/data_source.yaml` — newly inline-embedded base64 logos.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_yaml_loader.py` — `_compute_logo_url()` returns null when YAML has no inline logo.
- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx` — VendorCard logo source is now `rows.find(r => r.logo_url) ?? rows[0]`.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_data_sources_yaml_loader.py` — 2 new tests for null-when-no-inline behavior; existing legacy-route test renamed + updated.

### Closes

- #93 (this fix's tracking issue)

### Operator-visible

- F5 card now shows the F5 logo (from F5ASM's inline base64), not the placeholder.
- Barracuda card shows the Barracuda_Cloudgen_Firewall logo (or whichever Barracuda pack has one); not placeholder.
- BeyondTrust card shows its logo (was placeholder pre-fix).
- Linux/Auditd card shows the LinuxEventsCollection logo.
- ~21 individual data-source cards (BeyondTrust_Password_Safe, F5LTM, FortiManager, CiscoSMA, Infoblox, IronPort, McAfee*, MicrosoftECM, Symantec*, etc.) start showing their baked logos.
- First-page-load is measurably faster: 22 fewer round-trips for guaranteed-404 logo fetches.
- The 22 placeholder-icon vendors are unchanged visually (they were always placeholder; this release just doesn't make pointless requests for them).

### Files

- `bundles/spark/data-sources/**/data_source.yaml` (21 edits)
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_yaml_loader.py`
- `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_data_sources_yaml_loader.py`
- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx`
- `scripts/inline_embed_baked_logos.py` (NEW)
- `CHANGELOG.md`

## [v0.17.27] — 2026-05-25 — *Data sources UI fix pass: vendor grouping, missing logos, white-on-white regression*

**Operator-found bugs after the v0.17.25 +82-packs refresh.** Three visible regressions on `/data-sources`:

### Bug 1 — F5 family not grouped under F5 vendor card

After v0.17.25 added F5LTM, F5APM, and F5BigIPAWAF, those packs showed up as their own isolated cards instead of grouping under the existing F5 vendor card (which already held F5ASM).

**Root cause**: `scripts/migrate_missing_packs.py` (v0.17.25) sets `vendor: <pack_name>` whenever the pack has no `vendor_map.yaml` entry. Result: F5LTM's YAML had `vendor: F5LTM` → `vendor_key: f5ltm` → standalone card. Same pattern hit 75 of the 82 new packs across many vendor families.

**Fix**: `scripts/fix_v17_25_vendor_buckets.py` walks every bundled `data_source.yaml` and rewrites `vendor:` to the canonical display name based on a curated map. **75 YAMLs updated**, covering F5 (4), Cisco (8 incl. IronPort + cisco-meraki + cisco-ise), Apache (4), Microsoft (5), Symantec (3), VMware (3), Brocade (2), BeyondTrust (3), Tanium (2), Okta (2), Ivanti (2), Juniper (2), McAfee (2), Barracuda (2), MySQL (2), NGINX (2), Thinkst (2), Radware (2), plus 16 standalone vendors (Arista → "Arista", AWS_ELB → "Amazon", MacOS → "Apple", FortiManager → "Fortinet", IBM_AIX → "IBM", HashiCorp-Vault → "HashiCorp", etc.).

### Bug 2 — 67 packs with structured fields hidden from default Browse view

Same migration set `is_rawlog_only: true` on packs that had no upstream modeling-rule schema, but the v0.17.24 .xif alter-intermediate extractor THEN populated `fields[]` from regex captures. Result: 67 packs had `is_rawlog_only: true` AND non-empty `fields[]` — the default Browse filter (which excludes rawlog-only) was hiding packs with rich field schemas.

**Fix**: same script flips `is_rawlog_only` to `false` for any pack with `len(fields) >= 1`. **71 YAMLs updated**.

### Bug 3 — `BrowsePackCard` white-on-white logo regression

`v0.11.1` made all data-source card logo panels use `style={{ backgroundColor: "#F7F8FA" }}` (constant near-white) instead of the theme-coupled `bg-surface-container-low/40` class. That fix landed on `VendorCard`, `InstalledCard`, and `DetailDrawer` — but missed `BrowsePackCard`. White-fill `_dark.svg` legacy fallback SVGs disappeared on dark theme; the regression was operator-reported.

### Bug 4 — Empty panel when logo URL 404s

`onError` on all three card components called `style.display = "none"` on the failing `<img>` — without rendering the fallback `inventory_2` icon. Net result: the panel stayed empty (no logo, no icon). Most visible on the 82 v0.17.25 packs whose vendor has no baked SVG yet.

**Fix**: new `<LogoOrFallback>` component owns the panel background + img + state-based fallback icon. All four render sites (VendorCard, BrowsePackCard, InstalledCard, DetailDrawer) refactored through it. Identical behavior in every site, easier to maintain, impossible to forget the constant-bg or the fallback-icon for any future site.

### Bug 5 — Bundle packs ignore inline YAML `logo:` field

`_compute_logo_url()` always returned `/api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack>` for bundle packs (which only reads from the baked `vendor_map.yaml` + `vendor_svgs/` tree). If a bundle YAML carried an inline base64 SVG in its `logo:` block, that inline data was silently ignored.

**Fix**: when YAML has inline `logo:`, `_compute_logo_url()` now returns `/api/agent/data-sources/inline-logo/<id>` regardless of origin. New backend route + Next.js proxy stream the inline bytes. This makes bundle YAMLs SELF-CONTAINED for logos — future SVG sourcing can land directly in the YAMLs without rebuilding the baked tree.

### What ships

- `bundles/spark/data-sources/<82 directories>/data_source.yaml` — 75 vendor field updates + 71 `is_rawlog_only` flips (146 file edits total, some YAMLs got both fixes).
- `scripts/fix_v17_25_vendor_buckets.py` (NEW) — idempotent cohort fixer. Documents the canonical vendor map for reference.
- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx` — new `LogoOrFallback` component; all four render sites refactored through it.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_yaml_loader.py` — `_compute_logo_url()` now prefers inline-logo route when YAML has `logo:` block.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py` — new `GET /api/v1/data-sources/inline-logo/{id}` route. Existing `/user/{id}/logo` preserved for backward compat.
- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/inline-logo/[id]/route.ts` (NEW) — Next.js proxy.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_data_sources_yaml_loader.py` — 2 new tests covering bundle-with-inline + bundle-without-inline routing; existing user-route test renamed + updated.

### Closes

- #92 (this fix's tracking issue)

### Operator-visible

After installing v0.17.27 on phantom-vm:
- `/data-sources` Browse → F5 card now lists F5ASM, F5APM, F5LTM, F5BigIPAWAF together. Same for Cisco, Microsoft, Apache, Symantec, VMware, BeyondTrust, etc.
- 67 new packs (BeyondTrust*, Apache*, Arista, Brocade, Auditd, CitrixADC, BarracudaWAF, etc.) become visible in the default Browse view.
- All card logo panels render the `inventory_2` placeholder icon (light gray on near-white) instead of an empty box when the vendor SVG is missing.
- White-fill SVG packs (FortiGate, AWSGuardDuty, etc.) display correctly on dark theme.

### Known follow-up (separate release)

82 packs from v0.17.25 still have `logo: null` and no baked vendor SVG → operator sees the `inventory_2` placeholder. The plumbing for inline SVGs is now ready (`Bug 5` fix); SVG sourcing for those 82 vendors is its own content task.

### Files

- `bundles/spark/data-sources/**/data_source.yaml` (146 edits)
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_yaml_loader.py`
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py`
- `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_data_sources_yaml_loader.py`
- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx`

## [v0.17.26] — 2026-05-25 — *xlog: fix OverrideSender wire format (CEF/SYSLOG/LEEF, not JSON)*

**Root-cause bug discovered during deep E2E smoke** (10 vendors × 24 events each = 240 CEF events → 0 rows landed in `<vendor>_<product>_raw` datasets).

### Bug

`xlog/app/override_sender.py` always JSON-encoded records on the wire regardless of `data_type`. Comment in the code said *"kept JSON for v1 because Cortex's modeling rules typically accept JSON-over-UDP for testing"* — that assumption is wrong for the broker → XSIAM ingestion path.

### Why the broker dropped JSON

XSIAM brokers (syslog Collectors) route incoming data to `<vendor>_<product>_raw` datasets by **parsing the CEF/LEEF header out of incoming syslog**. The header `CEF:0|Fortinet|FortiGate|…` is what tells the broker *"this row goes in `fortinet_fortigate_raw`"*. Plain JSON has no such header — broker either drops it or routes to a generic fallback, which is what the smoke test showed.

### Fix

`OverrideSender.format_record()` now branches on `self.data_type`:

| data_type | Wire format |
|---|---|
| `CEF` | `<134>Mon DD HH:MM:SS hostname CEF:0\|Vendor\|Product\|Version\|SigID\|Name\|Severity\|key=value …` |
| `SYSLOG` | `<134>Mon DD HH:MM:SS hostname vendor-product: key=value …` |
| `LEEF` | `<134>Mon DD HH:MM:SS hostname LEEF:2.0\|Vendor\|Product\|Version\|EventID\|key=value …` |
| anything else (JSON / WINEVENT / Incident / XSIAM_*) | JSON (existing v0.12.0 behavior) |

- CEF header escapes `\` and `|` per ArcSight CEF:0 spec.
- CEF extension escapes `\`, `=`, `\n` (per the same spec).
- Lists/dicts in record values are JSON-stringified into the extension (no native CEF array form).
- Vendor/Product come from the constructor — they're what the agent passes via `schema_override` and what the broker uses for dataset routing.
- SignatureID/Name/Severity in CEF header are best-effort hoisted from record-level fields (`signatureId`/`signature_id`, `name`/`msg`/`event`, `severity`); fall back to neutral defaults (100 / "Event" / 5).

### What ships

- `xlog/app/override_sender.py` — helpers `_escape_cef_header()`, `_escape_cef_extension_value()`, `_flatten_extension()`, `_syslog_timestamp()`, `_hostname()`. Public `OverrideSender.format_record()` dispatches; `_format_cef()` / `_format_syslog()` / `_format_leef()` build the wire string.
- `xlog/tests/test_override_sender_format.py` — 22 new tests covering CEF/SYSLOG/LEEF/JSON branches + escaping + header hoisting + edge cases (empty values, lists, dicts, missing severity, non-int severity, lowercase data_type, None data_type).

### Closes

- #91 (this fix's tracking issue)

### Operator-visible

- After re-installing v0.17.26 on phantom-vm, any worker created via the UI's "Generate fake data → Send to destination" path (or via `createDataWorker` MCP tool) with `type=CEF`/`SYSLOG`/`LEEF` and a `udp:` or `tcp:` destination will now produce broker-parseable output. Logs sent to the operator's XSIAM broker will land in `<vendor>_<product>_raw` datasets as expected.

### Files

- `xlog/app/override_sender.py`
- `xlog/tests/test_override_sender_format.py` (NEW)
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

## [v0.17.24] — 2026-05-24 — *Field descriptions Phase 4k: extract .xif `alter` intermediates for rawlog packs — +1117 fields*

Operator surfaced that **Cisco Catalyst and several other packs only had 2-3 fields** in the schema preview drawer despite the vendors emitting much richer log data. Root cause: v0.17.9's parser captured only `xdm.X = <bare raw_field>` assignments. For vendors whose modeling rules parse fields from `_raw_log` via regex (`arrayindex(regextract(_raw_log, "..."), 0)`), the actual vendor field names live as INTERMEDIATE variables in `alter` clauses, mapped to `xdm.X` only via wrappers like `coalesce()` or `if()` that v0.17.9 didn't unwrap.

### What ships

- **`scripts/extract_xif_alter_intermediates.py`** — new maintainer script that walks the 232 `.xif` files, parses every `<identifier> = <expr>` line, captures intermediate vendor field names, derives descriptions from xdm mappings, infers field types from name patterns.

- **1117 fields added across 31 packs**. Biggest beneficiaries:

| Pack | Fields added |
|---|---|
| VMwareVCenter | +184 |
| VMwareESXi | +107 |
| FireEyeHX audit | +106 |
| LinuxEventsCollection | +78 |
| HuaweiFW | +70 |
| Trellix ePO | +44 |
| ProofpointCasb | +44 |
| MicrosoftIISWebServer | +41 |
| SymantecBlueCoatProxySG | +38 |
| ReblazeWAF | +26 |
| MicrosoftECM | +24 |
| GoogleChrome | +22 |

### Pre-deploy gate

- pytest: 498 pass
- All 260 YAMLs still parse + schema-validate

### Companion observation — not addressed in this release

Comparing our bundle to upstream demisto/content:
- Our bundle: 197 packs / 260 datasets
- Upstream: ≥184 packs with modeling rules (GitHub tree API truncated — actual count higher)
- **At least 49 missing packs**: F5APM, F5LTM, F5BigIPAWAF (operator's stated complaint), Cisco ASR/ISR/Nexus/SMA/UCM/Wireless, Citrix/CitrixADC, BeyondTrust (4 packs), Juniper SRX, Apache Tomcat/HTTP, MicrosoftNPS/WSUS, MacOS, NGINX, Kubernetes, Auditd.

Closing this gap requires refreshing `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/` from upstream. v0.17.25 candidate.

## [v0.17.23] — 2026-05-24 — *Hot-fix: SELECT was missing the description column (v0.17.7 instrumentation gap)*

**Post-deploy verification of v0.17.22 caught a second part of the same bug.**

v0.17.22 fixed `_compose_from_user_yaml` to pass `description` through on install. Verified the descriptions reached SQLite (`SELECT description FROM data_source_fields` showed proper values for F5ASM). But the schema endpoint STILL returned empty descriptions because **`get_with_schema`'s SELECT only fetched 4 columns** — `field_name, field_type, is_array, is_meta` — silently dropping `description`. The dataclass mapping then defaulted to `""`.

### Root cause

v0.17.7 instrumentation gap: added the column to the migration, the INSERT, and the dataclass + to_dict — but missed adding it to the SELECT. The `"description" in r.keys()` defensive check I added papered over the missing SELECT silently.

### What ships

- **`get_with_schema` SELECT** now includes `description`:
  ```sql
  SELECT field_name, field_type, is_array, is_meta, description
  FROM data_source_fields
  ```

### Pre-deploy gate

- pytest: 498 pass

---

## [v0.17.22] — 2026-05-24 — *Hot-fix: F5ASM install 500 (duplicate field name) + drop-of-description-on-install bug*

**Operator caught two real bugs trying to install F5 ASM after the v0.17.21 ship:**

### Bug 1 — Install 500 from duplicate field name (sqlite3.IntegrityError)

The v0.16.x manual F5ASM curation accidentally listed `date_time` twice in `fields[]`. Install routes via `_compose_from_user_yaml` for any bundle pack with populated `fields[]` (v0.16.1 extension), and the SQLite INSERT into `data_source_fields` has `UNIQUE (data_source_id, field_name)` — installing F5ASM crashed with `sqlite3.IntegrityError: UNIQUE constraint failed`.

### Bug 2 — Install was silently dropping ALL field descriptions

`_compose_from_user_yaml` built `DataSourceField` objects WITHOUT passing through `description`. Mirror bug to the one v0.17.10 fixed for the preview path. Net effect: when an operator installed any pack post-v0.17.11, the resulting `data_source_fields` rows had every description set to empty string. The drawer's preview overlay (`_yaml_field_descriptions`) still showed YAML-sourced descriptions, but **installed** rows lost them.

### What ships

1. **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py` — `_compose_from_user_yaml`** now:
   - **Dedupes** by field name (first occurrence wins) — defensive against operator-uploaded YAMLs too
   - **Pulls through `description`** when building `DataSourceField` objects

2. **`bundles/spark/data-sources/F5ASM__F5ASMModelingRules_1_3__F5_ASM_raw/data_source.yaml`** — removed the duplicate `date_time` entry.

3. **`tooling/validate/validate_all.py` — new check `check_bundled_data_sources_no_duplicate_fields()`**: every bundled `data_source.yaml`'s `fields[]` has unique names. Caught the F5ASM regression; would have caught it at v0.16.x curation if we'd had this gate.

### How it slipped past v0.16.x

We had `check_bundled_data_sources_yaml_valid` (schema-validates each YAML against `data_source.schema.json`) — but the JSON schema's `fields` array has no `uniqueItems` constraint on `name`. Adding `uniqueItems: true` wouldn't even help (it compares whole objects, not just `name`). A separate semantic check is the right fix.

### Pre-deploy gate

- pytest: 498 pass
- validator: **19/19** (new dup-field check passes after F5ASM cleanup)
- bug-family audit: grep for duplicate field names across all 260 YAMLs returned **only F5ASM** — no other packs affected

---

## [v0.17.16] — 2026-05-24 — *Field descriptions Phase 4e: Trend Micro + Claroty + FireEye + Mimecast*

Phase 4e bundles four vendor dictionaries; coverage now **82.3%**.

### What ships

- **`scripts/apply_phase4e_descriptions.py`** — four vendor dictionaries + extended CEF range (cs7-cs22):
  - **Claroty** (38 filled, **complete**) — Continuous Threat Detection uses CEF custom-string fields beyond the standard cs1-cs6 range. cs7-cs22 + their lowercase `cs<N>label` variants are auto-generated as "Device custom string N (see csNLabel)".
  - **Mimecast** (34 filled, **complete**) — Email Security Gateway: Sender, Recipient, MsgId, Subject, Virus, AttNames, fileExt/Mime/md5/sha256, ScanResultInfo, Audit fields.
  - **Trend Micro** (38 filled, near-complete) — Vision One (entityName, mitreMapping, impactScope, indicators, malName, score, severity), Deep Security (TrendMicroDsCve/Mitre/MalwareTarget/Tags), Email Security (deliveryTime, embeddedUrls, policyAction, etc.).
  - **FireEye** (12 filled in HX pack) — event_values, condition, categoryTupleDescription, cs7-cs10 with FireEye-specific semantic notes.

- **122 fields filled across 8 packs.**

### Coverage progression

| Phase | Coverage | Missing |
|---|---|---|
| v0.17.14 (+SF/NS/SN) | 79.9% | 1020 |
| **v0.17.16 (+TM/CL/FE/MC)** | **82.3%** | **898** |

### Insight: extending CEF beyond the spec

The ArcSight CEF spec officially defines `cs1` through `cs6` as device custom strings. Several vendors (Claroty most aggressively, FireEye HX) extend this to `cs7` through `cs22` for richer device-specific telemetry, following the same semantics: `cs<N>` holds a string, `cs<N>Label` names it. v0.17.11's CEF dictionary covered 1-6; v0.17.16's extends through 22, plus lowercase `cs<N>label` variants Claroty uses.

### Remaining top vendor gaps (Phase 4f)

| Vendor | Fields |
|---|---|
| Microsoft (specialty AzureWAF/AppService/AppSentinels) | 148 |
| Cloudflare | 33 |
| Workday | 33 |
| Oracle | 30 |
| Palo Alto Networks | 26 |

### Pre-deploy gate
