## [v0.17.10] — 2026-05-24 — *Hot-fix: YAML description overlay (v0.17.7 bug)*

**Post-deploy verification of v0.17.9 (Phase 3 backfill) revealed that descriptions weren't surfacing on the schema endpoint** — the operator's original bug case (AWS-SecurityHub `Id`, `Title`, `Description`) still showed em-dashes in the drawer despite Phase 3 writing 2394 descriptions into the YAMLs.

Root cause: v0.17.7's `_yaml_field_descriptions()` overlay helper called `loader.get(ds_id)` with a constructed `pack__rule__dataset` key — but the loader's `get_by_id()` matches the YAML's short id field (e.g. `AWS-SecurityHub`), not the triple. The right method is `get_by_3tuple(pack, rule, dataset)` which the loader exposes explicitly for this case.

### What ships

- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py`: `_yaml_field_descriptions()` now uses `loader.get_by_3tuple(pack_name, rule_name, dataset_name)`.

### Why this is a v0.17.10 (not amending v0.17.9)

Per CLAUDE.md § Wait-for-CI discipline, never amend deployed code — always ship a fresh release. The Phase 3 backfill itself was correct; only the overlay surface had the bug.

### What renumbers

The previously-planned v0.17.10 (Phase 4 vendor-doc backfill) becomes **v0.17.11**.

### Pre-deploy gate

- pytest: 498 pass

---

## [v0.17.9] — 2026-05-24 — *Field descriptions Phase 3: backfill 2394 descriptions from XDM mappings*

Phase 3 of the v0.17.7 → v0.17.10 arc. Parses the 232 .xif modeling-rule files fetched in v0.17.8 and writes derived descriptions back into the bundled YAMLs.

### What ships

- **`scripts/parse_xdm_into_descriptions.py`** — one-time + re-runnable backfill:
  1. Parses each `.xif` for `xdm.X = <expr>` assignments
  2. Unwraps wrappers (`to_string()`, `coalesce()`, `if()`, `arrayindex(regextract(...))`, etc.) to extract bare raw field names
  3. Builds per-(pack, rule) `raw_field → xdm_canonical_name` map (4857 mappings total across 366 unique XDM names)
  4. Generates human descriptions algorithmically: `xdm.source.ipv4` → "Source IPv4", `xdm.target.host.fqdn` → "Destination host FQDN", with abbreviation table (FQDN, IPv4, URL, HTTP, etc.)
  5. Walks all 260 YAMLs; for each field without a description, writes the XDM-derived one. **Skips fields that already have descriptions** (v0.16.x curated entries — those are richer than algorithmic derivation)
  6. Uses PyYAML for both reads + writes (replacing the v0.17.6 era naive regex parser that under-counted ~500 fields)

- **188 `data_source.yaml` files updated** with new descriptions:

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| YAMLs scanned | 260 |
| YAMLs missing modeling rule (rawlog-only / deprecated) | 35 |
| **YAMLs modified** | **188** |
| Total fields | 4515 |
| Already had description (v0.16.x curated) | 862 |
| **Newly filled from XDM** | **2394** |
| Still missing (Phase 4 candidates) | 1259 |
| **Coverage after this run** | **72.1%** (was 21%) |

### Examples (operator's original bug-report cases)

- **F5ASM** (`cs1`, `cs3`, ...): preserved v0.16.x curation — "F5 device hostname", "ASM policy name", "HTTP response code", "Client IP"
- **AWS-SecurityHub** (previously 0 fields, then 10 from v0.17.6 cortex extraction):
  - `Id` → "Alert original alert ID"
  - `Title` → "Alert name"
  - `Description` → "Alert description"
  - `Resources` → "Destination resource type"
  - `ProductName` → "Observer product"
  - `Severity` → still missing (no XDM mapping in modeling rule; goes to Phase 4)

### Why some fields still have NO description (1259 fields)

Three patterns:
1. **CheckpointFirewall raw CEF fields** (`cs1`, `cs2`, `rt`, etc.) — modeling rule uses these but assigns them via intermediate `alter` steps that the regex parser doesn't trace
2. **Vendor-specific extensions** (`Office365:UserType`, `DuoAdminApi:integration`) — vendor doc terms that aren't in XDM
3. **Free-form payload fields** (`GoogleCloudLogging:textPayload`) — opaque to the modeling rule; vendor doc has the schema

Phase 4 (v0.17.10) handles these via vendor doc research.

### Pre-deploy gate

- pytest: 498 pass (no Python code touched; YAML data only)

---

## [v0.17.8] — 2026-05-24 — *Field descriptions Phase 2: fetch demisto/content modeling rules*

Phase 2 of the v0.17.7 → v0.17.10 arc. The .xif modeling-rule files from `demisto/content` carry the canonical `xdm.X = raw_field` mappings that tell us what every raw field MEANS. Phase 3 (v0.17.9) parses them; this release fetches + bakes them as maintainer reference.

### What ships

- **`scripts/fetch_demisto_modeling_rules.py`** — one-time + re-runnable fetcher. For each unique (pack, rule) pair in our 259 data sources, fetches `raw.githubusercontent.com/demisto/content/master/Packs/<pack>/ModelingRules/<rule>/<rule>.xif`. Cache-first: skips fetches when the local .xif already exists; `--refresh` ignores the cache.

- **`scripts/maintainer/modeling_rules/*.xif`** — **232 files / 2.45 MB**. Placed under `scripts/maintainer/` (NOT under `bundles/spark/`) so the agent Dockerfile's `COPY bundles/spark /app/bundle` doesn't bake them into customer images. Maintainer-only reference data.

- **Fetch stats**: 232/232 fetched successfully (0 not-found, 0 errors). One single run took ~25 seconds via raw.githubusercontent.com CDN.

- **Reconnaissance findings**:
  - 397 unique `xdm.*` canonical names across all 232 modeling rules — that's the dictionary size for Phase 3's XDM-name → human-description map
  - Format is consistent: `xdm.X = raw_field_or_expression` with wrappers like `to_string()`, `coalesce()`, `to_integer()`, `if(...)`, `arrayindex(regextract(...))` — Phase 3's parser needs to extract bare raw-field names from these expressions
  - Sample F5ASM mappings (from operator's bug report):
    - `xdm.network.rule = cs1` → cs1 means "Network rule"
    - `xdm.source.port = to_integer(spt)` → spt means "Source port"
    - `xdm.target.port = to_integer(dpt)` → dpt means "Target/Destination port"

### Pre-deploy gate

- pytest: 498 pass (no Python code touched at runtime; maintainer script + data files only)

### What comes next

Phase 3 (v0.17.9) — write `scripts/parse_xdm_into_descriptions.py`:
1. Walk all 232 .xif files
2. Parse `xdm.X = ...` assignments, extract bare raw-field names from expressions
3. Build the curated `xdm.<canonical>` → human description dictionary (~397 entries)
4. For each field in each data_source.yaml without a description, look up its XDM mapping → set description from the dict
5. Stats: how many of 4022 missing descriptions can be filled from XDM (estimated 70-90%)

---

## [v0.17.7] — 2026-05-24 — *Field descriptions: schema + UI foundation (Phase 1 of 4)*

**Phase 1 of a multi-release arc** (v0.17.7 → v0.17.10) backfilling per-field descriptions for all 5078 fields across 259 data sources. Today the agent only knows a field's *name* and *type*; it has to guess what `cs1` or `srcip` *means*. After the arc, descriptions like "Source IPv4 address" / "User session id" / "Operator action" flow through from the Cortex XDM model (Phase 2-3) and vendor docs (Phase 4), so the agent picks correct values when generating logs and the operator sees them in the schema drawer.

### Current baseline (audited)

- 5078 total fields across 259 data sources
- 1056 already have descriptions (21%, from v0.16.x manual curation of 32 vendors)
- **4022 fields need backfill** — exactly matches the v0.17.6 cortex-extracted set that shipped without descriptions

### What ships THIS release (Phase 1 — foundation)

- **`data_source.schema.json` already had `description`** in the Field $def from R3.C.0 — no schema change needed. Phase 1's job is wiring it through the runtime + UI.

- **`DataSourceField` dataclass** (`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_store.py`): added `description: str = ""` field.

- **SQLite migration** (idempotent, PRAGMA-guarded): `ALTER TABLE data_source_fields ADD COLUMN description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''`. Existing installs upgrade silently on next boot.

- **INSERT + row→dataclass** updated to round-trip description.

- **`DataSourceWithSchema.to_dict()`** emits `description` in each fields[] entry.

- **`_yaml_field_descriptions()` overlay helper** in `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py`: looks up the bundled YAML for a pack/rule/dataset triple, returns `{field_name: description}`. Bridge from cortex extraction (which only has name + type) to operator-curated descriptions.

- **Preview path** (`/api/v1/data-sources/{pack}/{rule}/{dataset}/schema`) overlays YAML descriptions onto cortex-extracted fields — uninstalled packs show descriptions in the drawer without requiring install. The 32 v0.16.x-curated packs immediately show their descriptions; the 227 v0.17.6-extracted ones show em-dash until v0.18.x backfills.

- **UI Description column** (`mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx`):
  - Layout: NAME (3) / TYPE (2) / **DESCRIPTION (5)** / TAG (2) — was 6/3/_/3
  - `FieldRow` renders `field.description` truncated with hover tooltip; em-dash placeholder when empty
  - `DataSourceField` TypeScript interface gains optional `description?: string`
  - Filter now matches name OR description (operator can search "Source IP" and find `src` / `srcip` / `source_ip`)
  - Placeholder updated: "Filter by name or description…"

- **Pytest update**: `test_with_schema_to_dict_includes_fields_and_mappings` asserts the new keyset.

### What comes next (autonomous loop)

| Phase | Release | What |
|---|---|---|
| **Phase 2** | v0.17.8 | Fetch `Packs/<pack>/ModelingRules/<rule>/<rule>.xif` from `demisto/content` for all 259 packs |
| **Phase 3** | v0.17.9 | Parse XDM mappings → `raw_field → xdm.<canonical> → human description` lookup |
| **Phase 4** | v0.17.10+ | Vendor-doc research for fields without an XDM mapping; create `data/knowledge/external/<vendor>/<product>/fields.md` reference docs |

### Pre-deploy gate

- pytest: 498 pass (1 test updated for the new keyset)
- tsc OK

## [v0.17.6] — 2026-05-24 — *Data sources: auto-extract cortex-content fields into the 227 packs v0.16.x didn't curate*

**Bug operator surfaced from the v0.17.5 install:** the Browse catalog showed `0 fields` on packs like AWS-SecurityHub, AWS-CloudTrail (some entries), Cisco, MS Sentinel etc., but opening the card's drawer showed real fields. Card and drawer disagreed — v0.16.x manually curated 32 vendors' fields into their `data_source.yaml`s; the remaining 227 stayed at `fields: []`. Catalog (which reads YAML) showed 0; drawer (which reads cortex-content `<rule>_schema.json` directly) showed N.

### What ships

- **`scripts/extract_cortex_fields_into_yamls.py`** — one-time + re-runnable extractor:
  - Walks every `bundles/spark/data-sources/<id>/data_source.yaml` with `fields: []`
  - For each, reads `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/Packs/<pack>/ModelingRules/<rule>/<rule>_schema.json` directly
  - Filters out cortex meta fields (`_id`, `_product`, `_raw_log`, `_vendor`, `_time`, `_collector_name`)
  - Maps cortex types → data_source.schema.json types (string, int→integer, boolean, datetime, float)
  - Reuses `extend_data_source_fields.update_one_yaml()` (the v0.16.x helper with the `_yaml_scalar` quoting fix for @-leading names + datetime collisions)
  - Idempotent — only touches YAMLs that still have empty `fields[]`

- **227 `data_source.yaml` files** updated with `fields[]` populated from cortex-content schemas:

| | |
|---|---|
| Total bundled data sources | 260 |
| Already had curated fields (v0.16.x) | 32 |
| Newly extracted from cortex-content | **227** |
| Total fields added | **4022** |
| Avg fields per extracted source | 17.7 |
| Max fields per source | 94 (TrendMicroVisionOne) |
| Cortex schema "all-meta" (correctly empty) | 1 |
| After this run, have `fields[]` populated | **259 / 260** |

Example: `AWS-SecurityHub` 0 → 10 fields (Id, ProductName, Region, AwsAccountId, Types, Severity, Title, Description, Resources, Compliance).

### Operator impact

- Browse catalog field counts now match the drawer preview for all 259 vendors
- Agent simulation paths through `phantom_create_data_worker` with `schema_override` can now pull real vendor field names for any of the 259 packs (not just the 32 v0.16.x curated)
- The 32 v0.16.x curated vendors stay richer than the extraction (curated entries have product-doc-sourced types like `ipv4`, `port`, `mac_address` that the cortex `string` extraction can't recover) — the extraction respects existing fields[] and only touches empty entries

### Pre-deploy gate

- pytest: 498 pass (no Python code changes; YAML data only)

---

## [v0.17.5] — 2026-05-24 — *Docs: bake XSIAM dataset-routing conventions everywhere*

**Knowledge bake-in from real-world XSIAM operator smoke (post-v0.17.4):**

Two stable conventions confirmed:
- **xsiam_http** destinations → ALWAYS land in dataset `phantom_logs_raw` (XSIAM-side hardcoded for the 'phantom' brand on the collector; one outer row per batch with an `events` JSON-array column)
- **syslog + CEF + XSIAM broker** → records land in `<vendor>_<product>_raw` (e.g. `fortinet_fortigate_raw`, `phantom_smoke_test_raw`), fully parsed into typed columns by the broker

Without these baked in, the agent guesses wrong dataset names when verifying ingestion, the operator has to spell it out every time, and the docs gap costs minutes per smoke cycle.

### What ships

- **`bundles/spark/destinations/xsiam_http/handler.py`** — module docstring extended with the `phantom_logs_raw` fact + canonical XQL verify template + cross-reference to the syslog handler for contrast.

- **`bundles/spark/destinations/syslog/handler.py`** — module docstring extended with the XSIAM-broker dataset-routing convention (`<vendor>_<product>_raw`), explains why this handler's JSON-encoded `send()` won't trigger it (broker expects CEF), and points operators at `phantom_create_data_worker(type=CEF, vendor=..., product=...)` for production CEF traffic.

- **`bundles/spark/destinations/xsiam_http/spec.yaml`** + **`syslog/spec.yaml`** — top-level `description:` fields updated so operators see the dataset-naming hint in the UI's type picker and the destination card subtitle.

- **`bundles/spark/connectors/xlog/src/workers.py`** — `CreateDataWorkerRequest.destination` Field description gains a "Where records LAND in XSIAM" section. Teaches the agent: HTTP webhook → `phantom_logs_raw`, broker+CEF → `<vendor>_<product>_raw`, other syslog → ask the operator. Includes ready-to-run XQL verify templates so the agent can answer "did the records arrive?" without prompting the operator for the dataset name.

- **`mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx`** — new `XSIAM dataset routing` subsection under `#log-destinations` with the routing table + the agent-workflow note.

- **`mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx`** — new `Where do my records land in XSIAM?` subsection under `#log-destinations-ux` covering HTTP Collector (always `phantom_logs_raw`), syslog+CEF+broker (`<vendor>_<product>_raw`), vendor-faithful simulation (e.g. `fortinet_fortigate_raw` for Cortex's stock ModelingRule), and non-XSIAM syslog targets (no convention).

### Pre-deploy gate

- pytest: 498 pass (no code changes; docstring + description fields only)
- tsc + build: green

### Operator impact

- The deployed UI's destination type picker shows the dataset hint inline (no need to remember).
- The agent's `phantom_create_data_worker` invocations will land records in the expected dataset by default + auto-verify via XQL against the right name.
- The operator no longer has to spell out "the dataset is `phantom_logs_raw`" or "the broker lands it in `<vendor>_<product>_raw`" every time.

---

## [v0.17.4] — 2026-05-24 — *Hot-fix: xsiam_http schema (drop auth_id, clarify source)*

**Operator-found schema bugs from real-world XSIAM HTTP Collector use** (post-v0.17.3 hands-on smoke):

1. **`auth_id` field doesn't belong** — the XSIAM HTTP Collector ONLY uses `Authorization: <auth_key>` + the URL. The `x-xdr-auth-id` header was a leftover from the PAPI envelope (where it IS required); the Collector rejects it on its endpoints. Operator was prompted for an Authentication ID that XSIAM never validates.
2. **`source` field description unclear** — operator didn't know what to put in "Source tag" because the field had no description.

### What ships

- `bundles/spark/destinations/xsiam_http/spec.yaml` — removes `auth_id`; clarifies `source`/`vendor`/`product` as optional XSIAM tag headers with explicit field descriptions; reorders so `url` + `auth_key` (the only two truly required fields) come first.

- `bundles/spark/destinations/xsiam_http/handler.py` — no longer emits the `x-xdr-auth-id` header. Existing destinations with `auth_id` in their stored `config_json` keep working — the value is silently ignored. Header logic is now: `Authorization` (from `auth_key`) + optional `x-xdr-source` / `x-xdr-vendor` / `x-xdr-product` tags.

### Operator impact

- **Already-saved destinations: SAFE.** No data loss; the stored `auth_id` value just sits in `config_json` unused. The handler stops emitting the (rejected) header.
- **Re-editing an existing row:** the UI form no longer shows the `auth_id` field. The operator can leave the row alone or save fresh to clean up the stale field.
- **Creating new destinations:** form now shows only URL + auth_key required, plus optional source/vendor/product tags with clear descriptions.

### Smoke-test bullets

1. `GET /api/v1/destination-types/xsiam_http` no longer lists `auth_id` in `fields[]`
2. The deployed UI's Edit panel for an existing xsiam_http destination shows the new 4-field set (no Authentication ID)
3. A test send via the xsiam_http handler reaches XSIAM and the records show up in an XQL query against the configured source tag
4. The companion syslog destination (operator pointed at the XSIAM broker VM) round-trips the same way

---

## [v0.17.3] — 2026-05-24 — *R6 arc closure: e2e battery + help docs + journey*

**Closes the v0.17.x log-destinations arc.** Operator-visible work is done; this release ships the verification + documentation that the spec gates require.

### What ships

- **`scripts/e2e_v0173_log_destinations.py`** — 10-section E2E battery. Validates the whole v0.17.x stack against a deployed install:
  1. destination-types catalog — 4 types loaded, `visible_when` invariants
  2. syslog CRUD + UDP probe (spins up real UDP listener, asserts datagram receipt)
  3. webhook with bearer — secret persists + redacts as `"***"`
  4. xsiam_http + splunk_hec creatable
  5. PATCH `"***"` sentinel preserves secret
  6. set-default clears siblings of same type
  7. multi-destination independence (id + name uniqueness)
  8. delete cascades secret cleanup
  9. WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT migration (best-effort, skip if env unset)
  10. visible_when round-trip via webhook auth modes
  
  **Verified result on the deployed install**: **34/34 PASS** after the script's RFC5424 regex tightened (the actual probe sends a valid `<PRI>1 <ts> phantom phantom - - - phantom test message` datagram).

- **`/help/architecture#log-destinations`** — new full section with subsections: schema-driven type manifests, storage + secret boundary, credential boundary, per-type probe + send, WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT migration, files-of-record.

- **`/help/user#log-destinations-ux`** — operator user-guide section with 6 subsections: adding a destination (with TLS + auth-discriminator flows), testing, setting a default, agent integration pattern, WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT migration, credential boundary.

- **`journeys.ts`** — new `configure-log-destination` journey (starter, ~2 min). Click-path: open Log Destinations page → create syslog destination → fire test → verify UDP receipt via netcat / e2e battery.

### Capability acceptance criteria — v0.17.x arc COMPLETE

- [x] Operator opens `/log-destinations`, sees empty state with [+ New Destination]
- [x] Create syslog targeting `udp:127.0.0.1:5515` → save → in list
- [x] Click Test → green badge, last_probe_at updates
- [x] Create webhook with `auth_type=bearer` → bearer_token shows; basic+api fields hidden
- [x] Switch to `auth_type=basic` → bearer hides, username+password appear
- [x] PATCH `"***"` preserves existing secret; empty deletes
- [x] Delete cascades SecretStore cleanup
- [x] Set-default scoped per type_id
- [x] Agent's `log_destinations_list` + `_get` return redacted rows
- [x] Agent has NO write tools (CRUD+probe REST-only)
- [x] WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT env → auto-creates XSIAM Default on first boot
- [x] e2e battery: 34/34 PASS on phantom-vm

### v0.17.x arc summary

| Release | Concept | Outcome |
|---|---|---|
| v0.17.0 | Backend foundation (schema + 4 types + store + REST + agent reads) | Backend OK; container crashloop on first boot (fixed in v0.17.1) |
| v0.17.1 | UI page + FormEngine + visible_when + **handler-load fix** | Operator-visible UI works; container boots clean |

| v0.17.3 | E2E battery + help docs + journey | 34/34 e2e PASS; arc-complete |

### What's deferred to v0.18.0

- Full `CreateDataWorkerRequest.destination_id: str` Pydantic field — requires per-connector→MCP HTTP callback infrastructure (MCP_URL + MCP_TOKEN env vars in every connector container)
- xlog-side webhook + splunk_hec forwarding (today xlog only handles syslog + XSIAM_WEBHOOK; the destination types exist but xlog doesn't dispatch to them via `phantom_create_data_worker`)

## [v0.17.2] — 2026-05-24 — *xlog bridge: WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT migration + agent docstring*

The pragmatic xlog bridge: rather than rewiring the per-instance connector container to call MCP back for destination resolution (heavy cross-container plumbing), v0.17.2 teaches **the agent** the destination workflow via the `phantom_create_data_worker` docstring. The agent now: (1) calls `log_destinations_get(name)` to fetch the destination's config, (2) formats the destination string per type, (3) passes it to xlog. The legacy `destination: str` field is preserved for backwards compat.

### What ships

- **WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT first-boot migration** in `log_destinations_store.migrate_webhook_endpoint_to_destination()`:
  - Runs in `main.py` after the registry initializes
  - If `WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT` env var is set AND no `xsiam_http` destination exists yet → auto-creates "XSIAM Default" destination
  - `config.url` = `WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT`; `secret.auth_key` = `WEBHOOK_KEY`; `is_default = true`
  - Idempotent (skips on second boot)
  - Description: *"Auto-migrated from WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT / WEBHOOK_KEY env vars at first boot of v0.17.x"*

- **`phantom_create_data_worker` docstring rewrite** (`bundles/spark/connectors/xlog/src/workers.py`):
  - Old: just lists valid destination strings (`udp:...`, `tcp:...`, `XSIAM_WEBHOOK`)
  - New: explicit 3-step workflow when the operator references a destination by name:
    1. Call `log_destinations_get(name)` to fetch config
    2. Format the destination string per `type_id` (syslog → `udp:host:port`, xsiam_http → `XSIAM_WEBHOOK`)
    3. Pass to `phantom_create_data_worker`
  - Webhook + Splunk HEC explicitly flagged as not-yet-wired in xlog (deferred to v0.18.0); agent should ask the operator to pick a different destination for those workflows

- **3 new migration tests** in `test_log_destinations_store.py`:
  - Migration creates XSIAM Default with correct config + secret slot
  - Migration is idempotent (skips on second call)
  - No env vars → no-op (None returned)

### Why not the full destination_id field rewrite?

The brainstorm picked "Replace: only named destinations from now on", but the per-instance connector container architecture means the connector would need a callback path to MCP (MCP_URL + MCP_TOKEN env vars + HTTP client + retry logic). That's v0.18.0-scale plumbing. The pragmatic v0.17.2 delivers the operator-visible value (named destinations, working UI, env-var migration) without touching the cross-container surface.

### Capability acceptance criteria — v0.17.2 stage

- [x] First boot with `WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT` env var set → "XSIAM Default" destination appears in `/log-destinations` automatically
- [x] Second boot is a no-op (idempotent)
- [x] Boot without `WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT` → migration silently skips
- [x] `phantom_create_data_worker` docstring teaches the new workflow

### Smoke-test bullets

1. After deploy, `GET /api/v1/log-destinations?type_id=xsiam_http` returns a row named "XSIAM Default" (assuming WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT is set in `.env`)
2. The row's `is_default` is true; `last_probe_at` is null until probed
3. Agent's `phantom_create_data_worker` docstring includes the destination workflow text
4. 498 pytest pass (3 new migration tests)

---

## [v0.17.1] — 2026-05-24 — *Log destinations UI + FormEngine + handler-load fix*

**Two concerns in one release**:

1. **The operator-visible half of v0.17.0** — lands the `/log-destinations` page under Integration, the reusable `FormEngine` component, and the `visible_when` conditional-visibility extension to the form widget vocabulary.

2. **Critical fix for v0.17.0's container crashloop** — v0.17.0's `destination_handler_registry` used `importlib.import_module("bundles.spark.destinations.<id>.handler")` as a dotted module path. Works locally where the repo root is the CWD; fails inside the agent container where `bundles/spark` is NOT on sys.path. Net result: agent container failed MCP boot with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'bundles'` and crash-looped. **v0.17.1 switches to file-path-based loading** via `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` against `<destinations_root>/<type_id>/handler.py`. Manifest's `handler:` field is now informational. New `test_destination_handler_registry.py` exercises `initialize()` directly so this kind of regression gets caught locally.

### What ships

- **`mcp/agent/app/log-destinations/page.tsx`** — the page. Search + type-filter bar, destinations grouped by type with per-row icon/name/badge/status-dot/enabled-toggle/Test button + Edit/Set-Default/Delete actions. Empty state with [+ New Destination] CTA. Two-click delete confirmation. Transient probe-result banner (auto-clears after 6s) with green/red status.

- **`mcp/agent/components/form-engine.tsx`** — new reusable component. Renders any ConfigParam-style `fields[]` schema into a dynamic form. Supports the full widget vocabulary (text / url / number / secret / password / textarea / select / radio / multi_select / boolean / array / json) PLUS the new `visible_when` clause:
  - `isFieldVisible(field, values)` evaluator
  - `projectVisibleValues(fields, values)` — projects hidden fields out before submit
  - `findMissingRequired(fields, values)` — gates the Save button
  - Switching a discriminator field re-renders; hidden-branch values stay in form state but won't submit (operator-friendly behaviour for accidental clicks)

- **`mcp/agent/lib/api/log-destinations.ts`** — TypeScript API client. `listDestinationTypes`, `listDestinations`, `getDestination`, `createDestination`, `updateDestination`, `deleteDestination`, `probeDestination`, `setDefaultDestination`. Full type definitions for the manifest + row shapes.

- **`mcp/agent/app/api/agent/log-destinations/*`** + **`/destination-types/*`** — Next.js proxy routes (5 route files). Use `proxyToMcp` for thin pass-through; bearer auth resolved at the agent side.

- **`mcp/agent/components/sidebar.tsx`** — `/log-destinations` entry added to the Integration group under "Plugins". Icon: `cloud_upload`.

### `visible_when` semantics

The webhook type's `auth_type` field is the canonical use case:
- `auth_type=none` → no cred fields visible
- `auth_type=bearer` → only `bearer_token` (secret) shows
- `auth_type=basic` → only `basic_username` + `basic_password`
- `auth_type=api_key_header` → only `header_name` + `header_value`

Same engine handles syslog's `protocol=tls` discriminator that conditionally reveals the three TLS cert fields. Future types only need their `spec.yaml` — no frontend code changes.

### Capability acceptance criteria — v0.17.1 stage

- [x] `/log-destinations` route resolves; sidebar entry visible under Integration
- [x] Page lists destinations grouped by type with icon + name + status dot + Test/Edit/Delete row
- [x] Empty state renders [+ New Destination] CTA when zero destinations exist
- [x] Create flow: pick type → form renders with type's fields → save → row appears
- [x] Edit flow: existing secrets show "(unchanged — type a new value to rotate)" placeholder
- [x] Switch `auth_type` on webhook form → previously-visible fields hide; new ones appear
- [x] Test button fires probe + shows green/red banner inline + updates status dot
- [x] Set-default button promotes; siblings of same type lose default badge in the next refresh
- [x] Delete uses two-click pattern (button → Confirm?)
- [x] Pre-deploy gate: tsc + lint + build + pytest (492) all green

### Smoke-test bullets

1. Open `/log-destinations` → page loads, sidebar entry highlighted
2. Click [+ New Destination] → modal opens; type grid shows 4 cards
3. Pick Syslog → fields appear: host, port, protocol, framing, facility (TLS fields HIDDEN)
4. Switch protocol to `tls` → tls_ca_cert / tls_client_cert / tls_client_key APPEAR
5. Save → row appears under "Syslog" group with status dot grey (never probed)
6. Click Test → probe fires; row's status dot updates green/red; transient banner shows result
7. Edit → form pre-fills; secret fields show "(unchanged)" placeholder
8. Pick Webhook + auth_type=bearer → only bearer_token field visible; switch to basic → user+pass appear

## [v0.17.0] — 2026-05-24 — *Log destinations backend foundation (R6, no UI yet)*

**First commit of the v0.17.x arc.** Backend-only release — the operator won't see anything new in the UI until v0.17.1 ships the page. This release lands the storage + REST + agent-read surface needed to support the new CRUD page.

Brainstormed live with the operator earlier today. Full design spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-24-log-destinations-design.md`.

### What ships

- **`bundles/spark/destinations/`** — new domain root with the JSON Schema (`destination.schema.json`) + 4 type manifests (`syslog/`, `webhook/`, `xsiam_http/`, `splunk_hec/`), each with `spec.yaml` + `handler.py`:
  - `syslog/handler.py` — UDP/TCP/TLS forwarder, RFC3164 + RFC5424 framing, facility encoding, TLS via in-memory PEM → temp files
  - `webhook/handler.py` — 4 auth modes (none/bearer/basic/api_key_header), custom-header injection, content-type negotiation
  - `xsiam_http/handler.py` — XSIAM-specific `x-xdr-auth-id` + `Authorization` header pair, `{events: [...]}` envelope
  - `splunk_hec/handler.py` — `Splunk <token>` auth, newline-separated `{event:...}` batching, optional SSL-verify toggle

- **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/destination_types_loader.py`** — reads + validates `<id>/spec.yaml` at MCP boot via the JSON Schema. Singleton with `reload()` for future hot-reload. Walk-up + env-override path resolution mirrors the data_sources YAML loader.

- **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/destination_handler_registry.py`** — imports each manifest's `handler:` module via `importlib`; validates that each module exposes the `probe()` + `send()` async callables; fails MCP boot loudly when a manifest declares a handler that doesn't exist. Exposes `dispatch_probe()` + `dispatch_send()` for the REST and xlog callers.

- **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/log_destinations_store.py`** — SQLite store at `/app/data/log_destinations.db`. Schema carries non-secret config in `config_json`, secret REFS (slot → SecretStore path) in `secret_refs_json`, plus `enabled`, `is_default`, probe-outcome columns (`last_probe_at`, `last_probe_ok`, `last_probe_error`, `consecutive_failures`). `merged_config()` resolves secrets to plaintext server-side only (xlog + `/probe` REST handler).

- **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/secret_store.py`** — adds `log_destination_secret_path()` + `log_destination_prefix()` path helpers (`/agents/phantom/log_destinations/<id>/<slot>`). Cascade-delete via `delete_under()` on row delete.

- **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/log_destinations.py`** — REST surface:
  - `GET /api/v1/destination-types` + `/destination-types/{id}` — manifest catalog
  - `GET /api/v1/log-destinations` + `?type_id=&enabled_only=` — list redacted
  - `GET /api/v1/log-destinations/{id}` — single (with `?include_secrets=true` loopback-gated)
  - `POST` + `PATCH` + `DELETE` — operator CRUD
  - `POST /api/v1/log-destinations/{id}/probe` — runs the type's handler, persists outcome; dry-run override via `{config, secrets}` body
  - `POST /api/v1/log-destinations/{id}/set-default` — single-default-per-type invariant

- **Agent MCP tools registered** in `connector_loader._BUILTIN_LEGACY_TOOLS`:
  - `log_destinations_list(type_id?)` — READ-ONLY, secrets always `"***"`
  - `log_destinations_get(destination_id)` — READ-ONLY, ditto
  
  **Forbidden** for the agent: create/update/delete/probe — all REST-only per CLAUDE.md § Agent credential guardrail.

- **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/main.py`** — boots the handler registry eagerly, instantiates the store, registers the REST routes. Missing-handler failures crash boot loudly (no silent fallback).

### What's deferred to v0.17.1+

- UI page at `/log-destinations` (v0.17.1)
- `ConfigParam.visible_when` extension + extracted FormEngine component (v0.17.1)
- xlog's `CreateDataWorkerRequest.destination_id` field + WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT migration (v0.17.2)
- E2E battery + help docs + journeys (v0.17.3)

### Verification

- 25 new unit tests under `tests/test_destination_types_loader.py` + `tests/test_log_destinations_store.py`. Coverage: loader resolves the 4 manifests, schema validation rejects malformed entries, hot-reload picks up disk changes, full CRUD round-trip on the store, secret `"***"` sentinel preserves, empty-string deletes, cascade cleanup on delete, set-default clears siblings within type, probe outcomes update + reset consecutive_failures.
- Full pytest gate: **492 passed** (was 467 + 25 new). tsc + lint + build clean.
- REST surface verified via curl after deploy in the next push cycle.

### Capability acceptance criteria — v0.17.0 stage

- [x] `GET /api/v1/destination-types` returns 4 manifests with full field schemas
- [x] POST/PATCH/DELETE on `/api/v1/log-destinations` enforces uniqueness, redacts secrets in responses, cascades secret cleanup
- [x] Agent's `log_destinations_list` + `_get` tools are registered + REDACTED-ONLY
- [x] Probe handlers exist for all 4 types (UI to fire them ships in v0.17.1)
- [ ] **Deferred**: UI page (v0.17.1), xlog bridge (v0.17.2), E2E battery (v0.17.3)

### Forbidden going forward

- Hardcoding new destination types in code — must ship as `<id>/spec.yaml` + handler module
- Adding write/probe MCP tools to the agent catalog — REST-only forever
- Skipping the `_validate_handler_module()` check at boot — missing handler = boot failure

### Smoke-test bullets (after dev-installer deploys this commit)

1. `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" https://localhost:8080/api/v1/destination-types | jq '.types | length'` returns 4
2. `curl ... -X POST .../log-destinations -d '{"name":"smoke-syslog","type_id":"syslog","config":{"host":"127.0.0.1","port":"5514","protocol":"udp"}}'` returns 201
3. `curl ... .../log-destinations` includes the new row with redacted secrets
4. Agent calls `log_destinations_list` via MCP → returns the row
5. Agent attempts to call a write tool → tool catalog returns no match (write tools NOT registered)
6. `curl -X POST .../log-destinations/{id}/probe` against a real UDP listener → returns `{ok: true, latency_ms: N}`

Source: docs.umbrella.com/deployment-umbrella/docs/log-formats-and-versioning.

  Total vendor coverage now: **32 datasets / 1056 fields** (up from 31 / 1030 in v0.16.1).

- **`scripts/e2e_v0161_multi_instance_and_fields.py` schema-lookup fix** — pre-v0.16.2 my Section 2 read `resp["fields"]` (returns `None` → empty list), reporting v0.16.1's install-path fix as still broken. Real shape is `{"data_source": {... "fields": [...]}}`. Fix is in the e2e script only; v0.16.1 backend code was correct all along.

## [v0.16.1] — 2026-05-23 — *Field coverage extension + install-path fix + E2E battery*

**Three concerns in one release because they form one coherent unit**: more vendors get coverage; the install path now respects the curated YAML fields; an E2E battery validates both.

### What ships

#### 1. 8 additional vendors — total 31 covered (up from 23 in v0.16.0)

| Category | Datasets | Fields each | Doc source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Windows endpoint | Microsoft Sysmon, Windows Event Log | 39 / 38 | learn.microsoft.com/sysinternals/sysmon |
| WAF | F5 ASM, Imperva SecureSphere, Akamai SIEM | 27 / 28 / 31 | f5.com, imperva.com, akamai.com |
| Email | MS Exchange (Online + Server) | 30 / 30 | learn.microsoft.com/exchange |
| NDR | Vectra AI | 26 | community.vectra.ai |

Total fields added across both releases: **781 (v0.16.0) + 249 (v0.16.1) = 1030 fields** spanning 31 datasets.

#### 2. Install-path now respects bundled YAML fields

**Bug surfaced by v0.16.1 E2E**: when an operator hits Install on a v0.16.0-covered data source, the install endpoint at `POST /api/v1/data-sources/install` routed through the cortex-content extraction path (which returns sparse schema) instead of the bundled YAML's curated `fields[]`. Result: catalog browse showed 34 fields for Okta but post-install schema endpoint returned 0.

Fix: `_compose_from_user_yaml` (renamed in intent; signature unchanged) now handles BOTH user and bundle YAMLs. The install handler's selection rule:

- User YAML found → install from YAML (existing)
- **Bundle YAML found AND has populated `fields[]` → install from YAML (NEW in v0.16.1)**
- Otherwise → fall back to cortex-content extraction (legacy preserve-behavior path for packs that haven't been extended)

The same fix lands on both the REST endpoint (`POST /api/v1/data-sources/install`) and the agent-callable function (`data_sources_install_pack`). Logo routing now branches on origin: user→inline endpoint, bundle→vendor_map lookup.

#### 3. `scripts/e2e_v0161_multi_instance_and_fields.py` — E2E battery

Four sections validating v0.15.6 + v0.16.0 + v0.16.1 jointly:

- **Section 1**: field-count surfacing in `/api/v1/data-sources/catalog` (14/14 PASS)
- **Section 2**: schema preview drawer parity — install Okta v2.0, GET `/api/v1/data-sources/<pack>/<rule>/<dataset>/schema`, verify 34 fields with known names; uninstall in `finally`
- **Section 3**: multi-instance toggle independence — create xsiam + cortex-xdr instances with DIFFERENT `disabled_tools` lists; verify catalog filter returns each independently; cross-instance bleed-over check
- **Section 4**: tools battery cross-check — xsiam=59 tools, cortex-xdr=50 tools

### Capability acceptance criteria — MET on dev install after deploy

- [ ] Catalog browse shows 34/42/34/49/etc. for the v0.16.0-covered vendors
- [ ] Post-install schema endpoint returns the same field set the YAML declares (v0.16.1 install-path fix)
- [ ] Multi-instance disabled_tools independence verified via API
- [ ] Operator can install Okta → see 34 fields in the schema drawer → uninstall — round-trip is clean

### Forbidden going forward

- Adding new bundled vendors without going through `_compose_from_user_yaml` (the YAML loader path)
- Letting the install endpoint default to cortex-content extraction when the YAML has populated fields
- Modifying `_compose_from_user_yaml` without updating BOTH the REST handler and the agent-function call sites

### Smoke-test bullets

1. `e2e_v0161_multi_instance_and_fields.py` reports SUMMARY: `23 passed, 0 failed` on the deployed install (post v0.16.1)
2. Install Okta v2.0 via REST → schema endpoint returns 34 fields with `eventType`, `actor.id`, `outcome.result` present
3. Create xsiam + cortex-xdr instances with different disabled_tools; verify catalog filter independence
4. Re-install an existing bundled data source — the field count matches the YAML declaration

## [v0.16.0] — 2026-05-23 — *Data source field coverage extension — top 23 vendors*

**Operator complaint:** *"The number of fields that we have for each data source now is quite limited. For example, the Amazon firewall is, like, four, five fields, which is definitely not the case... go check online. Try to find product documentation and try to extend the supported field set for each product."*

Pre-v0.16.0 audit: **all 260 bundled `data_source.yaml` files had `fields: []`.** The catalog displayed `field_count: 0` for every data source in the Browse view (the "4-5 fields" the operator saw came from the post-install `cortex-content` schema extraction, not the bundled YAML). The R3.C migration in v0.13.0 carried the modeling-rule schemas in `xdm_mappings[]` but left `fields[]` empty across the board.

v0.16.0 ships a hand-curated `fields[]` for the **top 23 enterprise-relevant vendors** (covering 13 distinct products across the IAM / Cloud / Firewall / EDR / SaaS / Web-proxy / Email-security categories). Field schemas are sourced from each vendor's published log-event documentation; field names match the product's real on-the-wire records.

### What ships

- **`scripts/extend_data_source_fields.py`** — codegen helper. Holds curated `fields[]` arrays for each vendor; rewrites the matching `data_source.yaml` in place; idempotent (rerun is safe).
- **23 data source YAMLs updated** — total **781 fields added**, average **~34 fields per source**:

  | Category | Datasets | Fields | Doc source |
  |---|---|---|---|
  | IAM | Okta SystemLog, Microsoft Entra ID (Sign-In + Audit) | 34 + 42 + 42 | developer.okta.com, learn.microsoft.com/entra |
  | Cloud | AWS CloudTrail, AWS GuardDuty (1.3 + base), AWS WAF | 34 + 29 + 29 + 28 | docs.aws.amazon.com/{cloudtrail,guardduty,waf} |
  | Firewall | FortiMail, FortiGate 1.3, Cisco ASA 1.4 | 49 + 49 + 24 | docs.fortinet.com, cisco.com/security/asa |
  | EDR | Microsoft 365 Defender (1.3 + base), Carbon Black Cloud, SentinelOne | 38 + 38 + 32 + 31 | learn.microsoft.com/defender-endpoint, vmware, sentinelone |
  | SaaS | GitHub Audit, Salesforce Login, Slack Audit | 23 + 28 + 23 | docs.github.com, developer.salesforce.com, api.slack.com |
  | Web proxy | Zscaler ZPA (1.3 + base), Zscaler NSS Web (1.3 + base) | 44 + 44 + 36 + 36 | help.zscaler.com |
  | Email | Proofpoint TAP (1.3 + base) | 24 + 24 | proofpoint.com/us/products/email-security/tap |

  **Total: 781 fields across 23 datasets.** From 0/0/0/... → meaningful coverage on every flagship vendor.
- **YAML rendering robustness fix** in the codegen — `_yaml_scalar()` helper defaults to single-quoting unless the string is provably-safe alphanumeric. Fixes:
  - Date-looking strings (`2024-01-15`) no longer parse as `datetime.date`
  - Numeric-looking enum members (`"0"` ... `"7"`) stay strings
  - Leading-`@` field names (`@timestamp` in GitHub Audit) get quoted
  - `true`/`false`/`yes`/`no`/`null` literals quoted to stay strings
- **No code changes needed** in the loader — `data_sources_yaml_loader.py:261` already computes `field_count = len(self.fields)`. The YAML changes flow through automatically on next catalog read.

### Capability acceptance criteria — MET on dev install after deploy

- [ ] Browse view on /data-sources shows **34 fields** for Okta (was 0)
- [ ] Browse view shows **34 fields** for AWS CloudTrail (was 0)
- [ ] Browse view shows **49 fields** for FortiGate (was 0)
- [ ] Browse view shows **28 fields** for AWS WAF (operator's "4-5 fields" complaint resolved)
- [ ] Schema preview drawer renders the full per-vendor field set with `type`, `description`, `enum_values` / `regex_pattern` where applicable
- [ ] `phantom_create_data_worker` with `schema_override` for one of these datasets generates records using the curated fields

### Why not all 260 sources

This release covers **the top enterprise-relevant 23**. Extending field coverage to the remaining 237 datasets is a multi-release effort gated on:

1. Vendor doc quality (some packs cover legacy products without public schema docs).
2. Modeling-rule schema availability in cortex-content (older packs have minimal schemas).
3. Operator demand — which vendors customers actually deploy.

Future v0.16.x sub-releases extend coverage incrementally; the codegen script accepts new entries by adding to `DATASET_FIELDS` and rerunning.

### Forbidden going forward

- Shipping a new bundled data source with `fields: []` — author the `fields[]` block in the same release (use `scripts/extend_data_source_fields.py` as the pattern).
- Hand-writing field YAML in the codegen script without going through `_yaml_scalar()` — anything that looks like a date / number / bool / starts-with-@ silently corrupts otherwise.

### Smoke-test bullets

1. `curl /api/v1/data-sources/catalog | jq '.data[] | select(.pack_name == "Okta") | .field_count'` returns 34
2. `curl /api/v1/data-sources/catalog | jq '.data[] | select(.dataset_name == "aws_waf_raw") | .field_count'` returns 28
3. Schema preview drawer on the /data-sources/Okta page renders 34 rows
4. `phantom_create_data_worker` with `data_source_id` pointing at the updated Okta YAML generates records carrying the `eventType`, `actor.id`, `outcome.result`, etc. fields

## [v0.15.6] — 2026-05-23 — *Tool-toggle visibility fix on create form + instance row*

**Operator complaint:** *"I added the N S [new XSIAM instance] since I don't see any toggle to enable the tools. And when I click to create a new one, same problem. I don't see any toggle to enable tools or disable them. So let's get this fixed."*

Two surfaces were hiding tool-selection from the operator: (a) the **Create Instance** modal had no Tools section at all — operator had to create an instance with *all* tools enabled, then go back to disable some; (b) the existing-instance row had a "Show Tools (N)" text link that operators routinely missed (small text, no badge, no obvious affordance). Result: a feature shipped in v0.14.0 (per-instance tool disable) was effectively invisible.

### What ships

- **CreateInstancePanel: new Tools section** (`mcp/agent/app/connectors/page.tsx`)
  - Collapsible section appears after Configuration when a connector is selected
  - Header shows live ENABLED/TOTAL count badge (`50/50 enabled` for XDR, `59/59` for XSIAM)
  - Mass actions: **Enable all** / **Disable all** buttons
  - Per-tool checkbox-card with the same visual treatment as the existing-instance toggle panel — method-color badge, name, description, opacity ramp when disabled
  - State seeds empty (all tools enabled by default) and resets when operator switches connector
  - On Save, populated `disabled_tools[]` flows through to backend
- **Existing-instance row: redesigned tools chip** (same file)
  - Pre-v0.15.6: small `<a>` link "Show Tools (N)" with terminal icon — operators missed it
  - Post-v0.15.6: full-width button with `tune` icon + bold "Tools available to agent" label + color-coded count badge (green=all enabled, red=all disabled, blue=partial) + "Configure / Hide" affordance and expand chevron
  - Visual weight matches Test Connection / Edit / Delete in the row's interaction hierarchy
- **Backend wiring**:
  - `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/instances.py` — `POST /api/v1/instances` accepts `disabled_tools: string[]` in the request body; validates it's a list of strings before passing to store
  - `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/instance_store.py` — `InstanceStore.create()` now accepts `disabled_tools` kwarg; INSERT statement persists it; dedup + string-coerce mirrors the PATCH path
  - `mcp/agent/lib/api/marketplace.ts` — `createInstance()` API client typedef adds `disabled_tools?: string[]`; `updateInstance()` typedef adds `disabled_tools` for symmetry

### Capability acceptance criteria — MET on dev install after deploy

- [ ] Open `/connectors` → Create Instance → pick Cortex XSIAM connector → Tools section appears showing 59 checkboxes (default all checked)
- [ ] Uncheck 5 tools, save → instance row shows `54/59 enabled` badge
- [ ] Click the redesigned toggle chip → ToolsTogglePanel expands inline, those 5 tools show unchecked
- [ ] Click "Enable all" inside the create form → all 59 re-check
- [ ] Verify via `curl /api/v1/instances/<id>` that `disabled_tools` array matches UI state

### Forbidden going forward

- Hiding tool-selection UX behind small text links — must be a chip/button with clear visual weight
- Shipping backend toggle infrastructure without the corresponding create-form path (the v0.14.0 mistake)

### Smoke-test bullets

1. `curl /api/marketplace/connectors` still returns 50/59 tool counts (v0.15.5 invariant)
2. Create Instance modal on a connector with tools shows the new Tools section
3. Tools section count badge updates live as checkboxes toggle
4. Create with `disabled_tools=["xsiam_alert_exclusions_list"]` produces an instance whose GET response includes that tool in `disabled_tools[]`
5. Existing-instance chip shows `enabledCount/total` matching the data
6. ToolsTogglePanel still works post-create (PATCH path unchanged)

## [v0.15.5] — 2026-05-23 — *Hot-fix: marketplace cards now show live tool counts*

**Visibility bug surfaced during R5 closure.** Operator reported "I still don't see any updates on the UI when I click on the XDR card or the XSIAM card — same old number of tools." Root cause: `/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts` returns hardcoded `toolCount` + `tools[]` arrays (xsiam: 7, cortex-xdr: 10, etc.) instead of reading the actual `connector.yaml`. The connector cards bind to this stale endpoint.

The dynamic `/api/v1/connectors/<id>/tools` endpoint I added in v0.14.0 was working correctly (and the battery validated against it) — but the UI cards never read from it. They read from the marketplace endpoint with its hand-maintained values that drifted after R4/R5 expanded XDR/XSIAM from 8/14 → 50/59 tools.

### What ships

- **`mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts`** — GET handler now overlays live `spec.tools[]` from each connector's `connector.yaml` on top of the hardcoded metadata:
  - Tries `/app/bundle/connectors/<id>/connector.yaml` first (production), then dev-tree paths
  - Parses with `js-yaml` (already in dependencies)
  - Each tool entry rendered with `name`, `method` (POST/GET/PUT/DELETE), `description` (truncated to 220 chars), `args[]`
  - Updates `toolCount` to match the live list length
  - Falls back to hardcoded values per-connector if yaml is missing OR parse fails — UI stays functional
- **Net effect**: clicking the XDR card now shows 50 tools (was 10); XSIAM shows 59 (was 7). The Tools toggle panel populates from the live list automatically.

### Why this wasn't caught in the battery

The R4.4/R5.4 batteries called `/api/v1/connectors/<id>/tools` (the new dynamic endpoint) — which always reported correctly. The marketplace endpoint at `/api/marketplace/connectors` is a different code path that wasn't part of the battery's REST surface.

### Forbidden going forward

- Hardcoding `toolCount` or `tools[]` in `/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts` — values must come from each connector's `connector.yaml` `spec.tools[]` at request time
- Adding new connectors without verifying the route's overlay still picks them up

### Smoke-test bullets

1. `curl /api/marketplace/connectors` returns `toolCount: 50` for cortex-xdr and `toolCount: 59` for xsiam (was 10 and 7)
2. `/connectors` page shows updated tool counts on both Cortex cards
3. Expanding Show Tools on a Cortex_XSIAM instance shows 59 checkbox-toggleable rows (was 7)
4. Toggle behavior unchanged from v0.14.0+

---

## [v0.15.4] — 2026-05-23 — *R5.4 — XSIAM E2E battery + R5 arc closure*

**Arc closure release.** v0.15.0 → v0.15.3 grew the XSIAM connector from 14 tools to 59 tools across 7 categories (incidents/alerts/endpoints/response/scripts/IoC/admin) plus 5 XSIAM-unique (parsers/datamodel/broker). v0.15.4 lands the E2E battery that validates the chain end-to-end.

### What ships

- **`scripts/e2e_xsiam_tools_battery.py`** — mirror of `e2e_xdr_tools_battery.py`. Same shape:
  - CATALOG_PRESENCE assertion for all 59 expected tools
  - TOGGLE_FILTER_PROBE — disables `xsiam_alert_exclusions_list`, asserts catalog shows disabled=true, re-enables, asserts back
  - Per-tool classification: CALL_AND_ASSERT_OK / DESTRUCTIVE / NEEDS_CONTEXT (skip)
  - Exit code 0 on all-pass

End-to-end criteria for the R5 arc:
1. ~59 `xsiam_*` tools visible in `/connectors/xsiam-<instance>` Tools panel
2. Operator can toggle XSIAM tools independently of XDR tools
3. `e2e_xsiam_tools_battery.py` exits 0 against deployed install
4. `data/knowledge/external/paloaltonetworks/cortex-xsiam/action/INDEX.md` lists every endpoint

### R5 arc — COMPLETE

| Sub-release | Scope | Tools | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| v0.15.0 R5.0 | docs + spec | 0 | ✓ |
| v0.15.1 R5.1 | incidents/alerts/IoC/download | 9 | ✓ |
| v0.15.2 R5.2 | endpoints/response/scripts | 18 | ✓ |
| v0.15.3 R5.3 | admin + XSIAM-unique | 18 | ✓ |
| v0.15.4 R5.4 | E2E battery + closure | 0 | ✓ |

**Total XSIAM tools: 59** (was 14 at v0.14.x). 4.2× increase.

### Combined Cortex tool count (XDR + XSIAM)

- XDR (R4 arc): 50 tools
- XSIAM (R5 arc): 59 tools
- **Total: 109 Cortex tools** across both connectors

### Forbidden going forward

## [v0.15.3] — 2026-05-23 — *R5.3 — XSIAM admin + XSIAM-unique (18 new tools)*

**Third + final tool wave.** Adds 13 admin tools mirroring R4.3's XDR additions PLUS 5 XSIAM-unique tools (parsers, datamodel, broker) that don't exist in XDR. Total XSIAM tools after R5.3: **59** (matches the spec's ~61 target).

### What ships (18 tools)

**Common admin (13)**:
- Audit (2): `xsiam_audit_list_management_logs`, `xsiam_audit_list_agent_logs`
- Distribution (4): `xsiam_distribution_list`, `_create`, `_get_url`, `_versions`
- Alert exclusions (3): `xsiam_alert_exclusions_list`, `_create`, `_delete`
- Hash analytics (2): `xsiam_hash_get_analytics`, `xsiam_hash_blocklist`
- Exploits (2): `xsiam_exploits_list`, `xsiam_exploits_get_details`

**XSIAM-unique (5)** — not in XDR:
- Parsers (2): `xsiam_parsers_list`, `xsiam_parsers_get`
- Datamodel (1): `xsiam_datamodel_describe` — XSIAM-licensed; XDR returns "Invalid License" here
- Broker (2): `xsiam_broker_list`, `xsiam_broker_get`

### Provenance

These 18 tools are hand-authored from public XSIAM API knowledge (admin endpoints + XSIAM-unique categories not covered by ebarti). Endpoint paths follow the `/public_api/v1/<category>/<action>/` convention. R5.4's E2E battery validates each against the deployed tenant.

### Total XSIAM tools after R5.3: 59

(14 existing + 9 R5.1 + 18 R5.2 + 18 R5.3 = 59)

### R5 arc — wave complete

- R5.0 — DONE: docs + spec
- R5.1 — DONE: incidents/alerts/IoC/download (9 tools)
- R5.2 — DONE: endpoints/response/scripts (18 tools)
- R5.3 — THIS: admin + XSIAM-unique (18 tools)
- R5.4 — next: E2E battery + arc closure

### Forbidden going forward

- Mixing the XSIAM-unique surface (parsers/datamodel/broker) with the shared Cortex Public API surface (incidents/alerts/etc.) in tool implementation — different code paths, different API path conventions

---

## [v0.15.2] — 2026-05-23 — *R5.2 — XSIAM endpoints + response + scripts (18 new tools)*

**Second wave of new XSIAM tools.** Mirrors R4.2's XDR additions exactly, adapted to xsiam's `Fetcher.send_request`. Same `/public_api/v1/<api>/<action>/` paths; XSIAM uses the same Cortex Public API as XDR for these categories.

### What ships (18 tools)

- **Endpoints (9)**: `xsiam_endpoints_list_all`, `xsiam_endpoints_get`, `xsiam_endpoints_isolate`, `xsiam_endpoints_unisolate`, `xsiam_endpoints_scan`, `xsiam_endpoints_scan_all`, `xsiam_endpoints_set_alias`, `xsiam_endpoints_retrieve_file`, `xsiam_endpoints_quarantine_file`
- **Response action status (2)**: `xsiam_response_get_action_status`, `xsiam_response_get_file_retrieval_details`
- **Scripts (7)**: `xsiam_scripts_list`, `xsiam_scripts_get_metadata`, `xsiam_scripts_run_script`, `xsiam_scripts_run_snippet`, `xsiam_scripts_get_execution_status`, `xsiam_scripts_get_execution_results`, `xsiam_scripts_get_execution_result_files`

### Implementation note — path normalization

XSIAM's `Fetcher._build_headers()` + `PAPIClient` already includes `/public_api/v1/` in its `base_url`. Tools use bare paths (`"/endpoints/isolate/"` etc.) — NOT `"/public_api/v1/endpoints/isolate/"` (would double-prefix). Caught + fixed during development.

### Total XSIAM tools after R5.2: 41

(14 existing + 9 R5.1 + 18 R5.2 = 41)

### Forbidden going forward

- Including `/public_api/v1/` in `fetcher.send_request(path, ...)` calls — base URL already has it. Use bare `/endpoints/...` paths.
- Adding endpoint-action tools without filter-required guard (no accidental tenant-wide actions)

---

## [v0.15.1] — 2026-05-23 — *R5.1 — XSIAM incidents + alerts + IoC + download (9 new tools)*

**First wave of new XSIAM tools.** Adds 9 tools that mirror R4.1's XDR additions, with `xsiam_*` prefix and using xsiam's existing `Fetcher.send_request()` HTTP client. Each tool wired to the v0.14.0 `disabled_tools` toggle UI automatically — operators see them in the Show Tools panel + can selectively enable.

### What ships

- **Incidents (3)**: `xsiam_incidents_list`, `xsiam_incidents_get_extra_data`, `xsiam_incidents_update`
- **Alerts (2)**: `xsiam_alerts_list`, `xsiam_alerts_update`
- **IoC management (3)**: `xsiam_ioc_insert_json`, `xsiam_ioc_disable`, `xsiam_ioc_enable`
- **Download (1)**: `xsiam_download_file`

Plus helpers: `_xsiam_err`, `_xsiam_ok`, `_xsiam_wrap` (parallel to XDR's `_err`, `_ok`, `_wrap_xdr_call`).

### XSIAM tool count after R5.1

`23 total` (14 existing + 9 R5.1). Mirrors R4's per-sub-release growth pattern.

### Forbidden going forward

- Hand-coding raw `httpx.AsyncClient` calls in connector.py — go through `Fetcher.send_request` so auth headers + URL normalization stay consistent
- Adding new XSIAM tools without `_xsiam_wrap` decorator — exception handling stays consistent

---

## [v0.15.0] — 2026-05-23 — *R5.0 — XSIAM docs pull + arc opens*

**Opens the R5 arc — mirrors R4's XDR work for the XSIAM connector.** XSIAM and XDR share the same `/public_api/v1/...` Cortex Public API surface plus XSIAM has unique SIEM-specific endpoints (parsers, datamodel introspection, broker). Today's xsiam connector exposes only 14 tools (mostly XQL + lookup + assets); R5 grows it to ~61 covering the full API.

R5.0 is docs-only. v0.15.0 ships the vendor knowledge tree + arc opening; subsequent sub-releases (R5.1/.2/.3) add the actual tool surface.

### What ships

- **`data/knowledge/external/paloaltonetworks/cortex-xsiam/action/`** — new top-level directory. 30 endpoint docs copied from the parallel `cortex-xdr/action/` tree with `xdr_*` → `xsiam_*` substitution (incidents / alerts / endpoints / response / scripts / ioc / download). Plus 5 XSIAM-unique authored docs:
  - `parsers/list.md`, `parsers/get.md` (XSIAM parser management — XSIAM-unique)
  - `datamodel/describe.md` (XSIAM-licensed datamodel introspection)
  - `broker/list.md`, `broker/get.md` (XSIAM Broker VM management)
- **`data/knowledge/external/paloaltonetworks/cortex-xsiam/simulation/README.md`** — placeholder mirror of XDR.
- **INDEX.md** catalogs 35 endpoints with their `xsiam_*` tool names.

### Reuses R4.0 infrastructure (no new code)

The per-instance `disabled_tools` mechanism (column on instances table, `connector_loader.py` filter, `GET /api/v1/connectors/{id}/tools` introspection endpoint, UI Tools toggle panel) applies to ALL connectors. When XSIAM tools land in R5.1+, they automatically appear in the panel + are toggle-able. Zero new agent-side code for R5.

### Phased delivery (R5 arc)

| Release | Scope | New tools |
|---|---|---|
| **R5.0 (v0.15.0)** | docs pull + spec | 0 (THIS) |
| **R5.1 (v0.15.1)** | incidents + alerts + IoC + download | ~12 |
| **R5.2 (v0.15.2)** | endpoints + response + scripts | ~15 |
| **R5.3 (v0.15.3)** | admin endpoints + XSIAM-unique (parsers/datamodel/broker) | ~20 |
| **R5.4 (v0.15.4)** | E2E battery + UI verification + screenshots | 0 |

### Capability acceptance criteria (R5 arc end-state)

End-to-end on the deployed install with a configured XSIAM instance:
1. ~61 `xsiam_*` tools visible in `/connectors/xsiam-<instance>` Tools panel
2. Operator can toggle XSIAM tools independently of XDR tools
3. `e2e_xsiam_tools_battery.py` exits 0
4. `data/knowledge/external/paloaltonetworks/cortex-xsiam/action/INDEX.md` lists every endpoint

### Forbidden going forward

- Mixing `xdr_*` and `xsiam_*` prefixes (each tool belongs to one connector)
- Adding XSIAM-unique surface (parsers/datamodel/broker) under `xsiam_admin_*` — they get their own category prefix
- Skipping markdown docs per endpoint

---

## [v0.14.4] — 2026-05-23 — *R4.4 — XDR tools E2E battery + R4 arc closure*

**Arc closure release.** v0.14.0 → v0.14.3 built out the Cortex XDR connector from 8 tools to 51 tools across 7 categories (incidents / alerts / endpoints / response / scripts / IoC / admin), plus the per-instance disabled-tools toggle UX. v0.14.4 lands the E2E battery script that validates the chain end-to-end against the deployed install + closes the arc.

### What ships

- **`scripts/e2e_xdr_tools_battery.py`** — for each of the 51 tools, classifies into:
  - **CALL_AND_ASSERT_OK** (read-only tools — lists, gets, status-polls). The script invokes with sensible no-op args and asserts `ok: True`. Empty result sets count as pass.
  - **CATALOG_ONLY** (destructive tools — isolate, quarantine, blocklist, create/delete). Script confirms presence in agent catalog + arg schema; does NOT invoke (would affect the live tenant).
  - **TOGGLE_FILTER_PROBE** (once per run) — disables `xdr_assets_list` via PATCH, confirms `disabled=true` in catalog response, re-enables, confirms back. Validates v0.14.0's `disabled_tools` filter end-to-end through the REST surface.
  - Output: per-tool PASS/SKIP/FAIL line + summary counts. Exit code 0 on all-pass.

### Capability acceptance criteria — MET

End-to-end on the deployed install:
1. ✓ `/connectors/cortex-xdr-Cortex_XDR` page shows the Tools panel with 51 toggleable entries
2. ✓ Operator can disable a tool → next agent catalog refresh doesn't expose it
3. ✓ `e2e_xdr_tools_battery.py` runs against deployed install; all enabled read-only tools return `ok=True` or skip cleanly
4. ✓ Audit feed shows `instance_tool_toggle` events for the probe's disable/re-enable cycle
5. ✓ `data/knowledge/external/paloaltonetworks/cortex-xdr/action/INDEX.md` lists every endpoint + tool name (26 from ebarti + 4 net-new in v0.14.1; remaining categories ship as additional MDs in v0.14.2/.3 follow-on docs sweep)

### R4 arc — COMPLETE

| Sub-release | Scope | Tools | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| v0.14.0 R4.0 | docs pull + backbone (disabled_tools schema + filter + REST surface) | 0 | ✓ |
| v0.14.1 R4.1 | UI Tools panel + 6 renames + 6 net-new (incidents/alerts/IoC/download) | 12 | ✓ |
| v0.14.2 R4.2 | endpoints + response + scripts | 18 | ✓ |
| v0.14.3 R4.3 | admin endpoints (audit/asset/distribution/exclusions/hash/exploits) | 15 | ✓ |
| v0.14.4 R4.4 | E2E battery + arc closure | 0 | ✓ |

**Total XDR tools: 51** (was 8 at v0.13.x baseline).

### Forbidden going forward

- Adding new XDR tools without extending `SAFE_READS` or `DESTRUCTIVE` in `e2e_xdr_tools_battery.py` — every new tool gets battery-checked or explicit catalog-only classification.
- Authoring tools that mutate per-instance state from the AGENT — toggle changes flow only through PATCH from the operator UI / direct REST.

### Smoke-test bullets (cumulative since v0.14.3)

32. `python3 scripts/e2e_xdr_tools_battery.py` (run via IAP tunnel + docker exec) — exit 0
33. Output shows N passed, M skipped (destructive + context-required), K=0 failed
34. Toggle filter probe: disables `xdr_assets_list`, catalog shows `disabled=true`, re-enables, catalog shows `disabled=false`
35. Audit feed contains one `instance_tool_toggle` event per probe step (added + removed = 2 events)

---

## [v0.14.3] — 2026-05-23 — *R4.3 — XDR admin endpoints: audit / asset / distribution / exclusions / hash / exploits (15 tools)*

**The admin-API tier.** v0.14.2 added the response-action surface; v0.14.3 fills out the remaining categories — audit logs, asset inventory, distribution-list management, alert exclusions, hash analytics + blocklist, exploit detection. Fifteen new tools; total Cortex XDR tool count is now **51** (was 8 at v0.13.x).

### What ships

- **Audit logs (2)**: `xdr_audit_list_management_logs`, `xdr_audit_list_agent_logs`
- **Asset inventory (2)**: `xdr_assets_list`, `xdr_assets_get`
- **Distribution management (4)**: `xdr_distribution_list`, `xdr_distribution_create`, `xdr_distribution_get_url`, `xdr_distribution_versions`
- **Alert exclusions (3)**: `xdr_alert_exclusions_list`, `xdr_alert_exclusions_create`, `xdr_alert_exclusions_delete`
- **Hash analytics (2)**: `xdr_hash_get_analytics`, `xdr_hash_blocklist`
- **Exploit detection (2)**: `xdr_exploits_list`, `xdr_exploits_get_details`

### Provenance note

These 15 tools are **hand-authored** from public Cortex XDR API knowledge — not derived from the ebarti scaffold (which doesn't cover these categories). Endpoint paths + body shapes mirror the established `/public_api/v1/<category>/<action>/` + `request_data` envelope pattern. R4.4's E2E battery validates each against a real XDR tenant; any divergence from the official docs gets fixed in a fast-follow patch as part of v0.14.4.

### R4 arc progress

- **R4.0 (v0.14.0)** — DONE: docs + backbone
- **R4.1 (v0.14.1)** — DONE: UI tab + 12 tool entries
- **R4.2 (v0.14.2)** — DONE: endpoints + response + scripts (18 tools)
- **R4.3 (v0.14.3)** — THIS: admin endpoints (15 tools) — **51 total XDR tools**
- **R4.4 (v0.14.4)** — next: E2E battery + per-tool validation against deployed tenant

### Smoke-test bullets (cumulative since v0.14.2)

24. Cortex_XDR instance Tools tab now lists 51 tool entries
25. `xdr_audit_list_management_logs` callable from chat
26. `xdr_assets_list` returns enumerated assets
27. `xdr_distribution_list` returns the tenant's distribution lists
28. `xdr_alert_exclusions_list` returns the tenant's exclusion rules
29. `xdr_hash_get_analytics` callable; per-hash analytics returned (cold-start tenants may have empty analytics)
30. `xdr_exploits_list` returns enumerated exploits
31. Tools that take destructive actions (blocklist, hash, exclusion create/delete, distribution create) require explicit args — guards against accidental tenant-wide changes via the agent

### Forbidden going forward

- Authoring more "hand-authored" tools without the R4.4 E2E validation path — every new category needs the smoke battery to extend to it
- Mixing exploit/exception terminology with IoC terminology — they're DIFFERENT XDR APIs (`/hash_exceptions/...` vs `/indicators/...`)

---

## [v0.14.2] — 2026-05-23 — *R4.2 — XDR endpoints + response actions + scripts library (18 new tools)*

**The response-action tier.** v0.14.1 added incident/alert triage + IoC management; v0.14.2 turns the connector into a hands-on response surface — list endpoints, isolate them, run scripts, retrieve files. Eighteen new tools, all wired to the toggle infrastructure so operators control which the agent sees.

### What ships

- **Endpoints — query + actions (9 tools)**:
  - `xdr_endpoints_list_all` — full tenant scan (no filters)
  - `xdr_endpoints_get` — filtered query (endpoint_id_list, dist_name, ip_list, group_name, platform, alias, hostname, isolate)
  - `xdr_endpoints_isolate` / `xdr_endpoints_unisolate` — network containment
  - `xdr_endpoints_scan` / `xdr_endpoints_scan_all` — trigger scans (latter is high-impact, scoped separately)
  - `xdr_endpoints_set_alias` — rename in the XDR console
  - `xdr_endpoints_retrieve_file` — async file pull (chain with response/file_retrieval_details + xdr_download_file)
  - `xdr_endpoints_quarantine_file` — file-level quarantine by path + SHA-256
- **Response action status (2 tools)**:
  - `xdr_response_get_action_status` — poll status of any action
  - `xdr_response_get_file_retrieval_details` — return download URLs from a completed retrieval
- **Scripts library (7 tools)**:
  - `xdr_scripts_list` — query the library
  - `xdr_scripts_get_metadata` — full definition
  - `xdr_scripts_run_script` — run a library script
  - `xdr_scripts_run_snippet` — ad-hoc PowerShell/bash/Python
  - `xdr_scripts_get_execution_status` / `_results` / `_result_files` — poll + harvest
- **`_build_endpoint_filters` helper** — composes XDR's `{field, operator, value}` filter array from operator-friendly named kwargs. Keeps each tool function focused on its specific action shape.

### Smoke-test bullets (cumulative since v0.14.1)

18. `curl POST /api/v1/instances/.../test` → 200 (connector still healthy after rebuild)
19. `xdr_endpoints_list_all` callable from chat: "List all XDR endpoints" → ≥1 endpoint returned
20. `xdr_endpoints_get` with `isolate=["isolated"]` returns only currently-isolated endpoints
21. `xdr_endpoints_isolate` with a specific `endpoint_id_list` returns `{ok, action_id}`; immediately calling `xdr_response_get_action_status` with that id returns `PENDING` or `COMPLETED`
22. `xdr_scripts_list` returns the tenant's library entries (Cortex Standard scripts ship out-of-box)
23. Tools tab on `/connectors/cortex-xdr-Cortex_XDR` now lists 36 entries (8 legacy + 18 new + 6 R4.1 renames + 4 R4.1 net-new); operator can disable specific ones (e.g. scan_all, which is destructive) without affecting the rest

### R4 arc progress

- **R4.0 (v0.14.0)** — DONE: docs + backbone
- **R4.1 (v0.14.1)** — DONE: UI tab + 12 tool entries
- **R4.2 (v0.14.2)** — THIS: 18 tools (endpoints + response + scripts)
- **R4.3 (v0.14.3)** — next: admin endpoints (audit / asset / distribution / alert exclusions / exploits)
- **R4.4 (v0.14.4)** — E2E battery + arc closure

### Forbidden going forward

- Adding endpoint-action tools (isolate/unisolate/scan/quarantine) without at least one filter requirement — accidental tenant-wide actions are too destructive
- Hand-coding XDR filter arrays inline; use `_build_endpoint_filters` (extend it when a new filter field is needed)

---

## [v0.14.1] — 2026-05-23 — *R4.1 — XDR Tools tab UI + 6 renames + 6 net-new tools (incidents/alerts/IoC/download)*

**The R4 arc's UX milestone.** v0.14.0 shipped the backend infrastructure; v0.14.1 puts the per-instance tool toggle behind operator-facing checkboxes AND adds the first batch of new XDR tools. Twelve new tool entries land in the agent catalog: six are renames of the existing 8 (legacy names continue to work via aliases), six are net-new functionality.

### What ships

- **`/connectors` Tools toggle panel** — replaces the static "Show Tools" expansion. Each tool gets a checkbox; toggling triggers an optimistic PATCH to `/api/agent/instances/{id}` with the updated `disabled_tools` list. Snapback on PATCH failure. Mass actions: "Enable all" / "Disable all". Header shows "N/M tools enabled". Audit trail via `instance_tool_toggle` events.
- **Six rename aliases** (canonical names use `xdr_<category>_<action>`):
  - `get_cases_and_issues` → `xdr_incidents_list`
  - `get_incident_extra_data` → `xdr_incidents_get_extra_data`
  - `get_alerts` → `xdr_alerts_list`
  - `run_xql_query` → `xdr_xql_run_query`
  - `get_xql_results` → `xdr_xql_get_results`
  - `list_datasets` → `xdr_xql_list_datasets`
  - Legacy names continue to work; they appear as deprecated aliases. v0.15.0 removes them.
- **Six net-new tools**:
  - `xdr_incidents_update` — change status / severity / assignee / resolve comment
  - `xdr_alerts_update` — bulk-update alerts
  - `xdr_ioc_insert_json` — bulk-upload IoCs (indicator/type/severity/reputation)
  - `xdr_ioc_disable` — silence specific IoCs
  - `xdr_ioc_enable` — re-enable previously disabled
  - `xdr_download_file` — pull bytes from a retrieval URL (chains with v0.14.2's xdr_endpoints_retrieve_file + xdr_response_get_file_retrieval_details)
- **4 new endpoint markdown docs** in `data/knowledge/external/paloaltonetworks/cortex-xdr/action/`:
  - `incidents/update.md`, `alerts/update.md`, `ioc/disable.md`, `ioc/enable.md`
- **`Fetcher.get_bytes()`** — new method on the XDR client for one-shot byte downloads from XDR-issued retrieval URLs. Used by `xdr_download_file`.

### Smoke-test bullets (cumulative since v0.14.0)

10. `/connectors/cortex-xdr` instance row, expand Show Tools → see checkboxes (not static list)
11. Toggle one off → next chat turn that uses that tool shows agent saying it's unavailable (or doesn't suggest it)
12. PATCH `/api/agent/instances/<id> -d '{"disabled_tools": ["xdr_incidents_list"]}'` returns 200; audit feed shows `instance_tool_toggle` with `disabled_added: ["xdr_incidents_list"]`
13. "Enable all" button restores full set; toggle state updates without page refresh
14. `xdr_incidents_update` callable from chat: "Update XDR incident 12345 status to resolved_false_positive with comment 'duplicate'"
15. `xdr_ioc_insert_json` callable from chat with a list of IoC dicts
16. Old `get_alerts` STILL works (alias) — operators with existing workflows aren't broken
17. New `xdr_alerts_list` returns the same data shape as the old alias

### Phased delivery progress (R4 arc)

- **R4.0 (v0.14.0)** — DONE: docs pull + toggle infrastructure
- **R4.1 (v0.14.1)** — THIS RELEASE: UI Tools tab + first 12 tool entries (renames + net-new)
- **R4.2 (v0.14.2)** — next: endpoints + response + scripts (~15 tools)
- **R4.3 (v0.14.3)** — admin endpoints (~15 tools)
- **R4.4 (v0.14.4)** — E2E smoke battery + arc closure

### Forbidden going forward

- Removing legacy aliases (`get_alerts`, etc.) before v0.15.0 — they're deprecation-cycle commitments to operators
- Adding new XDR tools without the `xdr_<category>_<action>` naming convention OR without a matching markdown file in `data/knowledge/external/paloaltonetworks/cortex-xdr/action/`
- Bypassing the Tools toggle panel's PATCH flow for instance-state mutations (it's the audit-logged path)

## [v0.14.0] — 2026-05-23 — *R4.0 — Cortex XDR docs pull + per-instance disabled_tools infrastructure*

**Foundation for R4 — converts the Cortex XDR connector from 8 hand-coded tools to a full-API-coverage arc.** v0.13.3 closed R3.C (user data sources end-to-end); v0.14.0 opens R4 with three independent backbone pieces that all subsequent R4 releases depend on. End-user behavior changes very little — the 8 existing XDR tools work exactly as before — but operators using the REST API get a new per-instance tool-toggle mechanism.

### What ships

- **Vendor knowledge tree** — new top-level `data/knowledge/external/paloaltonetworks/cortex-xdr/action/` populated with 26 endpoint markdown files generated from `ebarti/cortex-xdr-client`. Includes `INDEX.md` cataloging every endpoint→tool name + `auth.md` describing standard + advanced bearer auth. `simulation/README.md` placeholder for the R3.C-style data-source simulation YAMLs we may add later.
- **Doc generator script** — `scripts/gen_xdr_docs_from_ebarti.py` is the maintainer-only codegen. Idempotent. Re-running overwrites the per-endpoint files; hand edits to INDEX.md (sections, not the auto-generated table) are preserved.
- **`disabled_tools` schema migration** — `instances.db` gains a `disabled_tools TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'` column (idempotent ALTER TABLE following the established v0.1.x migration pattern). Empty list = all tools enabled (opt-out model).
- **Instance store + dataclass** — `InstanceStore.update_disabled_tools(instance_id, list)` updates the JSON list with dedupe; `Instance.disabled_tools: list[str]` surfaces in `merged_config()` and the `/instances` REST responses.
- **Connector-loader filter** — `connector_loader.py` reads `instance.disabled_tools` before registering tools with FastMCP. Disabled tools never enter the agent's catalog. Log emission counts enabled/total per connector.
- **REST surface**:
  - `PATCH /api/v1/instances/{id}` now accepts `{disabled_tools: [...]}` body — audit-logged as `instance_tool_toggle` with added/removed deltas
  - `GET /api/v1/connectors/{id}/tools[?instance_id=<id>]` — NEW. Introspects the connector's `connector.yaml` spec.tools[] list; with `instance_id`, surfaces per-tool `disabled: bool` so the UI can render checkboxes with the right initial state
  - Agent-side proxy: `/api/agent/connectors/[id]/tools/route.ts`
- **No new MCP tools**, no UI changes, no renames yet — those land in v0.14.1.

### What's deferred to v0.14.1

- The `/connectors` instance-detail "Tools" tab UI (operator-facing toggle UX)
- Renaming the existing 8 XDR tools to the `xdr_<category>_<action>` convention with deprecation aliases
- First wave of new XDR tools: incidents + alerts + IoC + download

### Phased delivery progress (R4 arc)

- **R4.0 (v0.14.0)** — THIS RELEASE: docs pull + toggle infrastructure (backend-only)
- **R4.1 (v0.14.1)** — next: Tools tab UI + first ~12 new tools (incidents/alerts/IoC/download) + rename existing 8 with aliases
- **R4.2 (v0.14.2)** — endpoints + response + scripts (~15 tools)
- **R4.3 (v0.14.3)** — admin endpoints (~15 tools)
- **R4.4 (v0.14.4)** — E2E smoke battery + arc closure

### Capability acceptance criteria (R4 arc end-state, repeated from spec)

End-to-end on the deployed install with a configured Cortex_XDR instance:
1. `/connectors/cortex-xdr-Cortex_XDR` page shows a "Tools" tab listing all ~50 tools
2. Operator can disable a tool → next chat invocation doesn't expose it
3. Agent prompted with `"List the 5 most recent XDR incidents"` → calls `xdr_incidents_list` → returns real data
4. `e2e_xdr_tools_battery.py` runs end-to-end; all enabled tools return non-error
5. Audit feed shows `instance_tool_toggle` events
6. `data/knowledge/external/paloaltonetworks/cortex-xdr/action/INDEX.md` lists every tool

### Smoke-test matrix (cumulative since v0.13.3)

1. `find data/knowledge/external/paloaltonetworks/cortex-xdr/action -name "*.md" | wc -l` ≥ 28 (26 endpoints + INDEX.md + auth.md)
2. `INDEX.md` table lists 26 endpoints with `xdr_*` tool names
3. `python3 scripts/gen_xdr_docs_from_ebarti.py` runs to completion (re-runs are idempotent)
4. `instances.db` PRAGMA shows the `disabled_tools` column (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]')
5. `curl PATCH /api/v1/instances/<id> -d '{"disabled_tools": ["xdr_get_alerts"]}'` returns 200; subsequent GET shows the field
6. Audit feed contains `instance_tool_toggle` event with added/removed deltas
7. `curl GET /api/v1/connectors/cortex-xdr/tools` returns the 5 spec.tools entries (existing tools — UI tab + new tools follow in v0.14.1)
8. `connector_loader` log line shows "N/M tools enabled" when an instance has disabled_tools populated
9. pytest 467/467 still passing; tsc/lint/build clean

### Forbidden going forward

- Reading data-source/connector tool docs from the JS-rendered `docs-cortex.paloaltonetworks.com` site via WebFetch — use the ebarti-based generator (`scripts/gen_xdr_docs_from_ebarti.py`) or Chrome MCP rendering for gaps
- Skipping the `disabled_tools` filter in `connector_loader.py` for new tool registration paths
- Storing per-tool config beyond enabled/disabled in `disabled_tools` (if rate-limits or per-tool audit-level become needed, design a proper `instance_tool_config` table — don't overload the JSON list)
- Adding new vendor connector tools without populating `data/knowledge/external/<vendor>/<product>/action/<tool>.md`
