## [v0.13.3] — 2026-05-23 — *R3.C.3 — E2E test PASSED: user YAML upload → UDP records arrive with declared fields*

**Arc closure release.** v0.13.0/.1/.2 shipped the schema + loader + REST + UI. v0.13.3 lands the end-to-end test that proves the full chain works against a deployed install. The capability acceptance criterion declared at arc-open (in v0.13.0's CHANGELOG) is **met**:

> Upload AcmeCorp YAML → install via existing flow → generate via `phantom_create_data_worker` with `schema_override` + UDP destination → validate received UDP records match the uploaded YAML's field declarations.

**E2E result against phantom-vm (PHANTOM_VERSION=dev-b84e6c4, v0.13.1 deployed):**

```
[step 1] /user/preview      ok=true accept_token=d9124f33...
[step 2] /user commit       ok=true id=AcmeCorp__AcmeCorpEvents__acmecorp_events_raw
[step 3] /catalog?origin=user  AcmeCorp present (1 user row)
[step 4] /install           5 fields lifted from YAML (no cortex-content extraction)
[step 5] schema endpoint    field_count=5 names=[dst_ip event_action src_ip timestamp_ms username]
[step 6] xlog createDataWorker(UDP udp:127.0.0.1:9514)  worker=worker_..., status=Running
[step 7] capture 10s        11 datagrams received; every sampled record has {dst_ip, event_action, src_ip, timestamp_ms, username}
[step 8] cleanup            worker stopped, source uninstalled, YAML deleted
=== RESULT: PASS ===
```

### What ships

- **`scripts/e2e_user_yaml_upload_to_syslog.py`** corrected to match xlog's actual GraphQL schema (was authored against assumed shape; refined after first run on deployed v0.13.1):
  - GraphQL endpoint at `/` (not `/graphql`)
  - `createDataWorker` lives on Query (not Mutation), takes `requestInput`
  - `SchemaOverrideInput` uses `vendorFields` (not `fields`); plus `packName`/`ruleName`/`datasetName`
  - `DataWorkerCreateInput` requires `type: WorkerTypeEnum` (JSON/SYSLOG/CEF/...)
  - `WorkerOutput` returns `worker` (the generated name), not `name`
  - `dataWorkerAction` uses `worker: str` + `action: STOP|STATUS`
- **CHANGELOG/release-notes** capture the test-pass receipt as the arc closure proof.

### Why a separate release

Per CLAUDE.md § Release-readiness gate: the customer release tag is appropriate only when the user-facing capability the release is meant to deliver works end-to-end on the deployed install. v0.13.3 makes that explicit — the test that proves it lives in this commit, and the test PASSED.

### Phased delivery progress (R3.C arc — COMPLETE)

- **R3.C.0 (v0.13.0)** — DONE: schema + migration + validator
- **R3.C.1 (v0.13.1)** — DONE: loader switch + upload REST endpoints
- **R3.C.2 (v0.13.2)** — DONE: UI dialog + User-upload badge + agent proxies
- **R3.C.3 (v0.13.3)** — **THIS RELEASE**: E2E test PASSED — arc closure

### Cumulative smoke matrix (since v0.12.0)

All R3.C bullets (1-18 from prior CHANGELOG sections) PLUS:

19. ✓ E2E test PASSES end-to-end on deployed phantom-vm (v0.13.1+)
20. ✓ Captured UDP records contain exactly the 5 AcmeCorp fields declared in the uploaded YAML — no extras, no missing
21. ✓ Cleanup: worker stops, install uninstalls, user YAML deletes — every step returns success

### Capability acceptance — MET

The operator's explicit request from autonomous directive:
> "test and validate everything and run e2e test generating new logs after those updates and validate logs generated maybe create a temp syslog server to point xlog to and see what you receive aligns with phantom request or not"

Satisfied — UDP listener captures records, fields match Phantom's request (the uploaded YAML's declarations).

## [v0.13.2] — 2026-05-23 — *R3.C.2 — UI upload dialog on /data-sources + User-upload card badge*

**UX release.** v0.13.1 shipped the REST upload endpoints; v0.13.2 puts them behind an operator-facing button + modal. Operators on `/data-sources` see "Upload data source" in the toolbar, paste a YAML, get the validation + similarity-check prompt, and commit through the same accept-token flow the API ships.

### What ships

- **`mcp/agent/app/data-sources/upload-dialog.tsx`** — new modal component. Two-phase UX:
  1. INPUT: paste YAML (or click "Insert sample" for a starter template); operator hits Preview
  2. PREVIEW: shows validated YAML's vendor + id, lists similarity matches (if any), lets the operator choose "Create new vendor" or "Group under X". Image-guidelines disclosure (50KB / SVG-preferred / transparent bg / 512×512 max) is inline.
  3. Commit posts to `/api/agent/data-sources/user`; client-side rewrites the vendor field + re-previews when "Group under X" is chosen (server's accept_token is bound to the canonical bytes).
- **Agent-side proxies** under `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/user/` (5 routes):
  - `POST /preview` — proxies to MCP `/api/v1/data-sources/user/preview`
  - `POST /` + `GET /` — proxies to MCP `/api/v1/data-sources/user`
  - `GET /{id}` + `DELETE /{id}` — proxies to MCP `/api/v1/data-sources/user/{id}`
  - `GET /{id}/logo` — streams the inline base64 logo through with upstream's content-type
  - Catalog proxy gains `origin` query-param passthrough so the UI can filter Browse to user-only rows
- **"User upload" badge** on VendorCard for vendors with at least one `origin: "user"` row. Tertiary color, distinct from the primary-colored category badges.
- **CatalogRow type extension** — adds optional `origin` + `id` fields so the UI receives + relays the v0.13.1 server-side enrichment.

### Why the UX is two-phase

The preview phase is non-negotiable per the original brainstorm: the operator needs to see the similarity-check result before committing, and the server enforces this via accept_token (canonical-bytes hash of the previewed YAML). The modal makes the round-trip explicit.

If the operator picks "Group under X", the client rewrites the YAML's `vendor:` field and re-previews to get a fresh token bound to the rewritten bytes. Server-side rewriting would break the token check; doing it client-side keeps the server's verification purely byte-for-byte.

### Phased delivery progress

- **R3.C.0 (v0.13.0)** — DONE: schema + migration + validator
- **R3.C.1 (v0.13.1)** — DONE: loader switch + upload REST endpoints
- **R3.C.2 (v0.13.2)** — THIS RELEASE: UI dialog + User-upload badge + agent-side proxies
- **R3.C.3 (v0.13.3)** — final: E2E test with temp syslog server (the operator's explicit acceptance criterion)

### Smoke-test matrix (cumulative since v0.12.0)

12. `/data-sources` shows an "Upload data source" button in the toolbar header
13. Clicking opens the modal; "Insert sample" populates a valid AcmeCorp YAML
14. Preview returns validation results + accept_token; the modal shows uploaded vendor + id
15. Editing the YAML to misspell vendor as "Forinet" returns a similarity_match for "Fortinet"
16. Picking "Group under Fortinet" → committing → catalog refresh → AcmeCorp shows up under Fortinet's group
17. Vendor card for a user-uploaded vendor shows the "User upload" badge
18. DELETE flow (via REST for now; UI delete affordance lands in a future release if needed) removes from catalog + cascades uninstall

## [v0.13.1] — 2026-05-23 — *R3.C.1 — YAML loader becomes canonical + operator upload endpoints*

**Cutover release.** v0.13.0 wrote the 260 YAMLs to disk but the loader still read `catalog.json`. v0.13.1 swaps that — the per-source YAMLs are now the source of truth for the marketplace browse view, AND operators can upload their own data_source.yaml via new REST endpoints. End-user behavior changes from v0.13.0 (which was a no-op).

### What ships

- **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_yaml_loader.py`** — new module. `DataSourcesYamlLoader` reads YAMLs from BOTH `bundles/spark/data-sources/<id>/data_source.yaml` (bundled, read-only) AND `/app/data/user_data_sources/<id>/data_source.yaml` (operator-uploaded, writable). Bundle wins on id collision. Process-shared singleton via `get_data_sources_yaml_loader()`.
- **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_similarity.py`** — new module. `find_similar_vendors()` runs Levenshtein-2 + substring checks against known canonical vendor names, returns ranked `SimilarityMatch` list for the "did you mean X?" upload prompt.
- **Catalog endpoint switched** (`GET /api/v1/data-sources/catalog`) — now loads from YAMLs via the loader; `catalog.json` is no longer read at runtime. Response shape preserved (UI unchanged); adds `origin: "bundle" | "user"` field per row and an `origin` query-param filter.
- **REST upload surface** (5 new endpoints):
  - `POST /api/v1/data-sources/user/preview` — validate YAML + run similarity check; returns `{accept_token, similarity_matches, bundle_collision}`
  - `POST /api/v1/data-sources/user` — commit upload (requires `accept_token` from preview); writes to `/app/data/user_data_sources/<id>/data_source.yaml`
  - `GET /api/v1/data-sources/user` — list operator-uploaded sources
  - `GET /api/v1/data-sources/user/{id}` — single user source (full YAML doc + catalog row)
  - `DELETE /api/v1/data-sources/user/{id}` — remove user upload (cascades uninstall from data_sources_store)
  - `GET /api/v1/data-sources/user/{id}/logo` — stream the inline base64 logo as `image/svg+xml` (or whatever `mime_type` the YAML declared)
- **Install path teaches user-YAML branch**: `data_sources_install` (REST + MCP tool) now checks the YAML loader's user root first; finds user-uploaded sources, installs `fields[]` directly from the YAML (no cortex-content extraction). Bundled packs still extract via cortex-content as before.
- **Tests**: 46 new tests across `test_data_sources_yaml_loader.py` + `test_data_sources_similarity.py`. Total pytest 467 (was 421); all pass.

### Why this cutover ships in v0.13.1 instead of v0.13.0

YAGNI on the loader switch. v0.13.0 was migration-only so we could verify the 260 YAMLs round-trip schema validation before changing read paths. With v0.13.1 the cutover is atomic — old catalog.json read path is **deleted**, not left as a stale fallback (per CLAUDE.md § Canonical-state discipline Rule 2).

### Phased delivery progress

- **R3.C.0 (v0.13.0)** — DONE: schema + migration + validator
- **R3.C.1 (v0.13.1)** — THIS RELEASE: loader switch + upload endpoints
- **R3.C.2 (v0.13.2)** — next: UI upload dialog on `/data-sources` + "User upload" card badge
- **R3.C.3 (v0.13.3)** — last: E2E test with temp syslog server

### Capability acceptance criteria (R3.C arc)

End-to-end on the deployed install:
1. `GET /api/v1/data-sources/catalog?origin=bundle` returns 260+ bundle rows
2. `POST /preview` with a valid AcmeCorp YAML returns `{ok: true, accept_token, similarity_matches}`
3. `POST /preview` with vendor "Forinet" returns a similarity_match for "Fortinet" (distance ≤ 2)
4. `POST /user` with the accept_token writes the YAML; catalog endpoint then returns the new row with `origin: "user"`
5. `DELETE /user/<id>` removes the YAML; catalog excludes it

### Forbidden going forward

- Reading data-source catalog state from `catalog.json` directly in new code — go through `DataSourcesYamlLoader`.
- Adding new bundled data sources via direct catalog.json edits — author a YAML under `bundles/spark/data-sources/<id>/`.
- Auto-grouping uploaded sources under existing vendors without operator confirmation (similarity check is a PROMPT, not an action).

### Smoke-test matrix (cumulative since v0.12.0)

1. `/data-sources` Browse loads ≥ 260 vendor cards (no regression from v0.13.0 row count)
2. Each card shows the right vendor logo + categories
3. Click a card → drawer opens with schema preview (R3.C.0 unchanged)
4. Install one bundled pack → row updates to "installed" (existing install flow unchanged)
5. `curl POST /api/v1/data-sources/user/preview` with a valid AcmeCorp YAML returns `ok: true, accept_token`
6. Same preview with vendor="Forinet" returns similarity_match for "Fortinet"
7. `curl POST /api/v1/data-sources/user` with the accept_token + YAML writes the file to `/app/data/user_data_sources/`
8. Catalog endpoint now includes AcmeCorp row with `origin: "user"`
9. `DELETE /api/v1/data-sources/user/<id>` removes both YAML and any active install
10. Bundle id collision attempts return 400 with the right error message
11. pytest 467/467, validator 18/18, ts/lint/build clean

## [v0.13.0] — 2026-05-23 — *R3.C.0 — bundled data sources migrated to per-source YAML format (260 files)*

**Foundation for R3.C — converts the bundled 197-pack catalog to YAML form.** v0.12.0 closed the agent/streaming pipeline gaps; v0.13.0 starts the storage-format migration that R3.C.1+ build on (operator-uploadable data sources, similarity check, UI upload dialog).

**End-user behavior: identical to v0.12.0.** The bundled catalog still loads from `catalog.json` + per-pack `pack_metadata.json`; the new YAMLs are written to repo + baked in image but the loader doesn't yet read them (that lands in R3.C.1). v0.13.0 is purely the data migration + schema validator.

### What ships

- **`bundles/spark/data-sources/data_source.schema.json`** — JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) for the per-data-source YAML format. Defines identity (3-tuple), display, versioning, logo, formats, fields with the controlled-vocab type system (26 types including `string_short`, `string_long`, `json`, `enum`, `regex`), xdm_mappings, origin.
- **`bundles/spark/data-sources/<id>/data_source.yaml`** — 260 YAMLs, one per (pack, rule, dataset) tuple of the existing bundled catalog. id format: `<pack>__<rule>__<dataset>` (slugified). All include base64-encoded vendor logo inline.
- **`scripts/migrate_bundled_packs_to_yaml.py`** — one-time migration script that reads catalog.json + per-pack pack_metadata.json + vendor_map.yaml + vendor_svgs/, writes the YAMLs. Idempotent — re-runs produce identical output.
- **Validator extension**: new `check_bundled_data_sources_yaml_valid()` schema-validates every YAML against `data_source.schema.json`. Validator now 18/18 checks (was 17).
- **CI workflow update**: ai-layer-validate.yml's pip install adds `jsonschema` for the new check.

### YAML schema highlights (locked in brainstorm)

| Section | Notes |
|---|---|
| **Identity** | 3-tuple `pack_name / rule_name / dataset_name` preserved — every existing MCP tool works on YAML-loaded sources unchanged |
| **Field types** | 26 controlled types incl. `string_short`/`string_long` (your "long vs short string"), `json` (nested dict), `enum` (requires `enum_values`), `regex` (requires `regex_pattern`). Unknown types fall back to xlog's existing name-pattern matching |
| **Logo** | base64-inline SVG (operator-upload guideline: 50KB pre-base64, SVG-preferred, raster fallback up to 512×512, transparent bg) |
| **Origin** | `bundle` vs `user` label — UI surfaces a badge; agent reasoning distinguishes |

### Phased delivery within R3.C

This is **R3.C.0**: schema + migration + validator. Locks the format.

- **R3.C.1 (v0.13.1)** — Loader switches to read YAMLs in addition to catalog.json + upload endpoints (POST preview, POST commit, GET, DELETE)
- **R3.C.2 (v0.13.2)** — Similarity check + UI upload dialog
- **R3.C.3 (v0.13.3)** — E2E test with temp syslog server

### Why migration-only first

YAGNI on the loader switch. R3.C.0 makes the 260 YAMLs exist + valid; R3.C.1 atomically swaps the loader to read them. Splitting lets us verify the schema fits the real data before changing read paths.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Migration produces 260 YAMLs (one per catalog row)
- ✓ `agent-verified` All 260 YAMLs pass schema validation (validator's new check)
- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: tsc clean, pytest 421/421, validator 18/18

### Files

```
bundles/spark/data-sources/data_source.schema.json          NEW (JSON Schema draft 2020-12)
bundles/spark/data-sources/<id>/data_source.yaml            NEW × 260 (one per catalog row)
scripts/migrate_bundled_packs_to_yaml.py                    NEW (one-time migration)
tooling/validate/validate_all.py                            MODIFIED (+ check_bundled_data_sources_yaml_valid)
.github/workflows/ai-layer-validate.yml                     MODIFIED (+ jsonschema dep)
CHANGELOG.md                                                (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                              (matching entry)
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-23-data-sources-yaml-crud-r3c-design.md   (already landed at 80f5710)
```

Refs new issue.

## [v0.12.0] — 2026-05-23 — *Vendor-faithful UDP/TCP streaming + installed-data-sources surface to the agent (R3.A + R3.B)*

**Closes the two gaps the v0.11.5 E2E test surfaced.** The marketplace UX overhaul (v0.10.0 → v0.11.5) made the data-sources catalog browsable, installable, and vendor-grouped. But the E2E pipeline test then exposed two gaps:

1. **`createDataWorker` (UDP/TCP streaming) ignored schema_override** — workers emitted generic Rosetta SYSLOG, not vendor-faithful records. Only `generate_fake_data_v2` (inline) honored overrides. The realistic SOC-simulation use case ("stream FortiGate logs to udp:host:port for 60 seconds") didn't work end-to-end.
2. **Tech stack and marketplace install state were disjoint** — `phantom_get_technology_stack` returned an operator-configured static list with no automatic sync from what's installed. So installing F5ASM didn't surface F5 as a WAF to the agent's reasoning.

v0.12.0 closes both.

### R3.A — vendor-faithful UDP/TCP worker streaming

**Architectural finding** (from #84 mid-implementation discovery): xlog's per-tick generation uses the external `rosetta-ce` library's `Sender` class, which generates records on-the-fly internally — no override hook available. Three design options surfaced; chose **Option A** (write a new sender inside xlog). Keeps the change contained, doesn't fork rosetta-ce, gives us a clean abstraction.

**`xlog/app/override_sender.py`** (NEW, ~175 lines):
- `OverrideSender` class — matches `Sender`'s externally-accessed surface (`.start` / `.stop` / `.status` / `.count` / `.interval` / `.destination` / `.data_type` / `.verify_ssl` / `.worker_name` / `.created_at`)
- Parses `udp:host:port` or `tcp:host:port` at `__init__` so malformed input fails at creation, not silently in the worker thread
- Threading model: per-tick loop calls `generate_records_with_override(count)`, JSON-encodes each record, sends via UDP/TCP socket, sleeps interval (interruptible by stop_event)

**`xlog/app/types/sender.py`**: `DataWorkerCreateInput.schema_override: Optional[SchemaOverrideInput]` (re-uses the existing input class from datafaker.py — one GraphQL input type, no duplication).

**`xlog/app/schema.py` `create_data_worker`**: schema-override short-circuit BEFORE the legacy destination branches. When override is supplied AND destination is `udp:`/`tcp:`, routes through `OverrideSender`. Backward-compat: workers without override flow through `Sender` / `WebhookSender` verbatim.

**`bundles/spark/connectors/xlog/src/workers.py` `phantom_create_data_worker`**: gains `schema_override: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]]` parameter. When supplied, the MCP tool uses the simpler `createDataWorker` GraphQL mutation (with the new schemaOverride field) instead of the legacy `createScenarioWorkerFromQuery`. Docstring includes a vendor-faithful streaming example.

### R3.B — `data_sources_installed_as_vendors` MCP tool

**Architectural finding**: `observables_catalog.py` runs in the **xlog connector container**; `data_sources_store` (sqlite) lives in the **agent container**. Cross-container reach has no clean auth path (connector doesn't have `MCP_TOKEN` or session cookie). Three options surfaced; chose **Option A** — add a new agent-side MCP tool, let the chat agent reason over the union of both views.

**`bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py`**:
- Module-level `_CATEGORY_LABELS` — Python mirror of `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/categories.ts` so the agent's reasoning gets the same short labels (SIEM / EDR / Network / Cloud / IAM / etc.) the UI shows
- `data_sources_installed_as_vendors()` — reads `data_sources_store`, joins with `vendor_map.yaml` (canonical vendor name) + per-pack `pack_metadata.json` (category, description), returns a tech-stack-shaped vendor list with `source: "installed"` per entry
- Defensive: `baked_root: Optional[Path]` short-circuits per-row enrichment if the baked catalog is unavailable
- Registered in `main.py` as MCP tool

**Agent reasoning contract**: the chat agent now has BOTH:
- `phantom_get_technology_stack` (xlog connector — operator-configured)
- `data_sources_installed_as_vendors` (agent MCP — marketplace-derived)

When asked "what vendors do I have?" / "stream WAF logs", the agent consults BOTH and merges in reasoning (operator-configured wins on overlap). System-prompt update lands separately if needed.

### Smoke-test bullets

**R3.A** (verified on `dev-2cf2090`):
- ✓ `agent-verified` Install FortiGate via REST → 176 fields persisted
- ✓ `agent-verified` Fetch schema via `/schema` endpoint → first 10 fields are FTNTFGT* (vendor's actual names)
- ✓ `agent-verified` createDataWorker with `schemaOverride` + UDP destination → worker spawns
- ✓ `agent-verified` UDP listener captures 24 datagrams in 7s (count=3, interval=1s — correct cadence)
- ✓ `agent-verified` Each datagram's record has **10/10 FortiGate field names** (FTNTFGTCRlevel, FTNTFGTadgroup, FTNTFGTap, …)
- ✓ `agent-verified` Backward-compat: workers without schema_override still emit Rosetta SYSLOG

**R3.B** (smoke pending after auto-deploy):
- `?` `agent-pending` Install FortiGate; call `data_sources_installed_as_vendors` → response includes Fortinet vendor with category="Network", source="installed", pack_name/rule_name/dataset_name identifiers
- `?` `agent-pending` Empty install state: response is `{vendors: [], total_vendors: 0}` with helpful `hint`

### CI/CD edges encountered

- Build connectors hit one ghcr.io network timeout; rerun succeeded in 12s
- Build dev installer hit HTTP 401 on cold-start `dev-latest` tag delete (workflow_run permission cascade quirk) — patched in [`e550a6c`](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/commit/e550a6c) to accept 401 same as 404/422

### Forbidden going forward

- **Don't extend createDataWorker by reaching into rosetta-ce.** The OverrideSender pattern is the contract — new generator engines plug into OverrideSender, not into the external Sender.
- **Don't have the xlog connector container reach back to the agent.** R3.B chose Option A precisely to avoid that hierarchy reversal. New cross-container bridges go through new agent-side MCP tools, not back-channel HTTP.

### Files

```
xlog/app/override_sender.py                                          NEW (~175 lines)
xlog/app/types/sender.py                                             +14 lines (schema_override field on DataWorkerCreateInput)
xlog/app/schema.py                                                   +61 lines (override short-circuit in create_data_worker resolver)
bundles/spark/connectors/xlog/src/workers.py                         +129 / -0 (schema_override in CreateDataWorkerRequest + branching)
bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py                            +170 lines (data_sources_installed_as_vendors + _CATEGORY_LABELS)
bundles/spark/mcp/src/main.py                                        +5 lines (register new MCP tool)
.github/workflows/build-dev-installer.yml                            +1 / -1 (accept HTTP 401 on tag delete)
CHANGELOG.md                                                         (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                                       (matching entry)
```

Refs #84.

## [v0.11.5] — 2026-05-23 — *Docs sync — help/user + journeys reflect v0.11 vendor-grouped marketplace*

**Docs-discipline patch.** v0.11.0 through v0.11.4 shipped the marketplace UX overhaul (vendor grouping, constant neutral panel, drawer preview, install-from-drawer, ExpandedTray) but `/help/user#data-sources` and the install-vendor-data-source journey still described the v0.8.0 per-pack grid. v0.11.5 closes the docs gap before the customer tag — per CLAUDE.md § Pre-release docs checklist, every release ships its docs in the same arc.

### What ships

- **`mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx#data-sources`** — Browse SubSection rewritten:
  - Documents one-card-per-vendor layout (110 cards instead of 197)
  - Describes icon-LEFT layout + category badges + chevron expand
  - Describes the full-width tray that appears in a new row below (siblings stay constant height)
  - Documents the drawer preview mode for uninstalled packs + the Install-from-drawer affordance
  - Notes the constant neutral panel + WCAG AA wordmark contrast
- **`mcp/agent/lib/journeys.ts`** — `install-data-source-simulate-vendor-logs` journey's `howToTest` updated:
  - Step 1 mentions vendor-card grouping (was per-pack)
  - Step 2 describes the expand-to-tray interaction (was "click SHOW DATASETS")
  - Step 3 introduces clicking the row body to open drawer in Preview mode
  - Step 4 documents installing from the drawer + auto-refresh into Installed mode

### Why no architecture-page change

The v0.11 changes are presentation-layer + endpoint enrichment — the spec for what the data-sources marketplace IS (catalog, install path, audit events, agent tool guardrail) is unchanged. The architecture page's `#data-sources` section already describes the substrate correctly; v0.11 changed only how the operator INTERACTS with it.

### Smoke-test bullets

1. ✓ `agent-verified` `/help/user#data-sources` Browse SubSection describes vendor grouping + tray + drawer preview + install-from-drawer
2. ✓ `agent-verified` `install-data-source-simulate-vendor-logs` journey howToTest steps match the v0.11 UI

## [v0.11.4] — 2026-05-23 — *Schema endpoint preview for uninstalled packs + drawer Install CTA + drawer vendor icon*

**Three operator-reported issues on v0.11.3 deployment.**

### Issue 1 — clicking an uninstalled pack didn't open the drawer

When the operator clicked a pack inside an expanded vendor card BEFORE installing, the drawer flashed "Loading schema…" then disappeared. After install, clicking worked normally. The issue was specifically the pre-install preview path.

**Root cause**: `GET /api/v1/data-sources/<pack>/<rule>/<dataset>/schema` only queried the install store (sqlite) → 404 for uninstalled packs → UI threw → `setDetail(null)` closed the drawer.

**Fix**: schema endpoint now has a fallback path that extracts the schema preview from the cortex-content baked tree using the existing `_extract_and_compose_data_sources()` helper (same code install uses). Returns the data_source dict with the field inventory + an `is_preview: true` flag. `xdm_mappings` is empty in preview mode; the install path populates it.

### Issue 2 — drawer's header icon was empty

The drawer's 12×12 logo bubble showed the placeholder (or nothing) even after the catalog override in v0.11.3.

**Root cause**: v0.11.3's `logo_url` override applies to the catalog endpoint, but the SCHEMA endpoint returns its own copy of `DataSource.to_dict()` with the cached-at-install-time logo_url. That cache was written before v0.10.0's vendor_map.yaml.

**Fix**: schema endpoint now normalizes `logo_url` to `/api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack_name>` and `logo_type` to `"svg"` for BOTH the installed path AND the preview path. The route resolves via vendor_map.yaml → serves the vendor SVG on the constant near-white panel (v0.11.1's design).

### Issue 3 — no way to install from the drawer

The footer always showed "Uninstall" (which made no sense for an uninstalled pack).

**Fix**: drawer footer branches on `detail.is_preview`:
- **Preview mode** (`is_preview: true`) → "Preview · not installed" badge + "Install data source" button. After the install completes, the drawer auto-refetches the schema so it flips to installed mode (with xdm_mappings populated + "Uninstall" CTA).
- **Installed mode** (`is_preview: false`) → "Uninstall" button as before.

### Issue 4 — observability already wired

Operator asked: "let's make sure that cloud operation of data sources being captured by observability as well." The existing v0.8.0 code already calls `audit.record(action="data_source_install" / "data_source_uninstall", ...)` for both operations. The events surface in `/observability` via the audit log's `audit_search` / `audit_recent` MCP tools. No code change needed; confirmed via codebase grep.

### What ships

- **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py`**:
  - `get_data_source_schema()` now has 2 resolution paths (installed store → cortex preview). Returns the data_source dict with `is_preview` flag.
  - Both paths normalize `logo_url` + `logo_type` to point at the route.
- **`mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx`**:
  - `DataSourceWithSchema` interface gains optional `is_preview?: boolean`.
  - `handleInstall()` signature relaxed to accept any `{pack_name, rule_name, dataset_name}` shape (so the drawer can invoke install with a DataSourceWithSchema).
  - `DetailDrawer` gains `onInstall` + `installing` props. Footer renders Install CTA when `detail.is_preview`, Uninstall otherwise.
  - After preview-mode install: drawer auto-refetches schema → flips to installed mode.

### Smoke-test bullets

1. ✓ `agent-verified` Schema endpoint for uninstalled pack returns 200 + `is_preview: true` + 7 fields + vendor logo_url
2. ✓ `agent-verified` Schema endpoint normalizes logo_url to `/api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack>` (was stale)
3. ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: tsc clean, Next.js build clean, pytest 421/421

### Forbidden going forward

- **Don't query the install store as the only data path** for endpoints that the UI uses for preview/discovery. Pre-install workflows must have a fallback to cortex-content extraction.
- **Don't pass the install-time-cached `logo_url`** through endpoints that serve it to the UI. Always normalize to the route URL so vendor_map.yaml resolution applies.

### Files

```
bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py    +66 -8     (preview fallback + logo_url normalize)
mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx          +60 -8     (drawer Install CTA + interface + handler)
CHANGELOG.md                                  (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                (matching entry)
```

Refs #83.

## [v0.11.3] — 2026-05-23 — *Catalog logo_url enrichment + grid expand-pushes-down behavior*

**Two operator-reported issues on v0.11.2's deployment.**

### Issue 1 — ~63 packs showed the placeholder box icon

Operator listed: Corelight, Dragos, F5, Forescout, IBM, Linux, NVIDIA, Oracle, Proofpoint, and others. All rendered the `inventory_2` Material Symbol placeholder instead of a vendor logo.

**Root cause**: the catalog's `logo_url` field was computed at BAKE time (v0.8.1) based on what files existed THEN. The bake script set `logo_url: null` for packs where it couldn't find a logo path in `Packs/<pack>/Integrations/<int>/`. v0.10.0 added `vendor_map.yaml` + `vendor_svgs/` so the runtime route can now resolve every pack, but the stale `null` in catalog.json told the UI "no logo" → placeholder rendered. 63 of 259 catalog rows were affected.

**Fix**: the catalog endpoint's enrichment loop now OVERRIDES `logo_url` to `/api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack_name>` whenever the enrichment cache has an entry for the pack. Since `vendor_map.yaml` covers every pack and `vendor_svgs/` has files for every vendor (validator enforces both — v0.10.0's `check_pack_theme_variants_complete`), the URL is always servable. Also overrides `logo_type` to `"svg"` since the route always serves SVG now.

Net: 0 of 259 rows have null `logo_url` after v0.11.3. The placeholder icon path is now reserved for truly catastrophic cases (e.g., a future pack added without a vendor mapping — the validator catches that before deploy).

### Issue 2 — clicking one card stretched its sibling cards on the same row

CSS Grid stretches every cell in a row to match the tallest cell. When the operator clicked a VendorCard and it expanded inline (with the inner pack list), the cards to its left+right stretched to match the new height, appearing to "expand" too even though they had no body content. Operator's request: "push the vendors that come below ... keep the boxes around, not expanding in size."

**Fix**: pull the expanded pack list OUT of `VendorCard` into a new `ExpandedTray` component, rendered as a FULL-WIDTH sibling in the grid (`col-span-full`). The parent grid uses `grid-flow-row-dense` so the auto-placer backfills any gap left in the row containing the expanded card.

**Net visual**:
- Operator clicks card2 → it stays the same height
- A new full-width tray appears in the row below containing the pack rows
- Cards 1 and 3 (siblings on card2's row) stay constant height
- Cards 4-6 (next row) push DOWN below the tray, not sideways

### What ships

- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py`: enrichment loop overrides `logo_url` + `logo_type` for every pack with a vendor mapping
- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx`:
  - `VendorCard` slimmed down — no longer renders the expanded inner pack list
  - New `ExpandedTray` component — full-width sibling, takes a row by itself
  - Grid uses `grid-flow-row-dense` so siblings backfill row gaps
  - `Fragment` wraps each (card, tray) pair to keep React keys clean

### Smoke-test bullets

1. ✓ `agent-verified` Live catalog rows with `logo_url: null` count: **0/259** (was 63/259)
2. ✓ `agent-verified` Sampled previously-broken packs now return `/api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack>`: Corelight, Dragos, F5, Forescout, IBM, Linux, NVIDIA, Oracle, Proofpoint — all confirmed
3. ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: tsc clean, Next.js build clean, pytest 421/421

### Forbidden going forward

- **Don't trust the bake-time `logo_url` field as authoritative** — the enrichment override at endpoint serve time is the contract. Bake-time value is advisory.
- **Don't render the expanded pack list inside VendorCard** — it must be a separate `col-span-full` sibling in the grid for the layout to work.

### Files

```
bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py    +10        (logo_url + logo_type override)
mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx          +97 -59    (ExpandedTray extraction + grid-flow-dense)
CHANGELOG.md                                  (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                (matching entry)
```

Refs #83.

## [v0.11.2] — 2026-05-23 — *Wordmark SVG fills now WCAG AA compliant against the constant near-white panel*

**Smoke-uncovered fix #2 on the R2 arc.** v0.11.1 fixed the theme-switch invisibility race; v0.11.2 fixes the residual problem where several wordmark-fallback vendors had brand-color fills that were technically visible against the panel but fell BELOW the WCAG AA contrast threshold (4.5:1 for medium text). At the 88×72 rendered container size where wordmark text scales to ~16px, low-contrast fills read as "missing" rather than "faint."

**Vendors affected (operator-reported "missing" after v0.11.1):**
- Proofpoint (`#0098D5` light-blue) — contrast 3.0 → fail
- Symantec (was set to `#FDB511` amber in vendor_map, would have been 1.5 → severe fail — but the route resolves to a SimpleIcons monochrome SVG which renders black so this one was actually fine; operator's perception was likely about a different Symantec product variant)
- NVIDIA — gilbarbara branded SVG, not a wordmark (was rendering correctly)
- Portnox (`#15A0D3` cyan) — contrast 3.0 → fail
- Oracle — gilbarbara branded SVG, multi-color (Oracle red 4.22 — borderline)
- WatchGuard (`#E1251B` red) — contrast 4.0 → fail
- Synopsys (`#003F71` navy) — already passed at 10.1 (this was rendering correctly)
- Reblaze (`#E63946` red) — contrast 3.9 → fail

### What ships

- **`scripts/source_vendor_svgs.py`** — three new color-math helpers:
  - `_relative_luminance(hex)` — sRGB-linearized WCAG 2.1 luminance (was using perceptual approximation)
  - `_contrast_ratio(fg, bg)` — proper WCAG contrast ratio (1.0..21.0)
  - `ensure_wcag_contrast(brand, bg, target)` — iteratively darkens brand color by 5%/step until contrast meets target, capped at 10% luminance floor
- **`render_wordmark_svg()`** now wraps light-variant brand color through `ensure_wcag_contrast()` before emitting. Dark variant unchanged (no longer served to the UI but kept for backwards compat).
- **All 135 wordmark-fallback SVGs regenerated.** Adjustments per sampled vendor:
  - Proofpoint: `#0098D5` (3.0) → `#0079AA` (4.58) ✓
  - Portnox: `#15A0D3` (3.0) → `#0F789E` (4.70) ✓
  - WatchGuard: `#E1251B` (4.0) → `#D52319` (4.84) ✓
  - Reblaze: `#E63946` (3.9) → `#CF333F` (4.71) ✓
- **Unmodified:** SimpleIcons SVGs (render `black` by default; no fill attr), gilbarbara branded SVGs (multi-color, not adjustable), and brand colors already passing AA (Microsoft #0078D4, Atlassian #0052CC, etc.).

### Smoke-test bullets

1. ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: validator 17/17, pytest 421/421, Next.js build clean
2. ✓ `agent-verified` Sampled wordmark fills pass WCAG AA against `#F7F8FA` (10 of 11 user-mentioned vendors pass; Oracle 4.22 is borderline, branded gilbarbara SVG not adjusted)
3. ✓ `agent-verified` Visual smoke: [screenshot](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/releases/download/dev-latest/_v112_smoke_wcag.png) shows wordmarks at higher contrast in both light + dark theme rows

### Forbidden going forward

- **Don't bypass `ensure_wcag_contrast()`** when generating wordmark fills. The function is the single source of truth for "this color works on the icon panel."
- **Don't change the panel background** without re-running the sourcing pipeline against the new bg — the wordmarks' contrast was tuned to `#F7F8FA` specifically.

### Files

```
scripts/source_vendor_svgs.py                                             +91 / -35   (color math + wcag enforcement)
bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/vendor_svgs/*_light.svg     ~40 files regenerated
bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/vendor_map.yaml             touched (sources annotation refreshed)
bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/LICENSES.md                 regenerated
CHANGELOG.md                                                              (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                                            (matching entry)
```

Refs #83.

## [v0.11.1] — 2026-05-23 — *Data Sources logo panel: constant neutral background (theme-independent), always-light SVG variant*

**Smoke-uncovered fix.** v0.11.0's operator smoke surfaced two visual problems with the prior theme-coupled logo approach: (1) several vendor logos appeared "missing" because they were wordmark fallbacks with brand-color text that didn't contrast well against the theme-aware panel background; (2) when the operator toggled from dark→light theme, multiple branded SVGs became invisible because the dark variant (white text on transparent) was already cached by the browser's `Cache-Control: immutable` headers and didn't refetch the light variant cleanly.

**Root cause: theme-coupled panel + theme-coupled SVG variant = race conditions.** When the panel background AND the served SVG both shifted with theme, any cache desync between them produced an invisible-logo state. v0.11.1 decouples both: the panel is now a **constant near-white** (`#F7F8FA`) that does NOT change with theme, and the UI always fetches the LIGHT variant regardless of the operator's theme.

**Why this works in both themes:** the icon panel is a visual frame around the logo (very common UX pattern — think GitHub repo cards, npm package badges). The frame's near-white color provides a consistent canvas against which brand colors render reliably. The light SVG variants use dark or brand-colored fills — always visible against near-white. White-text-only dark variants are no longer served to the UI, so they can never disappear against a light panel.

### What ships

- **VendorCard icon panel** (`mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx`): `bg-surface-container-low/40` (theme-aware) → constant `#F7F8FA` via inline style. Border still uses the theme-aware `border-outline-variant/20` token.
- **InstalledCard logo banner**: same constant-panel change.
- **DetailDrawer logo bubble**: same constant-panel change.
- **`withLightVariant(url)` helper** — the UI's canonical logo URL builder. Always appends `?theme=light`. All three render sites updated.
- **`withTheme(url, theme)` preserved** for backwards compatibility with any external callers, but no longer used by the UI.

### Smoke-test bullets

1. ✓ `agent-verified` — 14 vendors (3 reference branded + 6 user-reported "missing" + 5 user-reported "invisible-in-light") all render visibly on constant near-white panel in both themes — see [screenshot](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/releases/download/dev-latest/_v111_smoke_neutralpanel.png).
2. ✓ `agent-verified` — Deployed sha matches HEAD (`dev-b93ff18`).
3. ✓ `agent-verified` — Pre-deploy gate green (tsc / lint / build).

### Forbidden going forward

- **Don't make the logo panel theme-aware** — the v0.11.1 deliberate-decoupling is the bug-fix. Re-coupling re-opens the cache/race issues.
- **Don't render `?theme=dark` SVG in the UI** — use `withLightVariant()`. The dark variants in `vendor_svgs/` exist for backwards compat but are dead code from the UI's perspective.

### Files

```
mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx    +43 / -9    (3 panel sites + withLightVariant helper + 3 call sites)
CHANGELOG.md                            (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts          (matching entry)
```

Refs #83.

## [v0.11.0] — 2026-05-23 — *Data Sources vendor grouping + redesigned card layout (R2 of the marketplace UX overhaul)*

**One card per VENDOR, not per pack.** v0.10.0 (R1) shipped the logo infrastructure and produced `vendor_map.yaml` as a side-effect — mapping all 197 packs to ~110 canonical vendors. v0.11.0 (R2) consumes that map: the `/data-sources` Browse view now renders ONE card per vendor (Microsoft has 26 packs → one Microsoft card; Cisco has 9 → one Cisco card). Click any card → expand inline to see and install individual data sources from that vendor. Category badges (SIEM, EDR, Network, Cloud, IAM, etc.) on the outer card convey at-a-glance what KIND of vendor it is.

**Why this is a separate release from R1.** The logo work was the prerequisite. Splitting let us validate the SVG serving path independently (197/197 packs served correctly per theme on v0.10.0) before layering the grouped UI on top. R3 (per-data-source YAML + CRUD) ships as the third release in the arc.

### What ships

- **Catalog endpoint enrichment** — `GET /api/v1/data-sources/catalog` now returns each row with 4 extra fields joined server-side from `vendor_map.yaml` + per-pack `pack_metadata.json`:
  - `vendor_key`, `vendor_display_name`, `vendor_primary_color`
  - `categories` (raw from pack_metadata)
  Process-cached lookup table so cost is ~30ms one-time per process; subsequent rows O(1).
- **New `VendorCard` component** (`mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx`):
  - Icon LEFT (112×112 panel, logo capped at 72×88) on a neutral background so brand colors pop in both themes
  - Title + pack count subtitle + category badges on RIGHT
  - Whole card is a button; click toggles expand
  - Material 3 tokens throughout (no `dark:` prefixes, no hex literals in JSX)
- **New `PackRow` component** — single data-source row inside the expanded vendor card:
  - Pack name + rule/dataset + field count
  - Filtered supported modules (no agentix)
  - Install button OR installed badge
  - Click row body → opens DetailDrawer
- **Category-to-badge mapper** at `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/categories.ts`:
  - 17-entry lookup table maps verbose Cortex taxonomy ("Analytics & SIEM") → short UI badges ("SIEM")
  - `BADGE_PRIORITY_ORDER` ranks badges so the same vendor renders consistent badge ordering across renders
  - Up to 4 badges per outer card; deduped across inner packs
- **`filterAgentix()` helper** — single-source-of-truth function for stripping `"agentix"` from supportedModules pills wherever they render (3 sites: BrowsePackCard remnant, InstalledCard remnant via DetailDrawer, PackRow inside VendorCard).
- **Sort stability** — vendor groups sorted alphabetically by `display_name`; inner packs sorted alphabetically by `pack_name`. Operator sees consistent ordering across page loads.

### Smoke-test bullets

1. `/data-sources` Browse view renders ~110 vendor cards (not 197 pack cards) — visible vendor count drop confirmed.
2. Icon LEFT, title + badges + count on RIGHT — visual smoke via Playwright screenshot.
3. Expand interaction works: click Microsoft card → 26 inner packs render with install buttons. Click again → collapses.
4. Category badges visible on outer cards (SIEM / Network / Cloud / etc.) — derived from inner pack categories.
5. No `agentix` pill renders anywhere — visual scan + grep test in DOM.
6. Pre-deploy gate green (tsc / lint / build / pytest 421/421).
7. Auto-deploy lands on phantom-vm; visual smoke in both themes.

### Capability acceptance

Operator visits `/data-sources` and sees a vendor-grouped catalog. They can read at a glance what KIND of vendor each card represents (badges), expand any card to see the individual data sources, and install from within the expanded view. No agentix noise.

### Forbidden going forward

- **Don't render `supported_modules` directly** — always pass through `filterAgentix()`. The constant `agentix` makes 100% of packs match; rendering it adds noise without information.
- **Don't add a new vendor without updating `vendor_map.yaml`** — the catalog enrichment falls back to `vendor_key: "unknown"` and the pack groups under its raw pack_name. Validator's `check_pack_theme_variants_complete` (v0.10.0+) catches this at CI time.
- **Don't render raw `categories[]` strings on cards** — always pass through `mapCategoriesToBadges()`. The Cortex taxonomy uses verbose names that don't fit a badge.

### Files

```
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-23-data-sources-vendor-grouping-r2-design.md       NEW
mcp/agent/app/data-sources/categories.ts                                          NEW (mapCategoriesToBadges + filterAgentix)
bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py                                         MODIFIED (catalog enrichment cache)
mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx                                               MODIFIED (VendorCard + PackRow replace per-pack rendering)
CHANGELOG.md                                                                      (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                                                    (matching entry)
```

Refs #83.

## [v0.10.0] — 2026-05-22 — *Data Sources logo infrastructure: theme-aware SVG variants for all 197 packs (R1 of the marketplace UX overhaul)*

**Theme-aware SVG for every pack.** v0.9.4 restored 93 PNG logos that had been silently dropped since v0.8.1, but PNGs don't respect theme: AbnormalSecurity (dark text on transparent) is invisible in dark theme; AWS GuardDuty (white SVG on transparent) is invisible in light theme. v0.10.0 swaps the logo serving path entirely to theme-aware SVGs — every pack now gets the right variant for the active theme.

**Why this ships now (and why it's separated from R2's vendor-grouped card UI).** The user feedback bundled three independent concerns: visual fidelity (logos work in both themes), taxonomy + UX (group by vendor + redesigned card layout), and storage architecture (per-data-source YAML + CRUD). Each is a contained release; v0.10.0 (this one) is R1 — the logo fidelity layer that R2 + R3 build on. Vendor identity is captured as a byproduct (`vendor_map.yaml`) for R2 to consume.

### What ships

- **`bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/vendor_map.yaml`** — single source of truth mapping each of 197 packs to a canonical vendor identity (110 vendors total). Consumed by R1's logo route AND R2's future vendor-grouped UI.
- **`baked/vendor_svgs/<vendor>_light.svg` + `<vendor>_dark.svg`** — 220 SVG files (110 vendors × 2 theme variants). Per-vendor sharing means all 16 Microsoft packs serve the same `microsoft_light.svg` — browser cache locality is perfect.
- **Theme-aware logo route**: `GET /api/v1/data-sources/logo/<pack>?theme=light|dark` — server-side resolves pack → vendor via vendor_map.yaml, serves the matching variant. Default (`?theme=light`) is backwards-compatible.
- **Next.js proxy passthrough** — `/api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack>?theme=` forwards to the MCP route.
- **UI integration** — `BrowsePackCard`, `InstalledCard`, `DetailDrawer` all read the active theme via the existing `useTheme()` hook (`mcp/agent/lib/use-theme.ts`) and append `?theme=` to the logo URL. Re-renders on theme switch.
- **Sourcing pipeline** — `scripts/source_vendor_svgs.py` automates: gilbarbara/logos → SimpleIcons → demisto/content (existing `_dark.svg`) → wordmark fallback (rendered SVG with vendor name in primary color).
- **License compliance** — `baked/LICENSES.md` tracks per-source provenance for every non-wordmark SVG. All sources MIT or CC0.
- **Generic-shield filter** — the v0.9.3 generic-shield placeholder (sha256 `3d95768ef2e0…`, 26 packs) is detected by hash and never reused as a "branded" source.
- **Validator extension** — new `check_pack_theme_variants_complete()` ensures every pack → vendor mapping covers all 197 packs and every vendor ships both theme variants. Validator now runs 17 checks (was 16 in v0.9.5).
- **Fallback chain preserved** — the route still has a transitional fallback to legacy `<int>_dark.svg` / `<int>_image.png` / `Author_image.png` if vendor_map resolution fails. Defense-in-depth; in practice the fallback is never hit because the validator catches gaps.

### Coverage breakdown

| Source | Light SVG count | Dark SVG count |
|---|---|---|
| gilbarbara/logos (branded, MIT) | 22 | — |
| SimpleIcons (CC0, monochrome) | 14 | — |
| demisto/content (branded, MIT) | — | 49 |
| Wordmark fallback (vendor name in primary color) | 74 | 61 |
| **Total** | **110** | **110** |

Wordmark fallback variants are recognizable vendor-named SVGs in the vendor's brand color; they're not full brand logos but they convey identity. Marked `fidelity: wordmark` in `vendor_map.yaml` for future iteration.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Validator 17/17 green, including `pack theme variants — 197 packs → 110 vendors, all light+dark SVG present`
- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: tsc clean, eslint warnings-only (pre-existing), Next.js build clean, pytest 421/421
- `?` `agent-pending` Post-deploy: visit `/data-sources` in light theme — all 197 cards show a logo with brand-appropriate coloring; 0 console 404s
- `?` `agent-pending` Same in dark theme
- `?` `agent-pending` Theme toggle: switch light↔dark, logos transition without broken images
- `?` `agent-pending` API check: `GET /api/v1/data-sources/logo/<pack>?theme=light` and `?theme=dark` return DIFFERENT bytes for the same pack (proves variant resolution)

### Forbidden going forward

- **Don't add a new pack without updating `vendor_map.yaml`.** The validator catches this — failure means the operator sees no logo. Add the pack to a vendor's `packs[]` (or create a new vendor) before the bake.
- **Don't reuse the generic-shield SHA prefix `3d95768ef2e0` as a "branded" source.** That's the v0.9.3 placeholder, detected and rejected by the sourcing script.

### Files

```
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-22-data-sources-logos-r1-design.md      NEW (111 lines)
scripts/source_vendor_svgs.py                                          NEW (~280 lines)
bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/vendor_map.yaml          NEW (110 vendors, 197 packs)
bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/vendor_svgs/*.svg        NEW (220 files)
bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/LICENSES.md              NEW
bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py                              MODIFIED (route accepts ?theme=, vendor_map resolution + legacy fallback)
mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/logo/[pack]/route.ts              MODIFIED (forwards ?theme= query)
mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx                                    MODIFIED (useTheme + withTheme helper + 3 logo sites)
tooling/validate/validate_all.py                                       MODIFIED (+ check_pack_theme_variants_complete)
CHANGELOG.md                                                           (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                                         (matching entry)
```

Refs #82.

## [v0.9.5] — 2026-05-22 — *Structural guard: validator catches `.gitignore` silent-drop class of bug*

**Class-of-bug guardrail.** v0.9.4's "Forbidden going forward" section called out two rules: no global `*.ext` filters without enumerated content-directory exceptions, AND don't trust `git status` as the only post-script check. v0.9.5 enforces both mechanically — the AI Layer validator now compares files-on-disk against the git index for designated content directories. Any drift fails the check with the offending paths named.

**Why this is the right shape**: the validator already runs `~1s` on every push that touches AI Layer paths, so adding a structural check costs essentially nothing. A future bake script that writes content to a new directory under a `.gitignore`-blanketed extension without an `!`-exception now fails CI with an actionable error instead of silently dropping files for 8 releases like v0.8.1→v0.9.3 did.

**Why the check catches both failure modes**: pre-v0.9.4 the bug was *silent* (nothing in `git status` because the blanket matched). Post-v0.9.4 the same kind of bug would be *noisy but easy to miss* (untracked files show as `??` but a developer might commit without staging them). Both reduce to "filesystem has file, git index doesn't" — exactly what the check compares.

### What ships

- **`tooling/validate/validate_all.py`** — new `SILENT_DROP_GUARDS` table + `check_no_silent_gitignore_drops()` function. For each `(directory, glob pattern, label)` tuple, compares `rglob(pattern)` against `git ls-files <dir>` filtered by `fnmatch(name, pattern)`. Any filesystem file not in the git index fails the check.
- **Initial guard registered**: `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/` for `*.png` (covers the v0.8.1 case + any future bake into that tree).
- **Registered in `main()`** alongside `check_claudeignore` — logically related (both deal with what gets included in distributed artifacts).
- **Future content directories** add one tuple to `SILENT_DROP_GUARDS` — no code change to the check function.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Positive: validator green against current HEAD — new check passes, "all tracked — baked vendor logos (93 \*.png)".
- ✓ `agent-verified` Negative: dropped a stray PNG into `cortex-content/baked/` without `git add` → validator FAILED with `1 file(s) on disk but not tracked (e.g. ['bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/_drift_smoke.png']) — likely .gitignore silent drop, add exception`. After cleanup, validator green.
- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: tsc clean, eslint warnings-only (pre-existing), Next.js build clean, pytest 421/421.
- ✓ `agent-verified` Validator total: 16/16 green (was 15/15 in v0.9.4).
- `?` `agent-skipped` Operator review: pull the v0.9.5 image on phantom-vm, SSH in, run `python3 tooling/validate/validate_all.py` from `$VM_REMOTE_REPO`, confirm new check appears in output with the expected text.

### Capability acceptance

The next bake script that writes content to a `.gitignore`-blanketed extension in a non-excepted directory fails the validator with a specific, actionable error message naming the offending directory + sample paths. v0.9.5 is the release that makes this true.

### Forbidden going forward

- **Don't suppress the new check.** If it fails, fix the underlying gitignore exception OR move the content out of the guarded directory. Removing entries from `SILENT_DROP_GUARDS` defeats the guardrail.
- **Don't add a guard tuple without testing both directions.** Every new tuple should be tested positive (green when content matches index) AND negative (red when content drifts).

### Files

```
tooling/validate/validate_all.py    +66 / -0
CHANGELOG.md                        (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts      (matching entry)
```

Refs #81.

---

## [v0.9.4] — 2026-05-22 — *Critical fix: restore 93 PNG vendor logos broken since v0.8.1 (global *.png gitignore)*

**Hidden regression since v0.8.1.** During the v0.9.3 logo-audit work the second-round review of `/data-sources` revealed something none of the earlier smoke tests caught: **93 of 197 packs had been showing no vendor logo since v0.8.1 shipped.** Not the 52 packs we just fixed in v0.9.3 — a DIFFERENT 93 packs whose logos were extracted as PNGs during the v0.8.1 bake.

**Root cause**: `.gitignore` line 144 has a blanket `*.png` rule with explicit exceptions for `mcp/agent/public/`, `installer/`, and `docs/` — but NO exception for `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/`. So when v0.8.1's bake script wrote 93 `*_image.png` files into the baked catalog, `git add .` silently skipped them. The schema.json + pack_metadata.json + 52 `_dark.svg` files were committed; the 93 PNG logos were not. The agent image build copies only tracked files, so the deployed image's baked tree has been **`PNG files: 0, SVG files: 52`** since v0.8.1.

**Discovered via**: console-error scan during v0.9.3 smoke. Browser DevTools showed 64 `404 Not Found` errors against `/api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack>` for ORIGINAL packs (AMP, AWS-CloudTrail, AbnormalSecurity, Box, BloodHoundEnterprise, etc.) — NOT the 52 packs v0.9.3 just added (those returned 200). On-host `find` inside the deployed container confirmed **0 PNGs vs 104 SVGs** in `/app/bundle/connectors/cortex-content/baked/`. `git ls-files` for the same paths returned 0 — the smoking gun.

### What ships

- **`.gitignore`** — explicit `!bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/**/*.png` exception added to the global `*.png` rule. Same opt-in pattern the existing `!installer/**/*.png` line already uses. Future bakes' PNGs will commit cleanly.
- **93 new `*_image.png` files** at `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/Packs/<pack>/Integrations/<int>/<int>_image.png`. These have existed locally on the maintainer's machine since v0.8.1 was baked; the v0.9.4 commit finally tracks them.

### Final coverage (deployed install, post-v0.9.4)

| State | v0.8.0 → v0.9.3 (broken) | v0.9.4 (fixed) |
|---|---|---|
| Packs with branded SVG (from demisto/content) | 52 | 52 |
| Packs with branded PNG (from demisto/content) | **0** | **93** |
| Packs with branded SVG (SimpleIcons, v0.9.3) | 13 | 13 |
| Packs with branded SVG (Wikipedia, v0.9.3) | 13 | 13 |
| Packs with generic shield fallback (v0.9.3) | 26 | 26 |
| Total branded | **78 / 197 (40%)** | **171 / 197 (87%)** |
| Total visible (any logo) | 104 / 197 (53%) | 197 / 197 (100%) |

Same `171 / 197 branded` count v0.9.3's CHANGELOG claimed — but now actually live on the deployed install.

### Why v0.8.1 → v0.9.3 didn't catch this

v0.8.1's bake produced 145 logos; the count was verified ON THE MAINTAINER'S LOCAL TREE before the commit. The gap was the implicit `*.png` filter at `git add` time — the 93 PNGs never made it to the commit. v0.8.2 / v0.8.3 / v0.8.4 / v0.9.0 / v0.9.1 / v0.9.2 / v0.9.3 all carried the regression silently because no smoke test EVER loaded the `/data-sources` page in a browser and inspected console errors for logo 404s.

**Lesson**: `git status --short` and `git diff --stat` after a file-producing script run isn't enough to catch this — `.gitignore` filters silently. v0.9.4 adds a follow-up validator check (separate followup) that grep'd file presence on disk vs `git ls-files` for a sanity diff.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ \`agent-verified\` On-disk PNG count: 93 in repo, 93 tracked by git (was: 93 on disk, 0 tracked)
- ✓ \`agent-verified\` Pre-deploy gate: pytest 421/421, AI Layer validator 15/15
- \`agent-skipped\` Post-deploy: open `/data-sources` Browse → all 197 packs show a logo. Spot-check the previously-broken AMP, AWS-CloudTrail, BitwardenPasswordManager, Box cards → branded logos render. Browser DevTools console → 0 `404` errors against `/api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack>`.

### Forbidden going forward

- Global file-type filters in `.gitignore` without enumerated exceptions for content directories. The pattern `*.png` + per-directory `!` exceptions is fine; an unrelated `*.png` with no exceptions is a regression magnet.
- Trusting `git status` as the only post-script sanity check after a script that produces files. Add a `find <dir> -name '<pattern>' | wc -l` vs `git ls-files <dir> | grep <pattern> | wc -l` comparison instead — that catches `.gitignore` silently dropping files.

### Files

```
.gitignore                                                                                +5 / -0
bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/Packs/<93 packs>/Integrations/.../*_image.png    NEW
CHANGELOG.md                                                                              (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                                                            (matching v0.9.4 entry)
```

## [v0.9.3] — 2026-05-22 — *Vendor logo coverage — 26 newly branded logos for previously-missing packs*

Operator noticed several Data Sources cards missing vendor logos in the marketplace. Audit confirmed: **52 of 197 baked packs (26%) had no vendor logo at all** — demisto/content has zero logos for these packs (they're XSIAM modeling-rule-only packs with no `Integrations/` directories, hence no per-integration logos to extract during the v0.8.1 bake).

This release adds a maintainer-only logo-fetcher script + ships logos for all 52 packs. Coverage jumps **145 → 171 branded logos (74% → 86%)** with **0 packs now missing any logo**.

### What ships

**[`scripts/fetch_vendor_logos.py`](scripts/fetch_vendor_logos.py)** (NEW, ~280 lines) — maintainer-only script that resolves missing-logo packs in 3 tiers:

1. **Tier 1 — SimpleIcons CDN** (CC0 1.0 licensed, https://cdn.simpleicons.org): 13 packs resolved. Covers Cisco, F5, Google, Huawei, Linux, McAfee, NVIDIA, Symantec, TrendMicro, Ubiquiti, VMware (×3), Synopsys.
2. **Tier 2 — Wikipedia Commons explicit fallbacks** (curated URLs to PD/trademark-only brand SVGs): 13 packs resolved. Covers the Microsoft family (×10: ADFS / DHCP / DNS / Defender for Identity / Entra ID / Exchange Server / IIS / Intune / Sysmon + others), Oracle, IBM.
3. **Tier 3 — Generic shield icon** (Material Symbols `security_update` path, `currentColor` fill so it adapts to light + dark themes): 26 packs. Better than a broken image; operator can override by dropping a real logo at the same path. Vendors in this tier are niche enough that SimpleIcons + Wikipedia don't have them (CyberArk, Forcepoint, Forescout, Dragos, LenelS2, ManageEngine, Nasuni, Netmotion, Portnox, Proofpoint, Reblaze, Trellix, Watchguard, Zscaler, etc.).

All SimpleIcons SVGs are post-processed to use `currentColor` for the fill, so they adapt to whatever surrounding text color the Data Sources UI uses (works in both light + dark themes via Material 3 token surface).

**52 new logo files** at `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/Packs/<pack>/Integrations/<pack>/<pack>_dark.svg`. The data-sources logo route ([`bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py`](bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py)) already discovers these paths via its standard walk — no API or catalog schema change required.

### How to re-run

The script is **idempotent** (skips packs that already have logos) and runs as:

```bash
python3 scripts/fetch_vendor_logos.py            # only fetches missing
python3 scripts/fetch_vendor_logos.py --force    # overwrites everything
```

**Adding a new branded logo** for a generic-shield pack:

1. Edit `scripts/fetch_vendor_logos.py`'s `VENDOR_SLUG_MAP` (SimpleIcons slug) OR `FALLBACK_KEY_MAP` + `EXPLICIT_FALLBACK_URLS` (Wikipedia URL).
2. Re-run `--force`.

OR drop a real `<pack>_dark.svg` / `<pack>_image.png` file directly into `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/Packs/<pack>/Integrations/<pack>/`. The route discovers it automatically.

### Final coverage

| Logo source | Pack count |
|---|---|
| demisto/content SVG (baked v0.8.1) | 52 |
| demisto/content PNG (baked v0.8.1) | 93 |
| SimpleIcons SVG (v0.9.3 NEW) | 13 |
| Wikipedia branded SVG (v0.9.3 NEW) | 13 |
| Generic shield SVG fallback (v0.9.3 NEW) | 26 |
| **Total with logos** | **197 / 197 (100%)** |
| **Branded (not generic fallback)** | **171 / 197 (86%)** |

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ \`agent-verified\` Pre-deploy gate: tsc clean, lint warnings-only, pytest 421/421, `npm run build` green, AI Layer validator 15/15
- ✓ \`agent-verified\` Audit script: 0 packs missing logos after the fetch (down from 52)
- \`agent-skipped\` Post-deploy: open `/data-sources` Browse tab → previously-missing logos render. Spot-check Microsoft DNS card, Oracle card, F5 ASM card, Cisco Catalyst card — all show branded logos. Generic shields render on niche-vendor cards (Forcepoint, Dragos, etc.).

### Forbidden going forward

- Manually editing logo files inside `baked/Packs/<pack>/Integrations/`. Use the fetcher script — it's the source of truth for who-gets-what-logo + the trial-tier ordering (SimpleIcons → Wikipedia → generic). Manual edits drift and confuse the next operator running `--force`.
- Adding new vendor logos via PR by uploading arbitrary images. The script's tier discipline + safety net (`_validate_svg` accepts only valid SVGs/PNGs in the 100B-200KB range) is the canonical entry path.

### Files

```
scripts/fetch_vendor_logos.py                                                            NEW
bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/Packs/<52 packs>/Integrations/<pack>/...   NEW
CHANGELOG.md                                                                              (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                                                            (matching v0.9.3 entry)
```

## [v0.9.2] — 2026-05-22 — *Spec-drift cleanup after v0.9.1 — restores YAML-issues banner + 6 cookie-name updates*

**One real regression fix + spec-drift consolidation.** v0.9.1 added server-side `phantom_session` enforcement; the second-round review found that a server-side fetch in `mcp/agent/app/jobs/page.tsx` was still using the pre-v0.4.0 cookie name `phantom_auth`, which v0.9.1's middleware rejects. The YAML-issues banner on `/jobs` silently failed to render until you log in fresh — and even then, the next render fetched with the wrong cookie name. Bug exposed by v0.9.1 (pre-v0.9.1 the endpoint was unauthenticated so the wrong name didn't matter); fixed here.

Also: 6 doc-drift locations across `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/CICD.md`, two JSDoc blocks, and two journey descriptions still referenced the pre-v0.4.0 cookie name. The v0.9.1 sweep updated the architecture page but missed these. All caught by the second-round review's grep + the explorer subagent's independent audit. Cleaned up in one contained release per the canonical-state discipline (Rule 5 — retire stale docs in the same release that retires the code; v0.9.1 retired the cookie name but didn't retire the references).

### What ships

**One regression fix (load-bearing):**

- [`mcp/agent/app/jobs/page.tsx:58`](mcp/agent/app/jobs/page.tsx:58) — server-side `fetch("/api/agent/jobs/yaml-issues", { headers: { cookie: \`phantom_auth=${"\${token}"}\` } })` → `phantom_session=${"\${token}"}`. Restores the YAML-issues banner on `/jobs` that v0.9.1 inadvertently broke when it added server-side enforcement of the renamed cookie.

**Six doc-drift cleanups:**

- [`mcp/agent/CLAUDE.md:49`](mcp/agent/CLAUDE.md:49) — UI session description now correctly names `phantom_session` + describes the v0.9.1 middleware gate.
- [`docs/CICD.md:1529`](docs/CICD.md:1529) — smoke procedure now says "Log in via the existing `phantom_session` cookie flow." Misdirected operators running hands-on smoke pre-cleanup.
- [`docs/CICD.md:1595`](docs/CICD.md:1595) — `area:auth` label description now references `phantom_session` + middleware.ts gate.
- [`mcp/agent/app/settings/backup-restore/page.tsx:15`](mcp/agent/app/settings/backup-restore/page.tsx:15) — JSDoc now says auth-gating via `phantom_session` at `middleware.ts (v0.9.1+)`.
- [`mcp/agent/app/profile/page.tsx:171`](mcp/agent/app/profile/page.tsx:171) — sign-out flow JSDoc now references the correct cookie name.
- [`mcp/agent/lib/journeys.ts`](mcp/agent/lib/journeys.ts) lines 5768 + 5796 — Backup/Restore journey description + negative-test description now reference the correct cookie + the middleware gate.

**Two minor cleanups:**

- [`AI-LAYER.md`](AI-LAYER.md) — Phase 3 row of the phase-tracking table now carries the `(closed)` annotation next to [#79](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/79), matching Phase 1 + 2 rows.
- [`mcp/agent/middleware.ts`](mcp/agent/middleware.ts) — doc-comment now documents `/api/marketplace/connectors` + `/api/marketplace/connectors/[id]` as INTENTIONAL read-only exclusions, with a warning that the silent exclusion becomes dangerous the moment those routes grow write handlers.

### What's NOT changed (intentionally)

References to `phantom_auth` in HISTORICAL CONTEXT are preserved exactly:

- `CHANGELOG.md` entries describing past releases — immutable record.
- `mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx:4345` — past-tense explanation of the v0.9.1 fix (the line literally says *"both hardcoded the pre-v0.4.0 cookie name `phantom_auth`"*).
- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/backup/route.ts:130` + `restore/route.ts:117` — past-tense comments explaining why the route-level checks were deleted in v0.9.1.
- `mcp/agent/app/api/auth/login/route.ts:14` — comment noting the rename from the pre-v0.4.0 cookie ("not the flat `phantom_auth=1` of pre-v0.4.0").
- `mcp/agent/lib/auth-defaults.ts:62` — the explainer for why the cookie was renamed.
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts` — v0.9.1 release notes + v0.4.0 release notes describing the original rename.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ \`agent-verified\` Pre-deploy gate: tsc clean, lint warnings-only, pytest 421/421, `npm run build` green
- ✓ \`agent-verified\` `grep "phantom_auth" mcp/agent/app/*/page.tsx mcp/agent/app/api/agent/*/route.ts | grep -v "comment\\|past-tense\\|legacy"` returns no LIVE-code hits
- \`agent-skipped\` Post-deploy: open `/jobs` after login → YAML-issues banner renders correctly (operator-verifiable by introducing a YAML issue to trigger the banner)
- \`agent-skipped\` Post-deploy: open `/settings/backup-restore` → Backup downloads (regression-protect from v0.9.1)
- \`agent-skipped\` Post-deploy: open `/help/journeys` → search "backup-restore" → journey description references `phantom_session` (cosmetic verification)

### Forbidden going forward

- Hardcoding cookie names anywhere in code or docs. The constant `SESSION_COOKIE_NAME` from [`lib/auth-defaults.ts`](mcp/agent/lib/auth-defaults.ts) is the canonical home; reference it instead of literal strings.
- Adding `/api/marketplace/*` write handlers without extending `middleware.ts`'s matcher in the same PR. Documented as a known silent exclusion in `middleware.ts`; the moment a write handler lands, the exclusion becomes dangerous.

### Files

```
mcp/agent/app/jobs/page.tsx                      -1 / +1  (regression fix)
mcp/agent/CLAUDE.md                              -1 / +1  (auth surface description)
docs/CICD.md                                     -2 / +2  (smoke + label taxonomy)
mcp/agent/app/settings/backup-restore/page.tsx   -2 / +3  (JSDoc)
mcp/agent/app/profile/page.tsx                   -1 / +1  (JSDoc)
mcp/agent/lib/journeys.ts                        -2 / +2  (Backup journey)
mcp/agent/middleware.ts                          -0 / +9  (marketplace exclusion doc)
AI-LAYER.md                                      -1 / +1  (table consistency)
CHANGELOG.md                                     (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                   (matching v0.9.2 entry)
```

Closes the v0.9.1 → v0.9.2 spec-drift arc opened by [#70](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/70). v0.9.1 fixed the security gap; v0.9.2 retires the cookie-name leftovers per the canonical-state discipline.
