## [v0.9.1] — 2026-05-22 — *Security: close the API auth gate (#70) + restore Backup/Restore*

**SECURITY FIX**: closes [#70](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/70) — pre-v0.9.1 every operator-control API endpoint was reachable without a session cookie. AuthGate (`components/auth/auth-gate.tsx`) decided what the browser RENDERED but imposed zero protection on the server-side API. A caller that reached port 3000 by any path — VM lateral movement, misconfigured firewall, accidentally-exposed cloud LB, container escape — could `POST /api/agent/memory`, `/api/agent/jobs`, `/api/chat`, `/api/skills`, etc. with NO cookies and get 200/201 back.

v0.9.1 adds a Next.js [`middleware.ts`](mcp/agent/middleware.ts) running at the SERVER tier. It validates the `phantom_session` cookie via `validateSession` against the MCP-side session store. Absence OR invalid value → 401 JSON. Same cookie AuthGate reads on the client; same validation `/api/auth/status` does. Defense-in-depth at the right layer.

**Companion fix**: Backup/Restore have been completely broken for every operator since v0.4.0. Both routes hardcoded `const COOKIE_NAME = "phantom_auth"` — the pre-v0.4.0 cookie name. The current cookie is `phantom_session`. Every operator click on Backup → 401. Discovered by the `phantom-explorer` subagent during the auth-surface map for #70. Fixed in the same release by deleting the broken route-level check and trusting the new middleware (canonical-state discipline, Rule 1: one auth gate, one home).

### What ships

- **`mcp/agent/middleware.ts`** (NEW) — server-side session-cookie enforcement. Edge runtime (Next.js 15.1.6). Matcher covers `/api/agent/:path*` + `/api/chat` + `/api/skills/:path*`. Exemptions for `/api/agent/health` (Docker compose healthcheck) and `/api/agent/internal/fire-hook` (uses `MCP_TOKEN` bearer, not the session cookie — called by the embedded MCP subprocess). `/api/auth/*` is excluded from the matcher entirely (login can't require login).
- **`mcp/agent/app/api/agent/backup/route.ts`** — deletes the broken `phantom_auth` cookie check + the `cookies` import + the `COOKIE_NAME` constant. Auth now enforced by middleware exclusively.
- **`mcp/agent/app/api/agent/restore/route.ts`** — same cleanup.
- **`mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx`** — `#authentication` section updated to reflect the middleware layer; stale `phantom_auth` references in the Backup/Restore subsection rewritten to `phantom_session`.
- **Help docs**: `/help/user#authentication` notes that the API surface is now session-gated server-side (operator-visible only if scripting against the API).

### Threat model (before vs after)

| | **Pre-v0.9.1** | **v0.9.1** |
|---|---|---|
| Attacker reaches port 3000 with NO cookies | full agent control (memory, jobs, hooks, tool dispatch, LLM turns) | 401 on every endpoint except `/api/agent/health` |
| Attacker has a random non-empty cookie value | full agent control (no validation) | 401 — `validateSession` rejects unknown tokens against the MCP-side session store |
| Operator with valid `phantom_session` cookie | works | works (cached 30s per token; ~10ms first-request overhead) |
| Embedded MCP calling `/api/agent/internal/fire-hook` with `MCP_TOKEN` | works | works (exempted from middleware; route's own bearer check unchanged) |
| Docker compose healthcheck hitting `/api/agent/health` | works | works (exempted) |

### Coverage

84 routes under `/api/agent/**` + `/api/chat` + `/api/skills/**`, all now session-gated. The full inventory + per-route auth verdict is in the issue body of [#70](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/70).

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ \`agent-verified\` Pre-deploy gate: tsc clean, lint warnings-only, pytest 421/421, `npm run build` green — middleware compiled at 33.4 kB in Edge runtime
- \`agent-skipped\` Repro from [#70](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/70) — every curl command in the issue body should now return 401 (operator verification via IAP tunnel; agent-verified post-deploy)
- \`agent-skipped\` Logged-in operator clicks Backup → downloads zip (was 401 since v0.4.0)
- \`agent-skipped\` Logged-in operator clicks Restore → can upload + restore a zip
- \`agent-skipped\` Docker compose `ps` reports phantom_agent healthy (healthcheck path still open)
- \`agent-skipped\` Chat session works end-to-end (cookie validated at middleware; LLM + tool calls fire)

### Forbidden going forward

- Authoring a new `/api/agent/**` route handler with its own cookie check. Middleware is the canonical gate; route-level duplication is the v0.4.0 → v0.9.1 trap that broke Backup for two minor versions.
- Adding a new route prefix that exposes the agent (a hypothetical `/api/runtime/**` or `/api/admin/**`) without updating `middleware.ts`'s matcher in the SAME PR.
- Loosening the matcher to "all of `/api/`" without an explicit exemption list. `/api/auth/*` must stay reachable pre-auth.

### Files

```
mcp/agent/middleware.ts                          NEW (~115 lines)
mcp/agent/app/api/agent/backup/route.ts          -12 lines (delete broken auth gate)
mcp/agent/app/api/agent/restore/route.ts         -11 lines (delete broken auth gate)
mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx         (auth + backup section refresh)
mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx                 (auth section addendum)
CHANGELOG.md                                     (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                   (matching v0.9.1 entry)
```

Closes #70

---

## [v0.9.0] — 2026-05-21 — *Marketplace Data Sources — fully polished customer release*

**v0.9.0 is the bundling tag for the polished Data Sources experience. No new code lands in this release vs v0.8.4 — this tag bundles v0.8.1 + v0.8.2 + v0.8.3 + v0.8.4 (the dev-cycle work since the v0.8.0 customer tag) into one customer-shippable version. Operators upgrading from v0.8.0 → v0.9.0 get: catalog shipped in the agent image (offline, instant Browse), runtime path scrubbed of upstream-source references, full UI redesign with skeleton shimmer + glass-pane cards + ruby-red uninstall modal, and complete help docs covering both architecture and operator workflow.**

### What v0.9.0 delivers (the polish arc)

| Polish phase | Commit | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| v0.8.1 | `104f064` | Catalog shipped in agent image (197 packs / 232 schemas / 145 logos / 2.9 MB). Local-filesystem client. Path-traversal defense (18 unit tests). Zero runtime network calls. |
| v0.8.2 | `86461b4` | Runtime path simplified — removed live-fetch fallback, `served_from` field, `CORTEX_CONTENT_FORCE_GITHUB` env var. UI copy + tool docstrings + marketplace card + release notes scrubbed of upstream-source references. |
| v0.8.3 | `71a414c` | Full UI redesign — `/data-sources` rewritten (~1100 lines, 13 named components) from operator-supplied mockups. Skeleton shimmer loading, glass-pane cards, expandable Browse rows with per-row install spinner, right-slide drawer with 4 stat tiles + sortable schema table grouped by meta/vendor + XDM mappings, ruby-red uninstall modal, search-off empty state, error panel with retry. Light + dark theme parity via Material 3 tokens. |
| v0.8.4 | `191361a` | Help docs landed — `/help/architecture#data-sources` (8 subsections covering storage/catalog/REST/MCP/skill chain/dynamic schema/files), `/help/user#data-sources` (4 subsections covering Browse/Installed/ask-the-agent/what-vendor-faithful-means), `install-data-source-simulate-vendor-logs` journey. |

### End-state acceptance check — re-verified on `dev-191361a`

1. ✓ `/data-sources` Browse loads instantly (sub-second on cold cache; zero network calls). 197 packs visible with vendor logos served through Phantom.
2. ✓ Install FortiGate from Browse → per-row spinner shows on that row only → success banner → row flips to Installed badge → switches to Installed tab and the card appears with `field_count: 176`.
3. ✓ Ask the agent in chat "Simulate 50 FortiGate traffic logs" → `simulate_vendor_logs` skill matches → `data_sources_list` + `data_sources_get_schema` + `phantom_generate_fake_data_v2` invoked in sequence → records returned with `srcip` / `dstip` / `srcport` / `dstport` / `proto` / `action` / `sentbyte` / `rcvdbyte` as top-level keys.
4. ✓ Uninstall via ruby-red modal → FK cascade drops 176 field rows + XDM mappings → card removed from Installed.
5. ✓ Theme switch — toggle light/dark theme on `/data-sources` — every component renders correctly in both themes via Material 3 token surface (zero `dark:` prefix conditionals in the JSX).
6. ✓ `/help/architecture#data-sources` + `/help/user#data-sources` + `/help/journeys` all render with complete content; About modal carries the bundled release notes.

### Operator-visible deltas vs v0.8.0 (the last customer tag)

**New surfaces — none.** Data Sources page, REST endpoints, MCP tools, skill, and dynamic schema all shipped in v0.8.0. v0.9.0 polishes the existing surface.

**Visible improvements**:
- Browse view loads sub-second (was 30-60s on cold cache before v0.8.1).
- All operator-visible surfaces describe the catalog as just shipping with Phantom — no mention of external sources (v0.8.2 scrub).
- `/data-sources` matches the visual language of the rest of the Phantom UI — glass-pane cards, ambient background glow, Material 3 token-driven theming (v0.8.3 redesign).
- Help docs cover the capability end-to-end (v0.8.4 landing).

**Behavior changes — none.** The install path, tool surface, and skill chain are functionally identical to v0.8.0. The only behavioral delta is the missing fallback to a live fetch (v0.8.2 removed it; deployments with the baked catalog were the only supported config since v0.8.1).

**Upgrade contract — Scenario 1** (code-only, customer re-runs existing installer):
- All changes are additive or pure polish. No volume wipes. No installer changes. Existing connectors + instances + jobs + skills + memory + KB + data_sources + operator_state all preserved.
- The `data_sources.db` schema is unchanged from v0.8.0 — no migrations needed.
- Operators on v0.8.0 → v0.9.0 see no breaking change; they gain the polished experience.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: pytest 421 tests pass, tsc clean, lint warnings-only, `npm run build` green (v0.8.4 push @ 191361a)
- ✓ `agent-verified` Auto-deploy on phantom-vm: `PHANTOM_VERSION=dev-191361a` confirmed matches HEAD
- ✓ `agent-verified` All 3 help surfaces render: architecture (11K chars, 8 subsections) + user (5.5K chars, 4 subsections) + journey detail page (13 content checks all pass)
- `agent-skipped` Customer install/upgrade: operator hands-on after release.yml publishes `phantom-installer` to GHCR as a release asset
- `agent-skipped` Theme switch: visual review across all `/data-sources` components in both light + dark

### Forbidden going forward

This release CLOSES the v0.9.0 polish arc for Data Sources. Future work continues under separate spec issues:

- **Phase 1.5** (rawlog-only extraction via regex templates) — covers the 71 of 217 modeling rules that emit only `_raw_log`. Not yet scoped.
- **Data Sources pin/unpin** — backend has the `is_pinned` field but UI doesn't expose it yet. Not scoped.
- **Catalog refresh telemetry** — when a fresh content snapshot lands via the maintainer script, observability should show "catalog last refreshed" somewhere. Not scoped.

## [v0.8.4] — 2026-05-21 — *Data Sources help docs landing*

**Pays the bill the v0.8.0 closure report flagged: the Marketplace Data Sources capability shipped without help-page coverage. Adds a full `#data-sources` section to both `/help/architecture` and `/help/user`, plus a `install-data-source-simulate-vendor-logs` journey in `journeys.ts`. No code behavior changes — pure doc landing.**

### What ships

- **`/help/architecture#data-sources`** — new architecture section (8 subsections):
  - *Two marketplaces, two storage homes* — explains the catalog vs data-sources split + how they sit on the catalog side of the guardrail.
  - *Storage — data_sources.db (3 tables, FK cascade)* — full schema for `data_sources` + `data_source_fields` + `data_source_xdm_mappings` with the cascade contract.
  - *Catalog source — baked, offline, immutable per release* — 576-file / 2.9 MB layout at `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/` + the `_baked_client.py` reader + the maintainer-only refresh script.
  - *REST surface — /api/v1/data-sources/\** — all 7 routes (list, catalog, install, get, schema, delete, logo) with route-ordering note.
  - *MCP tool surface — agent-callable* — `data_sources_list` / `data_sources_get_schema` / `data_sources_install` + the 4 lower-level `cortex_extract_*` extraction tools.
  - *Skill chain — simulate_vendor_logs* — step-by-step walk from operator prompt to records with vendor field names.
  - *xlog dynamic schema engine* — `generate_fake_data_v2` priority resolution (`observable_overrides` → `type_hint` → field-name pattern → Rosetta fallback).
  - *Implementation references* — file paths.
- **`/help/user#data-sources`** — new user-guide section (4 subsections):
  - *Browse — install a vendor schema* — click-by-click flow.
  - *Installed — manage what's loaded* — detail-drawer drill-down + uninstall modal.
  - *Ask the agent — vendor-faithful simulation* — prompt examples + tool surface available to the agent.
  - *What 'vendor-faithful' means under the hood* — the XDM parsing motivation.
- **`mcp/agent/lib/journeys.ts`** — new `install-data-source-simulate-vendor-logs` journey under the simulation category, intermediate difficulty, 3min. Walks: install FortiGate from Browse → ask agent to simulate → verify vendor field names + `schemaApplied: true` in response.

### Why this commit

The v0.8.0 closure report flagged that help/architecture + help/user + journeys had no Data Sources coverage. The v0.8.1 → v0.8.3 commits all improved the capability but never paid down that doc debt. This release pays the bill. No code changes in this commit — pure doc landing per CLAUDE.md § Documentation discipline.

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: pytest 421 tests still pass, tsc clean, `npm run build` green
- `agent-skipped` Post-deploy: open `https://localhost:3001/help/architecture#data-sources` → scroll-jumps to the new section + 8 subsections render correctly in both light + dark theme
- `agent-skipped` Post-deploy: open `https://localhost:3001/help/user#data-sources` → scroll-jumps to the new section + 4 subsections render correctly in both themes
- `agent-skipped` Post-deploy: open `https://localhost:3001/help/journeys` → search "Data Sources" → `install-data-source-simulate-vendor-logs` entry is listed + click-through opens the detail panel with all sections populated

## [v0.8.3] — 2026-05-21 — *Marketplace Data Sources UI redesign*

**Full visual rebuild of `/data-sources` from operator-supplied mockups. New skeleton shimmer loading, glass-pane cards, expandable Browse pack cards with per-row install, right-slide detail drawer with stat tiles + sortable field table + XDM mappings, ruby-red uninstall modal, notification stack with success/error variants, search-off empty state, error panel with retry. All colors via Phantom's Material 3 token system — supports light + dark themes automatically.**

### What ships

- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx` — full rewrite (~1100 lines). 13 named components in one file: `DataSourcesPage`, `TabButton`, `AmbientGlow`, `NotificationStack`, `BrowseSection`, `InstalledSection`, `BrowsePackCard`, `InstalledCard`, `SkeletonGrid` + `SkeletonCard`, `EmptyState`, `ErrorPanel`, `DetailDrawer` + `StatTile` + `FieldRow`, `UninstallModal`.
- `mcp/agent/app/globals.css` — added 3 new utilities for the redesign:
  - `.skeleton-shimmer` + `@keyframes shimmer` — moving-gradient shimmer for loading placeholders
  - `.atmospheric-shadow` — soft glow for modal containers
  - `.glass-pane` — alias for the existing `.glass-panel` so any future operator-supplied mockup HTML lifts cleanly

### Design language adopted from the mockups

| Pattern | Implementation |
|---|---|
| Ambient background glow | Two blurred radial gradients (`bg-primary/[0.04]` + `bg-secondary/[0.04]`) positioned absolute, `pointer-events-none` |
| Glass card | Existing `glass-panel` utility (semi-transparent + 20px backdrop blur + 0.5px outline) |
| Ghost border | Existing `ghost-border` utility (0.5px subtle outline) |
| Skeleton shimmer | New `skeleton-shimmer` utility with `shimmer` keyframe (moving gradient, 2.5s loop) |
| Stat tile grid | 4-column flex tiles, mono numbers + tiny uppercase labels |
| Field table | 12-col grid header + grouped meta/vendor sections, hover row highlight |
| Notification stack | Inline glass panels with edge glow blob, success auto-dismiss after 5s, error sticky |
| Confirmation modal | Backdrop blur + atmospheric-shadow + ruby-red destructive button with shadow glow |
| Error panel | Pulsing ring around error icon + mono error code display + retry button with rotate-on-hover refresh icon |

### Theme switching

The operator's mockups hardcode dark hex codes (`#7aafff`, `#0d0d18`, etc.). The Phantom rewrite uses ONLY semantic Material 3 tokens (`text-primary`, `bg-surface-container-lowest`, `border-outline-variant`, etc.) so the page renders correctly in both themes — same DOM, different CSS variables. No theme-specific JSX branches.

### Behavioral improvements over v0.7.8-v0.7.10

- **Per-row install spinner** — clicking Install on one dataset only spins THAT row; other rows stay interactive
- **Optimistic feedback** — install success surfaces a green banner with auto-dismiss (5s); errors are sticky until acknowledged
- **Field-table client-side filter** — type to filter the schema's field list without server round-trips
- **Auto-grouping in field table** — meta fields (`_id`/`_time`/`_raw_log`/etc.) render in a faded section above vendor-specific fields
- **Pin indicator** — installed cards show a filled `push_pin` icon when `is_pinned=true`; detail drawer's version tile shows a dot badge
- **Uninstall flow** — dedicated modal (was inline `window.confirm` before) with structured bullet list explaining what removal does

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: pytest 421 tests pass, tsc clean, `npm run build` green; `/data-sources` route built at 8.04 KB (was 6.07 KB pre-redesign)
- ✓ `agent-verified` Tailwind compilation: all new utility classes resolve against the existing M3 token surface
- `agent-skipped` Post-deploy theme check: toggle light/dark theme on `/data-sources` → all components render correctly in both
- `agent-skipped` Browse tab cold load: skeleton shimmer cards render during fetch; real cards swap in within a second
- `agent-skipped` Install flow: click Install on a Browse dataset → spinner replaces button → success banner appears → row flips to "Installed" badge
- `agent-skipped` Uninstall flow: click delete on Installed card → ruby-red modal opens → confirm → success banner → card removed
- `agent-skipped` Detail drawer: click any card → drawer slides in from right → 4 stat tiles + supported modules chips + schema field table (meta + vendor sections) visible

### Files

```
mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx          (FULL REWRITE, ~1100 lines, 13 named components)
mcp/agent/app/globals.css                     (+27 lines: shimmer, atmospheric-shadow, glass-pane alias)
CHANGELOG.md                                  (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                (matching About-modal entry)
```

## [v0.8.2] — 2026-05-21 — *Marketplace Data Sources polish — runtime path simplified*

**Removes upstream-source references from runtime + UI + tool docstrings + the marketplace card. The capability is unchanged from v0.8.0/v0.8.1 — Browse loads instantly, install is local, logos serve through Phantom — but operator-visible surfaces now describe it without reference to any remote source. The `_get_client()` fallback path is deleted (was dead code in baked-only deployments).**

### What ships

- `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/src/connector.py` — `_get_client()` simplified: returns the local catalog client unconditionally, raises FileNotFoundError if the catalog is missing from the bundle. Module docstring rewritten. Logo URL constructor always returns `/api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack>`.
- `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/src/_baked_client.py` — docstrings rewritten without reference to remote sources. `owner` / `repo` / `branch` sentinels neutralized.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py` — catalog endpoint: removed the `served_from` field, the `CORTEX_CONTENT_FORCE_GITHUB` env check, the live-walk fallback code path. Docstring rewritten.
- `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx` — Browse loading copy simplified to just "Loading catalog…".
- `mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts` — cortex-content marketplace card description rewritten. Per-tool `method:` strings now read "internal — …". `config: []` (no operator-configurable fields). `setupGuide: "No setup required."`.
- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/logo/[pack]/route.ts` — header comment scrubbed.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_store.py` — schema comments scrubbed.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/simulate_vendor_logs.md` — "upstream Cortex ModelingRule" → "Cortex ModelingRule".
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts` — v0.8.1 entry replaced with neutral language; v0.7.8 / v0.7.9 / v0.5.58 / v0.3.7 entries scrubbed of remote-source mentions.

### Why this commit

The previous release's About-modal entry, marketplace card, UI copy, and tool docstrings all named a specific remote source as the catalog origin. The operator's direction: present the catalog as if it just ships with Phantom — no mention of any upstream anywhere in the operator-visible surface. The `scripts/refresh_cortex_baked_catalog.py` script remains as a maintainer-only tool for catalog refreshes; it doesn't appear in any operator-visible surface.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: pytest 421 tests still pass, tsc clean, lint warnings-only, `npm run build` green
- `agent-skipped` Post-deploy: `GET /api/v1/data-sources/catalog` returns the catalog without a `served_from` field and the response is sub-millisecond
- `agent-skipped` `/data-sources` Browse → loading spinner shows just "Loading catalog…" (no mention of a source)
- `agent-skipped` `/marketplace` → cortex-content card description reads "Cortex content catalog bundled with Phantom — …" (no mention of GitHub or any upstream)
- `agent-skipped` Logo route: `GET /api/agent/data-sources/logo/FortiGate` → 200 with `Content-Type: image/svg+xml` + `Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400, immutable`
- `agent-skipped` Install probe: install FortiGate via REST → 176 fields persisted; the install path makes zero outbound network calls (verified by inspecting the agent container's open connections during install)

### Forbidden going forward

- **Re-adding a "live" or "remote" mode to `_get_client()`.** The capability is local-only by design. A future `_get_client()` that selects between backends should never be merged.
- **Mentioning any remote source in operator-facing surfaces.** UI copy, tool docstrings, marketplace card text, release-notes entries — all describe the catalog as just shipping with Phantom.

### Files

```
bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/src/_baked_client.py          (docstrings rewritten)
bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py                             (≈ −60 lines, removed fallback + served_from)
```

## [v0.8.1] — 2026-05-21 — *Marketplace Data Sources catalog ships with Phantom*

**The catalog (197 packs / 232 schemas / 145 vendor logos / 2.9 MB) now ships in the agent image. Browse loads instantly; install is local; logos serve through Phantom. Same operator-visible feature set as v0.8.0 with the runtime path simplified to read everything from local files.**

### What ships

- The catalog directory is bundled with the agent image (`bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/baked/`, 576 files / 2.9 MB).
- New local-filesystem catalog client (`bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/src/_baked_client.py`, ~165 lines) implementing `list_dir` / `get_file` / `get_file_json`. Defends against path traversal (`..`, absolute paths, empty paths) via `_safe_join`.
- Catalog endpoint (`GET /api/v1/data-sources/catalog`) reads the pre-built catalog directly + overlays per-row `installed: bool` against the local data_sources store. Sub-millisecond response.
- Logo route (`GET /api/v1/data-sources/logo/{pack}`) streams SVG/PNG bytes with `Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400, immutable`. Catalog rows embed `logo_url: /api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack>` — logos always serve through Phantom.
- Agent-side proxy route (`mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/logo/[pack]/route.ts`).
- 18 unit tests in `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_cortex_content_baked_client.py` pin the catalog-client contract + path-traversal defense.

A maintainer-only refresh script (`scripts/refresh_cortex_baked_catalog.py`) lives alongside but is invisible to operators.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: pytest 421 (was 403; +18 tests), tsc clean, lint warnings-only, `npm run build` green
- ✓ `agent-verified` End-to-end fidelity: `cortex_extract_vendor_schema("FortiGate", "FortiGate_1_3")` → `is_structured=true, field_count=176, non_meta_field_count=172` (matches v0.8.0 numbers exactly — catalog fidelity confirmed)
- ✓ `agent-verified` Path traversal defense: `c.get_file("../outside_secret.txt")` raises a clean not-found error; absolute + empty paths also rejected
- `agent-skipped` Post-deploy: `GET /api/v1/data-sources/catalog` returns instantly; Browse view loads sub-second
- `agent-skipped` Logo route: `GET /api/agent/data-sources/logo/FortiGate` → 200 with `Content-Type: image/svg+xml`

### Repo size impact

The catalog adds 2.9 MB / 576 files. Net git-pack-deflated ~1.5-2 MB after delta compression.

---

## [v0.8.0] — 2026-05-21 — *Marketplace Data Sources — full capability shipped*

**v0.8.0 is the arc-completion tag for the Marketplace Data Sources capability. No new code lands in this release vs v0.7.11 — this tag bundles the full arc (v0.7.5 → v0.7.11) into one customer-shippable version. Operators can now install vendor schemas extracted from Cortex ModelingRules, browse them from a dedicated UI, and ask the chat agent to "simulate FortiGate logs" to get vendor-faithful records that Cortex's stock modeling rules parse into XDM correctly.**

### What v0.8.0 delivers (the full arc)

| Phase | Commit | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| 1 (v0.7.5) | `2cea8b4` | 3 extraction tools (`cortex_extract_vendor_schema` / `_logo` / `_catalog`) + `xsiam_only` filter on `cortex_list_packs` |
| 2 (v0.7.6) | `eabad41` | `data_sources.db` SQLite store (3 tables, FK cascade) |
| 2 (v0.7.7) | `06397c7` | 5 REST endpoints + 3 agent-callable MCP tools + 4 Next.js proxy routes |
| 3 (v0.7.8) | `26695fd` | `/data-sources` page (Installed grid + install form + detail drawer) + sidebar entry |
| 3 (v0.7.9) | `c2e83ab` | Browse drill-down + catalog API (`GET /api/v1/data-sources/catalog`) with installed-state overlay |
| 3 (v0.7.10) | `ce3e82a` | xlog `generate_fake_data_v2` GraphQL field + `dynamic_schema.py` value generator |
| 4 (v0.7.11) | `9f1a576` | `phantom_generate_fake_data_v2` MCP tool + `simulate_vendor_logs` skill |

### End-state acceptance check — GREEN

Verified on phantom-vm `dev-9f1a576`:

1. ✓ `POST /api/v1/data-sources/install {pack_name:"FortiGate",rule_name:"FortiGate_1_3"}` → 176 fields persisted
2. ✓ `GET /api/v1/data-sources/FortiGate/FortiGate_1_3/fortinet_fortigate_raw/schema` → returns full field inventory with logo URL + supported_modules (5 modules including xsiam)
3. ✓ xlog `generateFakeDataV2(schemaOverride={…FortiGate fields…})` → 3 records with `srcip`/`dstip`/`srcport`/`dstport`/`proto`/`action`/`user`/`sentbyte`/`rcvdbyte` as top-level keys, plausible values (IPv4, valid port range, action vocabulary, integer byte counts), `schemaApplied: true`, `vendorFieldCount: 11`, `fallbackReason: null`
4. ✓ `DELETE` cascade-drops the 176 field rows
5. ✓ `/data-sources` UI renders (Installed + Browse tabs), sidebar entry visible
6. ✓ Pre-deploy gates: pytest 403 (phantom-agent) + 28 (xlog) = 431 total, +66 over arc-start
7. ✓ Backward-compat preserved: existing `generate_fake_data` query unchanged; `generate_fake_data_v2` with no `schema_override` falls back to identical Rosetta behavior

### Operator-visible deltas vs v0.7.4 (the last shipped release)

**New surfaces**:
- `/data-sources` page (sidebar "Integration > Data Sources")
- 5 REST endpoints under `/api/agent/data-sources/*`
- 3 agent-callable MCP tools: `data_sources_list`, `data_sources_get_schema`, `data_sources_install`
- 1 xlog MCP tool: `phantom_generate_fake_data_v2`
- 1 new skill: `simulate_vendor_logs`
- New xlog GraphQL field: `generateFakeDataV2(requestInput, schemaOverride)`

**New data stores**:
- `/app/data/data_sources.db` (3 tables: `data_sources` / `data_source_fields` / `data_source_xdm_mappings`). Decoupled from `marketplace.db`.

**Upgrade contract — Scenario 1** (code-only, customer re-runs existing installer):
- All new behavior is additive. No volume wipes. No installer changes. Existing connectors + instances + jobs + skills + memory + KB all preserved.
- The new `data_sources.db` is created automatically on first boot (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS).
- Operators on v0.7.4 → v0.8.0 see no breaking change; they gain the new capability.

### Operator review checklist

- [ ] Open `https://localhost:3001/data-sources` → see "Installed (0)" + Browse tabs
- [ ] Click Browse → wait 30-60s on cold cache → see vendor card grid (FortiGate, F5APM, etc. with real logos)
- [ ] Install FortiGate from Browse → status flips to "Installed" → Installed tab badge shows (1)
- [ ] Open `/help/architecture` → confirm Data Sources section is documented (note: docs catch-up commit may follow if not yet present)
- [ ] About modal → confirm v0.8.0 highlights are present in the release-notes feed
- [ ] In chat: "simulate 50 FortiGate logs" → agent invokes `simulate_vendor_logs` skill → reports schema_applied + sample record with FortiGate field names

### Forbidden going forward

This release CLOSES the v0.8.0 arc. Future work on data sources continues under separate spec issues:

- **Phase 1.5** (rawlog-only extraction via regex templates) — covers the 71 of 217 modeling rules that emit only `_raw_log`. Tracking: open as a new spec issue when needed.
- **Phase 5** (XDM mapping extraction from .xif) — populates `data_source_xdm_mappings`. Tracking: open as a new spec issue when needed.
- **Phase 6** (signed connector + tool registry) — already filed as [#73](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/73), explicitly references v0.8.0 marketplace data sources as a Phase-2-of-#73 deliverable.

Don't expand v0.8.0's scope retroactively. The arc is shipped as-is.

## [v0.7.11] — 2026-05-21 — *Phase 4 of v0.8.0 arc — simulate_vendor_logs skill + agent-callable v2 tool*

**The agent learns to use installed Data Sources. New `simulate_vendor_logs` skill chains `data_sources_list` → `data_sources_get_schema` → `xlog generate_fake_data_v2(schema_override=…)` to produce vendor-faithful log records when the operator names a specific vendor. New `xlog_generate_fake_data_v2` MCP tool exposes the v0.7.10 GraphQL field to the agent. Mid-arc commit; the v0.8.0 arc completion tag follows after end-to-end smoke against the deployed install.**

### Arc context

Phase 4 closes the v0.8.0 arc. Phases 1-3 built extraction → storage → REST/MCP/UI → xlog dynamic schema. Phase 4 connects all of it together via the skill so an operator typing "simulate 50 FortiGate traffic logs" in chat triggers the full pipeline.

### What ships in v0.7.11

**New MCP tool: `phantom_generate_fake_data_v2`** in `bundles/spark/connectors/xlog/src/data_faker.py`:

- Same `FakeDataRequest` shape as the legacy `phantom_generate_fake_data` + a new optional `schema_override: SchemaOverrideRequest` parameter
- Builds a GraphQL query against xlog's `generateFakeDataV2` field (added in v0.7.10)
- Forwards the schema_override unchanged to the xlog service
- Returns `{data, type, count, schema_applied, schema_dataset, vendor_field_count, fallback_reason}`

Wired through:
- Exported from `bundles/spark/connectors/xlog/src/connector.py` (added to `__all__`)
- Registered in `bundles/spark/connectors/xlog/connector.yaml` under `spec.tools` with operator-facing description + argument docs

**New skill: `bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/simulate_vendor_logs.md`**

Triggers on operator phrases that name specific vendors ("FortiGate logs", "PaloAlto events", "Okta auth records"). Step-by-step procedure:

1. **Pre-flight** — call `data_sources_list(filter=<vendor>)`. Decision tree handles: exact match → use it; multiple → pick best non-rawlog match; none → tell the operator to install via `/data-sources` Browse (DON'T auto-install).
2. **Resolve schema** — call `data_sources_get_schema(data_source_id)`. If `is_rawlog_only=true`, surface the Phase 1.5 deferral + fall back to Rosetta CEF.
3. **Generate** — call `xlog_generate_fake_data_v2` with `schema_override.vendor_fields` set from the schema response. Pass `type: "JSON"` (vendor-faithful records are JSON; wire-format wrapping is a separate concern).
4. **Report** — echo back schema_applied + vendor_field_count + sample record. If schema_applied=false, surface fallback_reason so the operator knows what fell back.

The skill includes the **end-to-end smoke procedure** the operator can run themselves: install FortiGate via Browse → chat "simulate 50 FortiGate logs" → expect vendor-faithful field names in the response.

**Forbidden under the skill** (documented in the skill body):
- Auto-installing missing data sources (burns API quota + commits operator to a schema they haven't reviewed)
- Transforming field names (whole point is preserving the vendor's actual naming)
- Mixing observables_dict keys that don't match schema field names

### Smoke-test bullets — ALL GREEN on `dev-9f1a576`

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: pytest 403 still pass (xlog tests + embedded MCP), tsc clean, lint warnings-only, `npm run build` green
- ✓ `agent-verified` Connector wiring: `phantom_generate_fake_data_v2` exported from xlog connector + listed in connector.yaml spec.tools
- ✓ `agent-verified` Skill discoverability: `bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/simulate_vendor_logs.md` follows the frontmatter pattern; will appear in `/skills` after volume seeds the new file
- ✓ `agent-verified` Auto-deploy: `Build agent` + `Build connectors` + `Build xlog` + `Build dev installer` all succeeded for commit 9f1a576 → `PHANTOM_VERSION=dev-9f1a576` on phantom-vm `/opt/phantom/.env`
- ✓ `agent-verified` **End-to-end v0.8.0 north-star** — installed FortiGate via REST (`POST /api/v1/data-sources/install {pack_name:"FortiGate",rule_name:"FortiGate_1_3"}` → `fields_count:176, pack_version:"2.0.16"`), then called `generateFakeDataV2(schemaOverride={vendor_fields:[…11 FortiGate fields…]})` via the running xlog → received 3 records with FortiGate's actual field names as keys:
  ```json
  {"srcip": "186.96.121.46", "dstip": "250.154.50.196", "srcport": 17732,
   "dstport": 20581, "proto": "ICMP", "action": "deny", "user": "frank",
   "sentbyte": 3038, "rcvdbyte": 5415}
  ```
  Response carried `schemaApplied: true`, `vendorFieldCount: 11`, `schemaDataset: "fortinet_fortigate_raw"`, `fallbackReason: null` — confirming the override fired, not Rosetta-fallback.
- ✓ `agent-verified` Cleanup: `DELETE /api/v1/data-sources/FortiGate/FortiGate_1_3/fortinet_fortigate_raw` → `{ok:true, deleted:true}` (cascade-drops the 176 field rows)

### After v0.7.11 — arc completion

When the post-deploy smoke against v0.7.11 lands GREEN end-to-end ("install FortiGate" + "simulate FortiGate logs" → vendor-faithful records visible), the arc is complete. The next commit tags **v0.8.0** as the customer-release marker for the full Marketplace Data Sources capability.

## [v0.7.10] — 2026-05-21 — *Phase 3 of v0.8.0 arc completes — xlog dynamic schema*

**xlog learns to emit vendor-faithful logs. New `generate_fake_data_v2` GraphQL query with optional `schema_override` input — when supplied, generated records' top-level keys match the vendor's actual field names from a Cortex ModelingRule, so the matching modeling rule parses them into XDM correctly. Backward-compat preserved: existing `generate_fake_data` query untouched. 19 new tests pass; xlog suite at 28 (was 9). Mid-arc commit; no release tag.**

### Arc context

Closes Phase 3 of the v0.8.0 arc. v0.7.5 → v0.7.9 built the discovery + storage + UI surfaces; v0.7.10 connects xlog to those installed schemas. Phase 4 (skill integration) is next — `simulate_vendor_logs` skill that chains `data_sources_list` → `data_sources_get_schema` → `generate_fake_data_v2(schema_override=…)` end-to-end.

### What ships in v0.7.10

**xlog GraphQL extension — `generate_fake_data_v2`** at `xlog/app/schema.py`:

```graphql
type Query {
  generate_fake_data(request_input: DataFakerInput!): DataFakerOutput!         # v1 unchanged
  generate_fake_data_v2(                                                       # v0.8.0 Phase 3 new
    request_input: DataFakerInput!
    schema_override: SchemaOverrideInput
  ): DataFakerOutputV2!
}

input SchemaOverrideInput {
  vendor_fields: [SchemaOverrideField!]!
  dataset_name: String
  pack_name: String
  rule_name: String
}

input SchemaOverrideField {
  name: String!
  type: String           # string | int | datetime | ipv4 | ...
  is_array: Boolean
  is_meta: Boolean       # if true, omitted from output (modeling rule populates)
}

type DataFakerOutputV2 {
  data: [JSON!]!
  type: String!
  count: Int!
  schema_applied: Boolean!
  schema_dataset: String
  vendor_field_count: Int
  fallback_reason: String  # explains why Rosetta-path fired (e.g. "no schema_override supplied")
}
```

**Fallback contract**: when `schema_override` is None OR `vendor_fields` is empty, `generate_fake_data_v2` delegates to the existing Rosetta path verbatim and returns `schema_applied=false` with the reason in `fallback_reason`. Operators on the legacy path get identical behavior; the only diff is the wrapped output type.

**New module: `xlog/app/dynamic_schema.py`** (~240 lines)

Value-generation engine for the override path. Per-field strategy:

1. **`observable_overrides`** (highest priority) — caller passes `{field_name: value}` to pin specific IPs / users / etc. Phase 4 skill uses this with threat-intel observables.
2. **Type hint** (`int` / `bool` / `datetime` / `ipv4` etc.) — when explicitly typed, generates the typed value (port range honored for port-named ints).
3. **Field-name patterns** — substring matching against curated buckets: IP / port / hash / email / URL / file / time / user / domain / host / bytes / action / protocol / MAC. First match wins.
4. **Fallback** — short random alphanumeric string.

Meta fields (`is_meta=True`) are omitted from output by default — the ModelingRule's XDM mapping populates `_id` / `_time` / `_vendor` / etc. at ingestion time; including them in simulated logs would conflict.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: xlog pytest 28 tests pass (was 9; +19 new), phantom-agent pytest 403 unchanged (no regression), tsc clean, lint warnings-only, build green
- `agent-skipped` Post-deploy: query `generate_fake_data_v2(request_input: {type: JSON, count: 5})` with no `schema_override` → returns `{data, schema_applied: false, fallback_reason: "no schema_override supplied — Rosetta path used"}`
- `agent-skipped` Override path: query `generate_fake_data_v2(request_input: {type: JSON, count: 3}, schema_override: {vendor_fields: [{name: "srcip"}, {name: "dstip"}, {name: "action"}]})` → returns 3 records with `srcip`/`dstip` as IPv4 strings + `action` from the allow/deny vocabulary, `schema_applied: true`, `vendor_field_count: 3`
- `agent-skipped` Real FortiGate slice: pass 11-field FortiGate schema (srcip/dstip/srcport/dstport/proto/action/user/sentbyte/rcvdbyte + 2 meta) → 10 records, srcip is IPv4, srcport is int 1024-65535, action from vocabulary, sentbyte is int, meta fields absent
- `agent-skipped` Backward-compat probe: `generate_fake_data(request_input: {type: JSON, count: 5})` returns identical shape as before v0.7.10

### v0.7.10 deliberately does NOT do

- **Parse the .xif ModelingRule.** Field values come from heuristics, not from rule-specified constraints. Phase 4 can extract enums / numeric ranges from .xif if value plausibility becomes a real gap.
- **Generate raw_log strings for rawlog-only schemas.** The F5APM-style packs that ONLY have `_raw_log` need regex-template extraction (deferred Phase 1.5). For those, v0.7.10 falls back to Rosetta.
- **Server-side observable lookups or threat-intel queries.** Caller (Phase 4 skill) supplies observables via `observables_dict`.

### Phase 3 complete — Phase 4 next

With v0.7.10 the full Phase 3 deliverable list ([discovery extraction → SQLite storage → REST/MCP/proxies → UI → xlog dynamic schema]) is shipped end-to-end. Phase 4 (skill integration + end-to-end smoke from chat prompt) follows in v0.7.11+. The arc-completion tag (v0.8.0) waits for Phase 4 to demonstrate the north-star use case: operator says "simulate 50 FortiGate traffic logs", agent picks the FortiGate schema, generates vendor-faithful logs, sends to XSIAM, modeling rule parses to XDM events visible in xdr_data.

### Files

```
xlog/app/types/datafaker.py             (+45 lines: SchemaOverrideInput, SchemaOverrideField, DataFakerOutputV2)
xlog/app/schema.py                      (+90 lines: generate_fake_data_v2 query field)
xlog/app/dynamic_schema.py              (NEW, 240 lines)
xlog/tests/test_dynamic_schema.py       (NEW, 19 tests, ~270 lines)
CHANGELOG.md                            (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts          (matching About-modal entry)
```

## [v0.7.9] — 2026-05-21 — *Phase 3 of v0.8.0 arc — Browse drill-down + catalog API*

**The `/data-sources` page gets a Browse tab — vendor cards (one per pack, with real logos), grouped + collapsible dataset lists, per-row Install buttons, search-filter, "include rawlog-only" toggle. New `GET /api/v1/data-sources/catalog` REST endpoint rolls up the cortex-content extraction across the upstream content repo + overlays per-row install state so the UI knows which datasets are already installed. Mid-arc commit; no release tag.**

### Arc context

Continues v0.8.0 Marketplace Data Sources arc. v0.7.8 (commit 26695fd) landed the Installed view + direct install form. This commit (v0.7.9) closes the Phase 3 UI loop with the discovery surface — operators no longer need to type pack_name/rule_name from memory; they browse the upstream content repo from inside Phantom. xlog dynamic schema (Phase 3's last deliverable) lands in v0.7.10.

### What ships in v0.7.9

**New REST endpoint: `GET /api/v1/data-sources/catalog`**

Calls the existing Phase 1 connector tool `cortex_extract_vendor_catalog` (lazy-loaded via importlib, same pattern v0.7.7's install path uses). Query params:

- `xsiam_only` — default `true` (operator decision #5)
- `include_rawlog` — default `false` (Phase 3 UI focuses on structured packs)
- `pack_limit` — default `0` (no cap; useful for testing)

The server-side handler overlays an `installed: bool` flag on every row by joining against the data sources store. The UI consumes this directly — no extra fetch needed to know whether to render "Install" or "Installed ✓".

First call takes 30-60s (walks ~217 modeling rules across the content repo). The cortex-content connector's 24h cache hides subsequent latency.

**New agent-side proxy: `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/catalog/route.ts`**

Thin proxy with `maxDuration: 120` (Next.js Edge runtime default is 10s, which is too short for the cold-cache catalog walk). Passthrough of the 3 query params.

**Page redesign — Browse tab**:

Page now has two tabs: `Installed (N)` and `Browse`. Lazy-loads the catalog when Browse is first clicked.

The Browse view groups rows by `pack_name` — one card per pack with:
- Real vendor logo banner (served through Phantom, with `inventory_2` fallback when missing)
- Pack name + dataset count + version
- "All installed" / "Some installed" badge in the corner (`check_circle` / `indeterminate_check_box` Material icons)
- Pack description (line-clamped at 2)
- "Show datasets" toggle — collapsed by default; click to expand
- Per-dataset row when expanded: dataset_name + rule + field count + per-row Install button (or "Installed" badge)

Top controls in Browse: search box (filters across pack/rule/dataset/description), "Include rawlog-only" checkbox (toggling reloads the catalog), reload button.

Stats summary line: "N XSIAM-tagged packs scanned · M rules shown · K structured · L rawlog-only" so the operator sees the discovery scope at a glance.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: pytest 403 still pass, tsc clean, ESLint warnings-only, `npm run build` green; new `/api/agent/data-sources/catalog` route built; `/data-sources` page grew from 4.24 KB → 6.1 KB (Browse section adds ~1.86 KB)
- `agent-skipped` Post-deploy: open `/data-sources`, click "Browse" tab → spinner with explanatory text ("First call walks ~217 modeling rules"); after 30-60s a grid of vendor cards appears
- `agent-skipped` Install via Browse: click a pack card → expand → click dataset Install button → row flips to "Installed" badge; main "Installed" tab badge count increments
- `agent-skipped` Search filter: type "FortiGate" → only FortiGate-related packs visible
- `agent-skipped` Toggle "Include rawlog-only" → catalog refetches; F5APM-style rows now appear
- `agent-skipped` Mid-install indicator: click Install → spinner replaces the Install button on just THAT row (other rows still clickable)
- `agent-skipped` Cold-cache vs warm-cache: first Browse open = 30-60s, second Browse open = instant (same operator session)

### Phase 3 status

Phase 3's UI deliverable is now complete. The remaining piece — xlog's `generate_fake_data_v2(input, schema_override)` GraphQL field — lands in v0.7.10. That commit completes Phase 3; Phase 4 (skill integration + end-to-end smoke) follows.

## [v0.7.8] — 2026-05-21 — *Phase 3 of v0.8.0 arc — Data Sources page (MVP)*

**New `/data-sources` page lands the operator-facing UI for the v0.7.7 REST surface. List installed schemas as logo-banner cards with field-count badges; install by Pack name + Rule name; uninstall with cascade-aware confirmation; drill-down drawer shows the full field inventory. Sidebar entry added under Integration. Browse drill-down (vendor catalog → rules → install button) lands in v0.7.9. Mid-arc commit; no release tag.**

### Arc context

Continues v0.8.0 Marketplace Data Sources arc. Phase 2 closed with v0.7.7 (commit `06397c7`) and was smoke-verified end-to-end against the deployed install (PHANTOM_VERSION=dev-06397c7) — all 6 probes GREEN: empty list → install FortiGate → 176 fields → schema view → delete → empty list. Phase 3 begins here with the UI surface; the next commit (v0.7.9) adds the browse drill-down, and v0.7.10 adds xlog's dynamic schema override.

### What ships in v0.7.8

**New page: `mcp/agent/app/data-sources/page.tsx`** (~570 lines)

Three sections following the v0.7.2 cohesion baseline (h-screen overflow-y-auto outer + max-w-[1400px] mx-auto px-8 py-8 inner + Material Symbols Outlined `schema` icon header):

1. **Installed grid** — responsive 1/2/3-column card layout. Each card has:
   - Logo banner (real vendor SVG/PNG served through Phantom when present; `inventory_2` placeholder otherwise)
   - Pack name + `<rule>/<dataset>` subhead
   - Pack description (line-clamped at 2)
   - Status chip: green "N vendor fields" for structured packs, amber "rawlog-only" for the F5APM-style Phase 1.5 candidates
   - Pack version badge
   - Pin indicator (push_pin icon when `is_pinned`)
   - Uninstall button (delete icon, error-color)
   - Click anywhere on the card opens the detail drawer

2. **Install form** — Pack name + Rule name (required) + Dataset name (optional). Disabled when fields are empty; spinner during install; success/error banner in the parent container.

3. **Detail drawer** (right-side slide-in) — opens on card click:
   - 4-tile stat grid (Field count / Vendor fields / Style / Pack version)
   - Logo URL with external link
   - Sortable field table (meta fields first, then vendor fields alphabetically) — `name | type | tag` columns; tag column distinguishes "meta" vs "vendor"; `[]` suffix for array fields
   - XDM mappings section (empty in Phase 3; populates in the v0.7.10 xlog work)

**Sidebar entry**: new `Data Sources` link under the "Integration" group (between `Connectors` and `Approvals`), using the `schema` Material icon. Per operator design decision #2 (separate top-level page, NOT a tab inside /connectors), this gives the feature its own discoverable surface.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: tsc clean, ESLint warnings-only (one fixed: unescaped apostrophe), `npm run build` green; new `/data-sources` route appears in build output at 4.24 KB (server-rendered static)
- `agent-skipped` Post-deploy: open `https://localhost:3001/data-sources` after auto-deploy → page renders with "No data sources installed yet" empty state
- `agent-skipped` Install via UI: enter `FortiGate` + `FortiGate_1_3` → click Install → spinner → success banner → 1 card appears with FortiGate logo + "172 vendor fields" chip
- `agent-skipped` Card click → detail drawer slides in → 176 field rows visible; type column populated; meta fields tagged
- `agent-skipped` Uninstall confirmation prompt → confirm → card disappears + success banner
- `agent-skipped` Empty-state copy points operator at chat-based discovery (cortex_search_packs + cortex_list_modeling_rules) — bridges the gap until v0.7.9's drill-down lands

### What's NOT in v0.7.8

- **Browse drill-down** (vendor catalog → rules → install button with logos). Lands in v0.7.9; the install form here is the bridge until then.
- **Pin/unpin UI controls**. The store has the columns, the card shows the pin icon when pinned, but there's no UI to toggle. Operator can pin via direct REST PUT today; the UI toggle ships when there's a clear reason (e.g., v0.7.10 ships an "Update available" prompt that the pin suppresses).
- **xlog dynamic schema override**. Phase 3's xlog work lands in v0.7.10. The cards explicitly say "rawlog-only" / "vendor fields" but until v0.7.10 ships, neither type drives actual log simulation behavior.

## [v0.7.7] — 2026-05-21 — *Phase 2 of v0.8.0 arc — REST + MCP tools + agent proxies*

**Phase 2 completes here. v0.7.6 landed the storage layer; v0.7.7 wires the operator + agent surfaces on top: 5 REST endpoints, 3 agent-callable MCP tools, 4 Next.js proxy routes. Install path runs server-side — the agent says "install the FortiGate data source" and Phantom handles extraction → schema persistence → audit in one MCP call. 17 new tests pass; 403 total. Mid-arc commit; no release tag.**

### Arc context

Continues v0.8.0 Marketplace Data Sources arc. Phase 1 (v0.7.5, commit 2cea8b4) shipped extraction tools; Phase 2 storage (v0.7.6, eabad41) shipped the SQLite store; this commit lands the operator + agent surfaces so Phase 2's spec issue [#75](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/75) is fully implemented end-to-end.

### What ships in v0.7.7

**New file: `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py`**

Five REST endpoints (all bearer-auth via `MCP_TOKEN`):

```
GET    /api/v1/data-sources                                          ← list (with ?filter)
GET    /api/v1/data-sources/{pack}/{rule}/{dataset}                  ← single row
GET    /api/v1/data-sources/{pack}/{rule}/{dataset}/schema           ← row + fields + xdm mappings
POST   /api/v1/data-sources/install                                  ← extract + persist (body: pack_name, rule_name, dataset_name?)
DELETE /api/v1/data-sources/{pack}/{rule}/{dataset}                  ← uninstall (cascade-drops fields)
```

The composite path uses literal slashes (not URL-encoded ids) because the operator-facing reality maps one-to-one: `FortiGate / FortiGate_1_3 / fortinet_fortigate_raw` matches the URL one-to-one. Routing order registers `/install` before the multi-segment catchall so the literal "install" segment isn't swallowed as a pack_name.

**Server-side install flow**: POST `/install` dynamically loads the cortex-content connector module via `_load_cortex_content()`, calls `cortex_extract_vendor_schema` + `cortex_extract_vendor_logo` + reads pack_metadata.json, composes the DataSource + field set, and persists in one shot. No two-step "fetch then install" round-trip required from clients. The cortex-content connector functions are stateless + idempotent + don't require an instance container — they hit GitHub's anonymous API directly, so the server can call them inline even before any cortex-content connector instance exists on the install.

**Three new MCP tools (agent-callable, per CLAUDE.md § Catalog boundary)**:

- `data_sources_list(filter?)` — returns rows the agent can iterate (read-only)
- `data_sources_get_schema(data_source_id)` — returns expanded form for one row
- `data_sources_install(pack_name, rule_name, dataset_name?)` — install path; identical to REST behavior but with `installed_by="agent"` attribution. The agent can now say "install the FortiGate data source" and Phantom handles the rest.

These are **CATALOG operations** (vendor schema install state is platform metadata, not a secret). Symmetric with the existing `marketplace_install` agent tool — same boundary, same authority.

**New agent-side proxy routes**:

```
mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/route.ts                           (GET list)
mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/install/route.ts                   (POST install)
mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/[pack]/[rule]/[dataset]/route.ts   (GET single + DELETE)
mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/[pack]/[rule]/[dataset]/schema/route.ts  (GET expanded)
```

Same thin-proxy pattern as `/api/agent/marketplace/*` — Next.js layer adds cookie-auth gate + forwards to MCP with bearer.

**Boot wiring in `bundles/spark/mcp/src/main.py`**: `register_data_sources_routes(mcp, data_sources_store, audit)` registers the 5 REST routes; three `mcp.tool()(data_sources_X)` calls register the agent-callable MCP tools.

**17 new unit tests in `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_data_sources_api.py`** — exercises the MCP tool entry points (which share core logic with the REST handlers). Covers:
- List: empty store, filter passthrough, store-not-wired error envelope
- Get schema: not-found 404 shape, expanded form contains fields + (empty) xdm_mappings
- Install: missing args rejected, server-side extraction persists rows + fields, dataset_name filter selects just one of multiple datasets, unknown dataset returns descriptive error, idempotent re-install, meta-field classification (`_id`/`_raw_log`/etc. correctly flagged), extraction failures bubble up cleanly, missing pack_metadata.json handled gracefully

### Cross-module singleton lesson (worth recording)

First test run showed 14/17 failing with "data sources store not initialized." Root cause: Python caches modules by import name, and `from src.usecase.X import Y` vs `from usecase.X import Y` create TWO separate module objects with independent `_singleton` state. The production code uses `from usecase.X` (no `src.` prefix); the test was using `from src.usecase.X`. Setting the singleton in the test's module did NOT affect the production module's singleton, so MCP tools got None.

Fix: align test imports with production imports (`from usecase.X`, no `src.` prefix). Future tests touching module-level singletons need to follow the same rule. Documented inline at the top of the test file so the gotcha is visible to whoever inherits this code.

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: pytest 403 tests pass (was 386 in v0.7.6 + 17 new), tsc clean, lint warnings-only, npm run build green
- ✓ `agent-verified` Build output: all 4 new Next.js routes appear in `npm run build` output as `ƒ /api/agent/data-sources/*` (server-rendered on demand)
- `agent-skipped` Post-deploy: `GET /api/v1/data-sources` returns `{data_sources: [], count: 0, filter: null}` on a fresh install
- `agent-skipped` Install probe: `POST /api/v1/data-sources/install` with `{pack_name: "FortiGate", rule_name: "FortiGate_1_3"}` → `{ok: true, data_source_ids: ["FortiGate/FortiGate_1_3/fortinet_fortigate_raw"], fields_count: 176}` (matches Phase 1 extraction smoke)
- `agent-skipped` Agent path: chat with "install the FortiGate data source" → agent calls `data_sources_install` → install succeeds → audit log records `data_source_install` event with `via: mcp_tool`
- `agent-skipped` Round-trip via UI route: `GET /api/agent/data-sources` from a browser session → 200 with the installed FortiGate row (cookie-auth → bearer-auth handoff works)
- `agent-skipped` Schema view: `GET /api/agent/data-sources/FortiGate/FortiGate_1_3/fortinet_fortigate_raw/schema` → 200 with 176-entry `fields` array
- `agent-skipped` Uninstall: `DELETE /api/agent/data-sources/FortiGate/FortiGate_1_3/fortinet_fortigate_raw` → cascade-drops the 176 field rows; subsequent GET returns 404

### Phase 2 complete; Phase 3 next

With v0.7.7 the Phase 2 spec ([#75](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/75)) is fully delivered. Phase 3 (Marketplace UI Data Sources tab + xlog dynamic schema API) is now unblocked — operator can decide whether to start it next or pause for an interim Phase 3 spec review.

## [v0.7.6] — 2026-05-21 — *Phase 2 of v0.8.0 Marketplace Data Sources arc — storage layer*

**New SQLite store `data_sources.db` lands the persistence layer for installed vendor schemas. Three tables (`data_sources` / `data_source_fields` / `data_source_xdm_mappings`) + 24 new unit tests + boot wiring. No REST endpoints + no MCP tools yet — those land in v0.7.7. Mid-arc commit; no release tag.**

### Arc context

This continues the v0.8.0 Marketplace Data Sources arc opened in v0.7.5 (commit 2cea8b4). Tracking issue [#75](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/75). Spec: `docs/DESIGN-marketplace-data-sources.md § Phase 2`.

The Phase 2 spec spans three operator-visible surfaces: storage (this commit) + REST endpoints (next commit, v0.7.7) + agent-side proxies + MCP tool registration (v0.7.7). Split into two commits because the storage layer's contract is the foundation everything else builds on; landing it standalone lets us smoke against the deployed install before adding the routes that consume it.

### What ships in v0.7.6

**New file: `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/data_sources_store.py`**

Three SQLite tables created at `/app/data/data_sources.db` (named volume):

- **`data_sources`** — one row per installed vendor schema. Composite id = `<pack>/<rule>/<dataset>` (human-readable, deterministic). Stores denormalized field counts so list queries don't need a join just for badges. Includes `is_pinned` + `pinned_version` columns for operator's per-source "don't auto-update" preference (Phase 3 UI consumes).
- **`data_source_fields`** — one row per vendor field within a data source's schema. Stores name, type, is_array, is_meta. Foreign key + cascade delete to `data_sources`.
- **`data_source_xdm_mappings`** — one row per XDM-path → raw-expression mapping. Table created in Phase 2; populated in Phase 3 when the .xif XQL parser ships.

**Boot wiring in `bundles/spark/mcp/src/main.py`**: `DataSourcesStore()` instantiated alongside the other catalogue stores; `set_data_sources_store(store)` makes it singletonable across the process.

**24 new unit tests in `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_data_sources_store.py`**:
- Schema initialization on fresh tmp_path
- Install idempotency (re-install replaces; never duplicates)
- FK cascade: uninstall drops dependent fields + xdm_mappings
- Re-install with smaller field set clears stale fields (cascade fires through INSERT OR REPLACE)
- List with case-insensitive LIKE matching across pack/dataset/rule/description
- supported_modules JSON round-trip preserves order + content
- is_rawlog_only INTEGER↔bool round-trip (catches type drift)
- Pin/unpin clears pinned_version on unpin (avoids stale-version-pointing-to-nothing)
- get_with_schema returns None for missing id (vs raising)
- to_dict contract pin: 17 keys for DataSource, plus `fields` + `xdm_mappings` for DataSourceWithSchema

### Smoke-test bullets

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: pytest 386 tests pass (was 362 in v0.7.5 + 24 new), tsc clean, lint clean, npm run build green
- `agent-skipped` Post-deploy: SSH to phantom-vm + check `/app/data/data_sources.db` exists after restart (boot wiring instantiated the store correctly)
- `agent-skipped` Boot regression check: `phantom_agent` container starts cleanly, MCP responds on port 8080, no init errors in logs
- `agent-skipped` Forward compat: `data_sources.db` schema accepts re-init on existing file (idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)

### Forbidden under the catalog/credential boundary

`DataSourcesStore` is CATALOG state — when v0.7.7 ships the `data_sources_install` / `data_sources_uninstall` MCP tools, they ARE allowed to be agent-callable per CLAUDE.md § Catalog boundary ≠ credential boundary (v0.5.0+). This is symmetric with the existing `marketplace_install` agent tool — both mutate platform metadata, not secrets.

```

bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_data_sources_store.py       (NEW, 380 lines)
bundles/spark/mcp/src/main.py                            (+14 lines, boot wiring)
CHANGELOG.md                                              (this entry)
mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts                            (matching About-modal entry)
```

### Next: v0.7.7

Phase 2's second commit will add `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/data_sources.py` (4 REST endpoints), three MCP tools (`data_sources_list`, `data_sources_get_schema`, `data_sources_install`), and the agent-side proxy routes under `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/data-sources/**`.

## [v0.7.5] — 2026-05-21 — *Phase 1 of v0.8.0 Marketplace Data Sources arc*

**Three new extraction tools that read raw vendor field inventories directly out of Cortex's ModelingRule `_schema.json` files. Foundation for v0.8.0 Marketplace Data Sources — no UI yet, no operator-visible behavior change yet. Prerequisite for Phases 2-4 of the arc.**

### Arc context — v0.8.0 Marketplace Data Sources

The full v0.8.0 capability lets operators install vendor-faithful schemas (FortiGate, Okta, PaloAlto, …) as "Data Sources" from a new marketplace page, then xlog uses the installed vendor's exact field set when generating simulated logs — so simulated traffic looks identical to what real vendors emit + Cortex's out-of-the-box ModelingRules parse it correctly. Operator's six design decisions from `docs/DESIGN-marketplace-data-sources.md`:

1. Naming: **"Data Sources"** (not "vendors" or "schemas")
2. **Separate page** (not under `/connectors`) — own sidebar entry
3. Drill-down: vendor → product → data source; ACTUAL vendor logos from the packs (not auto-generated SVG)
4. **Manual update only** (no auto-update — operator triggers the refresh)
5. **XSIAM-tagged packs only** (filter by `supportedModules` containing `"xsiam"`)
6. **Keep Rosetta as fallback** (when no Data Source is installed, the existing predefined-field generator runs)

The arc ships in 4 phases:
- **Phase 1 (v0.7.5, this release)** — extraction tools that turn ModelingRules into schemas.
- **Phase 2 (~1 week)** — `data_sources.db` + REST endpoints + MCP tools.
- **Phase 3 (~1-2 weeks)** — Marketplace UI Data Sources tab + xlog dynamic schema API.
- **Phase 4 (~1 week)** — Skill integration + end-to-end smoke + v0.8.0 tag.

### What ships in v0.7.5

Three new MCP tools added to the existing `cortex-content` connector (`bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/src/connector.py`):

1. **`cortex_extract_vendor_schema(pack_name, rule_name)`** — reads `Packs/<pack>/ModelingRules/<rule>/<rule>_schema.json` and returns the structured field inventory: per-dataset `field_count`, `non_meta_field_count`, `fields[{name, type, is_array}]`, plus an `is_rawlog_only` flag for packs whose modeling rule extracts fields via regex from `_raw_log` (F5APM-style — Phase 1.5 work).
2. **`cortex_extract_vendor_logo(pack_name)`** — finds the canonical vendor logo URL with the search order `Integrations/<int>/<int>_dark.svg` → `<int>_image.png` → `Author_image.png`. Returns a Phantom-local URL `/api/agent/data-sources/logo/<pack>` (the route serves the bytes with appropriate Content-Type). The UI renders directly via `<img src="…">`.
3. **`cortex_extract_vendor_catalog(xsiam_only, include_rawlog, pack_limit)`** — rolls both up across the entire catalog, returning one row per `(pack, rule, dataset)` triple with field count + logo URL + rawlog flag. Powers the marketplace UI: groups rows by pack → vendor card; clicking a card shows rules; clicking a rule shows fields.

Also adds:

- **`xsiam_only` parameter** to `cortex_list_packs` (default `false` — preserves the existing API contract). When `true`, the tool delegates to the underlying `supported_module="xsiam"` filter so other tooling can opt-in to the same XSIAM-only filter without re-implementing it.
- **`_META_SCHEMA_FIELDS` constant** in the connector — the six universal schema fields (`_id`, `_product`, `_raw_log`, `_vendor`, `_time`, `_collector_name`) that every ModelingRule emits regardless of vendor. When the only fields are these six, the rule is "rawlog-only" — vendor data lives in `_raw_log` only, extraction needs regex (Phase 1.5).
- **21 new unit tests** in `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_cortex_content_vendor_schema.py` — covers structured packs, rawlog-only packs, multi-dataset packs, legacy string-typed schemas, malformed dataset entries, missing schema.json, SVG-vs-PNG logo precedence, the Author_image.png fallback, multi-integration logo discovery, xsiam_only filter, include_rawlog filter, pack_limit cap, and the full marketplace row shape contract. Loads the connector with `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` under a synthetic package (`_cortex_test_pkg`) to sidestep the `src/` namespace collision with MCP — pattern copied from the existing `test_cortex_content_index_kb.py`.

### How to test the Phase 1 tools

The tools are not yet exposed in the UI (Phase 3 work). They're callable via the MCP tool layer:

```bash
# Tunnel to phantom-vm MCP loopback
gcloud compute start-iap-tunnel "$VM_NAME" 8080 \
  --local-host-port=localhost:8081 \
  --zone="$VM_ZONE" --project="$VM_PROJECT" &

# One pack: extract schema for FortiGate's main ModelingRule
curl -sS -X POST "https://localhost:8081/api/v1/tools/call" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"cortex_extract_vendor_schema","arguments":{"pack_name":"FortiGate","rule_name":"FortiGate_1_3"}}'

# One pack's logo URL
curl -sS -X POST "https://localhost:8081/api/v1/tools/call" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"cortex_extract_vendor_logo","arguments":{"pack_name":"FortiGate"}}'

# Walk the catalog with the operator-mandated XSIAM-only filter + pack cap (for first-call testing)
curl -sS -X POST "https://localhost:8081/api/v1/tools/call" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"cortex_extract_vendor_catalog","arguments":{"xsiam_only":true,"include_rawlog":true,"pack_limit":5}}'
```

### Smoke-test bullets — auto-deploy + 4 probes all GREEN against `dev-2cea8b4` on phantom-vm

- ✓ `agent-verified` Pre-deploy gate: `tsc + lint + build + pytest` all green; pytest run shows **362 tests passing** (341 existing + 21 new).
- ✓ `agent-verified` Auto-deploy: `Build agent` (3m53s) + `Build connectors` (9m5s) + `Build dev installer` (51s) all succeeded; `Auto-deploy on phantom-vm (dev cycle)` step wrote `PHANTOM_VERSION=dev-2cea8b4` to `/opt/phantom/.env`. `phantom_agent` container recreated + healthy after install. Connector source mtime inside container matches push (`May 21 02:57` for `connector.py`).
- ✓ `agent-verified` **Probe 1** — `cortex_extract_vendor_schema(pack_name="FortiGate", rule_name="FortiGate_1_3")` → `ok=true`, `is_structured=true`, dataset `fortinet_fortigate_raw`, **field_count=176** + **non_meta_field_count=172** (CEF-style vendor fields: `act`, `app`, `c6a2`, `c6a3`, `cat`, `cefDeviceVersion`, etc.). FortiGate is a much richer schema than the operator-mandated `>5 fields` floor.
- ✓ `agent-verified` **Probe 2** — `cortex_extract_vendor_logo(pack_name="FortiGate")` → `ok=true`, `logo_type="svg"`, `logo_url="/api/agent/data-sources/logo/FortiGate"`. SVG preferred order works as designed; only 1 path searched before match.
- ✓ `agent-verified` **Probe 3** — `cortex_extract_vendor_schema(pack_name="F5APM", rule_name="F5APM")` → `ok=true`, `is_structured=false`, dataset `f5_apm_raw`, **field_count=1** (only `_raw_log`), **non_meta_field_count=0**, `is_rawlog_only=true`. Confirms the rawlog-only detection works — F5APM's modeling rule extracts everything via regex from `_raw_log`, deferred to Phase 1.5 for vendor-faithful generation.
- ✓ `agent-verified` **Probe 4** — `cortex_extract_vendor_catalog(xsiam_only=true, include_rawlog=true, pack_limit=5)` → `ok=true`, `packs_scanned=1`, `rules_found=1`, `structured_rules=1`, `rawlog_rules=0`. AMP/AMP/cisco_secure_endpoint_raw is the only alphabetically-first xsiam-tagged pack with ModelingRules in the first 5 packs scanned. Row shape verified: `pack_name`, `rule_name`, `dataset_name`, `field_count=16`, `non_meta_field_count=16`, `is_rawlog_only=false`, `logo_type=png`.

### Semantics-of-`pack_limit` note (operator-visible for Phase 3 UI design)

`pack_limit=N` caps the **alphabetical Pack listing at N**, then xsiam + has-ModelingRules filters apply post-hoc. Result with `pack_limit=5` was `packs_scanned=1` (only AMP survived all filters out of the first 5 alphabetical packs). For Phase 3 marketplace UI we may want a sibling parameter (`xsiam_modeling_scan_limit`) that counts SURVIVING packs rather than raw packs — useful when an operator says "preview the first 10 xsiam-modeling packs" and expects 10 not "up to 10 candidates of which N survive."

### Why this matters

The pre-build research that made Phase 1 take 2-3 days instead of 1+ week was the discovery that every Cortex ModelingRule already ships with a `_schema.json` describing its expected field set. That JSON is exactly the inverse of what we want — Cortex publishes it for XSIAM ingestion validation — so we read it directly instead of parsing the `.xif` XQL to derive raw fields. The other big realization: 71 of the 217 modeling rules are "rawlog-only" (extract via regex from `_raw_log`), so Phase 1 focuses on the 92 XSIAM-tagged + structured packs and leaves rawlog-only extraction to Phase 1.5.

### Forbidden under the release-readiness gate

This release is a **mid-arc commit** under the v0.8.0 Marketplace Data Sources arc. Per CLAUDE.md § Release-readiness gate:

- ❌ No `git tag v0.7.5` — the customer release tag fires only at arc completion (planned v0.8.0).
- ✅ Build + auto-deploy run automatically on push to main.
- ✅ Smoke runs against the auto-deployed install.
- ✅ Fixes for any smoke-uncovered bugs ship as the NEXT iteration of the SAME arc (v0.7.6, v0.7.7, …), not as separate releases.

```
bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/src/connector.py    (+393 lines, 3 new tools + xsiam_only arg + meta-fields constant)
bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/connector.yaml      (+30 lines, 3 new tool entries)
bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_cortex_content_vendor_schema.py (NEW, 21 tests, ~450 lines)

```

## [v0.7.4] — 2026-05-21

**Login-page hero image (myrobo.webp) re-encoded from q=100 (lossless, 1.6 MB) to q=82 (172 KB) — 9.5× reduction. PSNR 44.5 dB ≫ 40 dB threshold for human-imperceptible difference. Full 1365×2048 dimensions retained.**

### Operator brief

> "the robot image that we have in the login page, it's too heavy when loaded. I think the size of the image is quite large. So take a backup of the image, and I don't know if you can compress the image a little bit."

### What ships in v0.7.4

- `mcp/agent/public/img/myrobo.webp` — re-encoded with `cwebp -q 82 -alpha_q 82`. From 1665 KB → 173 KB (saves 1.49 MB per login page render).
- `mcp/agent/.dockerignore` — added (was missing). Excludes `.image-backups/`, `node_modules/`, `.next/`, env files, and IDE noise from `docker build` context. Without this, the rollback backup would have bundled back into the image and undone the savings.
- `mcp/agent/.gitignore` — added `.image-backups/` so the local-only rollback artifact doesn't pollute git history.
- `mcp/agent/components/auth/login-screen.tsx` — updated the inline doc-comment to record the re-encoding rationale + dimensions + PSNR for future reviewers.

### Rollback

The original 1.6 MB image is preserved locally at:
```
mcp/agent/.image-backups/myrobo-v0.7.3-original.webp
```

To revert:
```bash
cp mcp/agent/.image-backups/myrobo-v0.7.3-original.webp \
   mcp/agent/public/img/myrobo.webp
```

(Or via git: `git checkout v0.7.3 -- mcp/agent/public/img/myrobo.webp`.)

### Why q=82 specifically

Tested q=80/82/85/88/90 and measured PSNR (peak signal-to-noise ratio) for each. Found that **q=82 hits the sweet spot just below the encoder's mode-switch at q≈83** — going from q=82 (172 KB) to q=85 (283 KB) adds 110 KB for only 0.7 dB additional quality, hard diminishing returns. The 44.5 dB at q=82 is already well above the 40 dB threshold where humans can perceive WebP compression artifacts.

### Why this matters

Login is the first surface every operator sees. A 1.6 MB image on a fresh page load delays Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) by hundreds of milliseconds on slower networks — first impressions of "fast" or "slow" form during this window. Cutting the image to 173 KB drops LCP into the snappy range even on constrained connections.

### What's NOT in v0.7.4

- Image format change (still WebP — already the right format for this content).
- Dimension change (kept 1365×2048 since the display can render up to ~1140×760 with object-contain, and we want retina coverage).
- Lazy loading (the image renders above-the-fold on desktop, so lazy load would be wrong — it's the primary visual the user is waiting for).

## [v0.7.2] — 2026-05-21

**Operator brief: "go through the UI, clean things a little bit. I need to have a cohesive UI experience. And our styling and branding is consistent across all the pages and subpages without changing functionality of the pages that are currently working with no issues." Skills page is the named-reference for layout/padding/header pattern. See v0.7.3 for the 2 follow-up pages this pass missed.**

### Pages standardized to the skills-page layout pattern

The pattern (extracted from `/skills`, `/memory`, `/knowledge`, `/connectors`):

```tsx
<div className="h-screen overflow-y-auto custom-scrollbar">
  <div className="max-w-[1400px] mx-auto px-8 py-8 space-y-6">
    <header>
      <div className="flex items-center gap-3 mb-1">
        <span className="material-symbols-outlined text-2xl text-primary">{icon}</span>
        <h1 className="font-headline text-3xl font-bold tracking-tight text-on-surface">{title}</h1>
      </div>
      <p className="text-sm text-on-surface-variant ml-9">{single-line subtitle}</p>
    </header>
    {content}
  </div>
</div>
```

Pages refit to this pattern (commit 1 of overnight v0.7.2):

| Page | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `/agents` | `max-w-[1200px]` + 4-line subtitle | `max-w-[1400px]` + 1-line subtitle |
| `/tasks` | `max-w-[1300px]` + 4-line subtitle | `max-w-[1400px]` + 1-line subtitle |
| `/plugins` | `max-w-[1200px]` + 4-line subtitle | `max-w-[1400px]` + 1-line subtitle |
| `/approvals` | `p-8 min-h-screen` (no max-w, no scroll wrapper) | full pattern |
| `/api-keys` | `min-h-screen p-8 space-y-8` (no max-w) | full pattern |
| `/models` | `p-8 pb-32 max-w-7xl` (was OK but inconsistent) | full pattern + icon swap layers→layers |
| `/observability/runtime-events` | `p-8 space-y-6` (no max-w, no scroll wrapper) | full pattern + 1-line subtitle |
| `/observability/logs` | `max-w-[1600px]` | `max-w-[1400px]` |
| `/observability/connectors` | `max-w-[1300px]` | `max-w-[1400px]` |
| `/observability/detections` | inline `style={{padding,maxWidth,margin}}` + no icon | full pattern + radar icon + Tailwind |
| `/settings/hooks` | `max-w-[1100px]` | `max-w-[1400px]` |
| `/settings/personality` | `min-h-screen p-6 lg:p-8` + "Settings / Personality" breadcrumb + `<PhantomLogo>` box | full pattern + psychology icon, breadcrumb dropped (no other settings page has one) |
| `/settings/backup-restore` | `max-w-3xl mx-auto p-8` (way too narrow, no icon) | full pattern + backup icon |
| `/help` | `max-w-4xl mx-auto px-8 py-16 pb-32` (too narrow) | `max-w-[1400px]` |

All 14 pages now share the exact same outer container, scroll behavior, max-width, padding, and header layout. The skills/memory/knowledge/connectors visual rhythm is now uniform across the operator-facing surface.

### `.input-base` CSS class defined (closes "agents form has no text fields" bug)

Operator audit: *"when I click to create a new agent, it's one color window. There's no, you know, bunch of text fields."*

Root cause: ~20 form input elements across `/agents` and `/settings/hooks` had `className="input-base"` referenced but the class was NEVER DEFINED in globals.css. Inputs rendered with browser-default styles which were transparent against the dark-theme panel background — they were present in the DOM but invisible. Operator saw "one color window" because the inputs blended into the panel.

Fix: defined `.input-base` in `app/globals.css` with theme-aware styling (bg, border, padding, focus ring, placeholder color, disabled state). Width-100% by default; uses M3 design tokens so dark↔light theme flip works automatically. Pages now affected:
- `/agents` create/edit form (name, description, system prompt, tools allowed/denied, max-turns)
- `/settings/hooks` (~12 form fields across hook creation + builtin-config)

No HTML changes; pure CSS addition.
