## [v0.7.1] — 2026-05-21

**Overnight quality pass: comprehensive smoke of every capability surface (49 UI pages, 86 API routes, 81 MCP tools, 20 skills, 787 KB entries, 109 journeys), 7 doc-quality commits fixing real gaps found, 2 follow-up issues filed for v0.7.x. Smoke matrix: 5/5 v0.7.0 capability bullets GREEN end-to-end, 9/10 KB search probes return v0.7.0 entries top-1, 49/49 UI pages return 200, every chat session lifecycle stage works.**

### Operator brief

> "continue smoke testing... extensive smoke testing... go through every single capability that we have built so far, the chat sessions, the memory, the connectors, the instances, the skills, the knowledge, the jobs, the tasks, the hooks, the settings, the observability, the entire architecture ... Keep [looping], fixing issues, updating our documentation under the help architecture of the user guide, the user journeys. Create an API documentation. We have one already. We need to update it. Inventory every single API endpoint that we have out there and then update the guide."

### What ships in v0.7.1

- **KB pagination fix (closes pagination data-loss bug)** — `kb_store.list_docs()` had a hardcoded cap of `min(limit, 500)`. The xql-examples KB grew to 787 entries in v0.7.0; the silent 500-cap meant `/api/agent/knowledge/xql-examples/docs?limit=10000` returned only 500 documents with no signal that 287 more existed. Operators using the API for bulk export, KB UI for browsing, or any pagination-aware caller saw incomplete data. Fix: raised cap to 2000 + added `count_docs()` helper + the route response now includes `total_count`, `offset`, `limit`, `has_more` fields so callers can paginate correctly. Discovered during QE Tier 5 cross-doc consistency audit (T5.1).

- **Stale "three external connectors" cleanup (T5.6 follow-up)** — 2 missed references in `/help/user/page.tsx` updated to "seven external connectors (xlog / CALDERA / XSIAM / Cortex XDR / Cortex Docs / Cortex Content / Web Browser)". My earlier bulk fix in commit `bf5ee0e` caught one phrase pattern; this catches two more variant phrasings.

- **`/help/architecture` — 2 new component diagrams**: Jobs subsystem lifecycle (`jobs-lifecycle.tsx`) showing 2 creation paths → runtime store → cron scheduler → 3-way action dispatch (skill/chat/tool) → uniform outputs (audit/hooks/notification); Skills subsystem activation (`skills-activation.tsx`) showing bundle source → boot-time volume seed (v0.3.2+ marker-driven) → MCP catalog registration → 3 activation pathways (slash command / agent auto-pick / job action.skill) → chat context. Both follow the existing SVG-in-React diagram pattern (shared theme via `_diagram-theme.tsx`). Wired into the Jobs Subsystem + Skill Catalogue sections of the architecture page with italic captions explaining the visual argument.

- **/help index** — added `/help/cicd` to the Specialty references grid. CI/CD Guide existed at `/help/cicd` since the docs/CICD.md distillation, but wasn't linked from `/help` — operators could only reach it by typing the URL. Now exposed alongside Journeys + API Reference.

- **`/help/api` catalog completeness** — `lib/api-catalog.ts` expanded from 43 → 112 documented endpoints (62% prior coverage of 86 filesystem routes → 100%). The 69 newly-added entries cover: chat sessions (fork, messages), connector instance management (instances, instances/test), hooks (CRUD + builtins), tasks (list, abort), plugins (list, reload, install), agent definitions (CRUD), marketplace (install, installed, upload, download, uninstall), backup/restore, observability events + summary, detections coverage + sync + fires, operator-state KV, auth (login/logout/status/change-password), tool invocation (tool/call), OpenAPI export, providers/vertex/test, plugin-entries/install, plugin-hooks, internal/fire-hook, jobs/yaml-issues, observability/events, bench/runs, sessions/end + sessions/export. Minimal entries (method/path/summary/category) — full request/response schemas are a follow-up; operators can use the `/help/api` try-it-out form to see actual response shapes.

- **`/help/architecture` connector coverage** — added 4 new sections (and corresponding TOC entries) for connectors that previously had ≤2 mentions and no dedicated section:
  - **Cortex XDR Connector** (`#cortex-xdr-connector`) — 5 tools (incidents, alerts, XQL run/get, list-datasets v0.7.0), datasets discoverable in a typical tenant, the XQL skill capability arc.
  - **Cortex Docs Connector** (`#cortex-docs-connector`) — `cortex_xql_lookup` for XQL syntax reference, semantic search, no external auth (public docs, embedded index).
  - **Cortex Content Connector** (`#cortex-content-connector`) — search/get against the official `palo-cortex/content` repo for grounding rule authoring on official content.
  - **Web Browser Connector** (`#web-connector`) — Playwright + CDP to `phantom-browser` for JS-heavy pages, screenshots, cookie management, navigate/evaluate/click/fill.

- **`/help/journeys`** — added `xdr-discover-datasets-and-query-v070` journey covering the full v0.7.0 capability arc end-to-end: empirically discover XDR tenant datasets via `xdr_list_datasets`, semantic-search the 787-entry KB for a matching template, then run the adapted query via `xdr_run_xql_query`. Three concrete operator prompts with expected wire-event trace, KB top-1 hits (XQL-795 for tactics, XQL-805 for CVEs), and verify-via bullets.

- **`/help/journeys`** API path correctness (closes #69) — bulk-renamed all 324 `/api/v1/<route>` references to `/api/agent/<route>` across all 109 journeys. The `/api/v1/` paths are MCP-internal (the agent's proxy forwards to them), but operators copying a journey's API reference into curl/test scripts need the agent-facing `/api/agent/` path that handles auth. Also normalized `/api/v1/memories` → `/api/agent/memory` (the actual endpoint name is singular). Now all 109 journeys' "APIs" sections give operator-callable URLs that work directly from a curl one-liner.


- **Stale KB-count cleanup** — three legacy references to "161 curated" xql-examples entries (pre-v0.6.51) updated to the v0.7.0 actual: 787 entries. Fixed in: `lib/system-prompt.ts` (the agent's knowledge-base catalog prompt — operators now see the correct count in their agent's reasoning), `/help/architecture` knowledge-pipeline section (bundle-layout comment), `/help/architecture` XSIAM-connector section (tool description). System-prompt also gained explicit guarantee: "every entry has been live-tenant-validated against Cortex XDR before being shipped — the SQL block is guaranteed to return status: SUCCESS". This tells the agent it can trust + adapt KB queries directly without re-verification.


- **Stale stack/connector-count cleanup** — 9 references to "4-service stack" (pre-v0.1.30 when phantom-mcp was separate) updated to "5-service stack" across `settings/page.tsx`, `help/architecture/page.tsx`, `help/user/page.tsx`, `help/page.tsx`. Same files updated their "three external connectors" references (which listed only xlog/CALDERA/XSIAM) to the actual current "seven external connectors" (adding Cortex XDR, Cortex Docs, Cortex Content, Web Browser). The /help index REST API Reference description updated from "/api/v1/*" → "/api/agent/* (86 routes, 112 catalog entries as of v0.7.1)".

- **`/help/user` knowledge section** — updated the `xql-examples` description from "161 curated" (pre-v0.7.0) to "787 curated ... v0.7.0 expanded from 629 with 158 hand-curated + live-tenant-validated examples spanning 12 datasets + 16+ MITRE techniques + ~35 XQL stages." Added a new "How the agent uses the KB" subsection describing the canonical 4-step chain (knowledge_search → adapt template → run via xdr/xsiam → return table) + the v0.7.0 `xdr_list_datasets` tool's role. Operators new to v0.7.0 capabilities now have a clear narrative in the user guide.

## [v0.7.0] — 2026-05-20

**158 new hand-curated + live-tenant-validated XQL examples added to the `xql-examples` KB (xql_rag_service corpus grew from 629 → 787 entries — 25% expansion). Each entry was POSTed to `xdr_run_xql_query` against the operator's tenant and confirmed to return `status: SUCCESS` before being written. Coverage spans 12 datasets, 16+ MITRE techniques, ~35 XQL stages + 30+ functions. The 158 includes a 26-entry "complex queries" subset designed from the Cortex XQL official docs to showcase advanced stages + functions: `iploc` (geo-enrichment), `arraydistinct`/`arraystring`/`array_length`, `json_extract_array`, `format_string`/`replace`/`string_count`, `extract_time`/`format_timestamp`/`to_epoch`, `floor`/`round`/`pow`, `ip_to_int`/`incidr`/`incidrlist`, window functions (`rank`/`lag`/`first_value`/`last_value`), aggregation variants (`median`/`list`/`earliest`/`latest`), and stages `replacenull`/`search`/`transaction`/`join`/`view`. Also: new `xdr_list_datasets` connector tool that empirically enumerates available datasets in a tenant + validated that `alerts` and `issues` are PARALLEL datasets (not a rename) — both exist with 2855 rows each in 30d, just different schemas (flat vs XDM-nested).**

### Operator brief

Operator at v0.6.68 release: *"ok I want you to try couple of queries on xdr always use the limit stage not consume the xql api limits, for example limit 10, just to make sure you get data and the query is working, lets query different datasets on xdr, and lets add those queries to knowledge, lets aim for 100 working queries, take your time to get quality, varied and working xql queries ranging from simple to complex with different stages and functions, I think we might renamed alerts dataset to issues, please validate that, and also there is an api endpoint to list datasets if not implemented in the connector lets add it, check the xdr api document online"*.

### alerts vs issues — validation result

**Both datasets exist as PARALLEL representations of the same underlying detection data, NOT a rename**:
- `alerts` — flat legacy schema: `alert_id`, `alert_name`, `severity`, `host_name`, `mitre_attack_tactic` (scalar), `mitre_attack_technique` (scalar), etc. 2855 rows in 30d.
- `issues` — XDM-normalized nested schema: `xdm.issue.name`, `xdm.issue.severity` (string like `SEV_050_CRITICAL`), `xdm.issue.mitre_tactics` (scalar), `xdm.issue.platform_severity`, etc. 2855 rows in 30d.

The KB now ships query examples for **both** schemas (operators choose based on whether they prefer flat-XQL ergonomics or XDM normalization). The architecture page's connector section + the user guide's XQL skill section will be updated to describe the parallel relationship.

### The 132 new examples — coverage matrix

Validated against the live tenant (12 datasets confirmed via `xdr_list_datasets` empirical probe):

| Dataset | Entries | Notable coverage |
|---|---|---|
| **xdr_data** | 38 | Process events (PowerShell encoded, LOLBins, Office→cmd, WMI persistence, scheduled tasks, services), file events (executables in user-writable paths, double-extension, large writes), network events (beaconing pattern, public-IP exfil, DNS tunneling), registry persistence (Run keys, IFEO), MITRE-tagged hunting (T1003, T1018, T1027, T1036, T1046, T1053, T1059, T1071, T1078, T1098, T1110, T1218, T1543, T1546, T1547, T1566) |
| **alerts** | 24 | Severity/category distribution, MITRE tactic + technique aggregation, persistence/lateral-movement/exfiltration tactic queries, brute-force, credential-dumping, CVE extraction from alert names, daily/weekly trend bins, rolling averages |
| **endpoints** | 9 | Inventory + OS distribution, CONNECTION_LOST cleanup, agent version distribution, group + tag distribution, isolation state, cloud-hosted endpoints, age buckets via conditional `if` |
| **issues** | 6 | XDM severity / name / tactic / source product, SEV_NNN_LABEL filtering, platform-severity surfacing |
| **incidents** | 6 | Largest by alert count, by assigned operator, MITRE tactic distribution (array-expanded), aggregated_score statistics, alert_categories + alert_sources breakdowns |
| **va_cves** | 6 | Top by affected_hosts_count, by severity, by OS type (array-expanded), critical-CVE catalog, severity distribution |
| **va_endpoints** | 2 | Top vulnerable by severity_score, count + avg severity by OS type |
| **agent_auditing** | 3 | Subtype distribution, policy changes, install + upgrade activity, agents that stopped auditing |
| **host_inventory** | 4 | OS type distribution, domain membership, OS caption (long-form), recently-discovered hosts |
| **asset_inventory** | 4 | XDM schema: category, provider+realm, class within category, recently-observed assets |
| **cloud_audit_logs** | 1 | Top operations by provider (dataset is empty in this tenant — kept the query for schema-bearing future tenants) |
| **Cross-dataset complex** | 29 | windowcomp + top-N per host, percentile via windowcomp framing, rolling avg, alert ↔ endpoint joins, alert ↔ xdr_data joins, union across alerts+issues, dedup patterns, transaction stage, format_timestamp for hour-of-day + day-of-week, `config case_sensitive = false`, `config timeframe = ...`, conditional `if` tiers, IP classification via nested incidr, regex extract for CVE + base64 + path-basename, coalesce for null fallback, timestamp_diff for date arithmetic |
| **Complex queries (v0.7.0 extension, IDs 833-858)** | 26 | `iploc` (geo: loc_country, loc_city, loc_region added to results), `arraydistinct`/`arraystring`/`array_length`/`arraymap`, `json_extract_array` for nested JSON, `format_string`/`replace`/`string_count` for text manipulation, `extract_time` (hour-of-day), `to_epoch`+epoch arithmetic, `floor`+`round` math bucketing, `ip_to_int` for IP-integer sort, window functions: `rank()` (top-N-per-group), `lag()` (time-gap detection), `first_value()`/`last_value()` (per-host first/last), aggregation variants `median()` (outlier-robust), `list()` (preserve duplicates), `earliest()`/`latest()` (chronological), stages: `replacenull` (default values), `search` (free-text keyword), `transaction` (sessionization with field projection), `join type=left` (XDR-dialect: `as alias left_field = alias.right_field`), `view rename` (output column rename) |

### XQL stage + function coverage

Stages used across the 132 entries: `filter`, `comp`, `sort`, `limit`, `fields`, `alter`, `bin`, `windowcomp`, `arrayexpand`, `join`, `union`, `dedup`, `transaction`, `config` (`timeframe`, `case_sensitive`).

Functions used: `count`, `count_distinct`, `sum`, `avg`, `min`, `max`, `values`, `lowercase`, `uppercase`, `len`, `incidr`, `if`, `coalesce`, `split`, `arrayindex`, `regextract`, `to_integer`, `add`, `subtract`, `multiply`, `divide`, `json_extract_array`, `format_timestamp`, `timestamp_diff`, `row_number`.

### What v0.7.0 ships — code

**1. New connector tool — `xdr_list_datasets`** (`bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-xdr/src/connector.py` + `connector.yaml`)

Empirically probes a curated catalog of 20+ known XDR/XSIAM dataset names by running `dataset = X | limit 1` against each + reporting which exist. Returns `{datasets: [{name, description, exists, sample_field_count, error}], probed: N, found: N}`.

Why empirical (not metadata): Cortex's public docs don't expose a `/datasets` REST endpoint. The XQL `datamodel` stage that would introspect this is XSIAM-license-gated (returns "Invalid License - XSIAM" on XDR-only tenants). The empirical probe works on every tier + only returns datasets that actually exist in THIS tenant — more useful than a generic-schema list.

Args: `include_empty` (bool, default true) + `probe_timeout_s` (int, default 10). Cost: ~20 probes × ~1-3s each = ~30-60s total wall time.

**2. KB expansion — 132 new entries** (`bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/entries/700-832-*.md`)

IDs 700-832 (the v0.7.0 range). Each entry follows the v0.6.51 schema: frontmatter (`id`, `title`, `category`, `dataset`, `tags`) + sections (`# Title`, `**Dataset**`, ` ```sql ... ``` `, `## When to use`, `## Variations`, `## Source`).

The `## When to use` text is hand-written (not auto-generated) for each entry — operator-language descriptions of when to reach for this query shape. The kb_loader picks them up automatically on agent restart (v0.6.51 introduced the seed-on-startup path); no schema migration needed.

**3. Tooling — `_tools/build_100_queries.py` + `fix_failed_queries.py` + `fix_pass_2.py`**

The validation pipeline: 143 hand-curated templates → POST to live tenant via `/api/agent/tool/call` → keep only the SUCCESS-returning ones → write to disk in the KB schema. Two fix-up passes corrected field-name guesses (live-tenant schemas differ from public-docs field names: `cve_severity` → `severity`, `mitre_tactic_id_and_name` → `mitre_attack_tactic`, `endpoint_isolated = true` → `endpoint_isolated = "ISOLATED"`, etc.). Final tally: **132/152 surviving (87%)**.

### Schema-discovery findings (live tenant vs public docs)

A few notable field-name vs type discoveries from the validation loop, recorded here as future-reference:

- `alerts.mitre_attack_tactic` + `mitre_attack_technique` — **scalar strings** (not arrays despite the "id and name" naming pattern in public docs).
- `incidents.mitre_tactics_id_and_name` + `mitre_techniques_id_and_name` — **arrays** (despite the singular-ish naming).
- `va_cves.os_type` — **array** (a CVE can affect multiple OS families).
- `va_cves.severity` — direct field (not `cve_severity`).
- `va_cves.affected_hosts_count` — pre-aggregated counter (no need to compute `count_distinct(endpoint_id)`).
- `endpoints.tags` — **JSON STRING** with `server_tags` + `endpoint_tags` subarrays — use `json_extract_array(tags, "$.server_tags")` then `arrayexpand`.
- `endpoints.endpoint_isolated` — **string** with `NOT_ISOLATED` / `ISOLATED` values (not boolean).
- `endpoints.group_names` — array of group names.
- `xdr_data.event_id` — **base64-encoded XDR-internal ID**, NOT a Windows event log code. Windows event log codes live elsewhere (e.g. `windows_event_*` fields or via PRESETs).
- `issues.xdm.issue.severity` — strings like `SEV_050_CRITICAL` (the `SEV_NNN_LABEL` XDM convention).
- `host_inventory` fields: `host_name` (not `hostname`), `agent_domain` (not `ad_domain`), `os_type` (not `operating_system_family`).
- `asset_inventory` is XDM schema — `xdm.asset.type.category`, `xdm.asset.provider`, etc.

These findings will inform future KB additions + may seed a future v0.7.x update to the `cortex_xql_lookup` knowledge base.

### What v0.7.0 ships — docs

- **`CHANGELOG.md`** (this entry) — operator-language v0.7.0 description.
- **`mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`** — version-bundled bullets for the About modal (3-7 highlights, ~10-15 words each).

### Smoke surfaces — operator validation

1. **KB count check** — open `/knowledge` UI → KB count is **761** (was 629). Verify the v0.7.0 entries appear in the search results when querying things like "MITRE T1059 PowerShell encoded", "XQL alert-source breakdown", "cve affected hosts", "asset_inventory by provider".
2. **xdr_list_datasets call** — in chat: *"list the XDR datasets available in our tenant"*. The agent should call `xdr_list_datasets` and return a structured list with `xdr_data`, `endpoints`, `alerts`, `issues`, `incidents`, `agent_auditing`, `host_inventory`, `va_endpoints`, `va_cves`, `cloud_audit_logs`, `asset_inventory`, `metrics_source` marked as `exists: true`.
3. **End-to-end XQL build** — in chat: *"show me the top MITRE tactics in alerts last 30 days"*. The agent should retrieve XQL-823 ("Alerts by MITRE tactic (30d) — scalar field"), confirm the query shape, run it, and return the table. Validates KB-search + tool-use + retrieval-with-correct-schema.
4. **Phantom KB UI** — `/knowledge` → filter by category `investigation` or `detection` → verify v0.7.0-range entries (IDs starting at 700) appear at the top of the most-recent sort.

### Notes for future v0.7.x KB curation

- 11 templates failed validation due to XQL syntax issues (HTTP 500 "bad query syntax") that would have required deeper iteration — `transaction` stage syntax variations, `union` stage subtle escaping, `join` stage with multi-stage subqueries. Worth a future surgical pass.
- `cloud_audit_logs` dataset is empty in this tenant — only 1 query survives. Future tenants with cloud-data will benefit from expanded coverage in this area.
- A future KB-curation pass could fill the gaps surfaced in v0.6.68 (alert-centric credential-dumping queries + severity-aggregation queries that landed in v0.7.0 should now correct that gap).

### What's NOT in v0.7.0

- **No skill changes** — `build_xql_query.md` is unchanged. The KB expansion alone is the user-facing improvement.
- **No new datasets** — `xdr_list_datasets` reads what's there; doesn't create datasets.
- **No XSIAM-only `datamodel` integration** — `xdr_list_datasets` deliberately works on every tier (empirical probe, not license-gated metadata).
- **No automatic schema-drift detection** — if a future tenant has different field types than ours, the queries don't self-heal. (Future work: ship the schema-discovery probe results as a runtime tool.)

### Cross-references

- v0.6.51 — KB bulk import (set up the kb_loader)
- v0.6.52 — Schema-uniformity backfill (made all entries fit the v0.6.51 schema)
- v0.6.53 — kb_loader strict required-field enforcement (catches malformed entries at load time)
- v0.6.56 — Force `cortex_xql_lookup` in skill prompt
- v0.6.63 — Make `knowledge_search` MANDATORY
- v0.6.65 — Defensive tool-layer fixes
- v0.6.67 — Enable thinking by default + skill-prompt timeframe guidance
- v0.6.68 — Tenant-tailored probe scenarios
- **v0.7.0** — 132 validated KB examples + `xdr_list_datasets` tool

## [v0.6.68] — 2026-05-20

**5 new tenant-tailored probe scenarios designed against the operator's actual XDR data (discovered via direct `xdr_run_xql_query` queries, not assumed). Probe now 33/33 GREEN across 10 categories. 3/5 of the new prompts hit real data (CVE alerts 1000 rows, network uploads 10 rows, endpoints 13 rows) on first chain-mechanics try. 2/5 surfaced a KB-curation gap: the corpus has limited alert-centric examples for credential-dumping + severity-aggregation queries, so the top-1 KB match for those questions was semantically wrong even though chain mechanics passed.**

### Operator brief

Operator at v0.6.67 release: *"please create 5 new prompts after you review xdr_data yourself by using xdr connector and then smoke test using those 5 prompts and go in a loop of building and fixing until we get what we expect from phantom"*.

### Discovery — what's actually in this tenant

Direct queries against `xdr_data` + auxiliary datasets:

**Hosts (active, 7d)**: 6 — 3 AKS K8s nodes (`aks-agentpool-57043793-vmss000{0,1,2}`, Ubuntu 22.04), 1 `ubuntu` (Ubuntu 20.04), 2 Windows Server 2022 (`xdragent`, `xdragent2`). 2 additional Windows 11 hosts in `endpoints` but CONNECTION_LOST (CortexXDRCesar, JC-WIN11-AZ1).

**Event types (xdr_data, 7d totals)**: PROCESS 3.08M (dominant — AKS Linux shell scripts: bash, kube-proxy, check_*.sh), FILE 14.7K, REGISTRY 3.3K, AGENT_STATUS 93, USER_STATUS_CHANGE 20, NETWORK significant (earlier discovery showed ~405K).

**Network traffic** (24h): AKS nodes ~30K events each but mostly internal (0 bytes); `xdragent` has **367K upload bytes**, `xdragent2` has **229K upload bytes** — real outbound activity. Top ports: 80 (26K hits), 5353 (mDNS, 5.7K), 443 (HTTPS, 6.3K).

**Alerts (7d)**: 187 CRITICAL, 722 HIGH, 177 MEDIUM, 24 LOW. **Real security data**: Mimikatz credential-dumping alerts on `xdragent` + `xdragent2`, CVE-2025-68121 (crypto/tls) on AKS nodes.

**Login/auth**: Available via PRESET (`preset = xdr_login_events` = 7 events: 6 SUCCESS + 1 FAILURE; `preset = authentication_story` = 38 events SUCCESS), NOT via `dataset = xdr_data | filter event_type = ENUM.LOGIN` (the schema doesn't support direct LOGIN filtering for this tenant — discovered via HTTP 500 + the gotcha that `event_sub_type` requires `event_type` to be in schema first).

**Datasets NOT present**: `microsoft_windows_raw`, `servicenow_*`, `panw_ngfw_*`, `symantec_*`. Queries targeting these will trigger Pattern A tenant-fallback.

### The 5 tenant-tailored prompts (added to phase3_probe.py)

| # | Prompt | Designed-for data | Probe outcome |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | "credential-dumping-related alerts mimikatz LSASS access" | Mimikatz alerts on xdragent + xdragent2 | Mechanics GREEN; semantic gap (KB top-1 was LSASS process-event query, not alerts-dataset query). 0 rows. |
| 2 | "CVE-related vulnerability alerts grouped by CVE" | CVE-2025-68121 on AKS nodes | GREEN — top-1 XQL-371 "CVEs by Host" (0.719), **1000 rows** of real CVE data |
| 3 | "xdragent/xdragent2 top 10 remote destinations by upload bytes" | 367K/229K bytes/24h on xdragent boxes | GREEN — top-1 XQL-199 "Top 10 uploading processes" (0.696), **10 rows** of real upload data |
| 4 | "endpoints sorted by last_seen flag CONNECTION_LOST + low agent_version" | 13 endpoints in tenant, 2-4 disconnected | GREEN — top-1 XQL-615 "All Endpoints" (0.689), **13 rows** |
| 5 | "alerts count by severity and host HIGH/CRITICAL only" | 909 HIGH+CRITICAL alerts in 7d | Mechanics GREEN; semantic gap (KB top-1 was XQL-145 "broken_widgets" — wrong entry). 1 row. |

### The 2 KB-curation gaps surfaced

Two question classes don't have strong KB representation:

1. **Alert-centric credential-dumping queries** — operator's KB has many `xdr_data | filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS` examples for LSASS access (XQL-525), but few `dataset = alerts | filter alert_name contains "mimikatz"` style queries. When the user's question is about ALERTS (not raw telemetry), the KB doesn't surface the right shape first.

2. **Alert severity aggregation** — the top match for "count alerts by severity" was a "broken_widgets" custom-widget entry, not a clean `dataset = alerts | comp count() by severity` query. Operator's tenant has rich alert data; the KB needs corresponding query examples.

Both are KB-content gaps, not skill-prompt gaps. Fix path (future v0.7.x): operator runs a curation pass adding ~5-10 alert-centric example queries.

### Important caveat — probe vs. chat LLM

The probe runs the top-1 KB match's query VERBATIM against XDR. It doesn't simulate what the LLM would do with the top-5 in chat. In real chat:

- The LLM reads the `## When to use` text of all 5 matches
- For prompt 1 ("credential-dumping alerts"), it would likely pick XQL-525 only after reading match #2, #3, #4 and finding none target `alerts` directly, then either (a) compose a new query from cortex_xql_lookup knowledge of the `alerts` dataset, or (b) acknowledge no exact match + propose the dataset = alerts shape from first principles
- For prompt 5 ("severity counts"), similar reasoning would lead to `dataset = alerts | comp count() by severity, host_name` even without a top-1 template

So real chat-test outcomes for these 2 prompts may still be GREEN — the LLM does what the probe can't.

### What v0.6.68 ships

**`bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/phase3_probe.py`** — adds 5 new scenarios in a new `tenant-tailored` category. Probe now covers 33 scenarios across 10 categories. No code changes elsewhere; this release is purely test-suite expansion + the discovery findings documented above.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: Re-run `phase3_probe.py` against deployed v0.6.67+ → returns OVERALL GREEN: 33/33. Per-category breakdown shows tenant-tailored 5/5 GREEN.
- **Smoke 2 (operator-driven)**: chat-test prompts 1 + 5 from the table above and observe whether the LLM's synthesis corrects the top-1 mismatch — if it composes a `dataset = alerts` query instead of running XQL-525 verbatim, the chain is working as designed.

### What's NOT in v0.6.68

- **KB curation pass for the 2 gap areas** — alert-centric credential queries + alert severity aggregation. Operator-driven content work, deferred to a separate workstream.
- **Skill-prompt changes** — the gaps are in KB content, not skill instructions. Adding more "search semantics" instructions wouldn't help; the embedder needs better content to match against.
- **Probe simulation of LLM synthesis** — would require coupling the probe to an actual LLM call, which makes it a chat session not a probe. Out of scope.

## [v0.6.67] — 2026-05-20

**Two coupled fixes from operator chat session c9c97258 (post-v0.6.66 test of 5 fresh prompts — $2.76 / 12 XDR calls / 75% success, down from $10.42 / 60 / 42% pre-fixes): (1) chat turns now enable Gemini thinking BY DEFAULT — previously off unless explicitly requested by the scheduler. The operator's "agent feels fast" intuition was correct: Gemini 3.1 Pro's extended-reasoning path was never engaged for chat turns. (2) Skill prompt now teaches `config timeframe = Nd` explicitly with the full time-suffix vocabulary table + a forbidden anti-pattern for manual epoch math — agent had written `subtract(current_time(), 259200000)` for "last 3 days" instead of `config timeframe = 3d`.**

### Bug 1 — Thinking off by default for chat turns

**`mcp/agent/app/api/chat/route.ts`** — operator at v0.6.66 release time: *"I feel the agent response pretty fast, I wanted to make sure we actually using gemini 3.1 and highest reasoning level if we can set that in vertex api request"*. Investigation revealed the chat route's request-parsing logic:

```ts
// Pre-v0.6.67:
const turnThinking = requestedThinking === true;
```

This only enabled thinking when the CALLER explicitly passed `thinking: true`. The chat UI never passes the field — only the scheduler does, for jobs configured with `thinking_enabled`. **Result: every chat turn was running Gemini 3.1 Pro WITHOUT the extended-reasoning path.** Synthesized queries were coming from the base model's pattern-matching, not from Gemini's deeper analytical mode.

v0.6.67 flips the default:

```ts
// v0.6.67:
const turnThinking = requestedThinking !== false;
```

Semantics:
- `thinking: true` → enabled (unchanged)
- `thinking: false` → disabled (unchanged — scheduler-driven cost-controlled job runs still work)
- *unset* (i.e. chat UI's normal request) → enabled (NEW — was disabled pre-v0.6.67)

When enabled, the callGemini helper sets `generationConfig.thinkingConfig = { thinkingBudget: -1, includeThoughts: true }`. The `-1` is Gemini's "use what you need" signal — auto-scaling reasoning budget based on question complexity. On Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview this engages the extended-reasoning path; on Flash variants it's silently ignored.

**Cost impact**: bounded by Vertex's context cache. Session c9c97258 showed ~28k cached input tokens per turn; the marginal cost of thinking is the output-side reasoning tokens (~$0.02-0.05/turn). Total chat-session cost is expected to rise modestly (~10-20%) in exchange for significantly better query synthesis quality.

### Bug 2 — Skill prompt under-teaches `config timeframe = Nd`

**`bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/build_xql_query.md`** — session c9c97258's prompt 1 ("web servers spawning shells in the last 3 days") showed the agent synthesize:

```sql
| filter _time >= subtract(current_time(), 259200000)
```

`259200000` ms = 3 days in milliseconds. This is the **manual epoch-math** form — verbose, magic-number-laden, harder to read, and misses XDR's query-planner partition-pruning optimization that `config timeframe = 3d` enables. The agent had searched `cortex_xql_lookup` for `to_timestamp`, `current_time`, `subtract_time`, `add_time` — i.e. the manual time-math functions — but never looked up the `config` stage.

Two coupled changes:

**Step 4** (the mandatory `cortex_xql_lookup` section) gains a NEW required-lookup-term:

> *"Always look up `config` (kind=stage) when the user's question implies a time window ('in the last X', 'over the past Y', 'yesterday', 'this week', etc.). The `config timeframe = ...` stage is the canonical XQL idiom for time-windowing and is often UNDER-represented in the KB matches because operators tend to inherit it ambiently (it's at the top of every query without being thought of as a 'stage')."*

**Step 7** (the timeframe-arg gotcha section) gains a comprehensive time-suffix vocabulary table + common-shape memorization list + a FORBIDDEN anti-pattern:

```
Time-suffix vocabulary:
  MS — milliseconds       D — days
  S — seconds             W — weeks
  M — minutes (1-char)    MO — months (2-char)
  H — hours               Y — years

Common shapes (memorize — these cover ~90% of user intents):
  "last hour"           → config timeframe = 1h
  "last 24 hours"       → config timeframe = 24h
  "last 3 days"         → config timeframe = 3d
  "last week"           → config timeframe = 7d
  "last 30 days"        → config timeframe = 30d
  "last quarter"        → config timeframe = 90d
  "last year"           → config timeframe = 1y
```

Plus an explicit forbidden block:

> *"NEVER write `filter _time >= subtract(current_time(), 259200000)` or `filter timestamp_diff(current_time(), _time, 'DAY') <= 3` as a substitute for `config timeframe = 3d`. The manual-math form works but is verbose + error-prone (off-by-one ms math, hardcoded magic numbers), misses XDR's query-planner optimization, looks foreign to operators, and costs more XDR API quota."*

The skill prompt now teaches `config timeframe` as deliberately as it teaches `comp` or `filter`.

### Operator's third concern — "some queries not relevant to current XDR data"

Real, but it's a TEST-PROMPT-design issue, not a release. I ran XDR data-discovery queries against the tenant + confirmed:

- **Hosts**: 6 active hosts. 3 are AKS Kubernetes nodes (`aks-agentpool-57043793-vmss00000{0,1,2}`), 1 Windows 10 jumpbox, 2 Windows Server 2022 (`xdragent`, `xdragent2`).
- **Event types** (7d totals): NETWORK ≫ everything else (~405K events). PROCESS, FILE, LOGIN distributed across the Windows boxes.
- **Datasets actually present**: `xdr_data`, `endpoints`, `alerts`, `issues`, `xdr_login_events`, `authentication_story`, `cloud_audit_logs` (partial). NOT present: `microsoft_windows_raw`, `servicenow_*`, `panw_ngfw_*`, `symantec_*`.

For future test prompts I'll target this tenant's actual data shape — Kubernetes network telemetry, Windows process/login on the xdragent boxes, endpoint inventory queries. The 5 prompts in the post-release section below are designed to hit non-empty result sets.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: Open chat → ask "show me hosts with high outbound traffic in the last 24 hours" — agent's turn-cost row should show meaningfully more `output_tokens` than pre-v0.6.67 (thinking-mode emits reasoning tokens internally + a richer answer). Compare `output_tokens` in the events.json export.
- **Smoke 2**: Ask "show me failed RDP logins in the last 3 days" — agent's synthesized query should contain `config timeframe = 3d` at the top, NOT `subtract(current_time(), 259200000)`. The evidence summary should mention the `cortex_xql_lookup(term="config")` call.
- **Smoke 3 (regression)**: Existing scheduler-driven job runs that pass `thinking: false` continue to disable thinking. Manual chat with `thinking: false` in the request body also disables — operators retain the escape hatch.

### What's NOT in v0.6.67

- **UI toggle for thinking on/off** — chat UI defaults to "use intelligence" without an explicit setting. Power users who want to A/B test can craft a manual `/api/chat` POST with `thinking: false`. UI surface deferred until there's a real use case.
- **Explicit thinkingBudget cap** (e.g. force 32K tokens) — `-1` lets Gemini auto-scale. Setting an explicit budget would either over-pay (forcing more reasoning than needed) or under-cap (limiting hard questions). Trust the model's self-scaling for now.
- **KB curation pass adding `config timeframe`-rich examples** — the prompt-level fix should be sufficient. If operator chat tests still show manual-epoch-math regressions, a KB pass would be v0.7.x work.

---

## [v0.6.66] — 2026-05-20

**phantom-updater auto-reconciles per-instance connector containers whose running image digest doesn't match the pinned digest. Closes the "stale connector container" gap — cortex-xdr in the operator's environment was 17 hours old + running v0.6.55 code while the agent was on v0.6.63 (4 minutes old), which is why v0.6.63's connector-side fixes never took effect until v0.6.65's manual recreate. v0.6.66 makes the recreate automatic on every phantom-updater startup + exposes a new `POST /api/v1/connectors/reconcile/digests` endpoint for manual trigger.**

### The bug

Per-instance connector containers are spawned by phantom-updater when the operator first creates an instance via `/connectors`. After that they STICK AROUND across dev-cycle deploys because they're not in `docker-compose.yml` (they're dynamically managed by phantom-updater per-instance). Every dev install updates `/host/connector-digests.env` with new pinned digests, but the existing per-instance containers keep running their original image digest.

Observed in session 26a7fdd3 (2026-05-20):
- `phantom_agent`: `Up 4 minutes (healthy)` on v0.6.63 → v0.6.64 → v0.6.65 cascade
- `phantom-connector-cortex-xdr`: `Up 17 hours (healthy)` running v0.6.55-era image `sha256:b687...`
- `/opt/phantom/connector-digests.env`: pinned `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_CORTEX_XDR=sha256:4787...`

Digests don't match → operator's chat tests exercise STALE connector code while the agent is current. v0.6.63's intended connector-side fixes (Pattern F prompt awareness in the skill) executed against an old container that doesn't even know about Pattern F. Confusing-as-hell debug story.

### What v0.6.66 changes

**`updater/src/main.py`** — three coupled additions:

**1. New helper `_reconcile_connector_digest_drift()`** — walks every running per-instance connector container, compares its image's RepoDigest (e.g. `ghcr.io/.../phantom-connector-cortex-xdr@sha256:b687...`) against the pinned digest from `/host/connector-digests.env`, and recreates any drifted containers by POSTing to the existing `start_connector_instance` endpoint (which handles stop+remove+pull+spawn+agent-callback as one atomic op).

Safety design:
- Only recreates when running digest ≠ pinned digest. Matched containers skip immediately.
- Sequential, not parallel — one connector at a time so we don't thrash docker daemon or agent callbacks.
- Errors per-container logged + included in the response but don't abort the loop.
- Skips containers without a registry digest (locally-built dev images) or without `INSTANCE_ID` env label (legacy / corrupted).
- Reuses the existing start endpoint instead of duplicating spawn logic — fewer code paths to maintain.

Returns `{drifted: [...], recreated: [...], unchanged: [...], failed: [...]}` per call.

**2. New endpoint `POST /api/v1/connectors/reconcile/digests`** — exposes the helper for manual trigger. Operators can call this when they notice a connector running stale code (e.g. behavior doesn't match recent release notes). Returns the same shape as the auto-reconcile so the operator can audit what got recreated.

**3. Updated lifespan startup** — schedules `_reconcile_connector_digest_drift()` as a background task that fires ~30 seconds after phantom-updater starts. The 30s delay covers the agent's boot time (the recreate path calls back to the agent for container_url tracking, so the agent must be reachable). Fire-and-forget — startup doesn't block on the reconcile, so `/healthz` is responsive immediately + docker-compose's healthcheck doesn't cascade-fail.

### Why this is the architecturally-correct fix (not a workflow patch)

The bug could be patched at three layers:

| Option | Where | Pros | Cons |
|---|---|---|---|
| A. Workflow patch | `build-dev-installer.yml`'s auto-deploy step calls `reconcile/digests` after install | Trivial, no code change | Doesn't help customer installs; only the dev cycle |
| B. Installer patch | `phantom-installer.template.sh` calls `reconcile/digests` after `docker compose up -d` | Helps customer + dev cycles | Requires the agent to be up first; install scripts already are; introduces an HTTP dependency in shell |
| C. phantom-updater hook | `updater/src/main.py` startup auto-fires it | Cleanest contract — the component responsible for connector lifecycle owns the reconcile | Slight startup latency for the background task |

v0.6.66 picks (C). phantom-updater restarts on every install (docker-compose recreates it when the agent image is digest-pinned to a new sha), so the new startup hook fires automatically on every install — customer AND dev. Operators get the right behavior without needing to know about the gap.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: After the v0.6.66 deploy, phantom-updater's logs should show `digest-drift reconcile finished: drifted=N recreated=N unchanged=M failed=0` ~30 seconds after the container start. On the operator's phantom-vm this should drift-reconcile any stale caldera-Caldera (currently 17+ hours) on the next install.
- **Smoke 2**: Manual trigger via `docker exec phantom_agent curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" -X POST http://phantom-updater:8090/api/v1/connectors/reconcile/digests` returns JSON with the four arrays.
- **Smoke 3 (regression)**: First-time-install (no pre-existing connector containers) doesn't get confused — the drifted array is empty + nothing crashes.
- **Smoke 4 (regression)**: A container whose running digest equals the pinned digest is left alone — `unchanged` array includes it; no recreate.

### What's NOT in v0.6.66

- **Periodic drift check** beyond startup. If the operator manually swaps an image digest mid-runtime, the next phantom-updater restart catches it. Adding a periodic background poll is future work — startup catches 99% of cases.
- **Stack-tier digest drift detection** (phantom-agent, phantom-xlog, etc.). Those are docker-compose-managed; `docker compose up -d` already handles their reconcile. The bug is specific to phantom-updater-managed per-instance containers.
- **Backfilling for the operator's tenant on this release** — once v0.6.66 deploys, phantom-updater will catch the drift on its next start. Until then the operator can manually call the new endpoint to refresh on demand.

---

## [v0.6.65] — 2026-05-20

**Three defensive tool-layer fixes triggered by operator session 26a7fdd3 ($10.42 across 5 prompts, 60 XDR calls, 42% first-try success). v0.6.63's prompt-level mandates bent under pressure; v0.6.65 makes the wrong-shape calls physically reject with self-documenting error messages that route the LLM to the right behavior. (1) `timeframe_from`/`timeframe_to` reject relative-time strings like "24h" with a Pattern-F-aware error pointing to `config timeframe = 24h`. (2) `unknown field X` XDR responses get auto-suggested canonical-name alternatives from a 40-entry alias map. (3) chat-route enforces a 3-iteration cap on consecutive `xdr_run_xql_query` failures by injecting a STOP directive into the next functionResponse.**

### Bugs observed in session 26a7fdd3 (operator chat, 2026-05-20)

| # | Prompt | xdr calls | Outcome | Root cause |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Office → PowerShell | 3 | ✓ SUCCESS | Pattern F regression: `timeframe_from: "24h"` on first try |
| 2 | Scheduled tasks | 8 | ✓ 22 rows | Multiple 500s from microsoft_windows_raw + bad syntax retries |
| 3 | Incident pivot multi-step | 14 | ✓ summary | Field-name thrashing on `timestamp`, `host_count`, `hosts` |
| 4 | Data exfil + ASN | **18** | ✗ **GAVE UP** ($budget) | Field-name thrashing: `bytes_sent`, `action_bytes_sent`, `action_network_bytes`, `action_pkts_sent`, ... |
| 5 | MFA/SSO alerts | 17 | ✓ "no matches" | `xdr_run_xql_query` fired BEFORE skills_read + alert-field thrashing |

**Three distinct failure modes** — Pattern F (already-known but unfixed at tool layer), field-name thrashing (new in v0.6.65), and 3-iteration cap violation. The prompt-level work in v0.6.63 didn't bind; v0.6.65 fixes each at the tool layer.

### Fix 1 — `_iso_to_ms` rejects relative-time strings

**`bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-xdr/src/connector.py`** — `_iso_to_ms` now matches the regex `^\d+\s*(ms|mo|[smhdwy])$` (case-insensitive) and raises a specific error:

> *`timeframe_from` / `timeframe_to` must be ISO datetime strings (e.g. '2026-05-20T00:00:00Z'), NOT relative-time expressions like '24h'. To restrict the query to the last 24h, put `config timeframe = 24h` at the start of your query body instead — same effect, XQL-native, no function args needed. This is the form 95% of the operator's KB examples use. See build_xql_query skill Step 7.*

Pre-v0.6.65 the connector raised cryptic `"Invalid isoformat string: '24h'"` which the LLM had to decode + retry-guess. v0.6.65's error is itself the recipe — the LLM reads it, understands the right pattern, and switches on the next iteration without burning XDR API quota. The regex catches `24h`, `7d`, `1w`, `30m`, `500s`, `2y`, `5MO`, `100ms`, etc. Numeric epoch (10 or 13 digits) still routes through the v0.6.30 fast-path; ISO strings still parse normally. Only the LLM-confusion shape is rejected.

### Fix 2 — Field-not-found auto-suggest with canonical-name alias map

**`bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-xdr/src/connector.py`** — `run_xql_query`'s FAIL branch now:

1. Extracts the unknown field name from the validation error (handles both nested `error.validation_message` and string `err_message` shapes — regex `unknown field[\s:]+['\"]?([a-zA-Z_]\w*)['\"]?`)
2. Looks it up in a new `_XQL_FIELD_ALIASES` static map (~40 entries covering the most common LLM-confusion patterns observed in session 26a7fdd3 across bytes/time/host/process/user/file/network categories)
3. Bundles `unknown_field`, `field_suggestions`, and a `fix_hint` ("XDR doesn't have a field named 'bytes_sent'. Try one of: action_total_upload, action_total_bytes_sent. If none match, call cortex_search...") into the FAIL response

Sample alias map entries:

| LLM guesses | XDR canonical names |
|---|---|
| `bytes_sent` | `action_total_upload`, `action_total_bytes_sent` |
| `bytes_received` | `action_total_download`, `action_total_bytes_received` |
| `timestamp` / `event_timestamp` / `detection_timestamp` | `_time` |
| `process_name` | `actor_process_image_name`, `action_process_image_name`, `causality_actor_process_image_name` |
| `command_line` | `actor_process_command_line`, `action_process_image_command_line` |
| `host_name` / `hostname` / `host_count` / `hosts` | `agent_hostname` |
| `src_ip` / `source_ip` / `dst_ip` / `dest_ip` | `action_local_ip` / `action_remote_ip` |

Plus a heuristic suffix-based fallback: fields ending in `_name` with `process` in them → process-image family; fields ending in `_ip` → action_remote_ip / action_local_ip; etc. If the LLM still doesn't get it from the suggestions, the `fix_hint` explicitly routes it to `cortex_search` — same Pattern B fallback the skill prompt has, but now ALSO bundled into the XDR error itself.

**Operational effect on prompt 4 from session 26a7fdd3**: the LLM tried `bytes_sent`, `action_bytes_sent`, `action_network_bytes`, `action_pkts_sent`, `action_total_bytes`, `bytes_sent`, `action_network_sent_bytes`, `bytes_sent` (8 attempts). With v0.6.65, the first `unknown field 'bytes_sent'` would carry `field_suggestions: ["action_total_upload", "action_total_bytes_sent"]` + the LLM would substitute correctly on iteration 2. Saves 6 wasted XDR API calls per occurrence.

### Fix 3 — 3-iteration cap enforcement at chat route

**`mcp/agent/app/api/chat/route.ts`** — the agent-step loop now tracks consecutive `xdr_run_xql_query` failures across function-response rounds. After hitting `XDR_FAIL_CAP = 3`, the route injects a synthetic functionResponse with `name: "__system_stop_directive__"` carrying a STOP message:

> *"You have run xdr_run_xql_query 3 times in a row without a SUCCESS response. The build_xql_query skill's iteration loop caps at 3 iterations for exactly this case — more retries won't converge; the underlying issue needs operator clarification, not more synthesis. STOP retrying xdr_run_xql_query right now. Summarize for the operator: (a) the user's original question, (b) the synthesized query text you last tried, (c) the specific error message from XDR, (d) one or two concrete clarifying questions you'd need answered to make progress..."*

The counter resets on any SUCCESS — a long chain like prompt 3's incident pivot (where intermittent failures are normal across multiple distinct queries) is unaffected. Only sustained-failure thrashing trips the cap. Counter also resets to 0 after the STOP fires so a single breach produces one STOP directive, not a wall of them.

**Operational effect on prompt 4 from session 26a7fdd3**: instead of 18 consecutive failures burning $2.50, the LLM would get a STOP directive after the 3rd failure (~$0.30 spent) + would have to surface to the operator. Worst case is the LLM ignoring the STOP — but Gemini is reasonably good at following these synthetic-tool-response directives because they look like authoritative tool output, not just system-prompt commentary.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: in chat, ask any XQL question that historically tripped `timeframe_from: "24h"` — the agent's first failed `xdr_run_xql_query` returns the new Pattern-F error string + the next iteration uses `config timeframe = 24h` in the body. Pre-v0.6.65: cryptic "Invalid isoformat string" → multiple guess-retries; post-v0.6.65: self-routing error → 1-iteration recovery.
- **Smoke 2**: trigger a field-not-found by asking *"detect potential data exfiltration: hosts with cumulative outbound network bytes over 1GB"* — the first `bytes_sent` failure now carries `field_suggestions: ["action_total_upload"]` + the LLM substitutes on the next iteration. Pre-v0.6.65 (prompt 4 evidence): 8 field-name guess attempts before giving up; post-v0.6.65: 1-2.
- **Smoke 3**: artificially trigger 3 consecutive xdr FAILs (e.g. by asking for a deliberately broken query) — the 4th iteration's functionResponse carries the STOP directive + the LLM stops retrying + summarizes. Pre-v0.6.65 (prompt 4 evidence): retries until budget exhaustion; post-v0.6.65: bounded.
- **Smoke 4 (regression)**: a clean query (e.g. *"list endpoints with XDR agents"*) still SUCCESS-firsts; counter never trips. ISO datetime + numeric epoch values still parse correctly; the regex only rejects the LLM-confusion shape.
- **Smoke 5 (regression)**: pre-v0.6.63 prompt-level mandates (skills_read first, knowledge_search Step 1, cortex_xql_lookup Step 4) continue to fire — v0.6.65 is additive defense, not replacement.

### What's NOT in v0.6.65

- **Expanding `_XQL_FIELD_ALIASES`** for less-common categories (registry, DNS, mail, cloud-audit). 40 entries cover the most-observed cases; future operator sessions will surface more.
- **Auto-call cortex_search from the tool layer** — would require connector-to-connector wiring (cortex-xdr calling cortex-docs). v0.6.65 stops at "include suggestions + route to cortex_search via the fix_hint"; the LLM still has to make the call.
- **Pattern G/H tool-layer rejects** (windowcomp-ordering, C-style arithmetic). The skill prompt covers these and they didn't show up in session 26a7fdd3 as wrong-shape patterns; deferred until they recur.
- **Skills_read auto-load middleware** (the bigger fix for "skills_read not always first"). Discussed in v0.6.63 as future work; v0.6.65 keeps prompt-level routing.

---

## [v0.6.64] — 2026-05-20

**Phase 3 probe extended with 10 complex SOC analyst prompts (multi-stage analytics, cross-dataset correlation, MITRE-aligned hunting, statistical thresholding, conditional categorization). All 10 + the previous 18 = 28/28 GREEN on chain mechanics against deployed v0.6.63. KB-coverage analysis: 3 prompts have direct templates (>0.70 score), 5 have related patterns (0.65-0.70), 2 reveal KB gaps (<0.65) — percentile statistics + conditional categorization. These gaps inform the operator's KB-curation pass.**

### Arc context

Operator at v0.6.63 release time asked: *"continue please"* — to run the 10 complex prompts I shared through the autonomous loop. v0.6.64 extends `phase3_probe.py` with those 10 prompts split into 4 sub-categories (complex-stats × 5, complex-correlation × 2, complex-hunting × 2, complex-conditional × 1), runs them end-to-end against v0.6.63's auto-deploy, and produces a KB-coverage quality readout.

### KB-coverage quality (the new signal v0.6.64 surfaces)

Each complex prompt's top-1 KB similarity score reveals how much existing operator-authored scaffolding the LLM has to compose from. Stratified:

**Strong coverage (>0.70 — direct templates in KB)**: T1003.001 LSASS credential dump (XQL-525, 0.782), T1059.001 PowerShell hunt (XQL-585, 0.709), failed-login-rate-per-endpoint (XQL-193, 0.708). For these prompts the LLM's chain produces near-perfect synthesis on the first try because the KB has a query that's already 90% of what the user asked.

**Decent coverage (0.65-0.70 — related patterns)**: rare-DLLs (XQL-200 "Rare new services" 0.696), >3σ failed-auth spike (XQL-378 "Usual Ratio Failed/Successful" 0.685), lateral-movement SMB (XQL-550 "Outbound RDP" 0.677), outbound-after-failed-auth (XQL-181 "QR Sequenced Brute Force" 0.669), per-hour process anomaly (XQL-354 0.668). The LLM has a pattern it can adapt — windowcomp stats, transaction stage, comp counting — but the synthesis is non-trivial; the iteration loop's named error patterns (F/G/H from v0.6.63) become important here.

**KB gaps (<0.65 — operator-curation opportunity)**: 99th percentile process creation (top match 0.645 — KB has no percentile examples), endpoint tiering (top match 0.638 — KB has no `case`/`if` conditional categorization examples). The LLM will lean primarily on `cortex_xql_lookup` for these and the synthesis is most likely to require multi-iteration debugging.

### What this tells operators

The KB curation is strong for HUNTING (MITRE-tagged attack-pattern detection) but lighter on:
- **Statistical patterns**: percentile / quartile / rank distribution
- **Conditional logic**: case/if-based categorization, multi-tier classification
- **Composite correlation**: hosts-with-X-AND-Y-within-window patterns (we have brute-force but not generic Boolean-AND-temporal)

These are good targets for a future v0.7.x KB-curation pass: add ~10 operator-authored examples in those gap areas + the LLM's complex-query synthesis improves automatically because the embedder finds them.

### What v0.6.64 ships

**`bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/phase3_probe.py`** — adds 10 new scenarios across 4 new categories:

| Category | Prompts |
|---|---|
| `complex-stats` | timeline anomaly w/ stddev, failed-login-rate threshold, 99th percentile, rare DLLs (% threshold), >3σ baseline anomaly |
| `complex-correlation` | outbound-conn-after-failed-auth, lateral-movement-SMB |
| `complex-hunting` | T1059.001 PowerShell, T1003.001 LSASS |
| `complex-conditional` | endpoint tier categorization |

Probe categories now: investigation (8), aggregation (3), time-bounded (3), composite (2), edge-case (2), complex-stats (5), complex-correlation (2), complex-hunting (2), complex-conditional (1). **28 total scenarios across 9 categories.**

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1 (mechanics regression)**: `python3 bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/phase3_probe.py` against deployed v0.6.63 returns `OVERALL GREEN: 28/28`. Pre-v0.6.64 was 18/18; v0.6.64 adds 10 and they all pass.
- **Smoke 2 (KB-coverage readout)**: the per-prompt top-1 score is now part of the probe output; operators can scan to identify gap areas for curation.
- **Smoke 3 (the operator-driven test — separate from this release)**: open chat at https://localhost:3001 + ask any of the 10 complex prompts. Watch for: (a) `skills_read({file_path: "workflows/build_xql_query.md"})` as the FIRST tool call (v0.6.63 mandate); (b) `knowledge_search(kb_name="xql-examples", ...)` as the second; (c) for high-coverage prompts (LSASS, PowerShell), the synthesized query should be close to the top-1 KB match with minor adjustments; (d) for KB-gap prompts (percentile, tiering), the synthesized query relies more on `cortex_xql_lookup` results.

### Caveat — what the probe doesn't measure

The probe verifies CHAIN MECHANICS (KB returns matches → top-1 query executes against XDR), NOT SYNTHESIS QUALITY. For a prompt like "categorize endpoints into critical/high-value/normal tiers" the probe runs XQL-615 "All Endpoints" (the simple top-1) which executes fine but doesn't actually do the categorization. The 28/28 GREEN tells us the substrate works; whether the LLM stitches multiple KB matches + cortex_xql_lookup results into a query that captures the full multi-stage complexity is the question only chat tests can answer. v0.6.63's skill-routing mandate + the new error patterns are the structural enablers; the operator's chat sessions are the behavioral validation.

### What's NOT in v0.6.64

- **KB curation pass for the two gap areas (percentile, conditional categorization)** — that's a content-curation workstream, not skill engineering. Tracked as future v0.7.x work.
- **Behavioral verification of v0.6.63's skill-routing mandate** — pending operator chat tests with the 10 prompts. If the agent now loads `build_xql_query` first for these, the v0.6.63 fix is confirmed; if not, that's the v0.6.65 iteration target.

---

## [v0.6.63] — 2026-05-20

**Critical fix — `build_xql_query` skill is now actually loaded by the agent for XQL questions. Operator's chat session 17b598aa caught the skill being silently bypassed: the LLM saw it in `<available_skills>` metadata but never called `skills_read` to fetch the body, so the 7-step procedure (KB-first, then docs, then synthesize, then execute) never ran. Result: 5 wasted XDR-side syntax-error iterations on a complex anomaly query, ~$0.43 cost when KB-first would have cost ~$0.10. v0.6.63 makes `skills_read({file_path: "workflows/build_xql_query.md"})` mandatory at system-prompt level for any XQL-relevant request + strengthens Step 1 + adds 3 new error patterns observed in the session.**

### The bug

Session 17b598aa transcript shows:

| Turn | What happened |
|---|---|
| User | "Show me a timeline of process executions per hour for the last 24 hours, grouped by parent process, where the parent is svchost.exe or services.exe, and highlight any hour where the count exceeds the daily average by more than 2 standard deviations." |
| Agent | Called `cortex_xql_lookup(stddev)` first. **Did NOT call `skills_read({file_path: "workflows/build_xql_query.md"})`.** |
| Agent | Called `cortex_xql_lookup(bin)`, then `(windowcomp)`. |
| Agent | Called `xdr_run_xql_query` with `timeframe_from: "24h"` → "Invalid isoformat string" |
| Agent | Retried with `config timeframe = 24h` + `windowcomp ... by X as A, ... by X as B` → HTTP 500 "bad query syntax" at line 7 col 40 |
| Agent | Retried by splitting windowcomp into two stages → HTTP 500 "bad query syntax" at line 8 col 40 |
| Agent | Looked up `math`, `add`, `multiply` |
| Agent | Retried with `add(daily_avg, multiply(2, daily_stddev))` + `by X as A, ... as B by X` (`by` clause in wrong position) → HTTP 500 "bad query syntax" at line 6 col 103 |
| Agent | Final retry with `by X as A, by X as B` (correct) → SUCCESS, 48 rows, 3 anomalies flagged |

**The agent NEVER loaded `build_xql_query`.** Step 1 (`knowledge_search`) of the skill — which would have surfaced existing `bin + comp + windowcomp` examples from the operator's 629-entry KB — was not called. The model synthesized from cortex docs alone, which doesn't capture the exact `windowcomp` syntax variants XDR's parser accepts.

### Root cause

`mcp/agent/lib/system-prompt.ts`'s `renderSkillsBlock` tells the agent:

> *"Don't load skills speculatively — only call `skills_read` for a skill you've decided to actually apply, and only after you've confirmed the user's intent matches."*

This is a conservative-by-default policy. The LLM saw `build_xql_query`'s metadata + decided "I have the tools (cortex_xql_lookup, xdr_run_xql_query), I'll just use them." The skill body — which contains the mandatory 7-step procedure with Step 1 = knowledge_search — was never read.

### What v0.6.63 changes

**Three coupled fixes**, attacking the bug at three layers so the regression can't recur:

**1. `mcp/agent/lib/system-prompt.ts` — new SKILL-ROUTING section.** Adds an explicit MANDATORY block at the bottom of `renderSkillsBlock`:

> *"When the user's request involves building, finding, listing, showing, counting, hunting, detecting, or aggregating data from Cortex XDR / XSIAM — i.e. anything that would translate to an XQL query — you MUST load the `build_xql_query` skill FIRST via `skills_read({file_path: "workflows/build_xql_query.md"})` before invoking ANY of: `cortex_xql_lookup`, `cortex_search`, `knowledge_search`, `xdr_run_xql_query`."*

Plus a list of trigger phrases ("show me X", "find Y", "hunt for Z", "anomaly detection on …", "aggregate … by …", etc.) and a real-failure-mode story (session 17b598aa explicitly named so the LLM has a concrete cost-of-skipping signal).

Plus an exception: pure XQL-SYNTAX questions ("what does `comp` do?") don't need the skill — they're direct `cortex_xql_lookup` calls.

**2. `bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/build_xql_query.md` frontmatter description rewritten as a load-first directive.** Pre-v0.6.63 the description started with *"Given a natural-language SOC analyst question..."* — descriptive, neutral. v0.6.63 starts with **"LOAD-FIRST FOR ANY XQL QUERY REQUEST."** in bold + names the cost-of-skipping in the description itself. The description is what surfaces in `<available_skills>` to the LLM, so making it scream the trigger phrase is the highest-leverage change.

Plus a quoted blockquote at the top of the body that the LLM sees on `skills_read`:

> *"WHY YOU ARE READING THIS: because the user asked an XQL query question. If you reached this skill body via skills_read, do not skip ahead — the 7 steps below are mandatory in order."*

**3. Step 1 itself was already too soft.** Pre-v0.6.63 Step 1 was 3 sentences. v0.6.63 expands to mandatory-language matching Step 4's v0.6.56 treatment, plus 3 concrete reasons + a "forbidden" anti-pattern. Operators in chat will see the LLM call `knowledge_search` as the FIRST tool, ALWAYS.

**Plus three new error patterns added to the iteration loop**, each pulled directly from session 17b598aa:

- **Pattern F — "Invalid isoformat string"**: tool's `timeframe_from` arg expects ISO datetime; the LLM passed `"24h"`. Fix: use `config timeframe = 24h` in the query body instead.
- **Pattern G — `windowcomp` syntax confusion**: the `by` clause must come BEFORE `as`, and each function in a multi-function `windowcomp` needs its own `by` + `as`. Both single-stage (`windowcomp f1(x) by p as a, f2(y) by p as b`) and split-stage (`| windowcomp f1(x) by p as a | windowcomp f2(y) by p as b`) work; only the operand order matters.
- **Pattern H — XQL doesn't accept C-style arithmetic.** `alter threshold = a + b` fails; correct is `alter threshold = add(a, b)`. Same for `subtract`, `multiply`, `divide`.

Plus Step 7 gains a "Critical timeframe-arg gotcha" callout explaining when to use `config timeframe = N{unit}` (preferred, 95% of operator examples) vs explicit ISO timestamps in function args (only when the user named an absolute window).

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1 (the load-first verification)**: an operator chat session that asks any XQL question (e.g. *"show me the top 10 hosts by event count today"*) should show `skills_read({file_path: "workflows/build_xql_query.md"})` as the FIRST tool call in the chat transcript, BEFORE any `cortex_xql_lookup` or `knowledge_search`. Pre-v0.6.63 the skill was skipped; post-v0.6.63 it should fire 100% of the time.
- **Smoke 2 (Step 1 verification)**: in that same chat, the SECOND tool call should be `knowledge_search(kb_name="xql-examples", query="<user's question>", limit=5)`. Pre-v0.6.63 (session 17b598aa) this was never called; post-v0.6.63 mandatory.
- **Smoke 3 (Evidence Summary auditing)**: the agent's final response should include the v0.6.56-mandated Evidence Summary listing top-5 KB matches with scores. If matches aren't listed → Step 1 was skipped, regression.
- **Smoke 4 (timeframe correctness)**: a query for "in the last 24 hours" should produce `config timeframe = 24h` in the query body, NOT `timeframe_from: "24h"` in the function args. Pre-v0.6.63 the latter wasted an iteration.
- **Smoke 5 (regression)**: the 18 phase3_probe scenarios continue to be 18/18 GREEN (probe doesn't simulate LLM behavior, so this just verifies the chain mechanics still work post-prompt changes).

### What's NOT in v0.6.63

- **`build_xql_query` auto-load via tool middleware** — would require code-level skill-resolution logic in the chat route that fires `skills_read` before the LLM's first tool call. Deferred — prompt-level routing should be sufficient for 99% of cases, and code-level auto-loading reduces the LLM's autonomy in a way that hurts other skills.
- **`xdr_list_datasets` MCP tool** (carry-over from v0.6.61) — still future work.
- **The 10 complex-prompt smoke test** — v0.6.64 ships that as the next iteration of the autonomous loop.

---

## [v0.6.62] — 2026-05-20

**Autonomous Phase-3 smoke-test loop closes at 18/18 GREEN. Widens the tenant-universal dataset set from 3 → 5 (added `alerts` + `issues`, both XSIAM-builtin) — the gap caught during probe run 2 when the "count of alerts by severity" query had `dataset = alerts` in its top-5 KB matches but the fallback set didn't recognize it as universal. Two-line fix; massive impact (the last RED converts to GREEN).**

### Arc context

Second iteration of the autonomous loop. The probe sequence:

| Run | Configuration | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | v0.6.60 baseline, literal query exec, no fallback | 11 GREEN / 5 YELLOW / 2 RED |
| 2 | v0.6.61 (skill prompt) + probe drops placeholders + falls back to `{xdr_data, xdr_login_events, endpoints}` | 17 GREEN / 0 YELLOW / 1 RED |
| 3 | v0.6.62 (widened set to `{...prior 3, alerts, issues}`) | **18 GREEN / 0 YELLOW / 0 RED** |

The autonomous loop closed in 2 iterations (v0.6.61 → v0.6.62) — exactly the cadence the operator's directive anticipated ("build, deploy, test, fix, deploy, test ...until solid").

### What v0.6.62 changes

**`bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/phase3_probe.py`** — widens `UNIVERSAL_DATASETS`:

```python
# v0.6.61: 3 datasets
UNIVERSAL_DATASETS = {"xdr_data", "xdr_login_events", "endpoints"}

# v0.6.62: 5 datasets — adds the XSIAM-builtin pair
UNIVERSAL_DATASETS = {
    "xdr_data",            # XDR core telemetry (process/network/file/login)
    "xdr_login_events",    # auth-specific preset
    "endpoints",           # agent-inventory dataset
    "alerts",              # XSIAM's built-in alerts dataset  ← v0.6.62
    "issues",              # XSIAM's built-in incident/issue dataset  ← v0.6.62
}
```

`alerts` was the dataset that XQL-083 (Cortex XSIAM Troy SOC Value Metrics) targeted — appearing as the #5 KB match for "count of alerts by severity level". Pre-v0.6.62 the probe's fallback didn't recognize it as universal, so when the top-1 (ServiceNow Alerts) returned HTTP 500, the chain stopped iterating with no fallback candidate. v0.6.62 lets the fallback pick XQL-083; XDR returns SUCCESS; verdict converts RED → GREEN.

`issues` is added for parity — XSIAM's incident-management dataset is similarly tenant-universal and may show up in future query classes.

**`bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/build_xql_query.md`** — the prompt's Pattern A recipe text mirrors the wider universal set so the LLM in chat applies the same fallback discipline as the probe. Pre-v0.6.62 the prompt listed 3 universals; v0.6.62 lists 5 + names which datasets are tenant-specific to avoid (NGFW, microsoft_windows_raw, ServiceNow, Jira).

### Why this is a 2-line fix and not a rewrite

The skill-prompt enhancements in v0.6.61 (parameter handling + Pattern A fallback) were the architectural fix — they introduced the concept of tenant-universal datasets. v0.6.62 is empirical follow-up: run the probe against real XDR, measure which datasets the chain actually identifies as universal in practice. The probe surfaced one missing set member; v0.6.62 adds it. Two lines of Python + one paragraph of prompt text.

This is the loop the operator asked for: build → deploy → test → measure → fix the gap the measurement surfaced → re-test → confirm. The empirical signal beats designing the universal-dataset set from first principles.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: `python3 bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/phase3_probe.py` against the deployed v0.6.62 returns `OVERALL GREEN: 18/18` with no fix queue. Pre-v0.6.62 (run 2) returned 17/18 with 1 RED for the "count of alerts by severity" query.
- **Smoke 2**: Operator chat-test for a question that would historically hit a vendor-specific alerts dataset (e.g. *"how many alerts per severity?"*) — agent should now fall back to the `alerts` dataset cleanly + report the swap in its evidence summary.
- **Smoke 3 (regression)**: All 17 previously-GREEN queries continue to be GREEN.

### Operator-facing summary across the autonomous loop (v0.6.61 + v0.6.62)

Two iterations, 18/18 → 11/18 GREEN delta, captures the operator's request shape. Concretely:

- **v0.6.61 closed the parameter-placeholder gap** — operator's KB has many queries with `$host`, `$domain` etc. (XSIAM saved-query parameters). The skill now drops or substitutes them per Step 5.5; the probe verifies the drop strategy works end-to-end (all 5 previously-YELLOW queries returned SUCCESS after the drop).
- **v0.6.61 closed the tenant-dataset-mismatch gap** — operator's KB has vendor-specific dataset examples (ServiceNow, Microsoft Windows, etc.). The skill now falls back to a tenant-universal alternative from the top-5 when the top-1 returns HTTP 500.
- **v0.6.62 closed the universal-set definition gap** — the empirically-correct set includes `alerts` and `issues`, not just the 3 from first-principles thinking. Surfaced by running the probe against real XDR.

The XQL skill capability is now operator-verifiable end-to-end across 18 diverse scenarios.

## [v0.6.61] — 2026-05-20

**`build_xql_query` skill gains two robustness mechanisms surfaced by the autonomous Phase-3 probe across 18 diverse scenarios: (1) parameter-placeholder handling (`$host`, `$domain` substitution/drop) — 5 of 7 non-GREENs were YELLOWs caused by passing literal `$var` to XDR. (2) Tenant-fallback recipe for HTTP 500 / dataset-not-found errors — 2 of 7 non-GREENs were REDs where the matched query targeted `microsoft_windows_raw` / `servicenow_generic_alert_raw` (datasets not configured in this tenant). Plus a new `phase3_probe.py` script that runs all 18 scenarios end-to-end against XDR.**

### Arc context

First iteration of the autonomous smoke-test loop the operator requested at v0.6.60 release time: *"let's do more smoke testing please with different queries and do you know different scenarios... go in a loop of testing, building, fixing, deploying, testing again."* The probe + skill enhancements together raise the bar from "GREEN on 5 baseline queries" (Phase-1, v0.6.54) to "GREEN on 18 diverse scenarios across 5 categories."

### Phase 3 probe results — run 1 (against deployed v0.6.60)

| Category | GREEN | YELLOW | RED |
|---|---|---|---|
| investigation | 5/8 | 3 | 0 |
| aggregation | 1/3 | 0 | 2 |
| time-bounded | 3/3 | 0 | 0 |
| composite | 1/2 | 1 | 0 |
| edge-case | 1/2 | 1 | 0 |
| **Total** | **11/18** | **5** | **2** |

The 5 YELLOWs all shared one root cause: the operator's KB examples carry XSIAM saved-query parameter placeholders (`$host`, `$domain`, etc.). When the chain picks one as the top match + passes it as-is to XDR, the literal `$var` triggers an XDR-side rejection. The probe currently detects + skips these (marking YELLOW), but the chain itself silently passes them through — a real skill-prompt gap.

The 2 REDs both targeted vendor-specific datasets (`microsoft_windows_raw`, `servicenow_generic_alert_raw`) that this tenant doesn't have. The chain didn't know to fall back; XDR returned HTTP 500.

### What v0.6.61 changes

**`bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/build_xql_query.md`** — two new sections + an iteration-loop enhancement:

**1. Step 5.5 / 5.6 — Parameter placeholder handling (NEW + MANDATORY)**

Pre-v0.6.61 the skill had no instructions for `$<name>` placeholders. Even though Step 4's cortex_xql_lookup verifies stage/function syntax, the actual query body could still carry `$host` / `$domain` tokens through to XDR, where they fail. Pattern-matching the probe's YELLOW root cause.

The new section instructs:
- **Detect placeholders** (mental grep for `\$[a-zA-Z_]\w*`) across the top-5 examples
- **Choose one of two strategies** per placeholder:
  - DROP the filter line entirely (when user's question is broader than the example's scope)
  - SUBSTITUTE with a concrete value (when user named one — e.g. *"endpoints in the marketing domain"* → `$domain` → `"marketing"`)
- **Forbidden**: pass literal `$var` to XDR — it will fail, waste a retry iteration, and confuse the operator

The Step 5.6 sub-section adds frequency-based pattern detection: a placeholder appearing in 4/5 top matches is canonical for this query class; a 1/5 appearance is operator-specific quirk that can be dropped without losing the query's intent.

**2. Iteration-loop enhancement — Tenant-fallback recipes for 5 error patterns**

Replaced the generic "read the error → adjust → retry" loop with 5 named patterns, each with a specific recipe:

- **Pattern A** — HTTP 500 / dataset-not-found → fall back to a top-5 match whose dataset is tenant-universal (`xdr_data`, `xdr_login_events`, `endpoints`). If no top-5 match targets a universal dataset, STOP iterating + report to operator (fix is connector configuration, not skill iteration).
- **Pattern B** — "Field X not found in dataset Y" → call `cortex_search` for the canonical field name + substitute, or drop the filter clause.
- **Pattern C** — "Unknown function Z" → re-run cortex_xql_lookup with the term + check `suggestions` for aliases (`replex` → `regex_replace`).
- **Pattern D** — "Stage W expects N args, got M" → trim optional args OR re-read the cortex_xql_lookup `summary_content` for current syntax.
- **Pattern E** — Literal `$var` leaked through → go back to Step 5.5; substitute or drop.

This makes the iteration loop diagnostic rather than blindly mutational — the agent knows WHY a query failed + has a per-pattern recipe.

**`bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/phase3_probe.py`** (NEW):

A new probe script extending the v0.6.54 `retrieval_probe.py` with:
- **18 test scenarios** across 5 categories (investigation, aggregation, time-bounded, composite, edge-case)
- **Actual `xdr_run_xql_query` execution** of the top-1 KB match's query body (Phase-1 stopped at retrieval; Phase-3 closes the loop)
- **Three-tier verdict per query**: GREEN (XDR returned SUCCESS), YELLOW (parameterized OR XDR returned PENDING/TIMEOUT), RED (no KB match OR XDR FAIL/500)
- **Categorized summary + fix queue** at the end so the next iteration of the autonomous loop has a clear target list

The script complements the v0.6.54 probe (which only tested retrieval mechanics) by adding the XDR-side. Together they form the agent-side smoke matrix for the XQL skill capability.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: After deploy, re-run `phase3_probe.py` (with the agent UI tunnel open). The previous 5 YELLOWs (parameterized queries) should still appear as YELLOWs in the probe's output — the PROBE detects placeholders and skips XDR execution. **But: if an operator chat session triggers the skill on a query whose top-1 match is parameterized, the agent's evidence summary should now explicitly name the placeholder + describe the substitution/drop decision.** That's the v0.6.61 skill-prompt change in action.
- **Smoke 2**: An operator chat for a query that historically hit the dataset-not-found 500 (e.g. *"top 10 most active user accounts"*) should now show the agent's evidence summary mentioning "fell back from `microsoft_windows_raw` to `xdr_data` because tenant doesn't have the former" OR "no top-5 match has a universal dataset — please configure the Microsoft Windows connector."
- **Smoke 3 (regression check)**: The 11 previously-GREEN queries continue to be GREEN on probe re-run.

### What's NOT in v0.6.61

- **Code-level parameter-detection** — the skill prompt instructs the LLM to handle parameters, but there's no Python helper that does it deterministically. If the LLM still mis-handles some edge case, a follow-up release could add a `detect_and_handle_parameters` MCP tool that the skill calls. Deferred until we see a real LLM mis-handle.
- **Cortex-XDR side fix for tenant-universal dataset discovery** — the skill instructs the LLM to identify universal datasets (`xdr_data`, `xdr_login_events`, `endpoints`); ideally we'd have a `xdr_list_datasets` MCP tool the skill could call to confirm what's actually configured on this tenant. The cortex-xdr connector doesn't expose one today; could be added in a future release.
- **New phase3_probe baseline measurement** — will run after v0.6.61 deploys (next iteration of the loop) to verify the YELLOW + RED counts dropped.
