## [v0.6.60] — 2026-05-20

**Unified event export (schema_version 2). Session "Export Events" now includes both persisted messages AND chat_* audit-log events (cache_hit, turn_cost, compaction_*, context_warning, plan_proposed, subagent_*) — no more dual-button confusion from v0.6.57. Closes the third and final operator-feedback carry-over from v0.6.55. Discovered (delightfully) that the chat route already persists most wire events to audit_log via `safeAudit` — they just weren't surfaced in the export. The fix was a server-side merge, not the bigger refactor I feared.**

### Arc context

Closes the last carry-over item from v0.6.58: unified event export. Operator at v0.6.58 release time: *"Unified event export and XQL syntax highlighting inside KB code blocks also presenting MD data in the chat session nicely incase the model returned md data."* — item 1 of three. (Items 2 + 3 shipped in v0.6.59.) Combined with v0.6.56-59, this closes the operator's full first-chat-test feedback batch from v0.6.55.

### What v0.6.60 changes

**`bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/cognitive.py`** — `events` format export route:

Pre-v0.6.60 the export derived events ONLY from persisted messages — user_message, assistant_text, tool_call, tool_result. Streaming SSE events like `cache_hit` and `turn_cost` weren't in the export, even though they ARE persisted server-side via the chat route's `safeAudit('chat_cache_hit', ...)` and `safeAudit('chat_turn_cost', ...)` calls. The audit log was a forgotten source of forensic data.

v0.6.60 queries the audit log scoped to `target=session:<sid>` and merges any rows matching the chat_* action prefix into the events timeline. Action names are mapped to friendly wire-event types (`chat_turn_cost` → `turn_cost`, `chat_cache_hit` → `cache_hit`, etc.) so the export shape matches what the live-telemetry panel renders. Schema version bumps to **2** to signal the new shape; old consumers that pinned to schema_version=1 see the bump and can re-validate.

The set of audit events now surfaced in the export:

| Audit action | Exported as `type` |
|---|---|
| `chat_turn_cost` | `turn_cost` |
| `chat_cache_hit` | `cache_hit` |
| `chat_compaction_start` | `compaction_start` |
| `chat_compaction_end` | `compaction_end` |
| `chat_compaction_failed` | `compaction_failed` |
| `chat_context_warning` | `context_warning` |
| `chat_plan_proposed` | `plan_proposed` |
| `chat_plan_failed` | `plan_failed` |
| `chat_subagent_started` | `subagent_started` |
| `chat_subagent_completed` | `subagent_completed` |

Each exported event carries the original audit row's `metadata`, `status`, and `duration_ms` — so the operator can post-process for per-session cost (`sum(turn_cost.metadata.cost_usd)`), cache-hit rate (`count(cache_hit) / count(turn_cost)`), etc.

Events are sorted by `ts` ascending after merge so the timeline interleaves naturally — turn_cost rows appear right after the assistant_text they belong to.

The export's `note` field is updated to reflect the new shape; the only intentional exclusion is `text_delta` (per-token deltas would 100× the row count per turn, and the operator cares about the streamed-text outcome, not the per-token cadence).

**`mcp/agent/components/chat/session-sidebar.tsx`** — Export Events tooltip:
- Pre-v0.6.60: said streaming events were NOT included.
- v0.6.60: says the export is unified — both persisted messages AND chat_* audit events. Only text_delta is excluded. Names this as the post-hoc-friendly path.

**`mcp/agent/components/chat/debug-panel.tsx`** — live telemetry export tooltip:
- Pre-v0.6.60: positioned as covering all streaming events the session export missed.
- v0.6.60: explicitly narrows the scope to "per-token streaming forensics" since the session export now covers cache_hit / turn_cost / compaction events. Recommends preferring the session export "for everything else."

### Why the live-telemetry export button stays

Initial v0.6.57 plan was to remove it entirely (operator's original ask: *"let's remove that other button, it's not needed"*). But the live-telemetry export still captures `text_delta` per-token cadence + the SSE-only events `model` + `meta` + `done` which we don't audit. For 95% of use cases the session export is now sufficient; the 5% case (streaming-forensics debugging) needs the live panel. Keeping the button + sharpening its tooltip beats removing it and finding out later we lost a useful capability.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: Export a session that ran ≥1 chat turn → JSON includes events with `type: "turn_cost"` carrying `metadata.cost_usd`, `metadata.input_tokens`, etc. + `events_derived_count` + `messages_derived_count` fields in the payload. Pre-v0.6.60: no turn_cost events at all.
- **Smoke 2**: Export a session that triggered a compaction → JSON includes `type: "compaction_start"` followed by `type: "compaction_end"` with `metadata.checkpoint_length` and similar. Pre-v0.6.60: not in export; only visible in /observability/events.
- **Smoke 3**: Export a session where the agent fired a sub-agent → JSON includes `subagent_started` + `subagent_completed` events. Pre-v0.6.60: missing.
- **Smoke 4**: Tooltip on "Export Events" in the session sidebar reads the unified language; tooltip on live-telemetry export button narrows the scope to per-token forensics.
- **Smoke 5 (regression)**: A session with zero chat_* audit rows (e.g. a fresh session with one user message and no model response yet) exports correctly with `audit_derived_count: 0` and just the messages-derived events. No crash on empty audit query.
- **Smoke 6 (schema check)**: New `schema_version: 2` field is present in the export payload. Old consumers parsing v1 should ignore unknown fields gracefully; ones that pinned on `schema_version === 1` will see the bump and can re-validate their parsers.

### Arc closure — all operator-feedback items from v0.6.55 chat test now landed

| Feedback | Release |
|---|---|
| Agent skipped cortex_xql_lookup in chat | v0.6.56 |
| KB search UI text "brute-force cosine" was misleading | v0.6.56 |
| Wire events panel: turn_cost + text_delta had no icon/color | v0.6.57 |
| Wire events panel: hover-truncation on long previews | v0.6.57 |
| Two export buttons — confusing | v0.6.57 (clarified) + v0.6.60 (unified) |
| KB pages need /skills-style visual polish | v0.6.58 |
| Dev-facing notes on KB pages | v0.6.58 |
| KB entry all-white-text — needs color | v0.6.58 |
| XQL syntax highlighting in KB code blocks | v0.6.59 |
| Chat assistant markdown rendering (model returns MD) | v0.6.59 |
| Unified event export | **v0.6.60 (this release)** |

11 feedback items, 5 releases (v0.6.56-v0.6.60), zero deferred. Pending only: the operator's next behavioral chat test to verify v0.6.56's mandatory `cortex_xql_lookup` step actually fires in practice.

## [v0.6.59] — 2026-05-20

**Shared markdown renderer + SQL/XQL syntax highlighting wired everywhere — KB entry drawer, chat assistant message bubbles. Operator-returned markdown (headings, code blocks, lists, tables) now renders properly in chat instead of raw `whitespace-pre-wrap` text. SQL/XQL keywords get color tinting in code blocks across both surfaces. Fourth post-v0.6.55 iteration on operator feedback.**

### Arc context

Closes two carry-over items from v0.6.58: (1) syntax highlighting in KB code blocks, and (2) markdown rendering in chat. Operator at v0.6.58 release time: *"Unified event export and XQL syntax highlighting inside KB code blocks also presenting MD data in the chat session nicely incase the model returned md data."* — items 2 + 3 of three. The unified-export refactor (item 1) ships separately as v0.6.60.

### What v0.6.59 ships

**New shared component**: [`mcp/agent/components/markdown-content.tsx`](mcp/agent/components/markdown-content.tsx) — the single source of truth for markdown rendering across the agent UI. Two callers today (KB drawer + chat); future surfaces (jobs descriptions, hooks, agent definitions) can adopt it incrementally.

Component contract:
- All v0.6.58 ReactMarkdown overrides centralized (h1/h2/h3, p, strong/em, lists, links, hr, blockquote, tables, inline code, fenced code).
- Fenced code blocks get **syntax highlighting** via `react-syntax-highlighter`'s `PrismLight` bundle. The light bundle only loads the languages we explicitly import (no full Prism — keeps the JS payload small). v0.6.59 ships: `sql`, `xql` (alias to sql — XQL is a SQL dialect), `python` + `py`, `bash` + `sh` + `shell`, `json`, `typescript` + `ts`, `javascript` + `js`. Theme: `vscDarkPlus` with phantom's surface-container-lowest as the code background.
- Inline `` `code` `` tokens get the tertiary-tint pill from v0.6.58 (function/field names visible in prose).
- Optional `compact` prop tightens vertical rhythm for dense chat bubbles vs. spacious KB drawers. KB calls without `compact`; chat calls with `compact`.

**`mcp/agent/app/knowledge/[name]/page.tsx`** — KB entry drawer:
- Pre-v0.6.59: inline ReactMarkdown overrides from v0.6.58 (100+ lines, no syntax highlighting on code blocks — primary-tint background only).
- v0.6.59: collapses to `<MarkdownContent>{activeDoc.content}</MarkdownContent>`. SQL code blocks now have keyword highlighting; the visual depth jump matches what operators expect from a "modern, beautiful" UI (their words at v0.6.55 release time).

**`mcp/agent/components/chat/message-list.tsx`** — assistant message bubble:
- Pre-v0.6.59: the answer portion of an assistant message rendered as `<span className="whitespace-pre-wrap break-words">{answer}{cursor}</span>` — model-returned markdown showed as raw `# Heading` literal text, `` `code` `` as literal backticks, ```` ```sql ```` blocks as raw fenced text. The v0.6.55 chat-test transcript showed the model returning a markdown table for the endpoint query result — that table rendered as pipe-separated raw text instead of an actual table.
- v0.6.59: renders through `<MarkdownContent compact>{answer}</MarkdownContent>`. The blinking-cursor positioning is preserved (the cursor is a sibling of the markdown content, not inside it). During streaming, partial markdown (mid-fence ```sql / no closing ```) renders gracefully — react-markdown treats unclosed fences as plain text until the closing arrives.

### Why this matters concretely

Three operator-facing wins:

1. **The XQL query the agent synthesizes is now visually distinct from the surrounding narration.** Pre-v0.6.59 the model would say *"Here is the query: ```sql dataset = endpoints | filter ... ```"* and the operator saw raw text indistinguishable from regular prose. v0.6.59 shows the SQL block on a dark code-background with keywords (`dataset`, `filter`, `fields`, `comp`, `sort`, `limit`) in their semantic colors.
2. **Tool result tables render as actual tables.** When the agent reports query results like the endpoint-list output from the v0.6.55 chat test (`| Hostname | IP Address | OS Type | Last Seen |`), the operator now sees a proper rendered table with borders + headings instead of pipe characters in a paragraph.
3. **Bulleted lists and section headings give the response visual structure.** The model's typical reasoning structure (*"### What I'll do:" → "1. Search KB... 2. Look up syntax... 3. Execute..."*) was lost in pre-v0.6.59's flat-text rendering; v0.6.59 surfaces it.

### Dependency added

- `react-syntax-highlighter` ^16.1.1 (+ `@types/react-syntax-highlighter` ^15.5.13). The package was not previously in the codebase. Using the Prism light variant + manual language registration keeps the runtime bundle increase to ~80KB gzipped — acceptable cost for the rendering quality jump across two operator-facing surfaces.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: Open `/knowledge/xql-examples` → click any entry → drawer's SQL code block has keyword color tinting (e.g. `dataset` and `filter` in tinted color). Pre-v0.6.59: code block had a primary-tint background but all characters were the same color.
- **Smoke 2 (the chat acceptance test)**: ask the agent a question that produces a markdown response with a table or code block (e.g. *"list endpoints with XDR agents deployed"*) → answer renders with proper table borders + code block syntax highlighting. Pre-v0.6.59 (v0.6.55 chat-test transcript): same query rendered the table as `|` separated raw text.
- **Smoke 3 (streaming)**: during a chat answer stream, the partial markdown renders gracefully. A mid-fence code block (``````sql\ndataset = ...`` with no closing fence yet) shows as plain text until the closing ``` arrives, then re-renders as a highlighted block. No flickering or broken HTML.
- **Smoke 4 (regression — non-markdown answers)**: an assistant message that's just plain prose ("OK, querying XDR now...") renders identically pre/post-v0.6.59. ReactMarkdown passes plain text through as paragraph elements.
- **Smoke 5 (cursor)**: the blinking-cursor on the last (streaming) assistant message still trails the answer text. Position preserved by keeping the cursor as a `<div>`-level sibling of the `<MarkdownContent>` rather than inside it.

### What's NOT in v0.6.59

- **Unified event export** (the third operator item) — v0.6.60. Bigger refactor: server-side persistence of streaming events (text_delta, cache_hit, turn_cost, model) so the session-events export captures them too. Closes the dual-button friction from v0.6.57.
- **Syntax highlighting for languages beyond the 7 registered** (sql/xql, python, bash, json, typescript). Adding more is a one-line import per language; we registered only what the chat + KB realistically produce today.
- **Behavioral re-verification of v0.6.56's mandatory `cortex_xql_lookup` step** — pending operator's next chat-test.

## [v0.6.58] — 2026-05-20

**Knowledge base pages get a visual lift to match the polish operators expect from /skills. Entry detail drawer renders markdown properly (headings, code blocks with primary-tint backgrounds, inline code in tertiary tint) instead of the all-monochrome `<pre>` block. Dev-facing notes ("Tier-3 runtime CRUD", "spec patch pending", "boot loader source-hash change detection") replaced with operator-language one-liners. Third post-v0.6.55 iteration on operator feedback.**

### Arc context

Final UI iteration on the operator's first-chat-test feedback batch. Operator: *"The knowledge base page... needs some, like the skills page — it looks amazing, modern, beautiful. Knowledge base is just a text."* The page already had glass panels + card grid + icons, but the entry detail drawer was a `<pre>` block and the footer + tab tooltips read like internal spec documentation.

### What v0.6.58 changes

**`mcp/agent/app/knowledge/page.tsx`** — KB index page:

- **Subtitle**: pre-v0.6.58 mentioned "schema-validated" + "edit under `bundles/spark/kbs/`" — read as developer documentation. v0.6.58 says *"Curated reference content the agent uses to ground its responses. Semantically searchable via Gemini text-embedding-004."* — operator-language, names the user-visible benefit + the underlying tech without jargon.
- **Footer note**: pre-v0.6.58 had a 4-line paragraph about "Tier-3 runtime CRUD ... spec patch pending ... source-hash change detection skips re-embedding unchanged files" — pure internal documentation. v0.6.58 replaces with a single line with a lightbulb icon: *"To add or edit entries, modify the markdown files under `bundles/spark/kbs/` and redeploy. Changes are picked up automatically."*

**`mcp/agent/app/knowledge/[name]/page.tsx`** — KB detail page:

- **Header subtitle**: pre-v0.6.58 said *"embedded via vertex / text-embedding-004 (or TextHash fallback)"* — surfaced an implementation fallback the operator doesn't need to think about. v0.6.58 says *"embedded with text-embedding-004"* — single source of truth, no fallback noise.
- **Disabled-tab tooltips** (Import / Settings): pre-v0.6.58 said *"Tier-3 runtime CRUD — not yet implemented (spec patch pending)"* — referenced internal phase tracking. v0.6.58 says *"Not yet available — to add or change entries, edit the markdown files under bundles/spark/kbs/ and redeploy."* — same information, operator-actionable.
- **Entry detail drawer**: pre-v0.6.58 rendered the markdown body as a `<pre className="whitespace-pre-wrap text-on-surface font-mono">` — all-white monospace text, no visual structure (operator's *"all white color text"* complaint). v0.6.58 wires `ReactMarkdown` (already in package.json since project start, just never imported) with custom component overrides:
  - `# H1` → bordered headline at the top
  - `## H2` → primary-tint uppercase section labels (e.g. "WHEN TO USE", "VARIATIONS", "SOURCE")
  - `### H3` → secondary-tint sub-headings
  - **Code blocks** (` ```sql ... ``` `) → primary-tint background with border (the SQL query body now stands out from the surrounding prose)
  - **Inline code** (`` `comp` ``, `` `xdr_data` ``) → tertiary-tint pill — function and field names pop visually
  - **Lists** (`-` / `1.`) → proper bullets/numbers with indent
  - **Links** → primary-color, open in new tab
  - **`<hr>`** → subtle divider

### Why this matters beyond aesthetics

The v0.6.55 chat-test transcript showed the operator inspecting individual KB entries to verify the skill's reasoning. With the pre-v0.6.58 detail drawer, the SQL query body was visually indistinguishable from the surrounding prose (`## When to use`, `## Source`) — both rendered as identical monospace text. Operators scanning to find "what does this query actually do?" had to read paragraph-form text to spot the code block. v0.6.58's code-block tint + section-heading coloring makes the scanning task O(1) — eyes go directly to the SQL block.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: Open `/knowledge` → confirm subtitle is operator-language ("the agent uses to ground its responses") not dev-language ("schema-validated"). Confirm footer is one-line with lightbulb icon, not the 4-line spec-patch paragraph.
- **Smoke 2**: Open `/knowledge/xql-examples` → header subtitle reads "embedded with text-embedding-004" (no "vertex / ... or TextHash fallback").
- **Smoke 3**: Click any entry → drawer opens with `## When to use` rendered as a primary-tint uppercase heading; the ` ```sql` block has a primary-tint backdrop; inline `` `comp` `` tokens are tertiary-tint pills. Pre-v0.6.58: all rendered as identical monospace white text.
- **Smoke 4 (regression)**: The KB search still works + result cards still render correctly. The category filter chips still work. No layout regressions on narrow viewport.

### What's NOT in v0.6.58

- **`/api/skills`-style icon/category visual grid for KBs** — the index page already has the grid, just with subtler badges. Operator may push for further visual lift after seeing v0.6.58; if so, v0.6.59 work.
- **Syntax highlighting for the SQL code block** — would need a syntax library (highlight.js / prismjs / shiki). The primary-tint backdrop differentiates the block visually but keywords are not highlighted within. Deferred — easy to layer on top of the markdown renderer if the operator wants it.
- **Unified event export** (carried over from v0.6.57 deferral) — pending operator feedback on whether dual-button-with-clear-tooltips is acceptable.
- **`build_xql_query` skill behavioral re-verification** (v0.6.56's Step 4 enforcement) — pending operator's next chat-test.

## [v0.6.57] — 2026-05-20

**Wire events panel: `turn_cost` + `text_delta` now have consistent icon/color styling, hover-truncation fixed so long previews wrap inline, and both export buttons have honest tooltips explaining what each captures (the two are NOT duplicates — live-telemetry has streaming events, session-events has persisted database state). Second post-v0.6.55 iteration on operator feedback.**

### Arc context

Mid-arc follow-up on UI gaps the operator caught while doing the v0.6.55 chat-test. The XQL skill capability arc continues — v0.6.57's changes don't touch the skill itself, but they make the operator's telemetry-debugging experience consistent (which matters because debugging the skill's behavior is exactly what the operator was doing when they found these issues).

### What v0.6.57 changes

**`mcp/agent/components/chat/debug-panel.tsx`** — three coupled fixes:

1. **Icon + color consistency for `turn_cost`**:
   - Pre-v0.6.57: `turn_cost` had no entry in `eventTypeClass` (defaulted to neutral grey `bg-white/5`) and no entry in `eventTypeIcon` (returned null). Visually inconsistent vs `cache_hit` (`bolt` icon + tertiary tint) and `tool_call` (`build` icon + primary tint).
   - v0.6.57: `turn_cost` gets the `payments` Material Symbols icon + tertiary tint (sharing the "wallet-relevant cost-signal" lane with `cache_hit`). Now scannable.

2. **`text_delta` intentional decision** — left without an icon, but documented:
   - Comment added: `text_delta` streams many-per-second; an icon would visually saturate the column. The neutral pill styling above is sufficient — the type-name in the pill carries the meaning.
   - Pre-v0.6.57 this was implicit (just no `case` in the switch); now the intent is in a comment so future maintainers don't "fix" it.

3. **Hover-truncation fix on wire-event rows**:
   - Pre-v0.6.57: long `preview` strings were `truncate`-clipped with an HTML `title` attribute as the fallback. Browser-native `title` tooltips are slow, OS-style, and themselves truncate on long content — exactly the failure mode the operator hit ("when I hover, it's truncated").
   - v0.6.57: replaces the HTML `title` with a CSS-only inline expansion. The row clips by default (preserves the dense scannable list) but switches to `overflow-visible whitespace-pre-wrap break-all` on hover so the full preview wraps inline. `group` + `group-hover` on the parent `li` controls the child span's expansion. A subtle hover-bg lifts the row above siblings so the wrap doesn't collide.

**Tooltip clarity on both export buttons** — operator's feedback was *"I don't know if it's the same thing or different... let's remove that other button"* — the two buttons capture DIFFERENT data, so the right fix is clarifying tooltips, not removing one:

- **Live telemetry panel export** (`debug-panel.tsx`): old tooltip was 1 sentence. New tooltip: *"Export THIS panel's live in-memory state (JSON) — includes streaming events (text_delta cadence, cache_hit, turn_cost, model) that aren't persisted server-side. Distinct from the session sidebar's 'Export events' which exports persisted user/tool/assistant messages from the database. Use this one when you need the streaming wire trace; use the session export for the post-hoc forensic shape."*
- **Session sidebar "Export Events"** (`session-sidebar.tsx`): old tooltip mentioned the exclusion but didn't say what to do instead. New tooltip: *"Export PERSISTED events (JSON)... Streaming-only events (text_delta cadence, cache_hit, turn_cost, model) are NOT included because they aren't persisted server-side. For those, open the live telemetry panel during the run and use its export button."*

The honest framing: persisted vs streaming is a fundamental architectural distinction. The right long-term fix is to persist streaming events server-side (so a single export captures both), but that's a bigger refactor than v0.6.57's scope. v0.6.57 makes the distinction discoverable through honest tooltips; v0.6.58+ can land the unified-export refactor if the dual-button approach proves friction-laden in operator testing.

### What's NOT in v0.6.57

- **Unified event export** (single export that includes both persisted and streaming events) — a real refactor; requires either server-side persistence of streaming events (new audit_log column or new wire_events table) OR client-side merge at export time. Deferred to v0.6.58+ pending operator feedback on whether the dual-button approach is acceptable.
- **Knowledge base UI redesign** — v0.6.58.
- **`build_xql_query` skill behavioral re-verification** (does v0.6.56's Step 4 enforcement actually cause `cortex_xql_lookup` to fire in chat?) — pending operator's next chat-test after v0.6.56 + v0.6.57 deploy.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: Live telemetry panel during a chat run → `turn_cost` events show with the `payments` icon + tertiary tint pill. Pre-v0.6.57: grey neutral pill with no icon.
- **Smoke 2**: Hover any wire-event row with a long preview string → row expands inline with the full preview wrapping, no truncation. Pre-v0.6.57: OS tooltip truncates anyway.
- **Smoke 3**: Hover the export buttons → tooltips are full sentences explaining what each captures (vs old one-liner that confused the operator).
- **Smoke 4 (regression)**: text_delta events still appear without an icon (intentional — high frequency would saturate the column).

## [v0.6.56] — 2026-05-20

**`build_xql_query` skill enforces Step 4 (`cortex_xql_lookup`) as mandatory after operator's first chat test caught the LLM skipping it for trivial queries. Plus: KB search UI text now accurately describes the embedding model (was misleadingly labeled "brute-force cosine"). First mid-arc fix after v0.6.55 customer release — landed within 30 min of the operator's chat-test feedback.**

### Arc context

Post-tag iteration on the XQL skill capability. v0.6.55 shipped + the operator drove the first behavioral test (session `4edfe87f`). The chat showed:

- ✓ Agent correctly selected `build_xql_query` for "list endpoints with XDR agents deployed"
- ✓ Called `knowledge_search` (top-1 match: `XQL-615 All Endpoints`, score 0.703)
- ✓ Called `xdr_run_xql_query` — returned `status: SUCCESS`, 5 rows
- ✗ **Did NOT call `cortex_xql_lookup`** for any stage or function — Step 4 was entirely skipped

The synthesized query was correct for this simple case (one stage, no functions), but the operator flagged the broader concern: a skill that skips its documentation-lookup step works only when the KB example is an exact template. For complex queries (combining stages in new ways, using functions the LLM half-remembers from training data, hitting tenant-specific syntax quirks), the lookup step is the difference between "syntax error → wasted API call" and "verified syntax → first-try success."

### What v0.6.56 changes

**`bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/build_xql_query.md`** — Step 4 rewritten as MANDATORY, not optional:

- Pre-v0.6.56 phrasing: *"For each unique stage in `stages_used` (top 5 by frequency is plenty — anything below that is noise)"* — the LLM read this as "look up if you feel like it"
- v0.6.56 phrasing: *"This step is NOT OPTIONAL, even when the top-1 KB match looks like an exact template for the user's question"*
- **Explicit minimum lookup contract**: at least all stages with frequency ≥ 2/5 AND the top 3 most-frequent functions. For a typical query: 5-9 cortex_xql_lookup calls before synthesis.
- **Audit-trail requirement**: the output's Evidence Summary section MUST list the cortex_xql_lookup calls + outcomes ("Looked up `filter` (HIT — 'filter narrows results'), `comp` (HIT — 'aggregation stage with `as` and `by`'), ... 6/6 lookups hit"). A summary that names KB matches but doesn't list lookups means Step 4 was skipped.
- **Three concrete failure-mode justifications baked into the prompt**: stage-syntax drift between XDR versions, function-name alias mismatches (`lowercase` vs `lower`), arity errors when combining stages in new ways. The LLM now has a "why this matters" story to ground the discipline.

**`mcp/agent/app/knowledge/[name]/page.tsx`** — KB search results bar:

- Pre-v0.6.56: *"N result(s) in Xms · brute-force cosine over the SqliteKnowledgeBase"*
- v0.6.56: *"N result(s) in Xms · query embedded via text-embedding-004 · cosine similarity ranked across all entries"*

The pre-v0.6.56 phrasing was technically correct (no ANN index → all-pairs scan) but misleading for operators reading it as "no embedding model involved, just SQL LIKE matching." The Phase 1 probe (v0.6.54) confirmed embedding is used; the UI text now matches reality.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: After deploy + agent restart, run the same 5 Phase 1 baseline queries via chat → confirm each chat transcript shows at least 3-5 `cortex_xql_lookup` tool calls before the `xdr_run_xql_query` call. The operator's session `4edfe87f` showed 0 lookups; post-v0.6.56 should show ≥3 per query.
- **Smoke 2**: Open the knowledge base search page → run a query → confirm results bar reads "query embedded via text-embedding-004 · cosine similarity ranked" not "brute-force cosine over the SqliteKnowledgeBase".
- **Smoke 3 (regression)**: The 5 baseline queries continue to find their expected top-1 KB match (the change is purely additive — adds lookups, doesn't change KB ranking).
- **Smoke 4**: A trivial query like *"list all endpoints"* should still produce a working synthesized query — Step 4 enforcement makes the chain longer but doesn't break the simple case.

### What's NOT in v0.6.56

- **Event export completeness** — operator's "some events missing" feedback (text_delta, cache_hit, turn_cost not in events.json export) is v0.6.57. Bigger refactor: server-side persistence of streaming events vs. unified client+server export.
- **Wire events UI consistency** — hover-truncation fix, missing icons/colors for turn_cost + text_delta, duplicate export-button removal — all v0.6.57.
- **Knowledge base UI redesign** — match /skills page styling, beautify entry view, remove debugging notes — v0.6.58.

These are tracked as discrete tasks; iteration continues per the autonomous-iteration directive.

---

## [v0.6.55] — 2026-05-20

**`build_xql_query` skill shipped — Phase 2 of the XQL skill capability arc. Operators can now ask natural-language SOC analyst questions and get a working Cortex XQL query in return, with full chain-of-evidence (top-5 KB matches, stage/function lookups, dataset selection rationale) + automatic execution + 3-iteration retry loop on FAIL responses. No new code — pure prompt engineering against the v0.6.51-v0.6.54 retrieval primitives.**

### Arc context

Mid-arc release for the XQL skill capability (v0.6.51 → v0.6.52 → v0.6.53 → v0.6.54 → **v0.6.55** → arc completion at v0.6.5N). NOT a customer tag candidate per the v0.6.53 use-case-completion gate. Phase 3 (the build + deploy + test + fix iteration loop verifying the skill works end-to-end against the operator's natural-language requests) closes the arc.

### What v0.6.55 ships

A single new file: **[`bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/build_xql_query.md`](bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/build_xql_query.md)**. The skill orchestrates the four authoritative knowledge sources verified in Phase 1:

1. **`knowledge_search`** (built-in) — embeds the user's question via `text-embedding-004`, returns top-5 KB matches from the 629-entry xql-examples corpus.
2. **`cortex_xql_lookup`** (cortex-docs connector) — looks up XQL stage + function syntax in public Cortex documentation.
3. **`cortex_search`** (cortex-docs connector) — optional dataset field-schema lookup when the user references specific field names.
4. **`xdr_run_xql_query`** (cortex-xdr connector) — executes the synthesized query; `status: "SUCCESS"` = working (empty results OK), `status: "FAIL"` = needs iteration with the error message as guidance.

The skill follows a 7-step procedure: KB search → fetch top-5 bodies → extract stages/functions/dataset → look up syntax → (optional) verify field names → synthesize → execute + retry up to 3 times. The output is a three-part response: synthesized query, evidence summary (top-5 matches + lookup hit rate), execution result.

### Design decisions baked into the skill prompt

These come directly from the Phase 1 findings in v0.6.54:

- **Use ALL top-5 examples, not just top-1.** Phase 1 measured the top-5 score spread as narrow (0.60-0.73 across queries, often within 0.10 of top-1). The lower-ranked entries carry comparable signal.
- **Ground in `summary_content`, not just `title`.** `cortex_xql_lookup` occasionally returns topic-page hits when an XQL term shares a substring with an unrelated documentation topic (`set` → "Set up incident scoring" is NOT the XQL `set` function; the summary makes the distinction clear).
- **"Working" = `status: SUCCESS`** per the operator's brief — empty results (`total_rows: 0`) STILL count as working. Only `FAIL`/`FAILED`/`CANCELLED`/`TIMEOUT` indicate the query needs fixing.
- **3-iteration retry cap.** Identical errors after iteration 2 mean the user's question needs clarification, not more synthesis attempts. Hand back to the operator after 3 with the query + last error + suggested next investigation.
- **Same-dataset signal.** When 4/5 or 5/5 top matches share a dataset, use it directly. When datasets diverge, either pick by reading the `## When to use` text or ask the operator to clarify.

### No code changes

This release is pure prompt engineering. Every tool the skill calls already exists:

- `knowledge_search` — already an MCP built-in
- `cortex_xql_lookup` / `cortex_search` — already in cortex-docs connector v0.3.2
- `xdr_run_xql_query` / `xdr_get_xql_results` — already in cortex-xdr connector

The skill MD file gets bundled into `phantom-agent`'s image via the existing `COPY bundles/spark/mcp/skills /app/mcp/skills-default` line in the Dockerfile. The per-release skills marker convention (v0.3.2+) auto-merges this new skill into the running volume on first boot after upgrade. No manual operator action required.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: After deploy + agent restart, `https://localhost:3001/skills` (UI route) lists `build_xql_query` under the "workflows" category with the displayName "Build a working Cortex XQL query from natural language (Cortex XDR / XSIAM)".
- **Smoke 2**: `GET /api/skills` returns the new entry in the JSON skills list with all frontmatter fields populated.
- **Smoke 3 (end-to-end — Phase 3 entry point)**: An operator chat session typing *"show me hosts with unusual outbound network traffic"* should cause the agent to load this skill, walk through the 7-step procedure visibly in the chat (tool calls visible), and return a synthesized query + execution result. **This is the arc-completion smoke** — when this works end-to-end against the deployed install across the 5 Phase 1 baseline queries, the arc is complete and a customer tag becomes appropriate.
- **Smoke 4 (regression)**: The other 4 platform skills (`run_phishing_kill_chain`, `xdr_verify_simulation_telemetry`, plus the foundation/scenarios/validation categories) remain listed + functional.

### What's NOT in v0.6.55

- **Phase 3 end-to-end verification.** Closes the arc in the next iteration.
- **A dedicated UI surface for the skill.** It surfaces via the existing `/skills` page + the chat agent's skill-loading mechanism. A dedicated wizard UI is future work, not part of this arc.
- **Auto-skill-selection logic.** The agent's existing on-demand skill loader handles this via context-budget + intent matching. If the loader doesn't pick this skill up reliably for XQL questions in Phase 3 testing, that's a Phase 3 fix (likely a system-prompt tweak in `mcp/agent/lib/system-prompt.ts`).

---

## [v0.6.54] — 2026-05-20

**Phase 1 of the XQL skill capability arc verified end-to-end against the deployed install: 5/5 representative SOC analyst queries return GREEN through the full retrieval chain (KB similarity → top-5 → stage/function extraction → cortex_xql_lookup). 47 of 48 unique terms across all queries resolved to authoritative Cortex docs. Probe tool committed for re-runnability + as the basis for Phase 2's skill design.**

### Arc context

This release is mid-arc for the **XQL skill capability** (v0.6.51 → v0.6.52 → v0.6.53 → **v0.6.54** → v0.6.5N). Per the v0.6.53 use-case-completion gate, this is NOT a tag candidate. The capability tags ONLY at arc completion (when the operator can ask the agent to build an XQL query end-to-end + the query executes against XDR). v0.6.54 ships the Phase 1 verification artifact + findings; the skill itself follows in v0.6.55+.

### What v0.6.54 ships

- **[`bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/retrieval_probe.py`](bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/retrieval_probe.py)** — a self-contained probe that exercises the full retrieval chain against the deployed install:
  1. Embeds a user query via the agent's KB search proxy
  2. Fetches top-5 KB matches
  3. Extracts stages + functions from each match's SQL body with frequency counts
  4. Identifies "same dataset across top-5" signal for the eventual field-info lookup step
  5. Calls `cortex_xql_lookup` for each unique stage + function term
  6. Emits per-query verdict (GREEN / YELLOW / RED) based on match-count + lookup-hit-rate thresholds

- **5 baseline test queries** covering network hunting, credential/identity, process anomaly, DNS, and geolocation — each chosen to have a known top-1 match in the KB so the probe doubles as a regression-test surface for future corpus changes.

### Phase 1 findings (verified against deployed dev-a6d587a)

| Test query | Top-1 KB match | Score | Lookup hit rate | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hosts with unusual outbound network traffic | Hosts Connected to Specific IP Address/Source in Last Month | 0.643 | 10/10 | GREEN |
| Find failed RDP login attempts | RDP Failed Logins | 0.677 | 9/10 (1 transient timeout) | GREEN |
| Detect rare PSEXEC process executions | Rare Executions of PSEXEC | 0.734 | 10/10 | GREEN |
| Outbound DNS queries to unusual domains | Machines making DNS queries for very long domain names | 0.669 | 8/8 | GREEN |
| Users authenticated from new countries | Geolocations Specific User Logged in From in Last Month | 0.666 | 10/10 | GREEN |

Total: **47/48 lookups hit (98%)**, **5/5 queries GREEN**, average top-1 score 0.678.

### Two quality observations for Phase 2 to design around

1. **Top-5 score spread is narrow** (0.60-0.73 across queries). The skill should use ALL top-5 examples for query-pattern synthesis, not just the top-1 — the next-best entries carry comparable signal.

2. **`cortex_xql_lookup` produces occasional false-positive hits** when an XQL term shares a substring with an unrelated documentation topic — e.g. `set` (function) returns "Set up incident scoring" (a topic), `preset` (stage) returns "Datasets and presets" (a topic page). The skill should instruct the LLM to ground decisions in the `summary_content` field, not just `title`, since the summary is more specific. Topic-page hits are still useful context, just not authoritative function-syntax references.

### Wiring fix discovered during probe development

The probe initially used `POST /api/v1/stream/mcp/tools/call` — the MCP transport endpoint — which returned HTTP 404 because the streamable-HTTP MCP doesn't expose REST tool-call paths. The correct route is `POST /api/agent/tool/call` on the agent's UI port (3000), which does the JSON-RPC handshake (`initialize` → `notifications/initialized` → `tools/call`) internally and unwraps the MCP envelope into a clean `{ok, resolved_name, result}` shape. The probe was updated; the v0.6.54 version of the probe documents the route choice in its module docstring so future probes don't recreate the same wrong wire-up.

This is a learning relevant to Phase 2: when the skill calls tools, the LLM uses the same `/api/agent/tool/call` route via the agent's chat surface. No separate tool-call API needed.

### What's intentionally NOT in this release

- **The `build_xql_query` skill itself**. Phase 2 ships in v0.6.55+.
- **The XDR-side test loop** (Phase 3 — run the built query against XDR, check 4xx/5xx, iterate). v0.6.54 stops at retrieval; Phase 3 closes the loop.
- **Dataset field-info lookup integration**. The probe identifies "same dataset across top-5" but doesn't yet call `cortex_search` to fetch the dataset's field schema. The skill will add that step.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: `python3 bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/retrieval_probe.py` against the deployed install (with the agent UI tunnel open at `localhost:3001`) returns `OVERALL: GREEN: 5` — all five queries verified.
- **Smoke 2**: Manual KB doc fetch — `GET /api/agent/knowledge/xql-examples/docs/XQL-190-f73ddcbe` returns the PSEXEC entry with `## When to use`, `## Variations`, `## Source` sections (v0.6.52 backfill remains intact).
- **Smoke 3**: Manual tool call — `POST /api/agent/tool/call {"name": "cortex_xql_lookup", "arguments": {"term": "comp", "kind": "stage"}}` returns `{ok: true, resolved_name: "cortex_xql_lookup", result: {found: true, title: "comp", reader_url: "...", summary_content: "..."}}`.

---

## [v0.6.53] — 2026-05-20

**`kb_loader` makes schema-required fields enforcing, not advisory. Pre-v0.6.53, docs missing required fields like `id` / `title` / `category` got upserted into the KB with empty metadata + a WARNING log line. v0.6.52's smoke matrix caught this as `xql-examples` doc_count=631 instead of 629 — `README.md` + `_tools/operator_dataset_2026-05-20.json` were leaking in as broken docs. v0.6.53 fixes by skipping required-field-failing docs at upsert time + adding an `invalid` counter to the per-KB summary log.**

### Arc context

This release is part of the multi-release **XQL skill capability arc** (v0.6.51 → v0.6.52 → v0.6.53 → ... → v0.6.5N), not a standalone release. Per the v0.6.53 use-case-completion gate codified in docs/CICD.md + CLAUDE.md, mid-arc commits ship through the dev cycle (auto-deploy + smoke) but do NOT trigger a customer release tag. The tag fires only when the XQL skill capability works end-to-end on the deployed install. v0.6.53's role in the arc: close a corpus-pollution gap that would otherwise inject noise into the skill's KB retrieval step.

### What was wrong

`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/kb_loader.py:_validate_against_schema` returned a flat `list[str]` of warnings — required-field violations were lumped together with softer issues (enum mismatches, type errors) and the caller had no way to distinguish them. The upsert loop logged the warnings + then upserted the doc anyway. For the `xql-examples` KB this manifested as:

| Doc | Source | Required fields present? | Pre-v0.6.53 result |
|---|---|---|---|
| `README` | `bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/README.md` (frontmatter-free) | none | Upserted with empty metadata |
| `_tools/operator_dataset_2026-05-20` | The v0.6.51 importer's source JSON sitting in the KB root's `_tools/` dir | none | Upserted with empty metadata |
| `XQL-001-bbfbd1c9` through `XQL-629-...` | `entries/*.md` (proper frontmatter) | all three | Upserted correctly |

The two spurious docs were a long-standing leak for `README` and a v0.6.51-introduced leak for `operator_dataset_2026-05-20.json`. The phantom_agent boot log showed 6 "missing required field" WARNING lines per boot (3 per leaked doc) and `doc_count=631`.

### What v0.6.53 changes

Three coupled edits in `kb_loader.py`:

- **`_validate_against_schema` now returns `tuple[list[str], list[str]]`** — `(warnings, missing_required)`. The required-field-missing names are split out into their own list so the caller can branch on "did this doc fail a required-field check?" without parsing warning strings.
- **Upsert loop honors `missing_required`** — when non-empty, log a WARNING explicitly tagged "skipping doc (missing required: X, Y)" + increment `counts["invalid"]` + `continue`. The doc never enters the KB; it's also not added to `seen_doc_ids` so the reaper at the end of the loop removes any previously-loaded copy.
- **Per-KB summary log + audit event include `invalid`** — `kb_loader: <kb> — insert=N update=M unchanged=K removed=R skipped=S invalid=I`. `/observability/events`'s `audit_type=knowledge_indexed` row's metadata gains an `invalid` field.

Inline comment in the loop explains the reaper interaction so future maintainers don't accidentally "fix" the (correct) absence of `seen_doc_ids.add(doc_id)` in the skip path.

### Test coverage

New `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_kb_loader.py` — 4 tests:

1. `test_invalid_docs_are_skipped_not_upserted` — the no-schema baseline path doesn't crash.
2. `test_required_field_validation_is_enforcing` — with a schema declaring required fields, 2 valid entries land + 2 invalid (broken frontmatter + README) are skipped via `counts["invalid"] += 1`. Verifies the upsert call list matches only valid doc_ids.
3. `test_previously_loaded_invalid_doc_is_reaped` — simulates "doc was valid yesterday, broken today" (operator edit). The reaper sees it as vanished-from-disk + removes it. Confirms the no-seen_doc_ids-add semantic.
4. `test_no_schema_means_no_strict_validation` — backwards compatibility check. KBs without a schema continue to upsert every parseable doc (pre-v0.6.53 behavior retained where the schema is the gate).

Total pytest count: 337 → 341.

### Bug-family audit (CLAUDE.md §7)

`grep -rn "validate.*schema\|validate_against_schema" bundles/spark/mcp/src/` returns 1 callable site — the one just fixed. Other matches in `main.py` + `marketplace.py` are docstrings + comments referencing connector-spec validation (a separate codepath that already halts on schema failure via `jsonschema.validate(..., raise_on_error=True)`).

`grep -rn "missing required\|required field" bundles/spark/mcp/src/` returns 0 sibling warn-and-proceed patterns post-fix. The bug class is closed.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: After deploy + agent restart, phantom_agent boot log shows `kb_loader: xql-examples — insert=... update=... unchanged=... removed=2 skipped=0 invalid=2`. Pre-v0.6.53: 6 "missing required field" WARNING lines + no `invalid` counter. Post-v0.6.53: 2 "skipping doc (missing required: id, title, category)" WARNING lines + `invalid=2` in the summary.
- **Smoke 2**: `curl -k -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" https://localhost:8081/api/v1/kbs | jq '.kbs[] | select(.name=="xql-examples") | .doc_count'` returns `629`. Pre-v0.6.53: `631`.
- **Smoke 3**: `curl -k -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" https://localhost:8081/api/v1/kbs/xql-examples/docs/README` returns `404`. Pre-v0.6.53: returned the broken doc with empty metadata.
- **Smoke 4 (regression check)**: a known-valid entry like `XQL-001-bbfbd1c9` still fetches successfully + has all three required-field metadata values populated. Required-field enforcement should NOT break any valid doc.

---

## [v0.6.52] — 2026-05-20

**Schema uniformity across all 629 XQL KB entries + corrected stale auto-deploy phrasing in CLAUDE.md. The old half of the corpus (entries 1-166, seeded pre-v0.6.51) was missing the `## When to use` / `## Variations` / `## Source` body sections that the v0.6.51 import standardized on. 161 entries backfilled; 100% of the corpus now has analyst-intent text in the embed payload.**

### Why this matters for the upcoming XQL skill

The embedder embeds the entire body text of each entry. With the old entries missing `## When to use`, their embedding vectors lived in a different subspace than the new entries' — similarity search would systematically prefer the new entries even when an old entry was the better match. Schema uniformity is an embedding-quality issue, not just an aesthetic one. v0.6.51 alone wasn't enough; v0.6.52 closes the corpus-wide gap so the upcoming `build_xql_query` skill (next release) gets uniform-quality matches regardless of which half of the corpus the right answer lives in.

### What v0.6.52 ships

- **`bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/backfill_old_entries.py`** — re-runnable, idempotent backfill tool. Walks the entries directory; for any entry missing `## When to use`, parses the existing frontmatter + title + dataset line + SQL block, then appends the three new sections using the same heuristic generators as `import_operator_dataset.py`. Entries that already have the new schema are skipped — running twice is a no-op.
- **161 entries updated** at `bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/entries/001-*.md` through `161-*.md` (the 5 entries in the 1-166 range that already had the curated body — e.g. the v0.5.72 hand-authored XDR patterns at 162-166 — are skipped). Each updated entry now carries:
  - `## When to use` — heuristic-generated intent text composed from title + dataset + first filter clause + stage signature. Marked in `## Source` as `operator-curation pass pending`.
  - `## Variations` — placeholder for the operator's curation pass.
  - `## Source` — provenance line declaring this is a backfill from the pre-v0.6.51 seed corpus.
- **`CLAUDE.md` auto-deploy phrasing corrected** — pre-v0.6.51 lines stated "CI does NOT install" + "install when ready (operator)" + "smoke testing requires no operator approval (operators run install on their own cadence)". This was stale: docs/CICD.md's v0.6.8+ contract has CI auto-deploy as the last step of `build-dev-installer.yml`. The agent was waiting for a deploy that already happened. Updated the approval-gates table, the "build freely / install / smoke / tag" pattern line, the OUTCOME paragraph in the smoke-test bullet contract, rule 4 of the headless-smoke contract, and the approval-phrasing snippet — all five spots now match the v0.6.8 auto-deploy reality.

### Bug-family audit on schema coverage (CLAUDE.md §7)

After backfill, the audit:

- `grep -L "^## When to use" bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/entries/*.md` returns no entries.
- `grep -L "^## Source" bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/entries/*.md` returns no entries.
- `grep -L "^## Variations" bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/entries/*.md` returns no entries.

`grep -c "by the backfill heuristic" bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/entries/*.md | grep -v ":0$" | wc -l` returns 161 — the count of operator-curation-pending entries from the old corpus, distinct from the 32 heuristic-marker entries from v0.6.51. The two heuristic-marker phrasings are intentionally different ("auto-generated by the importer's heuristic" vs "auto-generated by the backfill heuristic") so the operator can filter the two pre-curation populations independently.

### What's intentionally NOT in this release

- **No description rewrites of the 161 heuristic backfill entries**. Same posture as v0.6.51's 32 heuristic imports — the operator's curation pass is a separate workstream; the heuristic is conservative + tagged in metadata for future filtering.
- **No build_xql_query skill yet**. Same as v0.6.51's deferral — the skill is the next release (v0.6.53+). v0.6.52 finishes seeding the KB so v0.6.53 starts with uniform-quality corpus.
- **No re-indexing trigger**. SqliteKnowledgeBase auto-reindexes when entry mtimes are newer than the indexed copy — happens transparently on next agent boot.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: After auto-deploy + agent restart, `audit_type=knowledge_indexed` row appears in `/observability/events` with updated entry count for `kb_name=xql-examples`.
- **Smoke 2**: `curl -k -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" https://localhost:8080/api/v1/kbs/xql-examples/docs/XQL-001-bbfbd1c9 | jq '.doc.content' | head -50` — the content should contain `## When to use` and `## Source` sections. Pre-v0.6.52: only frontmatter + title + dataset + SQL.
- **Smoke 3 (similarity-search regression check)**: A query phrase from one of the old entries (e.g. "arista cloud vision wireless alerts") should still surface the right entry in the top-1 result. Schema change should not change which entry is the best match — just lift the similarity score because the body now embeds analyst intent.
- **Smoke 4 (idempotence check)**: `python3 bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/backfill_old_entries.py` after deploy reports `backfilled: 0, skipped_already_ok: 629` — running twice is a no-op.

---

## [v0.6.51] — 2026-05-20

**XQL example knowledge base grew from 166 → 629 entries via bulk import of the operator's XSIAM saved-query dataset. 91% of new entries carry operator-authored intent descriptions extracted verbatim from the source RTF's `description` field or inline `//Description:` SQL comments — the embedder-facing payload is now operator-grade across most of the corpus.**

### Why this matters for the upcoming XQL skill

The operator's roadmap calls for a `build_xql_query` skill that retrieves top-5 example matches → extracts stages + functions → looks up help via `cortex_search` → assembles the query → tests against the XDR API. The retrieval step's quality is bounded by description quality in the KB: noisy / mechanical descriptions produce noisy similarity matches. v0.6.51 pre-loads the KB with high-quality operator-authored intent text so retrieval has signal to work with on day one.

### What v0.6.51 ships

- **`bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/import_operator_dataset.py`** — re-runnable converter. Reads the operator's RTF-converted JSON; emits one markdown entry per query matching the existing schema; idempotent IDs (deterministic hash of original query id + title).
- **`bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/_tools/operator_dataset_2026-05-20.json`** — the source dataset, preserved in-repo so future re-runs don't depend on the operator's local filesystem.
- **463 new entries** at `bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/entries/167-*.md` through `629-*.md`. All 629 entries pass `schema.json` validation.

### Description provenance (per-entry transparency)

Each entry's `## Source` section declares where its `## When to use` description came from:

| Provenance | Count | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `rtf-field` | 424 | Carried over from the operator's XSIAM-saved-query `description` field. Examples: *"Display CGO + LOLBINS processes + child processes seen fewer than 10 times in a given timeframe"*. Analyst-grade. |
| `inline-comment` | 7 | Extracted from `//Description:` comments in the SQL body. Combined with `//Filters:` rationale when present. The operator's `QR -` (Quick Response) hunting queries follow this template. |
| `heuristic` | 32 | Falls back when neither prior source is populated. Auto-composed from title + dataset + first-filter clause. Marked in `Source` as `operator-curation pass pending`. |

### Bug-family audit on description coverage

`grep -L "^## When to use" bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/entries/*.md` returns no results — every entry has a `When to use` section.

`grep -l "auto-generated by the importer's heuristic" bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/entries/*.md | wc -l` returns 32 — matches the heuristic count. These are the operator's queries to prioritize for description curation.

### What's intentionally NOT in this release

- **No reindex trigger**. The `SqliteKnowledgeBase` auto-reindexes when entry mtimes are newer than the indexed copy — happens transparently on next agent boot. No manual command needed.
- **No `build_xql_query` skill yet**. The skill is the next release (v0.6.52+). v0.6.51's role is just to seed the KB.
- **No description rewrites of the 32 heuristic entries**. Operator-curation pass is a separate workstream; the importer's heuristic is intentionally conservative + tagged in metadata for future filtering.

### Smoke surfaces

- **Smoke 1**: After deploy + agent restart, `curl -k -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" https://localhost:8080/api/v1/knowledge_search -d '{"kb_name": "xql-examples", "query": "rare PSEXEC executions"}' | jq '.data.results[:3] | .[] | {id, title}'` should surface XQL-190's PSEXEC entry in the top results. Pre-v0.6.51: not in the corpus.
- **Smoke 2**: `audit_type=knowledge_indexed` row appears in `/observability/events` after agent boot, with `kb_name=xql-examples` and updated entry count.
- **Smoke 3 (regression check)**: The original 166 entries continue to be retrievable. Try a query phrase from one of the existing entries — top-1 result should still be the right entry.
