## [v0.6.29] — 2026-05-19

**reset-admin CLI gains non-interactive `--password-stdin --skip-confirm` mode. Pre-v0.6.29 the documented `docker exec -i ...` flow silently broke under piped stdin, leaving locked-out operators with no working CLI recovery path.**

Surfaced when the operator was locked out of the UI (couldn't remember the custom password they'd set via /profile on May 13). The documented recovery path is `docker exec -it phantom_agent node /app/cli/reset-admin.mjs`. In SSH/remote contexts where the `-it` TTY allocation isn't available, the recommended workaround is `printf "RESET\\n<pw>\\n<pw>\\n" | docker exec -i phantom_agent node /app/cli/reset-admin.mjs`.

Pre-v0.6.29 that workaround silently failed. The CLI's first prompt ("Type RESET...") was consumed correctly, but readline.question() with piped stdin reads the ENTIRE buffer at once on its first call — leaving the password + confirm prompts empty. The validation branch *"password must be at least 8 characters"* or *"passwords did not match"* fired, the CLI exited 1 with no reset performed, and the operator had no working escape hatch.

The actual recovery in this lockout incident: directly curl `POST /api/v1/ui/auth/admin_reset` with bearer auth (the same endpoint the CLI calls). That works fine, but it bypasses the CLI's safety prompts entirely — the wrong tradeoff to leave as the only option.

### What v0.6.29 changes

`mcp/agent/cli/reset-admin.mjs`:

- **Two new flags**:
  - `--password-stdin` — read the new password from stdin (entire stream up to EOF, with trailing newline stripped). Argv NEVER carries the password (safe for `ps -ef`, shell history).
  - `--skip-confirm` — bypass the "Type RESET" typo-prevention prompt. Required to use `--password-stdin` (the RESET prompt is interactive by nature).
- **`--help` flag** — prints usage + examples for both interactive and scripted modes.
- **Non-interactive path** added BEFORE the interactive path in `main()`. If `--password-stdin` is set, the CLI reads stdin → trims trailing newline → validates min length → calls the admin_reset endpoint → exits. The original interactive flow is unchanged when neither flag is set.

`mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx`:

- Authentication section's "Forgot password" subsection now documents the v0.6.29+ non-interactive flag pair with a copy-pasteable shell snippet.

### Scripted recovery flow

```bash
# From the host (any non-TTY context — SSH session, CI, ansible task, etc.)
echo -n 'NewPassword' | docker exec -i phantom_agent \
  node /app/cli/reset-admin.mjs --password-stdin --skip-confirm
```

Same safety as the interactive path — same MCP bearer auth, same `admin_reset` endpoint, same session revocation. Just doesn't require TTY allocation.

### Forbidden going forward

- **Don't add a `--password` argv flag.** Passwords on argv leak into shell history (~/.bash_history) AND `ps -ef` AND process accounting logs. The `--password-stdin` pattern (used by docker login, kubectl, helm, etc.) is the standard safe-automation approach.
- **Don't accept `--password-stdin` without `--skip-confirm`.** If the CLI is being driven non-interactively, the RESET prompt is meaningless (no human to type "RESET"). Requiring both flags together prevents an ambiguous middle-ground where the script half-confirms the operator's intent.
- **Don't deprecate the interactive flow.** It's the right default — operators on the box should see the irreversibility warning + type RESET. Non-interactive is for automation only.

### Files touched

- `mcp/agent/cli/reset-admin.mjs`
- `mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

---

## [v0.6.28] — 2026-05-19

**Chat UI now visually separates the agent's "I'll call X tool" narrative from its final answer. Tool-call preamble + interludes render in a muted/italic box; the final answer renders in the normal prominent style.**

Pre-v0.6.28 the assistant message bubble showed ONE continuous text blob mixing the agent's tool-call narration ("I'll check Caldera first to see what agents are checked in...") with its final answer ("Found 2 agents: xdragent and xdragent2..."). Operators had to skim past several paragraphs of narration to find the actual answer, and there was no visual cue separating the two.

The operator's stated workflow: *"messages like 'I will call this tool, I will call this tool' which we already have in the live telemetry page — in the chat I want to box it or differentiate it somewhere from the final response of the agent in the chat window."* So the change is purely visual — the same content, but with phase-aware styling.

### How it works

The chat-data model gains one field: `ChatMessage.boundaryIndices?: number[]` — content-position offsets where `tool_call` SSE events fired during the assistant turn, in chronological order.

The `useChat` hook records a boundary at `content.length` every time a `tool_call` event arrives. By the end of the turn, `boundaryIndices` lists every position where the agent switched from "narrating its next tool" to "calling the tool."

The message-list renderer uses the LAST boundary as the split point:
- `content.slice(0, lastBoundary)` is **narrative** → muted box, italic, smaller font, `auto_awesome` icon
- `content.slice(lastBoundary)` is the **final answer** → prominent normal style (same as pre-v0.6.28)
- If `boundaryIndices` is empty/undefined (no tool calls fired, or message loaded from MCP storage), the entire content renders as one block — backwards-compatible fallback.

### Why the LAST boundary, not segment-per-tool

A single split point handles every multi-tool case cleanly:
- The text BETWEEN tool calls is "narrative for the next tool's prep" — semantically part of narration, not final answer.
- The text AFTER the last tool_call (and its result) is the "final answer."
- Using only the last boundary captures all of this without needing per-segment styling.

Trade-off: on multi-tool turns there's a small visual snap when the second `tool_call` fires — what was in "answer" zone retroactively becomes "narrative." This is rare (multi-tool turns are minority) AND conveys correct info ("the agent was prepping the next tool, not finalizing an answer"). A more invasive per-segment timeline rendering can come later if the simple split's flicker is bothersome.

### What v0.6.28 changes

- `mcp/agent/lib/api/chat.ts` — `ChatMessage` interface gains `boundaryIndices?: number[]` field
- `mcp/agent/components/chat/use-chat.ts` — `tool_call` SSE handler records `content.length` as a new boundary on the live assistant message
- `mcp/agent/components/chat/message-list.tsx` — assistant bubble renders narrative vs answer with phase-aware styling when boundaries are present

### Smoke matrix

After deploy:

- [ ] Type a prompt that triggers a tool call (*"List the caldera agents"*) → agent's bubble shows a muted italic box at top with the "I'll call caldera_get_all_agents" narration, followed by the final answer in normal prominence
- [ ] Type a prompt that doesn't need tools (*"Hello"*) → bubble renders as one block (no narrative split) — backwards-compatible path
- [ ] Run the kill-chain skill → see multiple narrative paragraphs in the muted box as the agent walks through 20 steps; the final summary (Step F markdown table + Step H gap analysis) renders prominently below
- [ ] Reload a session via `/sessions` → historical messages render as single block (boundaries not persisted to MCP storage today; only live-streamed messages benefit from the split)

### Files touched

- `mcp/agent/lib/api/chat.ts`
- `mcp/agent/components/chat/use-chat.ts`
- `mcp/agent/components/chat/message-list.tsx`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

## [v0.6.27] — 2026-05-19

**Kill-chain skill now closes the simulation→detection loop: mid-chain XDR pulse + final XDR sweep + per-technique gap analysis. New skill `xdr_verify_simulation_telemetry` is the reusable building block.**

Pre-v0.6.27 `run_phishing_kill_chain` did the 20-step Caldera attack flawlessly + produced a markdown summary of the abilities. But it told the operator *"Open /observability/events after the operation finishes — events from the agent appear there (audit). For Windows-side telemetry, RDP into xdragent + eventvwr.msc..."* — a manual hand-off. The agent never queried XDR. So the operator's question *"did my simulation show up in XDR?"* required them to leave the chat and manually check.

This was a real gap. The operator was running kill chains, seeing them complete in Caldera, then having to context-switch to the XDR UI to verify detection-side coverage. The connector + tools existed (`xdr_get_cases_and_issues`, `xdr_run_xql_query`); only the skill orchestration was missing.

### What v0.6.27 adds

**New skill: `bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/xdr_verify_simulation_telemetry.md`** (~290 lines)

Reusable building block, invokable two ways:
- **Standalone** by the operator (*"did my last kill chain show up in XDR?"*, *"check XDR for incidents from the last hour"*) — skill infers `op_start_epoch` from the prompt + defaults to sweep mode
- **As a sub-step** from an orchestration skill — caller passes `op_start_epoch`, `affected_hosts`, `expected_techniques`, `mode` (pulse/sweep)

Two modes:
- **Pulse** (mid-simulation) — quick `xdr_get_cases_and_issues` with time filter, returns 🛰️ one-liner + top-3 incidents. Non-blocking; reports inline so the operator sees XDR activity while the kill chain is still running.
- **Sweep** (post-simulation) — exhaustive `xdr_get_incident_extra_data` drill-down for each incident, severity-grouped markdown table, **per-technique gap analysis** that flags expected MITRE techniques without matching XDR incidents.

Step 1 of the sub-skill is a connector probe — if cortex-xdr isn't configured/reachable, the sub-skill aborts cleanly with operator-facing guidance (*"Configure /connectors → cortex-xdr → verify api_url + api_id + api_key"*). The parent orchestration never blocks on XDR being absent.

**Updated skill: `run_phishing_kill_chain`**

- **Step B** now captures `op_start_epoch = int(time.time())` before `caldera_create_operation`. This is the lower bound for the XDR query window in Steps D and H.
- **Step D** (polling loop): every other poll (~60s cadence) starting after the first 60s, invokes `xdr_verify_simulation_telemetry` in **pulse** mode. The operator sees `🛰️ XDR pulse @ 4min 30s: 3 new incident(s)...` lines interspersed with the per-step Caldera progress. Self-aborts cleanly if XDR connector unavailable — kill chain continues regardless.
- **New Step H** (final XDR sweep): after Step F's markdown summary, invoke the sub-skill in **sweep** mode. Produces the per-technique gap analysis (which of the 22 expected MITRE techniques fired in XDR, which didn't) + actionable recommendations (likely-missing detection rules, filter mismatches).
- **New Forbidden lines**: never let XDR pulse/sweep block the kill chain; never modify XDR state from this skill.

### Why pulse + sweep, not just one of them

XDR detection rules can take 30s-5min to fire after the underlying telemetry lands. A single post-simulation sweep is enough to answer *"did any rule fire?"* — but it misses the operator's real workflow desire: *"tell me NOW that something showed up, don't make me wait for the chain to finish."* Pulse gives intermediate confidence; sweep gives the comprehensive gap analysis. Both are needed.

### What deploys

The next dev-installer cycle bakes both skill MDs into the agent image's `/app/mcp/skills-default/workflows/` directory. Per v0.3.2's per-release marker-driven auto-merge (entrypoint.sh §1), the new + updated MDs propagate to the persistent `phantom_mcp_skills` volume on first boot of v0.6.27. New installs see the new skill out-of-the-box; existing installs get the merge on upgrade.

### Operator-facing surface

When the operator types *"Run the phishing kill chain"* in chat after v0.6.27:
- LLM picks the `run_phishing_kill_chain` skill from the registry
- Caldera fires (same as before)
- ~60s in: first 🛰️ XDR pulse line lands inline in chat — *"3 new incidents detected so far"*
- ~60s later: second pulse — *"7 new incidents"*
- ...
- Caldera finishes (~12 min): 🛰️ XDR sweep produces the gap analysis table
- Operator sees end-to-end coverage in one chat session, no UI context switch

### Files touched

- `bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/xdr_verify_simulation_telemetry.md` (new, ~290 lines)
- `bundles/spark/mcp/skills/workflows/run_phishing_kill_chain.md` (Step B captures op_start_epoch; Step D pulses XDR; new Step H sweeps XDR; new Forbidden lines)
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

### Smoke matrix

After v0.6.27 auto-deploys to phantom-vm:

- [ ] `/skills` page shows `xdr_verify_simulation_telemetry` in the workflows category
- [ ] Type *"check XDR for incidents in the last hour"* in chat → LLM picks the new skill, runs `xdr_get_cases_and_issues`, returns a sweep-mode markdown table
- [ ] Type *"Run the phishing kill chain"* → kill chain fires; after ~2 min the first 🛰️ XDR pulse appears inline; after completion the Step H sweep produces the gap analysis
- [ ] Chat session for the kill-chain run can be reloaded later from `/sessions` and the XDR pulses are preserved in the rendered transcript
- [ ] `/observability/runtime-events` shows `rt.tool.completed` events for both `xdr_get_cases_and_issues` (Step D pulses) and `xdr_get_incident_extra_data` (Step H sweep drill-downs)

## [v0.6.26] — 2026-05-19

**v0.6.25 detections Coverage tab had wrong response shape — MCP returns dict, page expected list. Empty-state Coverage tab worked by accident; populated state would have thrown a render error.**

v0.6.25 shipped the `/observability/detections` page with the Rules tab + Coverage tab. The post-deploy smoke confirmed the proxy routes returned data, but inspection showed:

```
/api/agent/detections                       → {"rules": [], "count": 0}                                                 ✓
/api/agent/detections/coverage/techniques   → {"techniques": {}, "total_techniques": 0}                                ✗
```

`techniques` is a DICT keyed by T-code (`{T1059.001: {rules_count, fires_*, ...}}`), per `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/detection_inventory.py::technique_coverage()`. My page code at `setCoverage(coverageBody.techniques ?? [])` assigned the dict to a list-typed state, and the `coverage.map(...)` in the Coverage tab would have iterated the dict's properties and rendered garbage (or thrown).

The empty-state render worked because both `[]` and `{}` are falsy through `.length === 0` checks (well, only `[]` is — `{}` would skip the empty-state branch and try to map over an empty dict, which JavaScript silently allows but renders nothing). So the bug was latent until detection data was synced.

I also got the field names wrong: page used `rule_count` / `fire_count` / `last_seen`; MCP emits `rules_count` / `fires_24h` / `fires_7d` / `fires_30d` / `last_fire_at`.

### What v0.6.26 changes

- `mcp/agent/app/observability/detections/page.tsx`:
  - `TechniqueCoverage` interface — fields aligned with MCP: `technique_id`, `rules_count`, `fires_24h`, `fires_7d`, `fires_30d`, `last_fire_at`
  - `refresh()` now does `Object.values(coverageBody.techniques ?? {})` + sorts by `fires_30d` descending
  - Coverage tab table headers: split single "Total fires" column into 24h / 7d / 30d (matching MCP's window granularity); "Last seen" → "Last fire"

### Lesson logged

When adding a UI page consuming a backend endpoint, always probe the actual response shape AFTER the proxy is wired up, not just trust the route docstring. The MCP docstring at `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/detections.py:5` said *"per-MITRE-T-code aggregation"* but didn't specify dict-vs-list — I assumed list. Verifying the actual shape with a probe before drafting the page would have caught this in v0.6.25.

### Files touched

- `mcp/agent/app/observability/detections/page.tsx`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

---

## [v0.6.25] — 2026-05-19

**Detection inventory now has a UI surface — closes a CLAUDE.md rule-6 gap that's been open since Phase 12.**

The MCP has exposed `/api/v1/detections/*` since Phase 12 (`bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/detections.py` — list rules, single rule, fires, coverage, sync). The chat-driven `detections_list` MCP tool worked. But there were three holes:

- **No agent-side proxy route.** A direct browser GET to `/api/agent/detections` returned 404. Any non-LLM consumer (other UI pages, future automation) had no path in.
- **No observability page.** Operators couldn't browse the rule inventory or MITRE coverage without typing a `^detections_list` chat command.
- **No sidebar nav entry.** Even if the page existed, operators had no discovery path.

Per CLAUDE.md § "Documentation discipline" rule 6: *"No backend feature without a UI surface (or a documented deferral)."* v0.6.25 closes the gap completely.

### What v0.6.25 changes

**Proxy routes** (5 new files under `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/detections/`):

| Path | Verb | Forwards to | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/api/agent/detections` | GET | `/api/v1/detections` | List rules with aggregated fire counts |
| `/api/agent/detections/[rule_id]` | GET | `/api/v1/detections/{rule_id}` | Single-rule summary |
| `/api/agent/detections/[rule_id]/fires` | GET | `/api/v1/detections/{rule_id}/fires` | Recent fires for one rule |
| `/api/agent/detections/coverage/techniques` | GET | `/api/v1/detections/coverage/techniques` | Per-MITRE-T-code coverage aggregation |
| `/api/agent/detections/sync` | POST | `/api/v1/detections/sync` | Bulk upsert (for external tooling) |

All five use the standard `proxyToMcp()` pattern — bearer auth + query-string forwarding attached server-side.

**Observability page** at `/observability/detections`:
- Rules tab: table of detection rules with severity badge, MITRE technique chips, fire counts (24h / 7d / 30d / total), last-fire timestamp. Severity + technique-id filters with refresh button.
- Coverage tab: aggregated by MITRE T-code, showing rule_count + fire_count per technique
- Empty-state guidance points operators at the `detections_sync` skill or POST sync endpoint

**Sidebar nav entry** (per CLAUDE.md rule 6a — same-release UI nav for new pages):
- Added under "Observability" group, between Runtime Events and Connectors, with the `radar` material symbol icon (distinct from siblings).

### Smoke matrix

Operator review checklist on the next dev-installer auto-deploy:

- [ ] Open `https://localhost:3001/observability/detections` — page renders without console errors
- [ ] Both tabs (Rules + Coverage) load with empty-state guidance if no detections have been synced
- [ ] Filters (severity, MITRE T-code) accept input + refresh re-fetches
- [ ] Sidebar: "Detections" entry visible under Observability group with `radar` icon
- [ ] `curl /api/agent/detections` returns `{rules: [], count: 0}` (or with data after a sync)

### Files touched

- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/detections/route.ts` (new)
- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/detections/sync/route.ts` (new)
- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/detections/[rule_id]/route.ts` (new)
- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/detections/[rule_id]/fires/route.ts` (new)
- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/detections/coverage/techniques/route.ts` (new)
- `mcp/agent/app/observability/detections/page.tsx` (new — ~290 lines)
- `mcp/agent/components/sidebar.tsx` (nav entry)
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

## [v0.6.24] — 2026-05-19

**v0.6.22 was incomplete — compose's `XLOG_URL=http://xlog:8000` overrode the code fallback. v0.6.24 flips the compose env values to `https://xlog:8000` too.**

v0.6.22 fixed the agent code's xlog fallback defaults (`runtime-config.ts:126`, `reports/route.ts:14`, marketplace catalog). But the post-deploy smoke confirmed those fallback defaults never fired — the compose's `environment:` block hardcodes `XLOG_URL=http://xlog:8000`, which means `process.env.XLOG_URL` is always set, the fallback never kicks in, and the agent still talks plain HTTP to xlog. Surfaced when `/api/agent/health` continued to report `xlog-api: failed` and `/api/agent/reports` continued to return 502 after the v0.6.22 deploy.

### Root cause

The compose comment claimed entrypoint.sh would rewrite `XLOG_URL` http→https when SSL_CERT_PEM was set. v0.1.34 explicitly removed that rewrite block (per the InstanceStore-as-source-of-truth spec). The comment was stale; the env var was wrong; the fallback never fired.

### What v0.6.24 changes

- `installer/docker-compose.yml:363` — `XLOG_URL=http://xlog:8000` → `XLOG_URL=https://xlog:8000` + comment updated to reflect v0.1.34's removal + the v0.4.0 TLS-always reality
- `docker-compose.yml:273` (dev compose) — same flip + reference to installer compose comment

The InstanceStore-as-source-of-truth spec still holds: operators who want a different baseUrl override via `/connectors → InstanceStore` (per `/help/architecture#setup-wiring`). The compose default just needs to MATCH REALITY (xlog serves HTTPS) so the system works on a fresh install without operator intervention.

### Pre-deploy gate

For docker-compose changes, the gate is `docker compose config` (validates compose syntax). Both files pass.

### Files touched

- `installer/docker-compose.yml`
- `docker-compose.yml`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

---

## [v0.6.22] — 2026-05-19

**`/api/agent/reports` returned 502 because the fallback URL hardcoded `http://xlog:8000` — xlog has served HTTPS unconditionally since v0.4.0.**

xlog's `_resolve_ssl_args()` priority 1 is the shared phantom_tls volume (mounted from phantom-agent's auto-generated `/tls/cert.pem`). Since v0.4.0's TLS rollout, every Phantom deployment has TLS-on by default — including xlog. But the agent's code still had `http://xlog:8000` baked in three places:

| Location | Pre-fix | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp/agent/lib/runtime-config.ts:126` | `http://xlog:8000` | `runtimeConfig.XLOG_URL` defaults to plain HTTP. `/api/agent/health` reports `xlog-api: failed`. |
| `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/reports/route.ts:14` | `http://xlog:8000` | `/api/agent/reports` fallback. Returns 502 "Empty reply from server." |
| `mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts:139` | `defaultValue: "http://xlog:8000"` | Marketplace catalog default shown in `/connectors → Add xlog Instance` form. Operators who accept it get a broken instance. |

The architectural truth: `process.env.XLOG_URL` is intentionally NOT flipped to HTTPS by entrypoint.sh (per v0.1.34's spec — the InstanceStore is the canonical source of truth). The env var stays as compose declared it (`http://xlog:8000` in installer/docker-compose.yml line ~525). But xlog itself ignores the env-var protocol — it serves HTTPS from the mounted cert.

Why the chain worked partially:
- The MCP's internal calls to xlog (via `pkg/graphql_client.py`) read `XLOG_URL` from InstanceStore (v0.1.34 fix) → operator-configured URLs → if the operator pasted `https://`, those work.
- The agent's `/api/agent/health` and `/api/agent/reports` had no InstanceStore for xlog because the operator never created an xlog instance via /connectors. The default fallback to `http://` then failed.

### Why TLS verification works with the URL flip

Node's `fetch()` in the Next.js process trusts the self-signed cert because the agent's entrypoint exports `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=/tls/cert.pem` before starting Next.js (`mcp/agent/entrypoint.sh:257`). The cert's SAN includes `DNS:xlog`, `DNS:caldera`, `DNS:phantom-agent`, `DNS:localhost` so hostname verification passes too.

Verified empirically on phantom-vm: with `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=/tls/cert.pem` set, `node -e 'fetch("https://xlog:8000/health")'` returns status 200; without it, fails with `DEPTH_ZERO_SELF_SIGNED_CERT`. The entrypoint sets it; the v0.6.22 URL flip lets the existing trust path do its job.

### What v0.6.22 changes

- `mcp/agent/lib/runtime-config.ts:126` — default `https://xlog:8000`
- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/reports/route.ts:14` — default `https://xlog:8000`
- `mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts:139` — `defaultValue: "https://xlog:8000"` + `setupGuide` string updated
- `mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx:552` + `:6311` — docs updated to reflect HTTPS-since-v0.4.0
- `mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx:4008` — pipeline-page troubleshooting example uses HTTPS

### Migration note

Operators with existing xlog connector instances configured with `http://xlog:8000` (pre-v0.6.22 default) will continue to see `xlog-api: failed` until they edit the instance's baseUrl via `/connectors → xlog → Edit`. The fix is one click. v0.6.22 only changes defaults for NEW instances + the agent-internal fallback chain.

### Files touched

- `mcp/agent/lib/runtime-config.ts`
- `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/reports/route.ts`
- `mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts`
- `mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx`
- `mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

## [v0.6.21] — 2026-05-19

**`/api/v1/jobs` returned 404 on every fresh install — the route handlers only registered when the bundle's `manifest.yaml` declared at least one job. Catch-22: operators couldn't create their first job via the `/jobs/new` UI page because the POST endpoint didn't exist.**

`bundles/spark/manifest.yaml` ships with `jobs: []`. Pre-v0.6.21 `main.py:1037` gated the scheduler construction + `register_job_routes()` call inside `if defs:`. With an empty manifest, `defs` was empty, `register_job_routes()` was skipped, and every `GET /api/v1/jobs` + `POST /api/v1/jobs` returned a Starlette default 404.

The operator's `/jobs/new` UI page POSTs to `/api/v1/jobs` (via the agent's proxy) to create runtime jobs. Without the routes, the form 404'd silently — operators couldn't create their first job ever. The only way to bootstrap was to manually edit `manifest.yaml` to ship at least one job, OR drop a YAML file into `/app/data/jobs/` via `docker exec` — neither of which is a documented operator workflow.

### Root cause (v0.3.11 echo)

This is the same fix-shape as the v0.3.11 dispatcher fix one block above in `main.py`. Pre-v0.3.11 the dispatcher was also only constructed inside `if defs:`; that was fixed by building it unconditionally. The scheduler + routes weren't part of that earlier fix, so the same gate persisted on a sibling code path until v0.6.21 caught it via the post-v0.6.20 smoke sweep.

### What v0.6.21 changes

`bundles/spark/mcp/src/main.py:1037-1051`:
- The scheduler is now constructed unconditionally (no `if defs:` gate)
- `register_job_routes(mcp, sched)` is called unconditionally
- `sched.start()` is called unconditionally — the asyncio loop is cheap when there are no jobs (periodic poll for due ones). Starting it always means a POST `/api/v1/jobs` mid-process picks up the new job immediately, without an MCP restart.

### Smoke matrix

Pre-v0.6.21:
```
GET /api/v1/jobs              → 404 Not Found
POST /api/v1/jobs (UI form)   → 404 Not Found
GET /api/v1/jobs/yaml-issues  → 404 Not Found
```

Post-v0.6.21:
```
GET /api/v1/jobs              → 200 {"jobs": [], "count": 0}
POST /api/v1/jobs             → 201 {"job": {...}}  (creates row + schedules immediately)
GET /api/v1/jobs/yaml-issues  → 200 {"issues": [], "count": 0}
```

### Files touched

- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/main.py`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

---

## [v0.6.20] — 2026-05-19

**`phantom-connector-cortex-xdr` was missing from the release manifest + dev-installer digest loop — surfaced as a "image not found" failure when phantom-updater tried to restart a cortex-xdr instance.**

cortex-xdr was added in v0.5.61. `build-connectors.yml` was extended to build it (lines 260-279), and the image has been pushed to `ghcr.io/kite-production/phantom-connector-cortex-xdr` at every `:dev` + `:vX.Y.Z` tag since. But TWO downstream CI/CD plumbing files were never updated:

- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — never added cortex-xdr to its IMAGES array, its changed-detection step, its first-release defaults, its runtime-rebuild force list, its outputs, or its rebuild-decisions summary table. Customer release manifests since v0.5.61 have shipped without `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_CORTEX_XDR`.
- `.github/workflows/build-dev-installer.yml` — never added cortex-xdr to its `CONN_IMGS` map or its `for KEY in ...` loop. Dev manifests since v0.5.61 have shipped without `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_CORTEX_XDR`.

So `/opt/phantom/connector-digests.env` on every customer install (and every dev install) has been missing `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_CORTEX_XDR` for the entire lifetime of the connector.

### Why it didn't surface immediately

The cortex-xdr connector's instance container was first created at some past point in time. On creation, phantom-updater resolved the image via `_connector_image_ref()`, hit the tag-pinning fallback (because `_connector_digest("cortex-xdr")` returned None), and pulled `phantom-connector-cortex-xdr:<then-running-PHANTOM_VERSION>`. That tag DID exist at that moment because `build-connectors.yml` had just pushed it for that release. The container started and ran for ~34 hours.

When v0.6.17's KEK fix landed and the operator restarted cortex-xdr to pick up the corrected `PHANTOM_SECRET_KEK`, phantom-updater re-ran `_connector_image_ref()`. This time the running `PHANTOM_VERSION` was `dev-321d002` (a dev build sha). `_connector_digest("cortex-xdr")` STILL returned None (the manifest plumbing gap is independent of release). The tag-pinning fallback tried `phantom-connector-cortex-xdr:dev-321d002`. That tag doesn't exist — `build-connectors.yml` only fires when `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-xdr/` changed, and v0.6.18 didn't change connector source. So the pull failed with `manifest unknown` and the restart returned HTTP 502.

This was the canary that exposed the entire plumbing gap.

### What v0.6.20 changes

`.github/workflows/release.yml`:
- First-release defaults (line ~224): add `CONNECTOR_CORTEX_XDR=1`
- Changed-detection (line ~266): add `CONNECTOR_CORTEX_XDR=$(changed bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-xdr/)`
- Runtime-rebuild force list (line ~273): add `CONNECTOR_CORTEX_XDR=1` (was a 6-item list, now 7)
- Outputs (line ~290): add `echo "connector_cortex_xdr=$CONNECTOR_CORTEX_XDR"`
- Rebuild-decisions table summary (after the cortex-docs row): add cortex-xdr row. Also fixed a pre-existing gap — `cortex-content` was missing from the summary table too (the rebuild decision still happened, but operators couldn't see it in the workflow summary). Both rows added together.
- IMAGES array (line ~830): add `phantom-connector-cortex-xdr`
- New "Build or retag phantom-connector-cortex-xdr" step (after the cortex-content step): rebuilds on source change, retags from PREV_V otherwise — with a first-release fallback that builds from source if `:$PREV_V` doesn't exist (because pre-v0.6.20 customer releases didn't push cortex-xdr at versioned tags). The first release after v0.6.20 will build from source; subsequent releases retag normally.

`.github/workflows/build-dev-installer.yml`:
- `CONN_IMGS` map (line ~242): add the cortex-xdr entry
- `for KEY in ...` resolution loop (line ~251): add `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_CORTEX_XDR`

No installer template changes needed — `phantom-installer.template.sh` is data-driven; it iterates over whatever `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_*` values are in the embedded manifest. Adding cortex-xdr to release.yml's IMAGES array is enough.

### What deploys

The first dev-installer cycle after v0.6.20 will:
1. Pull `phantom-connector-cortex-xdr:dev` from GHCR (build-connectors.yml has pushed this continuously since v0.5.61)
2. Resolve its digest into the dev manifest
3. Auto-deploy to phantom-vm → `/opt/phantom/connector-digests.env` now has `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_CORTEX_XDR=sha256:...`
4. Operator-triggered restart of the cortex-xdr instance via phantom-updater now resolves to the pinned digest — no more `dev-<sha>` tag-fallback path

The first customer release after v0.6.20 will:
1. Run the new "Build or retag phantom-connector-cortex-xdr" step
2. PREV_V tag doesn't exist for cortex-xdr (pre-v0.6.20 releases didn't push it at customer tags) → fallback builds from source
3. Pushes cortex-xdr at the new customer tag
4. Manifest now includes cortex-xdr → customer install writes it to `/opt/phantom/connector-digests.env`

### Files touched

- `.github/workflows/release.yml`
- `.github/workflows/build-dev-installer.yml`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

### Forward discipline

When a new connector is added to `build-connectors.yml`, the change MUST also touch `.github/workflows/release.yml` (5 sites) AND `.github/workflows/build-dev-installer.yml` (1 site, 2 line changes). The "add a connector" checklist deserves a dedicated section in `docs/CICD.md`. Filing as a follow-up issue.

---

## [v0.6.19] — 2026-05-19

**`GET /api/v1/instances/{id}` was always redacting secrets even when the caller asked for cleartext — asymmetric with the list endpoint.**

Pre-v0.1.36 every callsite redacted secrets unconditionally. v0.1.36 added the `?include_secrets=true` query param to support the backup flow (it needs cleartext to write a restore-complete zip across deployments). The list endpoint at `/api/v1/instances` got the new behavior in v0.1.36. The single-instance GET at `/api/v1/instances/{id}` was missed — it kept calling `_instance_to_dict(instance)` with no kwargs, so the redact-by-default path always fired.

Symptom: any caller that hit the single-instance GET expecting cleartext got `{"apiKey": "***"}` instead of the real value, even with the right bearer token + `?include_secrets=true`. No customer hit this in practice (the agent's UI surface routes through the list endpoint), but the asymmetry blocks future features like "test this specific instance with its persisted secrets" or "export one instance for migration without round-tripping through the full backup zip."

### What v0.6.19 changes

`bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/instances.py:435-442` — `get_instance` now reads the same `include_secrets` query param as `list_instances` and passes `redact_secrets=not include_secrets` + the secret store through to `_instance_to_dict`. Bearer auth is still the gate — same threat model as the list endpoint.

The agent-side proxy at `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/instances/[id]/route.ts` requires no change — `proxyToMcp` already forwards `${url.search}` (the query string) to the MCP. So `?include_secrets=true` flows through automatically.

### Audit closure (CLAUDE.md § "Bug-family audit (v0.5.80)" rule 7)

The audit pattern is `grep -nE "_instance_to_dict\(.*instance.*\)" bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/instances.py`. Five hits in total:

| Line | Endpoint | Reason for v0.6.19 decision |
|---|---|---|
| 417 | `POST /api/v1/instances` (create) | **Left as-is.** Write-style; caller just supplied the secrets in the request body. Echoing redacted "***" is a reasonable security default. |
| 442 | `GET /api/v1/instances/{id}` | **Fixed.** Read-style; asymmetric with the list endpoint was the actual bug. |
| 589 | `PATCH /api/v1/instances/{id}` (update) | **Left as-is.** Write-style; same reasoning as line 417. |
| 764 | `POST /api/v1/instances/{id}/test` (probe-not-implemented branch) | **Left as-is.** Diagnostic write-style; response shape is a connector-state report, not a serialized-instance read. |
| 810 | `POST /api/v1/instances/{id}/test` (probe-implemented branch) | **Left as-is.** Same as line 764. |

The other four call sites are intentionally write-style; they're not asymmetric with each other (all four redact), and the security default of "don't echo secrets the caller just supplied" is correct for them. v0.6.19 is the minimal fix that closes the read-style asymmetry without expanding to a separate-discipline change in the write paths.

### Files touched

- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/instances.py` (`get_instance` handler, ~10 lines)
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

---

## [v0.6.18] — 2026-05-19

**Bug-family audit completion for v0.6.17's KEK fix — `PHANTOM_AGENT_INTERNAL_URL` and `PHANTOM_TLS_VERIFY` were reading from `os.environ` too, so the operator's `.env` overrides were silently ignored.**

v0.6.17 fixed `PHANTOM_SECRET_KEK` by switching its read site in `updater/src/main.py` from `os.environ.get()` to `_host_env_get()` (the `/host/.env` cached helper). CLAUDE.md § "Bug-family audit (v0.5.80)" mandates that when fixing a bug in a connector-system file, sibling instances of the same pattern get audited in the same release-discipline window — or one of them gets fixed and the others get a documented justification for being left alone.

The audit ran `grep -nE 'os\.environ\.(get\(|\[)"(PHANTOM_|DIGEST_)' updater/src/main.py` and found two hits beyond the KEK that landed last release:

```
305: override = os.environ.get("PHANTOM_AGENT_INTERNAL_URL", "").strip()  # _agent_tls_verify
308: return os.environ.get("PHANTOM_TLS_VERIFY", "1").strip() not in ...
345: override = os.environ.get("PHANTOM_AGENT_INTERNAL_URL", "").strip()  # _resolve_agent_internal_url
```

These are operator escape-hatch overrides — the **defaults always work**, so the bug was dormant rather than active (no customer impact today). But the override paths were silently broken: an operator pasting `PHANTOM_AGENT_INTERNAL_URL=https://corp-proxy/agent` into `/opt/phantom/.env` and restarting would see no behavior change, because `os.environ.get("PHANTOM_AGENT_INTERNAL_URL")` returns `""` — the compose `phantom-updater.environment` block intentionally doesn't pass it through (per v0.5.51's docker-compose env-stability invariant).

### What v0.6.18 changes

`updater/src/main.py`:
- `_agent_tls_verify()` line 305: `os.environ.get("PHANTOM_AGENT_INTERNAL_URL", "")` → `_host_env_get("PHANTOM_AGENT_INTERNAL_URL", "")`
- `_agent_tls_verify()` line 308: `os.environ.get("PHANTOM_TLS_VERIFY", "1")` → `_host_env_get("PHANTOM_TLS_VERIFY", "1")`
- `_resolve_agent_internal_url()` line 345: same swap

Three call sites, all using the same `_host_env_get` helper already established for `PHANTOM_VERSION`, `PHANTOM_SECRET_KEK`, and the per-service `DIGEST_PHANTOM_*` reads.

### Audit closure

After v0.6.18, `grep -nE 'os\.environ\.(get\(|\[)"(PHANTOM_(AGENT_INTERNAL_URL|TLS_VERIFY|SECRET_KEK|VERSION)|DIGEST_)' updater/src/main.py` returns **zero matches**. The bug-family is fully resolved.

`PHANTOM_REGISTRY*` keys at lines 61–64 remain `os.environ.get()` reads — these are passed through the compose env block intentionally (for `docker login` at boot), and they predate the v0.5.51 stability invariant. They're left alone consciously, and this entry is the documented justification per CLAUDE.md § "Bug-family audit (added v0.5.80)" rule 7.

### Files touched

- `updater/src/main.py` (3 call sites in `_agent_tls_verify` + `_resolve_agent_internal_url`)
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts`

---

## [v0.6.17] — 2026-05-19

**phantom-updater was passing an EMPTY `PHANTOM_SECRET_KEK` to connector containers, so all instance secrets resolved to empty strings.**

After v0.6.16 made dev-cycle connector images flow through to phantom-vm, restarting the Caldera connector container pulled the new image — only to fail at the next layer: *"caldera instance has no apiKey configured."* But the SecretStore actually had a 32-char apiKey in plaintext. Investigation showed:

```
HOST KEK length: 43 (real KEK in /opt/phantom/.env)
CONTAINER KEK length: 0 (empty)
```

The container log made the actual root cause explicit:

```
WARNING runtime.instance_store_client: instance caldera/Caldera: could not 
resolve secret slot 'apiKey' at '/agents/.../apiKey' — using empty (secret 
is encrypted (v1 envelope) but PHANTOM_SECRET_KEK is not set on this 
connector container.)
```

Without the KEK, the SecretStoreReader.read() can't decrypt; the connector-runtime swallows the error and sets the secret slot to `""` (empty string). Empty string is falsy in Python, so the connector's `apiKey or api_key or ...` chain evaluates to None and the operator-facing error fires.

### Why the KEK wasn't propagating

`updater/src/main.py:1860` (pre-v0.6.17) read:

```python
"PHANTOM_SECRET_KEK": os.environ.get("PHANTOM_SECRET_KEK", ""),
```

The comment claimed *"The customer docker-compose's `environment:` block sets these from the host's .env."* — but the compose `phantom-updater.environment` block INTENTIONALLY DOESN'T pass `PHANTOM_SECRET_KEK`, per v0.5.51's docker-compose env-stability invariant (any env block change forces phantom-updater container recreate on stack upgrade, defeating the digest-pinning state-preservation). So `os.environ.get()` returned `""`.

This bug has been latent since v0.5.51. It surfaced now because v0.6.x finally exercised the connector-container start path end-to-end.

### v0.6.17 fix

Switch to the same pattern as `PHANTOM_VERSION` / `DIGEST_PHANTOM_*` reads — `_host_env_get()` reads `/host/.env` at runtime, no compose env-block dependency:

```python
"PHANTOM_SECRET_KEK": _host_env_get("PHANTOM_SECRET_KEK", "") or "",
```

This preserves the env-block stability invariant (phantom-updater container doesn't recreate on KEK rotation) AND propagates the KEK correctly to spawned connector containers.

### Files

- `updater/src/main.py` — one line: env propagation source changed from `os.environ` to `_host_env_get`.

### Layer 6 of the kill-chain unblock chain

- v0.6.11/13/14: agent-URL fix chain (HTTPS + verify=False)
- v0.6.15: config-key alignment (calderaUrl → baseUrl)
- v0.6.16: connector-image flow (per-connector :dev resolution)
- **v0.6.17: secret-decryption propagation (KEK from /host/.env)**

---

## [v0.6.16] — 2026-05-19

**Bring per-connector images into the dev cycle. Pre-v0.6.16 connector source changes never reached phantom-vm even though build-connectors.yml was pushing fresh `:dev` tags.**

Surfaced after v0.6.15. The marketplace catalog + Caldera connector fallback chain were both updated and pushed. build-connectors.yml ran and pushed a new `phantom-connector-caldera:dev` image with the `calderaUrl` fallback baked in. But when I restarted the Caldera instance via phantom-updater's restart endpoint, it pulled the SAME old digest: `sha256:93ce722b…` — the v0.6.4 customer release's connector image.

Root cause: `/opt/phantom/connector-digests.env` is populated by phantom-installer from the dev manifest. The dev manifest's `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_*` values were fetched from the LATEST CUSTOMER RELEASE (v0.6.7 right now), not from each connector's `:dev` tag. So phantom-updater on phantom-vm was always pulling v0.6.x customer-release connector images regardless of what build-connectors.yml had pushed.

This is the SAME class of issue as v0.6.12 (phantom-updater wasn't in dev cycle) — fixed for one image, repeated for seven.

### v0.6.16 fix

`build-dev-installer.yml`'s connector-digest resolution loop now:
1. `docker pull` each connector's `:dev` tag from GHCR
2. Capture the resolved sha256 digest and write to the dev manifest
3. If `:dev` doesn't exist for a connector (bootstrap case, or a connector whose source has never been pushed in this repo's history), fall back to that key's value from the LATEST CUSTOMER RELEASE manifest
4. If neither :dev nor stable has a value, log a warning and continue (phantom-updater handles missing per-key with a tag-pinning fallback that would fail visibly at instance-create time)

Net effect: when a developer changes any `bundles/spark/connectors/<id>/src/` file, build-connectors.yml rebuilds `<id>:dev`, the build-dev-installer cascade picks it up, the dev manifest carries the fresh digest, auto-deploy writes it to `connector-digests.env`, and the next instance restart pulls the new image. Same architectural pattern as agent/xlog/caldera/updater.

### Files

- `.github/workflows/build-dev-installer.yml` — connector-digest resolution loop rewritten.

---

## [v0.6.15] — 2026-05-19

**Marketplace catalog drift fix: `calderaUrl`/`xlogUrl` → `baseUrl`. Config field names in the marketplace catalog disagreed with their `connector.yaml` source-of-truth, so UI-created instances stored the wrong key + connector runtime couldn't find the config.**

Surfaced after v0.6.14 made the reconcile + caldera dispatch path work end-to-end. The Caldera connector container came up but immediately failed:

```
caldera instance has no baseUrl configured. Edit the instance at /connectors.
```

Investigation showed the operator's Caldera instance had `config: {calderaUrl: "http://caldera:8888"}`, while the connector at runtime was looking for `baseUrl` / `caldera_url` (snake_case) / `url`. None matched the camelCase `calderaUrl`.

Root cause: `mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts` hard-codes the connector catalog the UI form renders from. The Caldera entry declared `name: "calderaUrl"` (line 204); the xlog entry declared `name: "xlogUrl"` — but both connectors' `connector.yaml` schemas say `baseUrl`. The marketplace catalog drifted from the connector source of truth (probably during a rename refactor where one side updated but not the other). UI-created instances stored the catalog's (wrong) name; runtime tried the schema's (right) name; mismatch.

### v0.6.15 fix

**Two parts** — make the source of truth alignment AND support migration of existing instances:

1. **Marketplace catalog corrected**:
   - `mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts:204` (caldera): `name: "calderaUrl"` → `name: "baseUrl"`
   - Same file (xlog): `name: "xlogUrl"` → `name: "baseUrl"`
   - Display labels ("Caldera URL", "xlog URL") unchanged — only the wire name changes.

2. **Runtime fallbacks added** for operator instances created pre-v0.6.15:
   - `bundles/spark/connectors/caldera/src/connector.py`: `calderaUrl` added to the `baseUrl` resolution chain.
   - `bundles/spark/mcp/src/service/phantom_mcp/server.py` `get_xlog_url()`: `xlogUrl` added to the resolution chain.

   These let already-created instances keep working without forcing the operator to recreate them.

### Deeper issue (noted, not fixed in v0.6.15)

The marketplace catalog should be DERIVED from each connector's `connector.yaml` (single source of truth), not hard-coded TypeScript. Today they're two separate sources that can drift. Filing as a follow-up: replace the hard-coded TS array with a server-side derivation that reads + serializes from the yaml at request time.

For now: any new connector added in v0.6.15+ MUST have its marketplace catalog entry checked against its `connector.yaml` configSchema. The pattern: `field.name` in catalog === key in `configSchema.properties`.

---

## [v0.6.14] — 2026-05-19

**Default `verify=False` on the compose-internal agent URL. v0.6.13 got us to HTTPS, but verify=True against a self-signed cert never succeeds.**

After v0.6.13 deployed (always-HTTPS for `phantom-agent:8080`), testing the reconcile endpoint surfaced the next layer of the problem:

```
{"detail":"could not list instances from agent: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] 
 certificate verify failed: self-signed certificate (_ssl.c:1010)"}
```

Progress — TLS handshake worked. But the call sites read `PHANTOM_TLS_VERIFY` with default `"1"` (verify=True), and the agent's auto-generated `/tls/cert.pem` is a self-signed cert with no CA chain. Verify=True can NEVER succeed against that cert.

### The architectural fact

The intra-cluster trust boundary is the docker network alias (only the legitimate `phantom-agent` container answers on that DNS name within the compose network) — NOT the cert chain. The agent's cert exists to encrypt the wire, not to authenticate the host; authentication is done by the docker bridge. So `verify=False` is the architecturally correct setting for the default compose-internal URL.

### v0.6.14 fix

New `_agent_tls_verify()` helper:
- When `PHANTOM_AGENT_INTERNAL_URL` env override is in use → honor `PHANTOM_TLS_VERIFY` (default `"1"`). Operator can opt in to chain validation if they front the agent with a CA-signed cert.
- When the default URL is in use → return `False` unconditionally (the cert is always self-signed).

Two call sites updated:
- `_agent_set_container_url()` (line ~1737) — instance container_url PUT
- `reconcile_connectors()` (line ~2150) — /api/v1/instances fetch

Both now use `_agent_tls_verify()` instead of inline env reads.

This is the layer-3 fix in the agent-URL chain:
- **Layer 1** (v0.6.11): HTTP → HTTPS scheme — wrong derivation signal
- **Layer 2** (v0.6.13): always HTTPS by default — got past handshake
- **Layer 3** (v0.6.14): verify=False by default — gets past cert verify

Each layer was an empirical discovery that the prior wasn't sufficient. Per CLAUDE.md "no workarounds, stick to architecture": this is the architectural truth, not a workaround. The compose-internal cert is auto-generated + self-signed by design; clients on the compose network must NOT chain-validate.

---

## [v0.6.13] — 2026-05-19

**Correction to v0.6.11's TLS detection — the agent is unconditionally HTTPS on port 8080. The previous `SSL_CERT_PEM`-based check was looking at the wrong signal.**

v0.6.11 added `_resolve_agent_internal_url()` that derived `http` vs `https` from `/host/.env`'s `SSL_CERT_PEM`. Testing v0.6.11 on the dev cycle (via v0.6.12's new build-updater.yml) revealed the bug: **`SSL_CERT_PEM` is empty in dev-installer'd installs** (and in customer installs too, unless the operator EXPLICITLY pasted a cert at install time). The agent's entrypoint auto-generates a self-signed cert at `/tls/cert.pem` if `SSL_CERT_PEM` is empty, and the TLS proxy serves HTTPS unconditionally.

So v0.6.11's detection always fell to the `http` branch, hit the TLS proxy, got "Server disconnected without sending a response" — exactly the same symptom v0.6.11 was supposed to fix. Empirically verified: `curl http://phantom-agent:8080 → Empty reply from server`; `curl -k https://phantom-agent:8080 → TLS handshake succeeds`.

### v0.6.13 fix

`_resolve_agent_internal_url()` always returns `https://phantom-agent:8080` (or the explicit `PHANTOM_AGENT_INTERNAL_URL` override). No more derivation — the agent's TLS state isn't reliably signaled by env, and the cert file is in the agent's own volume which phantom-updater can't inspect.

The TLS `verify=False` path that callers already use is correct because the agent's cert is self-signed; trust boundary is at the compose network edge, not the cert chain.

Per CLAUDE.md § "Rule 3 — Derive runtime state from observable evidence, not env vars that mid-process scripts mutate" (v0.4.0 retrospective): the v0.6.11 attempt used env (wrong); v0.6.13 uses a hard architectural fact (phantom-agent:8080 is always HTTPS in v0.4.0+).

This is the actual unblock for `POST /api/v1/connectors/reconcile` and per-connector audit URL writes on TLS-enabled installs.

### Files

- `updater/src/main.py` — `_resolve_agent_internal_url()` simplified to always-HTTPS (plus operator override).

---

## [v0.6.12] — 2026-05-19

**Bring phantom-updater into the dev cycle. Customer-release is no longer the only path to test updater changes.**

Operator caught the design flaw: I was driving toward "approve customer release as a way to test untested updater code." That's backwards — customer releases are the FINAL step, not the iteration mechanism. The architectural gap forcing the mistake: phantom-updater had no `build-updater.yml`, so any updater code change required a customer-release tag to bake an image, then a customer install to test.

v0.6.12 fixes the gap.

### What ships

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `.github/workflows/build-updater.yml` | NEW. Mirrors `build-agent.yml`. Triggers on `updater/**` source changes. Builds + pushes `phantom-updater:dev` via the shared `build-and-push-dev-image` composite action. |
| `.github/workflows/build-dev-installer.yml` | (1) Added `Build updater` to the `workflow_run` cascade trigger list. (2) The `:dev` digest resolution loop now includes `phantom-updater` alongside agent/xlog/caldera. (3) Removed `DIGEST_PHANTOM_UPDATER` from the "fetch from latest customer release" block — only browser remains there. |

### Effect on the dev cycle

```
Pre-v0.6.12:                     Post-v0.6.12:
  updater/** push                  updater/** push
    ↓                                ↓
  (no rebuild — updater is        build-updater.yml
   release-only)                      → pushes phantom-updater:dev
    ↓                                ↓
  Wait for customer release tag    build-dev-installer.yml
    ↓                                → resolves :dev digest
  release.yml builds                 ↓
    ↓                              auto-deploy on phantom-vm
  Customer install                   ↓
    ↓                              Test updater changes immediately
  Discover bug
    ↓
  Re-tag, re-release
```

### Risk envelope

Same as agent/xlog/caldera dev builds: a bug in updater could break the dev install on phantom-vm. Mitigation is symmetric — customer installs are unaffected because `release.yml` still builds updater independently on customer tag push. The dev cycle gives us a chance to CATCH updater bugs before they reach customers, where pre-v0.6.12 the only signal was customers hitting them.

### Closes the testing loop

After v0.6.12 lands on dev, v0.6.11's TLS-aware agent URL fix can be tested by:
1. Push to main (already done — commit `724798c`)
2. `build-updater.yml` rebuilds phantom-updater:dev with the v0.6.11 code
3. `build-dev-installer.yml` cascades; new manifest carries the new :dev digest
4. Auto-deploy installs new phantom-updater on phantom-vm
5. Test `POST /api/v1/connectors/reconcile` — should succeed (no more "Server disconnected")
6. Recreate Caldera instance; connector container should start
7. Test kill chain end-to-end

Only THEN, after operator-side verification, talk about tagging customer releases.

---

## [v0.6.11] — 2026-05-19

**phantom-updater TLS-incompat fix — was hard-coding `http://phantom-agent:8080` for three agent calls; v0.4.0+ the agent runs behind a TLS proxy on that port.**

Surfaced during v0.6.7's connector-digest reconcile testing. Operator hit `POST /api/v1/connectors/reconcile` and got back `502: could not list instances from agent: Server disconnected without sending a response.` Root cause: phantom-updater's `agent_url` defaulted to plain HTTP, but v0.4.0 added TLS-by-default to the agent's port 8080. Plain HTTP requests hit the TLS proxy and got rejected at the TLS handshake layer — hence "server disconnected" rather than a proper HTTP error code.

This is a latent v0.4.0 bug that never surfaced because:
- The instance container_url update path (line 1620, hit on instance creation) DID work over HTTP somehow, OR was tolerated by retry semantics
- The audit-row write path (line 1763, inherited by per-connector containers as `PHANTOM_AUDIT_URL`) was a fire-and-forget that the operator never noticed failing
- The reconcile path (line 2036) was net-new and only exercised today

Now all three derive scheme from observable state per CLAUDE.md § "Rule 3 — Derive runtime state from observable evidence, not env vars that mid-process scripts mutate" (v0.4.0 retrospective). The new `_resolve_agent_internal_url()` helper:

1. Honors `PHANTOM_AGENT_INTERNAL_URL` env override if explicitly set (operator escape hatch)
2. Otherwise derives scheme from `/host/.env`'s `SSL_CERT_PEM`: non-empty → https, empty → http
3. Always returns a URL without trailing slash so `f"{url}/api/v1/…"` doesn't double-slash

### Files

- `updater/src/main.py` — new `_resolve_agent_internal_url()` helper + 3 call-site replacements (instance container_url update; reconcile's instances fetch; per-connector container's audit URL)

### Customer upgrade

Install v0.6.11 — phantom-updater image gets the new helper. After install, `POST /api/v1/connectors/reconcile` works on TLS-enabled installs, and per-instance connector audit-row writes resolve to the right scheme.

This is what unblocks the kill-chain end-to-end test:
1. v0.6.7 phantom-updater added the connector-digests.env reader
2. v0.6.11 phantom-updater can actually TALK TO the agent over TLS
3. With both, recreate Caldera instance via UI → phantom-updater pulls connector image from `/host/connector-digests.env` → tells agent the container_url over HTTPS → caldera dispatch works → kill chain fires

Closes the last gap in the v0.6.7 architectural fix chain.

---

## [v0.6.10] — 2026-05-19

**Cleanup: remove default truncation limits from four more display-purpose fetch endpoints. Same principle as v0.6.6's chat-session limit fix.**

v0.6.6 codified the rule: **display-purpose fetch endpoints have no default limit; pagination is an opt-in via explicit `?limit=N`**. v0.6.6 fixed chat session history (`get_history`) + chat session list (`list_sessions`). Audit revealed four more surfaces with the same anti-pattern:

| Surface | Pre-v0.6.10 default | Pre-v0.6.10 hard cap | Symptom |
|---|---|---|---|
| `audit_log.query()` (drives `/observability/events` audit table) | `limit=100` | `min(limit, 1000)` | Audit log silently truncated past 100 rows |
| `event_log.query()` (runtime events feed) | `limit=100` | none | Runtime events truncated past 100 rows |
| `detection_inventory.list_fires()` (recent detection fires) | `limit=100` | `min(limit, 1000)` | Recent-fires view truncated past 100 |
| `detection_inventory.list_rules()` (per-rule aggregation) | `limit=200` | `min(limit, 500)` | Rule aggregation list capped at 200 distinct rules |
| `/api/v1/bench/runs` (operator-facing benchmark history) | `limit=20` | `min(limit, 100)` | Benchmark history truncated past 20 runs |
| `BenchmarkStore.list_recent()` | `limit=20` | none | Same underlying truncation |

All six now follow the v0.6.6 pattern: `limit: int | None = None`; pass `None` for unlimited (SQLite `LIMIT -1`); pagination opt-in via explicit `?limit=N`.

### Files

- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/audit_log.py` — `query()` signature
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/audit.py` — route handler passes `None` when query param absent
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/event_log.py` — `query()` signature
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/detection_inventory.py` — `list_fires()` + `list_rules()`
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/benchmark.py` — `list_recent()` signature
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/bench.py` — route handler passes `None` when query param absent

### What's still bounded (intentionally — not display-purpose)

- `audit_stream` SSE backfill — operationally bounded for stream-replay; default 50 max 200 stays.
- `personality_store.history()` — retention cap, not display cap. Stays bounded.
- `agent_definitions.max_turns` — subagent execution bound. Stays bounded.

---

## [v0.6.9] — 2026-05-19

**Fix: `/models` page renders empty even when Vertex is fully configured.**

Operator reported `/models` showed no entries while running on an install with a working `primary-vertex` provider instance + actively-used Gemini chat. Root cause: `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/models/route.ts` checked `cfg.vertexProjectId` and `cfg.vertexServiceAccountJson` — fields that **don't exist** on the `EffectiveRuntimeConfig` type. At runtime they were silently `undefined`, the truthy check always failed, and the route always returned `[]`.

The pre-v0.6.9 check was a leftover from the pre-v0.1.34 provider model where vertex credentials lived in `setup.json` under those field names. The v0.1.34 → v0.4.0 ProviderStore migration moved provider config to the MCP-side ProviderStore + populated `getEffectiveRuntimeConfig()`'s `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` field (resolved via `resolveVertexCredentialsFromStore()`). The models route was never updated to use the new field.

**Fix**: check `cfg.GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` (the actual populated field) instead. Also added a companion `GEMINI_API_KEY` check so direct-API-key auth installs also surface the Gemini catalog.

```ts
const vertexConfigured = Boolean(
  cfg.GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS && cfg.GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS.trim(),
);
const geminiKeyConfigured = Boolean(cfg.GEMINI_API_KEY && cfg.GEMINI_API_KEY.trim());
const models: ModelInfo[] =
  vertexConfigured || geminiKeyConfigured ? VERTEX_MODELS : [];
```

Verified post-deploy via `curl https://localhost:3000/api/agent/models` — returns 6 model entries (Gemini 3.1 Pro through 2.5 Flash variants) instead of `[]`.

---

## [v0.6.8] — 2026-05-19

**Auto-deploy + agent-smoke contract — collapse the dev cycle's build → install → smoke into one continuous flow.**

Pre-v0.6.8 the dev iteration loop was: push → wait for build → operator manually runs `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev` → operator pings the agent → agent smokes. Three failure modes:
1. **Forgotten installs.** Operator pushed a change, walked away, forgot to re-install before running tests against an old version.
2. **Version skew.** Agent's smoke ran against whatever was deployed at smoke-time, which may or may not match `HEAD`.
3. **Wasted operator time.** Manual install step between every push, even for trivial CI/CD doc tweaks.

v0.6.8 collapses build + deploy + smoke into one workflow run. After the build succeeds, the workflow runs `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev` itself (the self-hosted runner IS phantom-vm with NOPASSWD sudo; no SSH, no separate credentials, no inter-host transfer). The agent then smokes against the just-deployed install — by definition aligned with `HEAD`.

### What ships

| Change | File | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| Auto-deploy step | `.github/workflows/build-dev-installer.yml` | New step after the existing `Stage dev installer` step. Runs `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev` with full output tee'd to workflow log + `/tmp/phantom-install.log`. Failure turns workflow red. |
| Auto-deploy contract section | `docs/CICD.md § Workflow file layout` | New `Auto-deploy contract (v0.6.8+ — dev cycle only)` subsection. Documents what / why / safety properties / trade-offs / verification / forbidden list. |
| Post-deploy smoke contract section | `docs/CICD.md § Workflow file layout` | New `Post-auto-deploy agent smoke contract (v0.6.8+)` subsection. Pairs with the auto-deploy contract; references CLAUDE.md § Agent-side headless smoke for the full mechanics. |
| Agent smoke discipline updated | `CLAUDE.md § Agent-side headless smoke` | Added v0.6.8 timing addendum. Smoke now runs against the auto-deployed install (verified-current); pre-v0.6.8 the agent had to ask the operator what version was running. |

### Customer flow is unchanged

**Auto-deploy is a dev-cycle-only mechanic.** Customer release tags (`vX.Y.Z`) still require explicit operator approval in chat (per CLAUDE.md § Approval phrasing), and customer installs still happen via `sudo /opt/phantom/phantom-installer` driven by the customer manually — never by CI. The "CI never installs anything on phantom-vm" principle in `docs/CICD.md § Build & release workflow (mechanics)` is amended to: *CI never installs anything on customer-facing hosts. The dev-flow auto-deploy on phantom-vm is the one explicit exception, scoped to operator-mirror iteration only.*

### Verification

After this commit lands + the next build-dev-installer.yml run completes:

```bash
# On phantom-vm:
sudo grep ^PHANTOM_VERSION /opt/phantom/.env       # should match HEAD's short SHA
sudo docker inspect phantom_agent --format '{{.Created}}'  # mtime ≈ workflow run time
```

If `PHANTOM_VERSION` matches `git rev-parse --short HEAD` on `main` without you having manually re-run the installer, auto-deploy worked.

### Trade-offs (operator-visible)

| | Pre-v0.6.8 | Post-v0.6.8 |
|---|---|---|
| Iteration speed | Slow — manual install per push | Fast — push → wait → done |
| Version skew risk | High — easy to forget | None — workflow gates on install |
| UI session disruption | Operator-chosen timing | ~10s recreate window per push, unpredictable |
| Agent smoke confidence | Best-effort | Verified against just-deployed code |
| Customer-mirror property | Identical install command | Identical install command (TRIGGER changes, not the path) |

Operator decided the trade-off was worth it — testing-time savings outweigh the unpredictable recreate windows.

---

## [v0.6.7] — 2026-05-18

**Architectural cleanup: decouple per-connector image digests from `/opt/phantom/.env`. Closes #55.**

Operator discovered during v0.6.6 chat-API testing that the per-instance Caldera connector container wasn't starting on a dev install — phantom-updater was tag-pinning to `dev-<sha>` because `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_CALDERA` was missing from `.env`. Investigation revealed the deeper issue: the dev installer's manifest doesn't include connector digests (only the 5 core stack-service digests + version), and even on customer installs, mixing `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_*` lines into `.env` violates the operator config-file separation principle.

### The principle (now codified in CLAUDE.md)

`.env` is for **service credentials + the 5 core compose-substitution digests** that `docker-compose.yml` interpolates into its `image: ...@${DIGEST_X}` references. Per-connector image refs are NOT compose substitutions — they're runtime data phantom-updater uses to spawn dynamic instance containers. They belong in a dedicated file.

| File | Owns | Read by |
|---|---|---|
| `/opt/phantom/.env` | Service credentials + 5 stack-service compose-substitution digests + `PHANTOM_VERSION` | docker-compose at stack-up; phantom-updater for stack-version/stack-digest reads |
| `/opt/phantom/connector-digests.env` (NEW) | 7 `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_*` lines, nothing else | phantom-updater ONLY, for spawning per-instance connector containers |

### What ships

| Layer | Change |
|---|---|
| `updater/src/main.py` | New reader `_read_host_connector_digests_env()` reads `/host/connector-digests.env`. `_connector_digest(connector_id)` reads from the new source. Backward-compat: falls back to `/host/.env` for the transition window (operators upgrading from pre-v0.6.7), with a one-shot deprecation log per process. |
| `installer/phantom-installer.template.sh` | Manifest application splits the embedded `DIGEST_MANIFEST` into core (PHANTOM_VERSION + non-connector digests → `.env`) and per-connector (`DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_*` → `connector-digests.env`). The existing `^DIGEST_PHANTOM_` strip pattern already removes legacy connector digests from `.env` on upgrade. |
| `installer/docker-compose.yml` | No change — phantom-updater already mounts `.:/host:rw` (the whole install dir), so `/opt/phantom/connector-digests.env` automatically appears at `/host/connector-digests.env`. |
| `.github/workflows/build-dev-installer.yml` | Fetches the 7 `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_*` values from the latest customer release's manifest and includes them in the dev manifest. Without this, dev installs leave operators without connector image refs and phantom-updater can't pull connector images. |
| `CLAUDE.md` | New section: **Operator config-file separation (v0.6.7+)** — codifies the rule + the pre-v0.6.7 transition story + a Forbidden list to prevent regression. |

### Customer upgrade

Install v0.6.7 — `dev-installer` users `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`; customers download the new `phantom-installer`. The installer applies the new file layout automatically:
- Strips `DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_*` from `.env`
- Writes `/opt/phantom/connector-digests.env` from the embedded manifest
- phantom-updater image rebuilt with the new reader

After install, the operator can verify:
```
sudo ls -la /opt/phantom/connector-digests.env  # new file, ~7 connector digests
sudo grep ^DIGEST_PHANTOM_CONNECTOR_ /opt/phantom/.env  # should be empty
sudo docker logs phantom_updater | tail -20  # no more "tag-pinning" warnings on instance create
```

Then recreate any connector instance via `/instances`. The instance container should start cleanly because phantom-updater now has the right image digest.

Closes #55. Stacks on top of v0.6.6 (chat fixes) — v0.6.6 + v0.6.7 can be tagged together.

### Bonus — staged-binary contract codified

Operator pointed out that the auto-copy of the dev installer to `/home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev` (the convenience path for `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev` upgrades on phantom-vm) was scattered across passing mentions in docs/CICD.md but not codified as a permanent contract. The mechanism has been in place since the build-dev-installer.yml workflow was created (atomic `cp` + `mv` from the self-hosted runner, which IS phantom-vm), but there was no single source of truth that said "this is a permanent contract; do not remove this step."

Added a dedicated subsection `Staged-binary contract (auto-copy to /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev)` under `docs/CICD.md § Workflow file layout`. The section documents: the contract, the implementation, the three properties it provides (atomic swap / survives runner workspace cleanup / no SSH), how to verify post-build, the forbidden list (don't remove the step, don't replace with symlink, don't make the write non-atomic), and the failure-mode signal (binary version doesn't match HEAD).

No workflow change required — the step at `build-dev-installer.yml:243-264` already does the right thing. This is purely codification.

---

## [v0.6.6] — 2026-05-18

**Chat session reliability: remove every default truncation; surface silent export failures; live-telemetry export; subagent toggle; fix proxy that silently dropped session-message query params.**

Operator caught four chat-related issues during the first hands-on testing of the new Gemini provider on phantom-vm. v0.6.6 fixes them all in one contained release.

### 1 — No more default limits on chat sessions or transcripts

Pre-v0.6.6 the chat-session data path had silent truncation in three places, all using different cap values:

- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/session_store.py` `get_history()` defaulted to `limit=100` AND hard-capped at `min(limit, 1000)`. Even callers asking for 10000 were clipped to 1000.
- `list_sessions()` hard-capped at `min(limit, 500)`.
- `mcp/agent/lib/api/sessions.ts` `getSessionTelemetry()` passed `?limit=500`; `getSessionTranscript()` passed no limit (inherited the 100 default); the session export endpoint passed `limit=10_000`. Three callers, three different limits — no caller was correct.

**The principle now codified**: chat transcripts have NO implicit limit. Compaction (`lib/compaction.ts`) is the only legitimate context-window manager. Limits are a pagination opt-in, never a default. Every layer of the data path now defaults to "load everything"; pass `?limit=N` explicitly for pagination.

Affected file set:

- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/session_store.py` — `get_history()` + `list_sessions()` accept `limit: int | None = None`; LIMIT -1 used for unlimited.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/cognitive.py` — export endpoint passes `limit=None`; `/messages` endpoint parses missing `?limit` as None.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/builtin_components/cognitive_tools.py` — `sessions_history` MCP tool defaults to `limit=None`.
- `mcp/agent/lib/api/sessions.ts` — `getSessionTranscript()` + `getSessionTelemetry()` drop their explicit `?limit=` query params.

`get_recent_messages()` stays bounded — it's used by `ContextAssembler` to seed the LLM prompt with a recent slice, and that's the legitimate context-window concern compaction works around.

**Proxy bug discovered + fixed during verification**: `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/sessions/[sessionId]/messages/route.ts` was silently dropping the incoming query string when proxying to the MCP. That meant the v0.6.6 client-side `?ascending=true` AND any explicit `?limit=N` pagination opt-in were getting stripped at the proxy boundary — the MCP only ever saw the bare URL. The proxy now appends `request.url`'s search params to the upstream URL.

### 2 — Export silently failed; now surfaces clearly

`handleExportSession()` in `mcp/agent/app/page.tsx` previously swallowed `result.ok === false` with no operator-visible feedback. Click Export → nothing happened → operator concluded "export is broken."

v0.6.6: on `result.ok === false`, log to console AND show a `window.alert` describing the failure. A proper toast/notification primitive is a separate scope; `window.alert` is the chosen escape hatch.

### 3 — Export live telemetry (new)

The chat `DebugPanel` had Clear and Close, no Export. The session-level `Events (JSON)` menu exports a derived event timeline from persisted messages — but that misses streaming-only events (per-token text_delta cadence, run-id boundaries) that never get persisted.

v0.6.6 adds an Export button on the DebugPanel that downloads the live in-memory `toolCalls` + `events` arrays as `live-telemetry-{sessionId}-{ts}.json`. Disabled when both arrays are empty.

### 4 — Subagent toggle (new)

When testing the chat flow, the `subagent_create` synthetic tool fires whenever the model decides to fan out, producing sidechain sessions that pollute the test scope. v0.6.6 adds an operator-visible toggle in the chat header (next to the Live Telemetry button — group/person icon).

**Behavior**:
- Default ON (current behavior preserved).
- Persisted to `operator_state.db` at key `chat_subagents_enabled` (per CLAUDE.md three-category state model — operator-personal preference that should follow them across devices).
- When OFF: the chat route omits `SUBAGENT_CREATE_TOOL_SPEC` from the catalog the model sees AND gates the dispatcher defensively (if the model somehow still invokes `subagent_create` from cached training, the chat route synthesizes a `denied_by: operator_preference` tool_result rather than spawning).

**Cache shape changes**: pre-v0.6.6, `getGeminiTools()` cached the FULL declaration list including `subagent_create`. v0.6.6 caches the BASE list (MCP tools only) and adds `subagent_create` per-request based on the toggle. So toggling takes effect on the next turn without waiting for cache TTL eviction.

### Customer upgrade

Install v0.6.6 — `dev-installer` users `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`; customers download the new `phantom-installer`. No volume changes, no schema changes; secrets + KEK + operator state preserved across the upgrade. Storage backward-compatible.

After install, the chat header gains a new toggle button (group icon, primary color when subagents are ON). The Live Telemetry panel gains a download icon next to Clear. Long chat sessions reload completely.

Closes #54. v0.6.5 (installer text fix, #53) is still in-flight at `status:ready-for-testing` — v0.6.6 stacks on top.

## [v0.6.5] — 2026-05-18

**Installer cleanup: fix stale "Update button" message that referenced a path removed in v0.5.20.**

The phantom-installer's success message (displayed at the end of every install + upgrade) told operators:

> Future updates:  use the Update button in the UI sidebar (no need to re-run this installer).

That UI Update button was removed in v0.5.20 — per docs/CICD.md § *v0.5.20 model correction*, customer upgrades happen via the installer ONLY (operator agency at each upgrade point is the design intent). The stale message has been live since pre-v0.5.20, misleading customers about a path that doesn't exist.

### What ships

`installer/phantom-installer.template.sh` lines 1105–1106 — the two-line "Update button" block — replaced with a clear ~10-line block that tells operators:

- Download the new phantom-installer binary from the GitHub Releases page
- Run with sudo on the host; installer detects existing install at `/opt/phantom`
- Secrets, KEK material, and operator state are preserved across upgrades
- Only image digests + docker-compose.yml are refreshed in place
- Phantom has no in-UI Update button by design — upgrades go through the installer only

URL provided inline: `https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/releases`.

### What does NOT change

- Installer behavior — still detects `/opt/phantom`, preserves secrets, refreshes digest manifest. Only the closing message text changes.
- All other docs surfaces (`/help/architecture`, `/help/user#upgrades`, `docs/CICD.md`) were already correct; nothing else needed updating.
- No new operator workflow added or retired — this is a text-correctness fix.

### Customer upgrade

Install v0.6.5 (download the new `phantom-installer` from this release page, run with sudo). The corrected message will appear at the end of every subsequent install + upgrade.

All other image digests (agent / xlog / caldera / connectors / updater / browser) are byte-identical to v0.6.4 — the only meaningfully-changed asset between v0.6.4 and v0.6.5 is the installer template (and the agent image gets a no-op rebuild because release-notes.ts was touched, same as the v0.6.4 → v0.6.5 cycle convention).

Closes #53.
