## [v0.6.4] — 2026-05-18

**Hotfix continuation: v0.6.3's fix is correct but the v0.6.0-v0.6.3 vanilla-retag chain needed a content-touch to break out.**

The v0.6.3 fix made `release.yml` pull `:dev` instead of rebuilding from vanilla source when `CHANGED=1` — but between v0.6.2 → v0.6.3 commits, ONLY workflow + docs files changed (no caldera-content), so the detection correctly said "unchanged" and retagged v0.6.2's (vanilla) digest as v0.6.3. The retag chain propagated forward.

v0.6.4 breaks the chain by adding a real content marker tag to `phantom-master-killchain.yml`. The detection now fires `CHANGED=1`, the v0.6.3 fix kicks in (`docker pull :dev` + retag), and the customer image gets the actual `:dev` content (which `build-caldera.yml` has correctly built with the Phantom + CTID overlay since v0.5.91).

After v0.6.4, the chain restarts on a correct baseline. Future v0.6.x patches that don't touch caldera-content will retag v0.6.4's digest (which IS correct) — same caldera-state-preservation behavior as designed, just from a non-vanilla baseline.

### Customer upgrade — install v0.6.4 to get the actual Phantom content

Same content as v0.6.3 intended to ship. The marker tag in `phantom-master-killchain.yml` is the only content difference vs. `:dev`, and is operator-visible only as an extra tag string on the adversary in Caldera UI.

---

## [v0.6.3] — 2026-05-18

**Critical hotfix: this is the FIRST customer release that actually ships the Phantom plugin content in the caldera image.**

If you installed v0.5.57 or any later version through v0.6.2, your caldera image is vanilla aymanam-style Caldera. **No Phantom adversaries (Master Killchain, Ransack curated, Super Spy, Thief, etc.) have ever been in a customer image.** v0.6.1's hotfix corrected one bug (detection); v0.6.2's path-fix exposed a deeper bug; v0.6.3 finally ships the actual content.

### What went wrong

`release.yml`'s "Build or retag phantom-caldera" step had `docker build third_party/caldera/` with NO overlay step. The Phantom plugin content + CTID adversary emulation plans are only overlaid by `build-caldera.yml` (the `:dev` builder), never by `release.yml`. Every customer release rebuild produced vanilla Caldera.

v0.6.1's bug was a SYMPTOM (detection said "no rebuild needed" so retagged v0.6.0). v0.6.2's CHANGED=1 rebuild ALSO produced vanilla output because of this deeper issue.

### v0.6.3 fix

`release.yml` now pulls the `:dev` image (which `build-caldera.yml` correctly builds with the full Phantom + CTID overlay) and retags it as `:vX.Y.Z`. Single source of truth for caldera content: `build-caldera.yml`. Falls back to vanilla rebuild only if `:dev` pull fails.

### Customer upgrade

**If you installed v0.5.57 through v0.6.2**: download + install v0.6.3 to get the actual Phantom adversary content. Caldera UI → Adversaries should show "Phantom: Master Killchain", "Phantom: Ransack (curated)", "Phantom: Super Spy (curated)", "Phantom: Thief (curated)", "Phantom: Lateral Movement Sweep". If those are missing, you're on a vanilla image.

What v0.6.3 actually ships (effectively v0.6.1's + v0.6.2's intended content + everything since v0.5.57):
- Phantom Master Killchain (22-step adversary, 9 MITRE tactics validated against Cortex XDR)
- Phantom Ransack / Super Spy / Thief / Lateral Movement Sweep curated adversaries
- Phantom Phishing v3.3 (Office macro → WINWORD → cmd → PSh → BITS → dropped Caldera implant with dynamic discovery)
- T1518.001 modern PowerShell discovery (Cortex XDR enumerated by name, replaces wmic-deprecated stockpile)
- CTID Adversary Emulation Plans (APT29 / FIN6 / OilRig / etc.) baked into the emu plugin
- Lab-safe lookalike abilities + decoy file setup

All other image digests (agent / xlog / connectors / updater / browser) are byte-identical to v0.6.2 — only phantom-caldera changes between v0.6.2 and v0.6.3.

---

## [v0.6.2] — 2026-05-18

**Hotfix release: fixes v0.6.1's silent regression where the caldera image was retagged from v0.6.0 instead of rebuilt with the new content.**

If you installed v0.6.1, **download + install v0.6.2 to get the actual phishing v3.3 + #52 fix content**. Your v0.6.1 caldera image was bit-identical to v0.6.0 — none of the v3.3 phishing dropper or T1518.001 PSh discovery changes shipped. v0.6.2 republishes the caldera image correctly. All other image digests (agent / xlog / connectors / updater / browser) are byte-identical to v0.6.1 — only phantom-caldera changes between v0.6.1 and v0.6.2.

### Why this happened

`release.yml`'s per-service "what changed since the previous tag?" detection only watched `third_party/caldera/` (the submodule path). It missed our content overlay at `bundles/spark/caldera-content/` — even though `build-caldera.yml` already includes both paths when producing `:dev` images. The v0.6.1 release.yml run reported "success" but functionally retagged the v0.6.0 caldera digest as v0.6.1. release.yml's per-service path detection is now updated to watch both paths.

Documented as failure mode #13 in `docs/CICD.md` failure-mode catalog.

### What v0.6.2 actually delivers (effectively v0.6.1's intended contents)

Same as the v0.6.1 CHANGELOG entry below — all those changes ship for real in v0.6.2.

## [v0.6.1] — 2026-05-18

**Patch release: Phantom T1518.001 modern-PowerShell discovery + dynamic Caldera-implant detection in the phishing-dropper ability (Scenario 1 — code-only, no installer protocol changes, but the caldera image digest changes so re-running the v0.6.0 installer pulls the new content).**

This release rolls up 4 dev commits (v0.5.91-94) into a focused customer-visible improvement to the Phantom-curated adversary catalog. The headline gains: Phantom-bundled Ransack + Super Spy now ship modern Win10/Server-compatible AV/EDR + Firewall discovery (closing #52), and the phishing dropper auto-detects whatever the operator names their sandcat (no more hardcoded mars/venus list).

### Headline changes

- **Closes #52** — Phantom T1518.001 PowerShell replacements. Stockpile abilities `2dece965` (wmic AntiVirus) and `8c06ebf8` (Get-WmiObject Firewalls) fail systematically on Windows Server 2022 (wmic deprecated + `root\SecurityCenter2` namespace absent on Server). Replaced with:
  - **Phantom: Discover AV/EDR (4-path)** (`a52d1e10`) — Get-CimInstance SecurityCenter2 + Get-MpComputerStatus Defender + Get-Service filter for known EDR names + Get-Process filter. **Validated**: enumerated Cortex XDR (`cyserver` service) + Microsoft Defender on both Server 2022 hosts.
  - **Phantom: Identify Firewalls** (`b81f4c93`) — Get-NetFirewallProfile + Get-NetFirewallRule with netsh fallback. Returns per-profile Domain/Private/Public state + inbound/outbound rule counts. Stockpile mis-name fixed (the original "Identify Firewalls" ability actually queried AV WMI).
  - Curated Ransack + Super Spy adversaries updated to reference the new ability IDs. Master Killchain unaffected (didn't include the broken abilities).
- **Phishing v3.3 — dynamic Caldera-implant discovery** via process-tree walk. v0.6.0 shipped v3.1 with a hardcoded candidate list (`mars.exe`, `venus.exe`, `sandcat.go-windows.exe`). v3.3 walks the process tree from `$PID` upward, skipping known interpreters (`powershell`, `cmd`, `conhost`, `wsmprovhost`, `svchost`, etc.) until it finds the first non-interpreter ancestor — that's the running implant by definition. Ability is now immune to operator-renames; works for any sandcat binary name.
- **Validated end-to-end**: curated Ransack chain success rate jumped from 78.6% → **94.4%** after the #52 fix. Only T1018 `nltest` workgroup-environment failure remains (env-dep, not fixable). Master Killchain re-run with v3.3 phishing produced identical 88.6% chain quality + 9 MITRE tactics + 22 techniques + 74 new Cortex alerts rolling into incident 1794 (140 → 214 total).

### Bug fixes

- Stockpile T1518.001 abilities now SUPERSEDED by Phantom modern replacements in curated adversaries. Stock adversaries unchanged (Phantom doesn't modify stockpile in place).
- v0.6.0's hardcoded mars/venus list could break for operators using non-standard sandcat names. v0.6.1's process-tree walk has no such failure mode.

### Documentation

- `docs/caldera-adversary-battery.md` — new "Curated Ransack re-run with #52 fix" section + "Master Killchain end-to-end run #2" section. Both validate the v0.6.1 improvements with full per-ability scorecards + decoded EDR-detection outputs (Cortex XDR + Defender names enumerated).

### Upgrade notes for customers

- Re-run the v0.6.1 phantom-installer on your host. The caldera image digest changes (new content baked in), agent + xlog + connectors retain v0.6.0 digests. Storage backward-compatible (Scenario 1). Volumes preserved.
- In Caldera UI → Adversaries → the "Phantom: Ransack (curated)" + "Phantom: Super Spy (curated)" adversaries now run the new Phantom T1518.001 PowerShell discovery; you'll see Cortex XDR + Defender enumerated in the run output instead of wmic-deprecated errors.
- The phishing simulation ability no longer requires the implant to be named `mars.exe` or `venus.exe` — it auto-detects whatever's running.

## [v0.6.0] — 2026-05-18

**Major release: comprehensive Caldera adversary battery + Cortex XDR connector + Phantom Master Killchain (Scenario 2 — code + installer change, storage backward-compatible, customer installer rebuild required, volumes preserved).**

This release rolls up 84 dev commits since v0.5.5 into one customer-facing milestone. The headline: Phantom is now a detection-validation platform end-to-end. You run a Phantom-curated adversary in Caldera; the operator's chat agent queries Cortex XDR via the new connector; the loop closes inside Phantom without leaving the UI.

### Headline features

- **Phantom Master Killchain (curated)** — flagship 22-step adversary that combines the v0.5.57 phishing chain + Phantom decoy pre-condition + PowerKatz mid-chain. Validated against Cortex XDR: produces 60+ alerts across 7 MITRE tactics (Execution, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Defense Evasion, Credential Access, Lateral Movement, C2). Use this as the standard "does the lab work end-to-end" smoke test for new deployments. (See `docs/caldera-adversary-battery.md` for per-tactic alert breakdown.)
- **Phishing simulation v3.1 — Office macro drops Caldera implant**. The phishing-emailclient-spawn ability (`85d04954`) now produces the FULL initial-access EDR sub-tree: WINWORD.EXE (renamed cmd, T1036.003 Masquerading) → cmd /c → powershell (simulated VBA macro) → BITS transfer (T1197) → dropped implant binary (T1105 + WildFire Malware verdict). Lab-safe: dropped implant runs briefly with invalid beacon URL so no new paw is created. Auto-detects venus.exe vs mars.exe vs sandcat.exe on disk.
- **Cortex XDR connector** — the agent can now query Cortex cases + issues + run XQL queries directly via `^xdr_get_cases_and_issues`, `^xdr_run_xql_query`, `^xdr_get_incident_extra_data`. Detection-validation feedback loop now lives inside Phantom — no jumping to the XDR console mid-test.
- **Phantom-curated adversary catalog**: Ransack (`9c7f4d5a`) + Super Spy (`8b2f4d56`) + Thief (`7d3e1f8a`) + Lateral Movement Sweep (`e5a9c1b2`) + Master Killchain (`9d7b5a3c`). Each bypasses a structural limitation in stockpile adversaries (workgroup-environment DNS PTR gap, missing decoy files for ransomware-staging) so they actually dispatch on a clean lab. See `docs/caldera-release-plan.md` for the selection guide.
- **Phantom decoy + lateral target pre-condition abilities**: `phantom-ransack-decoy-setup.yml` (`7e88a8b1`) drops 27 lab-safe decoy files in `C:\Users\Public\Documents\Decoys` so T1005 sensitive-file discovery actually finds something; `phantom-lateral-target-facts.yml` (`a4f1b2c8`) documents the hardcoded lateral target for the Lateral Movement Sweep adversary.
- **`^command` chat invocation** — type `^xdr_get_cases_and_issues limit=5` in chat to call any MCP tool directly, bypassing the LLM. Works even without a provider configured. The detection-validation feedback loop is finally usable in development.
- **CTID Adversary Emulation Plans baked into the caldera image** — APT29/FIN6/OilRig/etc. adversary profiles ship by default. Operators no longer need to manually install the CTID emu plugin.
- **Lab-safe lookalike abilities** for techniques Cortex signature-blocks (e.g. mimikatz-arg lookalikes that produce detection telemetry without the actual LSA acquire being attempted).
- **Cortex KB search via the cortex-docs connector** — `^cortex_search` returns Palo Alto documentation pages indexed via Fluid Topics API. Useful for grounding agent responses on XDR-specific behavior questions.

### Bug fixes

- **XDR connector instance creation flow** (v0.5.61-77 chain) — fixed: api_key persisting to config instead of SecretStore (v0.5.73); edit-dialog feedback invisible (v0.5.73); KNOWN_CONNECTORS missing cortex-xdr (v0.5.73); functools.wraps signature mismatch causing FastMCP rejection (v0.5.75); instance-name slug for paths with spaces (v0.5.75); function-prefix vs connector-id mismatch (v0.5.76); `from usecase.instance_store` import crashing connector containers (v0.5.77).
- **Bug-family audit (v0.5.80)** — extended the v0.5.77 `from usecase.*` fix across caldera + xsiam + cortex-content connectors. New CLAUDE.md Rule 7 codifies the audit discipline.
- **Connector instance form widget vocabulary** — supports 11 widget types (text/url/string/number/password/secret/textarea/select/radio/multi_select/boolean/array) so per-connector schema declarations actually render the right UI (v0.5.70).
- **Test Connection button dirty-tracking** — fixed staleness in the /providers UI test-connection state (v0.5.71).
- **Caldera image install** — phantom + ctid emu plugins now reconciled at startup so neither overwrites the other (v0.5.65-66).
- **/help/cicd page** added with 6 SVG diagrams covering installer separation, build pipeline, change scenarios, release lifecycle, customer upgrade, and smoke testing discipline (v0.5.78-79).

### Documentation

- `docs/caldera-adversary-battery.md` — durable matrix of 14 adversaries run against the lab, per-tactic Cortex coverage, per-ability impact ranking, XDR cross-reference (cases 1794+1795). The detection-validation source-of-truth.
- `docs/caldera-release-plan.md` — operator-facing release plan with smoke-test matrix.
- `docs/CICD.md` — CI/CD pipeline mechanics, change scenarios, failure-mode catalog (~11 patterns encountered + fixed).

### Upgrade notes for customers

- **Storage**: backward-compatible (Scenario 2). Volumes preserved. Re-run the new installer; pass `WIPE_VOLUMES=false` (the default).
- **Caldera adversaries**: when you open Caldera UI → Adversaries, you'll see 6 new "Phantom: ..." profiles. Use Master Killchain for broad-coverage testing.
- **XDR connector**: install via Marketplace → cortex-xdr, then `/connectors → Add Cortex XDR Instance`. Pre-existing customers: optional install. New deployments: encouraged.
- **Skills volume**: the per-release marker auto-merges new image-baked default skills on first boot (v0.3.2+ behavior). No manual `docker exec` needed.

---

## [v0.5.80] — 2026-05-17

**Bug-family audit: extend the v0.5.77 cortex-xdr `usecase.*` fix across all sibling bundle connectors + add Rule 7 (Bug-family audit) to the smoke discipline (closes #51).** Operator's hands-on smoke caught the gap when `^cortex_search` failed with `no container_url` — same bug family as the pre-v0.5.77 cortex-xdr error. Their critique: "you have tested one connector command, one instance command, but did not review the rest to fix for the same problem, and they are related... your testing should be covering the different angles of the feature." This release is the postmortem.

### Why

The v0.5.75-77 cortex-xdr fix chain (functools.wraps, __all__, `from config.config import get_config` replacing `from usecase.instance_store`, function-prefix mismatch, instance-name slug) addressed a connector bug family. I fixed the ONE connector that surfaced the bugs but didn't audit the SIBLING connectors for the same patterns. The blast radius of a connector-system bug is rarely one connector — usually a code pattern copy-evolved across the family.

Bug-family audit results (every bundle connector is `runtimeMapping.style: container`):

| Connector | `from usecase.*` (crashes container) | Missing `__all__` | Already fixed |
|---|---|---|---|
| caldera | YES — `usecase.instance_store` | YES | NO |
| cortex-content | NO (try/except fallback) | YES | partial |
| cortex-docs | NO | NO (has `__all__`) | clean |
| cortex-xdr | NO (v0.5.77 fixed) | NO (v0.5.75 fixed) | YES |
| web | NO | NO | clean |
| xlog | NO | NO | clean |
| xsiam | YES — `usecase.instance_store` + `usecase.kb_store` (fallback) | YES | NO |

caldera + xsiam both had the same `from usecase.instance_store import instance_store` pattern that crashed in container mode. cortex-content's three `usecase.*` references all had `try/except ImportError` fallbacks (returned clean operator-facing errors instead of crashing), but cortex-content was missing `__all__`. Operator's `^cortex_search` failure traced to a different bug (cortex-docs instance had no container_url because the container never started — same v0.5.73 #46-bug-3 family that we manually unblock with `docker run`).

### What ships

#### `bundles/spark/connectors/caldera/src/connector.py`

- `_get_caldera_config()` rewrote to use `from config.config import get_config` — the runtime-native config proxy. Same fix pattern as cortex-xdr v0.5.77.
- Added `__all__` listing all 58 public tool functions. The runtime no longer falls back to auto-discovery for caldera.

#### `bundles/spark/connectors/xsiam/src/connector.py`

- `_get_xsiam_config()` rewrote to use `from config.config import get_config`. The function returns a flat dict with all field names downstream `_get_fetcher` looks up (api_url + papiUrl + xsiam_papi_url + baseUrl aliases; api_id + papiAuthId + xsiam_api_id + api_key_id aliases; etc.). Backward-compatible field-name fallbacks preserved.
- The `usecase.kb_store` import at line 619 is the `find_xql_examples_rag` tool path; it already has a try/except ImportError that returns a clean operator-facing error in container mode. No change needed.
- Added `__all__` listing the 14 public tool functions.

#### `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-content/src/connector.py`

- Already uses `from config.config import get_config` in `_get_client()` — gold-standard pattern; no source change needed.
- Added `__all__` listing the 10 public tool functions.

#### `CLAUDE.md` — new Rule 7 (Bug-family audit)

The v0.5.75 "Agent-side headless smoke" addendum had six rules. v0.5.80 adds a seventh — codifying the discipline gap the operator just caught:

> **Rule 7 — Bug-family audit.** When fixing a bug in a connector-system file, audit sibling connectors for the same bug pattern in the same release. Identify the bug pattern as a grep expression. Run it across `bundles/spark/connectors/*/src/` and `bundles/spark/mcp/src/` (also `phantom-connector-runtime/runtime/` if the pattern could live there). For each hit, fix it in the same release OR document the gap inline with a tracking-issue reference. Do NOT silently leave a known-broken sibling.

Forbidden under Rule 7: fixing one connector for a class of bug + walking away from the audit. The fix isn't done until grep returns no hits OR every remaining hit has a documented reason for being left alone.

#### `mcp/agent/app/help/cicd/page.tsx`

- Smoke Testing Discipline section: "six rules" → "seven rules" + new bullet 7 in the rules list. Cross-links to the CLAUDE.md addendum's full Rule 7 text.

#### `mcp/agent/components/diagrams/cicd-smoke-discipline.tsx`

- Diagram refactored to include Rule 7 as a full-width strip below the original 6-card grid. Bordered with the "shared" accent (orange) so it visually reads as a discipline ADDITION rather than an original rule.
- Bottom panels (state classification + postmortem loop) shifted +45px down to accommodate. viewBox grew 880 → 920.
- Postmortem-loop label updated: "POSTMORTEM LOOP (RULE 6)" → "POSTMORTEM LOOP (RULES 6+7)" — both rules are loop-driven additions.

### Smoke-test bullets

After install (re-run `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`):

- [ ] ✓ **agent-verified** (during cascade): build-connectors rebuilds caldera, xsiam, cortex-content with the new source.
- [ ] ⨯ **agent-verified-blocked**: end-to-end deployment validation gated by operator re-install + the v0.5.73 #46-bug-3 KNOWN_CONNECTORS-on-updater-image issue (still customer-release-only).
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: `^cortex_search query="Broker VM Azure"` returns 20+ real Cortex docs hits (already verified — top result was "Set up Broker VM on Microsoft Azure"). Should persist across re-install.
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: `^xdr_get_cases_and_issues limit=3` still returns real XDR incidents (regression check across v0.5.77 + v0.5.80).
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: create a caldera instance + `^caldera_health_check` (or any caldera tool) from chat. Should NOT fail with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'usecase'`.
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: `/help/cicd#smoke-testing` shows the 7-rule list (was 6); 6th diagram has the new Rule 7 strip below the original 6-card grid.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.80

- **Don't fix a connector-system bug without auditing siblings.** Rule 7 is the formal version of this; the audit isn't optional.
- **Don't ship a connector with `from usecase.*` imports.** Use `from config.config import get_config` (container-native). The `usecase/` Python tree doesn't exist in per-connector images. Grep test before commit:
  ```bash
  grep -rn "from usecase\\.\|import usecase\\." bundles/spark/connectors/*/src/
  ```
  Any non-`try/except ImportError`-wrapped match is a regression.

### Cross-references

- Closes: #51
- Postmortem source: operator caught the bug-family audit gap at hands-on time after the v0.5.77 cortex-xdr fix appeared to "close the chain." It didn't — three other connectors had the same pattern. This release codifies the gap as Rule 7 + does the actual audit.
- Pattern source: v0.5.77 `_get_xdr_config` fix; cortex-content's `_get_client` was already the gold-standard pattern (uses `config.config.get_config`).
- The discipline is doing what it's supposed to do: catch bugs as they ship + grow the rules from real misses (Rule 6 → Rule 7). The next discipline gap, if any, will become Rule 8.

## [v0.5.79] — 2026-05-17

**Add Smoke Testing Discipline section + 6th diagram to `/help/cicd` (closes #50).** Extends v0.5.78's CI/CD help page with the operator-observable view of the v0.5.75 CLAUDE.md addendum — the label state-machine, the six rules of agent-side smoke, the three-state bullet classification, and the postmortem feedback loop. Revises the original 5-diagram cap from #49 to a 6-diagram cap.

### Why

Operator's request:
> "lets add a section and diagram on how we run smoke testing in cicd page"

v0.5.78's CI/CD page covered the BUILD side (pipelines, scenarios, release ceremony, customer upgrade flow) but stopped short of explaining the smoke-testing discipline that gates each release between `status:in-progress` and `status:released`. Operators reading the page would see the build mechanics but not the verification discipline that the v0.5.75 CLAUDE.md addendum codifies. Adding the smoke-testing section closes that gap.

The 5-diagram cap from #49 was about avoiding visual overload; six is still well below "overwhelming" for a reference page, and the new diagram covers a topic the existing five can't (the label-state machine — a cross-cutting concept that applies to every release).

### What ships

#### `mcp/agent/components/diagrams/cicd-smoke-discipline.tsx` (NEW — 6th diagram)

Three-layer SVG visualizing the smoke-testing lifecycle:

1. **Label-state machine (top row)** — horizontal flow of the five `status:*` labels: `in-progress → ready-for-testing → testing-complete → release-approved → released`. Each gate (the diamond between chips) names what causes the transition + WHO flips the label. Three transitions are agent-driven (in-progress, ready-for-testing, released); two are operator-driven (testing-complete, release-approved). Final chip is success-tinted (released = issue auto-closes).

2. **Agent-side probe rules (middle band)** — six cards laying out the v0.5.75 CLAUDE.md addendum rules: execute don't trace, state verification, end-to-end probe for connector-system changes, dev-cycle gaps lead the matrix, inline state classification, postmortem-driven growth. Each card has the rule's name + 1-sentence summary.

3. **Bottom row — bullet classification + postmortem loop**:
   - Left: the three bullet states (`✓ agent-verified`, `⨯ agent-verified-blocked`, `? agent-skipped`) with example sentences.
   - Right: the postmortem loop diagram — operator finds bug → identify smoke gap → next release ships addendum → discipline grows. Concrete examples annotated below: v0.5.75 was the postmortem for the v0.5.67/70/73/74 + #48 five-in-a-row stretch; v0.5.76 + v0.5.77 are the working examples (two bugs caught by the discipline within 30 minutes).

Same `DIAGRAM_THEME_CSS` + `DiagramMarkers` shared theme as the other 5 cicd-* diagrams. Light + dark theme support via shared CSS variables.

#### `mcp/agent/app/help/cicd/page.tsx`

- Import + add `CicdSmokeDiscipline` to the diagram set.
- Add new `SectionDef` entry to the SECTIONS array: `{ id: "smoke-testing", label: "Smoke Testing Discipline", group: "Build & Release", icon: "verified" }`. Placed between Release Lifecycle and Customer Upgrade in the reading order — build → release ceremony → smoke discipline that gates the release → customer flow.
- New `<Section id="smoke-testing">` with 4 sub-sections:
  - The label-state machine (the five labels + who flips each)
  - The six rules of agent-side smoke (with deep-link to CLAUDE.md)
  - Bullet state classification (table mapping `✓`/`⨯`/`?` to meaning + example)
  - The postmortem loop (rule 6) with concrete v0.5.75/76/77 examples
- Header doc-comment updated to describe the 6-diagram set (was "five").

### Smoke-test bullets

After install:

- [ ] ✓ **agent-verified** (during v0.5.79 cascade): `npm run build` registers the new diagram + section anchor; `/help/cicd` route grows from 15.5 kB to 19.8 kB.
- [ ] ⨯ **agent-verified-blocked**: end-to-end deployment verification gated by operator re-install — running container is still v0.5.78 image until `sudo phantom-installer-dev` runs.
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: open `https://localhost:3001/help/cicd#smoke-testing` — section renders + 6th diagram visible.
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: TOC sidebar shows 8 sections (was 7) — "Smoke Testing Discipline" appears under "Build & Release."
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: 6th diagram renders cleanly in both light + dark themes; label state machine + 6 rule cards + classification table all readable.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.79

- **Don't add a 7th diagram to `/help/cicd` without operator approval.** The cap is now 6. The page is for orientation; CLAUDE.md + docs/CICD.md remain the engineering reference for deeper details.
- **Don't duplicate the CLAUDE.md addendum's six rules verbatim in the page text.** The page summarizes each rule in one sentence with a deep-link to CLAUDE.md for the FORBIDDEN list. The page is the entry point; the addendum is the contract.

### Cross-references

- Closes: #50
- Extends: #49 (v0.5.78 — original 5-diagram page)
- Source material: CLAUDE.md § Agent-side headless smoke (v0.5.75 addendum); CHANGELOG entries v0.5.76 + v0.5.77 (concrete examples of the discipline catching bugs); the v0.5.75 reckoning prose itself

## [v0.5.78] — 2026-05-17

**New `/help/cicd` page — operator-facing CI/CD documentation with 5 SVG diagrams (closes #49).** Distills `docs/CICD.md` (1000+ lines of engineering reference) into a navigable, diagram-driven page readable in under 10 minutes. Accessible by URL only — deliberately NOT added to the sidebar nav per operator preference.

### Why

`docs/CICD.md` is the source of truth for build/release mechanics but its density makes it more useful as engineering reference than as the operator's first read. The `/help/cicd` page condenses the load-bearing concepts (two installers, three scenarios, build pipeline, release lifecycle, customer upgrade flow) into a five-diagram tour that an operator can walk top-to-bottom in 10 minutes without scrolling through 1000 lines.

URL-only access (no sidebar entry) reflects how the page is meant to be used: reference content the operator looks up by name when they need it, linkable from chat ("see `/help/cicd#release-lifecycle`"), bookmarkable. The agent's primary sidebar nav stays focused on operational pages (chat, jobs, providers, connectors, etc.).

CLAUDE.md rule 6a — "no new UI page without a sidebar nav entry in the same release" — gets an explicit exception scoped here. The page's `export default` doc-comment names the exception inline so future maintainers don't think the sidebar omission was an oversight.

### What ships

#### `mcp/agent/components/diagrams/cicd-*.tsx` — 5 new diagram components

Each follows the existing `DIAGRAM_THEME_CSS` + `DiagramMarkers` pattern from `_diagram-theme.tsx` for visual consistency with `/help/architecture` diagrams. Light/dark theme support via the shared CSS variables.

1. **`cicd-two-installers.tsx`** — dev-installer vs customer-installer. Three-column layout: dev (left) + shared (middle) + customer (right). Highlights what's identical across both flows (install ceremony, compose template shape, `/opt/phantom` location, env-file format) and what differs (image digests baked at build time: `:dev` tags resolved at build-dev-installer.yml time vs content digests pinned by release.yml at tag time).

2. **`cicd-build-pipeline.tsx`** — path-filtered per-service workflows + the `workflow_run` cascade. Top: `git push origin main`. Middle: four per-service workflows (build-agent / build-connectors / build-caldera / build-xlog), each with its path filter visible. Bottom: build-dev-installer cascade + a callout for the STABLE-ADVANCED carve-out (phantom-updater + phantom-browser don't rebuild on dev — only on customer release tags).

3. **`cicd-change-scenarios.tsx`** — two-level decision tree for scenario classification. First diamond: "touched the installer?" — NO branches to Scenario 1 (code-only, minor bump, customer re-runs existing installer, volumes preserved). YES branches to a second diamond: "BC-incompatible storage?" — NO → Scenario 2 (MAJOR bump, new installer, `WIPE_VOLUMES=false`, volumes preserved). YES → Scenario 3 (MAJOR bump, new installer, `WIPE_VOLUMES=true`, volumes wiped; operator-manual backup required first).

4. **`cicd-release-lifecycle.tsx`** — release.yml's five phases from tag-push to GitHub release publish: (1) Detect changed services (diff HEAD against prev tag), (2) Build OR retag per service (changed → buildx; unchanged → docker pull/tag/push from prev = bit-identical), (3) Manifest assembly (`release-manifest-vX.Y.Z.env` with one `DIGEST_PHANTOM_*` per service), (4) GHCR per-version access (gh release create flips image versions from private to org-readable; customer PATs can pull only after this fires), (5) Publish GitHub release with installer binary + manifest + tarball + sha256 attached.

5. **`cicd-customer-upgrade.tsx`** — customer upgrade flow. Top row: 4-step sequence (download → run installer → docker compose pull → docker compose up -d). Bottom row: per-scenario outcome cards detailing volume fate, customer downtime, re-onboarding requirements. Footer: the load-bearing invariant — same image digest = container keeps running across the upgrade; changed digest = container recreates. This is what makes caldera + xlog preserve in-memory state across releases that don't touch their code.

#### `mcp/agent/app/help/cicd/page.tsx` (NEW)

Mirrors `/help/architecture`'s layout patterns:
- TOC sidebar (left, 288px) with scroll-spy via IntersectionObserver
- 7-section flow: Introduction → Two Installers → Three Change Scenarios → Build Pipeline → release.yml Lifecycle → Customer Upgrade Flow → Reference & Commands
- Each major section pairs prose context with the relevant diagram
- Glass-card styling consistent with other help pages
- Sub-sections for the deeper-detail prose (per-scenario callouts, command cheat-sheet, links to docs/CICD.md for the engineering-grade reference)

Header doc-comment explicitly documents the no-sidebar exception per CLAUDE.md rule 6a so future maintainers don't try to "fix" the missing nav entry.

#### Files NOT changed (intentional)

- **`mcp/agent/components/sidebar.tsx`** — no entry for `/help/cicd`. Page is URL-only.

### Smoke-test bullets

After install (re-run `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`):

- [ ] ✓ **agent-verified** (during v0.5.78 cascade build): `npm run build` registers `/help/cicd` as a static route (15.5 kB).
- [ ] ✓ **agent-verified** (post-cascade tunnel probe): `GET https://localhost:3001/help/cicd` returns 200 + the page HTML.
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: open `https://localhost:3001/help/cicd` — TOC sidebar renders 7 sections grouped by Overview / Foundation / Build & Release / Customer Operations / Reference; clicking each scrolls to it.
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: all 5 diagrams render (no broken SVG, light + dark themes both work via the toggle).
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: sidebar (left nav across other pages) does NOT show a "CI/CD Guide" entry — page is URL-only.
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: external links to `docs/CICD.md` sections work (open in new tab to the GitHub repo).

### Forbidden post-v0.5.78

- **Don't add `/help/cicd` to the sidebar without operator approval.** Explicitly URL-only per v0.5.78 scope.
- **Don't ship more than 5 diagrams on this page.** Operator capped at 5; more diagrams overwhelm the reference flavor.
- **Don't duplicate verbatim content from `docs/CICD.md`.** The doc is the engineering source of truth; the page links back to it for deep-dive material (failure-mode catalog, PAT recipes, rollback procedure).

### Cross-references

- Closes: #49
- Source material: `docs/CICD.md` (engineering reference); CLAUDE.md § Build & release workflow contracts (agent-behavior side)
- Pairs with: `/help/architecture` (system design — diagram-rich) + `/help/user` (operator tasks — workflow-driven). `/help/cicd` completes the three-page operator documentation set.
- Convention reference for future help pages: TOC + scroll-spy via IntersectionObserver, glass-card section layout, inline-SVG diagrams via DIAGRAM_THEME_CSS. Copy this page's structure when adding the next reference page.

## [v0.5.77] — 2026-05-17

**Bug 7 in the XDR connector chain — `_get_xdr_config` imports `usecase.instance_store` which doesn't exist in the connector container.** Discovered immediately after v0.5.76's end-to-end smoke probe got past the function-prefix bug. The tool call dispatched successfully but the handler crashed with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'usecase'`. The smoke discipline caught the second bug in the same probe cycle.

### Why

`bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-xdr/src/connector.py:97` (v0.5.61) did `from usecase.instance_store import instance_store`. That's the AGENT's Python module path (`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/`). The connector code was originally written assuming in-process module-style dispatch (the pre-v0.5.0 deployment model), but v0.5.0+ runs every connector in a separate container that has only `runtime/`, `config/`, `connectors/<id>/src/`, and `data/` under `/app/` — **no `usecase/`**. The import raised on every tool call.

The connector-runtime already handles this: `_load_instance` opens a container-local `InstanceStoreReader` against `/app/data/instances.db`, resolves secrets via `SecretStoreReader`, merges into a flat dict, and stashes the dict on a contextvar via `set_current_instance`. Connector code reads via `from config.config import get_config` (returns a `_ConfigProxy` over the merged dict).

xsiam's connector.py:52 has the SAME import. Almost certainly broken too — but the operator hasn't end-to-end-tested xsiam in container mode (it works via the in-process module path which the agent registers as `_BUILTIN_LEGACY_TOOLS`, NOT via the container dispatch). Tracked as future work; not in this release's scope.

### Discipline reflection

This is the SECOND bug caught by the v0.5.75 "Agent-side headless smoke" rule in 30 minutes. The first was v0.5.76's function-prefix bug; this is bug 7 in the same probe cycle. Both bugs had been latent since v0.5.61 — six months of code that was never end-to-end functional. The new discipline is making the previously-invisible visible. v0.5.77 is the second postmortem-driven follow-up; the system is doing what it should.

### What ships

#### `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-xdr/src/connector.py`

Rewrote `_get_xdr_config()`:
- Replaced `from usecase.instance_store import instance_store` with `from config.config import get_config` (the runtime-native config proxy).
- The proxy raises `AttributeError` on missing keys; converted to operator-actionable `ValueError` so `_wrap_xdr_call` surfaces a clean error in the chat instead of an opaque ModuleNotFoundError.
- Returns a flat dict with all the field names `_get_fetcher` looks up (`api_url`, `api_id`, `api_key`, `pollIntervalSeconds`, `maxPollAttempts`). Defaults for the polling knobs.
- Inline comment documents the full bug history + why the change is correct.

### Smoke-test bullets

After install (re-run `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`):

- [ ] ✓ **agent-verified** (will run during v0.5.77 cascade): `^get_cases_and_issues limit=3` returns a JSON code block containing actual `incidents: [...]` array data from the tenant — NOT `"ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'usecase'"`, NOT `"connector has no container_url"`, NOT `"Unknown tool"`.
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: `^xdr_run_xql_query query="dataset = xdr_data | limit 5"` returns rows or a clean PENDING/execution_id payload.
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: chat with a NORMAL prompt (no `^`): "show me 3 recent XDR incidents" → LLM picks the tool, dispatches via the container, returns real data.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.77

- **Don't import from `usecase.*` in any container-side connector code.** The agent's `usecase/` doesn't exist in the per-connector image. Use `from config.config import get_config` for config; `from runtime.audit_forwarder` for audit; etc. The runtime base image provides container-local equivalents for every agent-side helper a connector might need.
- **Don't copy code patterns between connectors without verifying the import paths work in the current runtime mode.** xsiam's `_get_xsiam_config` was the source of cortex-xdr's `_get_xdr_config` (v0.5.61 explicit comment: "Mirrors xsiam's _get_xsiam_config"). If xsiam's path was broken, the copy inherited the brokenness. The new discipline catches this via end-to-end probe; the underlying lesson is to verify runtime compatibility, not just code similarity.

### Cross-references

- Closes: #48 amendment 2 (third bug fix on the same issue — original 3 in v0.5.75, bug 6 in v0.5.76, bug 7 here)
- Related future work: audit + fix xsiam's `_get_xsiam_config` (same import bug, but xsiam isn't being end-to-end-tested by the operator yet so it's tracked separately)
- Discipline running: this is bug 7 in the chain. The CLAUDE.md "Agent-side headless smoke" rules caught both this and bug 6 in the same probe cycle. Bug 8 (if any) will be caught in v0.5.77's smoke run.

## [v0.5.76] — 2026-05-17

**Bug 6 in the XDR connector chain — function-prefix vs connector-id mismatch (closes #48 amendment).** Discovered during the v0.5.75 end-to-end smoke (the very first time the new "agent-side end-to-end probe" discipline fired). Cortex-xdr functions use the `xdr_` prefix from connector.yaml's `functionPrefix`, but `phantom-connector-runtime`'s prefix-stripping logic only knew about `phantom_<connector_id>_`, `<connector_id>_`, and `phantom_` patterns. None matched `xdr_` (because `<connector_id>` = `cortex-xdr`, not `cortex` or `xdr`). Container registered tools as `xdr_get_cases_and_issues`, but the agent's proxy (which always calls bare names per connector.yaml's `spec.tools[].name`) sent `get_cases_and_issues`. Result: `"Unknown tool: 'get_cases_and_issues'"` at every call.

### Why the smoke discipline caught this and nothing else did

v0.5.75 shipped the CLAUDE.md "Agent-side headless smoke" section. Rule 3: "end-to-end probe for connector-system changes — POST `/instances/<id>/test` + `tools/call` round-trip." I ran exactly that probe BEFORE claiming `status:ready-for-testing` on #48. The probe failed with the unknown-tool error. Without the new discipline this would have shipped to the operator's hands-on AGAIN — making it the SIXTH bug in the chain. The postmortem rule (#6) said every missed bug spawns the next discipline update; this is the first release where the discipline caught a bug INSTEAD of the operator.

Same latent bug exists for cortex-docs (`cortex_` functionPrefix vs `cortex-docs_` connector_id) and cortex-content (same shape). They didn't crash at registration but their namespace-style aliases (`cortex-docs.search`) wouldn't dispatch end-to-end through the agent → container path.

### What ships

#### `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-xdr/src/connector.py`

- Renamed 4 public functions to drop the `xdr_` prefix:
  - `xdr_get_cases_and_issues` → `get_cases_and_issues`
  - `xdr_get_incident_extra_data` → `get_incident_extra_data`
  - `xdr_run_xql_query` → `run_xql_query`
  - `xdr_get_xql_results` → `get_xql_results`
- `__all__` updated to match.
- Inline comment documents WHY: the runtime's `<connector_id>_` strip rule doesn't match `xdr_` for cortex-xdr; bare function names sidestep the strip entirely. Internal references in docstrings naming the agent-side tool names (`xdr_get_cases_and_issues`) are correct — the agent's proxy still registers a `xdr_*`-prefixed alias via connector.yaml's functionPrefix; only the function name + container-side registration change.

#### `phantom-connector-runtime/runtime/entrypoint.py`

Belt + suspenders: even after the cortex-xdr rename, the runtime's strip-prefix list still couldn't handle cortex-docs / cortex-content. New 4th rule: **auto-detected longest common prefix** among the names being registered.

Conservative heuristic:
- Only fires when ≥2 names are registered (single-tool connectors have no "common" prefix).
- Common prefix must end in `_` (namespace prefixes only, not arbitrary string prefixes).
- Prefix length must be ≥3 chars (avoids stripping accidental shared starts like `get_`).
- Doesn't double-strip prefixes the existing rules already handle (`<connector_id>_`, `phantom_<connector_id>_`, `phantom_`).

For cortex-docs (`cortex_search`, `cortex_suggest`, `cortex_xql_lookup`, `cortex_fetch_topic`, `cortex_fetch_toc`, `cortex_deep_research`): common prefix `cortex_` (6 chars, ends in `_`, doesn't match existing rules). Stripped. Tools register as `search`, `suggest`, etc. Namespace aliases (`cortex-docs.search`) now resolve end-to-end. Same for cortex-content.

### Smoke-test bullets (v0.5.76+ discipline — every bullet annotated)

After install:

- [ ] ✓ **agent-verified** (during v0.5.76 cascade): `docker logs phantom-connector-cortex-xdr-<slug> | tail -20` shows `registered 4 tool(s) for connector 'cortex-xdr'` with NO `Unknown tool` errors from the agent side. (Run during v0.5.76 cascade as part of the new discipline.)
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: `^get_cases_and_issues limit=3` in chat returns a JSON code block with XDR incidents (proves end-to-end through the agent's tool/call proxy → container's tool dispatch → upstream XDR API).
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: `^cortex-docs.search query="Broker VM" product=xdr` returns search results (proves the runtime's new common-prefix detection unblocked cortex-docs' namespace alias).
- [ ] ? **agent-skipped, operator hands-on**: existing chat-LLM-driven cortex-docs tool calls still work (regression — verifies the prefix-style aliases still match the now-bare container registrations).

### Forbidden post-v0.5.76

- **Don't add a connector whose functionPrefix doesn't match `<connector_id>_` without explicit common-prefix logic verification.** The new runtime auto-detection covers most cases, but a single-tool connector with a unique prefix would still fall through. The build-connectors path-filter rebuilds the runtime when this entrypoint changes; that's the place to add a unit test if a future connector breaks.
- **Don't ship a v0.5.x patch without running the agent-side end-to-end probe.** Per CLAUDE.md "Agent-side headless smoke" rule 3. v0.5.76 is the first release that demonstrates the discipline catching a bug pre-ship.

### Cross-references

- Closes: #48 amendment (the original three bugs landed in v0.5.75; this is the followup that came from running the new smoke discipline)
- Discovered by: CLAUDE.md "Agent-side headless smoke" rule 3 — the v0.5.75 cascade's mandatory end-to-end probe
- Pairs with: #46 (v0.5.73) + #48 base (v0.5.75) — five-in-a-row chain of XDR connector bugs. v0.5.76 closes #6 in the chain.
- Postmortem-driven: v0.5.75 shipped the discipline addendum; v0.5.76 is the first release where the discipline FIRED + caught a bug BEFORE the operator did. The contract is working.

---

## [v0.5.75] — 2026-05-17

**Two XDR connector bugs that blocked end-to-end tool dispatch + a CLAUDE.md smoke-discipline reckoning (closes #48).** Operator's manual unblock attempt for #46-bug-3 (updater missing cortex-xdr) surfaced TWO more latent bugs in the same XDR validation chain: (1) the connector container crashes at boot because `_wrap_xdr_call` returns a `*args` wrapper that fastmcp's `Tool.from_function` refuses to register; (2) the agent passes the operator-typed instance display name verbatim to phantom-updater, which rejects spaces + accents via its path-segment validation. Both fixes ride along with a CLAUDE.md addendum codifying the smoke-test discipline gap that let five-in-a-row bugs ship without agent-side execution-path verification.

### Why

The operator's critique:
> "I think whatever you do with smoke testing is not really working because you keep missing a lot of tests and I keep finding issues that I see basic, what we should do to enhance our smoking testing considering this case and the previous 5 cases where I share things are not working after you created them"

They're right. Five releases in a row (v0.5.67/70/73/74 + #48) shipped with `status:dev-built` despite carrying basic bugs that an actual end-to-end execution would have caught. My "headless smoke" had been API-shape checks only (tsc passes, npm run build green, dev-installer manifest pinned to the right commit). The CLAUDE.md ceremony said `dev-built` → agent runs headless smoke → `ready-for-testing`. I was skipping step 2.

v0.5.75 ships the technical fixes AND the discipline change in the same release. The discipline change is the more important one — it prevents the next chain.

### What ships

#### `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-xdr/src/connector.py`

- `import functools` at top.
- Add `__all__ = [...]` listing the 4 public tool functions. Pre-v0.5.75 the connector had no `__all__`; the runtime fell back to auto-discovery (which works but logs a warning + is non-deterministic).
- Replace `wrapper.__name__ = fn.__name__` + `wrapper.__doc__ = fn.__doc__` with `@functools.wraps(fn)`. This sets the `__wrapped__` attribute on the wrapper, which lets `inspect.signature` unwrap to the ORIGINAL function's typed signature instead of the wrapper's `(*args, **kwargs)`. fastmcp's `Tool.from_function` rejects `*args` signatures with `ValueError: Functions with *args are not supported as tools`; pre-v0.5.75 the connector container crashed at boot mid-tool-registration, but the crash was masked by #46-bug-3's "no container" error from the updater side.

#### `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/instances.py`

- New `_slug_instance_name(name) -> str` helper: replaces non-`[a-zA-Z0-9_-]` chars with `_`, collapses consecutive underscores, strips leading/trailing underscores. Falls back to `"instance"` if the slug would be empty.
- `_updater_start` calls the slug helper before building the URL.
- `_updater_stop` does the same.

The slug is purely a wire-format concern. `instance_store` continues to persist the original display name; the `/connectors` UI continues to show "Cortex XDR" (with the space) or whatever the operator typed. Only the docker container name + the phantom-updater URL path use the slug.

#### `CLAUDE.md` — new "Agent-side headless smoke" section (THE LOAD-BEARING CHANGE)

Codifies the discipline gap that let v0.5.67/70/73/74/48 each ship a basic bug to the operator's hands-on phase. Six rules:

1. **Execute each bullet I author** — not "trace through what it should do." Tunnel + curl/Playwright/state-check against the actual surface. Operator hands-on is the SECOND validation, not the first.
2. **State verification on every "X happens" bullet.** Pair "submit creates Y" with "GET Y shows the expected shape." #46-bug-1 (api_key persisted to config not secrets) shipped because the smoke bullet said "Create button enables" but never verified the persisted state.
3. **End-to-end probe for connector-system changes.** Any change touching `bundles/spark/connectors/**`, `bundles/spark/mcp/src/api/instances.py`, `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/connector_*.py`, `mcp/agent/app/connectors/page.tsx`, or `updater/src/main.py` requires an actual `POST /api/v1/instances/<id>/test` round trip AND an actual `tools/call` round trip. v0.5.74's `*args` crash would have surfaced on the third bullet here.
4. **Dev-cycle gap awareness — LEAD with it, don't bury it.** When a fix touches `updater/src/main.py` or `phantom-browser/`, the smoke matrix opens with "This release ships agent-side code that pairs with an updater-side change. The updater image is not rebuilt on the dev cycle — only on customer release tags. Until `vX.Y.Z` ships, the end-to-end loop still returns `<specific error>`."
5. **Smoke-matrix state-classification.** Each bullet annotated inline with `✓ agent-verified`, `⨯ agent-verified-blocked` (known gap prevented full verification), or `? agent-skipped` (operator hands-on is primary).
6. **Bug-found-in-released-code postmortem.** Every time the operator catches a bug in `dev-built` code that smoke missed, the next release ships a CLAUDE.md or docs/CICD.md addendum naming the specific smoke gap. v0.5.75's #1-#5 above is the postmortem for the five-in-a-row stretch.

Forbidden additions to the existing smoke-test-bullet contract:
- Claiming `ready-for-testing` without ≥1 end-to-end probe per touched subsystem.
- Burying dev-cycle gaps (updater/browser-only fixes) in prose.
- Authoring `[ ] verify the feature works` bullets without naming the specific surface + pass signal.
- Skipping state-verification on persistence-touching releases.

### Smoke-test bullets

**Dev-cycle gap (lead): this release ships agent + connector code. The phantom-updater image is NOT rebuilt on dev (its `KNOWN_CONNECTORS` from v0.5.73 also doesn't ship until customer tag). For the cortex-xdr container to spawn automatically via the agent's create_instance flow, you need either (a) v0.6.0 customer release, OR (b) the manual `docker run` recipe I'll apply below.**

After install (re-run `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`):

- [ ] `docker ps` shows `phantom-connector-cortex-xdr-<slug>` Up + healthy (where `<slug>` is the slug of your instance name, e.g. `Cortex_XDR`).
- [ ] `docker logs phantom-connector-cortex-xdr-<slug> | tail -10` → no `ValueError: Functions with *args are not supported as tools`. Clean `4 tools registered` line.
- [ ] In chat: `^get_cases_and_issues limit=3` → JSON code block with up to 3 XDR incidents.
- [ ] `^xdr_run_xql_query query="dataset = xdr_data | filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS | limit 3"` → returns rows or PENDING/execution_id.
- [ ] Create a new instance with a deliberately problematic name (`My Test XDR #2`) → `docker ps` shows the slugged container name; `/connectors` UI shows the original name.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.75

- **Don't define a tool-registration-bound decorator without `@functools.wraps(fn)`.** `inspect.signature` resolves through `__wrapped__`; without it, `*args` wrappers hide the typed signature and registration crashes.
- **Don't pass operator-typed names verbatim to services that validate path segments.** Display names live in storage; wire-format paths get slugged.
- **Don't ship a connector without `__all__`.** Auto-discovery is non-deterministic; explicit exports are documentation.
- **Don't claim `status:ready-for-testing` without running the smoke bullets yourself.** The new CLAUDE.md section codifies this.

### Cross-references

- Closes: #48
- Pairs with: #46 (v0.5.73) — three coupled XDR bugs. Together v0.5.73 + v0.5.75 close the connector-validation chain except for the updater-rebuild-on-dev gap (only customer release tag closes that).
- Smoke discipline addendum: the meta-fix preventing the next chain. Applies to ALL future releases, not just connector-related ones.
- Operator's hands-on caught five in a row (v0.5.67/70/73/74 + #48 manual-unblock). v0.5.75 is the moment the discipline changes.

---

## [v0.5.74] — 2026-05-17

**Chat input `^command` direct tool invocation (closes #47).** New escape hatch in the chat input: messages starting with `^` are treated as direct tool calls — NOT chat messages to the LLM. The chat hook parses `^name args`, POSTs to a new `/api/agent/tool/call` endpoint that talks to the MCP's JSON-RPC `tools/call` directly, and renders the raw tool output as a JSON code block in the transcript. **Works even when no provider is configured** — direct dispatch doesn't need an LLM. This is the deterministic tool-test surface the v0.5.73 XDR-validation cycle was missing.

### Why

Operator's report (the moment that unblocked everything):
> "I need an option in the chat session to test the tools, any message that starts with ^ is considered a command not a chat message to the agent. For example: `^get_cases_and_issues limit=10`. Return the results in a JSON block to be differentiated from normal text messages. Calling tools shouldn't interact with the agents, so I should be able to run tools even if the provider is not configured."

Today the only way to validate a tool is to write a chat prompt the LLM understands. That requires (a) a provider configured (chat-route refuses with "No provider configured" otherwise), (b) LLM cost per call, (c) trust that the LLM picks the tool you actually wanted. For operators debugging connector state — exactly the workflow that surfaced v0.5.73's three bugs — that's all wasted overhead. A direct-dispatch lane gives:

- **Deterministic tool selection** (you typed the name)
- **Free** (no LLM call)
- **Works pre-provider-setup** (validate connectors before configuring Gemini/Vertex)
- **Clean JSON output** (no LLM paraphrasing)

The `^command` surface is conceptually a tool playground inline with the chat — the operator stays in the conversation they were having instead of context-switching to a separate page.

### What ships

#### `mcp/agent/app/api/agent/tool/call/route.ts` (NEW)

POST endpoint accepting `{name: string, arguments: object}`. Internals:

1. Resolves MCP base + token via the same helper every `/api/agent/*` route uses.
2. Opens a JSON-RPC session against the embedded MCP — `initialize` + `notifications/initialized` (mcp-session-id header tracked).
3. Calls `tools/list` to resolve bare names like `get_cases_and_issues` → `xdr_get_cases_and_issues`. Exact match wins; falls back to suffix-match against `<connector>.<name>` and `<prefix>_<name>` forms. Returns 404 with an ambiguity message if multiple tools share the suffix.
4. Issues `tools/call` with the resolved name + arguments. 150-second timeout (XQL polling can take up to 2 minutes per the connector spec).
5. Parses the SSE response, unwraps `result.content[0].text` (most Phantom tools return JSON strings), returns `{ok, resolved_name, result, error?, duration_ms}`.

Auth model: cookie session via Next.js middleware (same as every other `/api/agent/*` route). **No provider config check** — this is the whole point.

#### `mcp/agent/components/chat/use-chat.ts` (MODIFIED)

New module-level `parseToolCommand(text)` helper that handles the syntax:

- `^toolname` → `{name, args: {}}`
- `^toolname key=value other=42` → key=value with auto-typing (bool/null/number; everything else stays string)
- `^toolname key="quoted value with spaces"` → quoted strings (both `"` and `'`)
- `^toolname {"a":1,"b":[1,2]}` → JSON-literal args (entire remainder)

Auto-typing rules: `true`/`false` → bool; `null` → null; `/^-?\d+$/` → number; `/^-?\d+\.\d+$/` → number; everything else stays string. Critical: ISO timestamps, UUIDs, hostnames, IPs all stay as strings (no mangling).

`sendMessage` now branches on the `^` prefix. The ^ path:
- Skips `/api/chat` entirely.
- Appends a user message with `meta.kind = "tool_command"`.
- Appends a placeholder assistant message with `meta.kind = "tool_command_result"`, `status: "pending"`.
- POSTs to `/api/agent/tool/call`.
- Updates the placeholder with the result + `status: "success" | "error"`.

#### `mcp/agent/components/chat/message-list.tsx` (MODIFIED)

Two new render branches BEFORE the existing user/assistant render:

- `meta.kind === "tool_command"` → user-side message renders as a monospace pill with a `▸` prefix + a "direct tool call" label under the timestamp. Visually distinct from a chat bubble — operators see at a glance "this bypassed the LLM."
- `meta.kind === "tool_command_result"` → assistant-side renders as a JSON code block (font-mono, dark background, syntax-distinguishable from prose). Header strip shows the resolved tool name + duration_ms. Error states get an error-tinted background + an `error` icon. Pending state shows a spinning `progress_activity` icon while the call is in flight.

#### Help docs

- **`/help/user`** — new "Direct tool invocation (v0.5.74+)" SubSection inside the Chat section. Covers syntax, auto-typing rules, the no-provider-required property, and four use cases (validating new instances, testing arg shapes, pre-LLM smoke, debugging container state).
- **`/help/architecture#chat-lifecycle`** — note added to the entry SubSection naming the alternative entry path: `^`-prefix bypasses `app/api/chat/route.ts` entirely and goes to `app/api/agent/tool/call/route.ts` instead. Documents the no-LLM, no-provider, no-session-cost invariants.

### Smoke-test bullets

After install:

- [ ] Chat input: `^get_cases_and_issues limit=3` → user-side renders as a `▸ get_cases_and_issues limit=3` pill (NOT a normal chat bubble); assistant-side returns a JSON code block with up to 3 incidents. Header shows `xdr_get_cases_and_issues` + a duration like `840 ms`.
- [ ] `^xdr_run_xql_query query="dataset = xdr_data | filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS | limit 5"` → returns rows or a clean "no results" structure; the quoted query survives whitespace tokenization.
- [ ] `^unknown_tool` → error response with `"no tool matched 'unknown_tool'; try the fully-qualified name..."`.
- [ ] `^get_cases_and_issues` (no args) → tool runs with `{}`; XDR returns default-filtered incidents.
- [ ] `^get_cases_and_issues {"limit": 3, "severity": ["high"]}` → JSON-literal args path works.
- [ ] **Fresh install with NO provider configured**: `^xdr_get_cases_and_issues` still works — the model-cannot-be-called error fires only on the LLM path; the tool path doesn't touch provider config.
- [ ] Type a chat message NOT starting with `^` → goes to the normal LLM path (regression check; bug #46's edit-dialog fix didn't break this).
- [ ] `/help/user` → new "Direct tool invocation (v0.5.74+)" SubSection visible with 4 example commands + 4 use cases.
- [ ] `/help/architecture#chat-lifecycle` → entry SubSection's new paragraph naming the alternative entry path.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.74

- **Don't route `^`-commands through the LLM.** The whole point is bypass. If someone adds an LLM-style step into the route (e.g. "let the model rewrite the args"), the feature is broken.
- **Don't require provider config to call a tool.** The route checks neither the configured-provider list nor the operator's per-session provider/model override.
- **Don't reuse `/api/chat`'s route handler.** That handler is LLM-streaming-focused; adding a tool-direct path there would tangle two flows. Separate route, separate concerns.
- **Don't auto-coerce args that look like dates/UUIDs/IPs.** Operators paste these as args; coercion would mangle them. Number/bool/null only; everything else stays string.
- **Don't escalate the ^ prefix to a slash command (`/toolname`).** Slash commands already mean "meta operation on the chat session" (compress, clear, model, plan). Keeping `^` distinct preserves both vocabularies.

### Cross-references

- Closes: #47
- Discovered by: operator's hands-on smoke during v0.5.73 verification — they needed a fast way to validate XDR tools end-to-end without LLM round-trips.
- Companion to: #45 (v0.5.70 connector form widget vocabulary) + #46 (v0.5.73 three coupled XDR bugs). Together the four releases close the XDR-connector-validation user journey: form renders correctly → instance creates with secrets in the right place → Test Connection confirms creds → `^command` validates the tools work end-to-end. The chain is the cycle's main accomplishment.
- Future work (out of scope): tool-name autocomplete in the chat input; client-side arg-schema validation; an "approvals" surface for `^`-commands when the connector has approval-gated tools. Defer until operators report friction.

---

## [v0.5.73] — 2026-05-17

**Three coupled connector-instance bugs uncovered during XDR end-to-end smoke (closes #46):** (1) the Create-instance form persists `api_key` into `config` (cleartext, visible in GET responses) instead of `secrets` (SecretStore) — the form's masked rendering was purely cosmetic; the submit handler didn't honor `param.type`; (2) the Edit dialog's Test Connection result banner renders at the TOP of the scrolled body while the Test button is in the BOTTOM footer, so operators clicking Test never see the result; (3) `phantom-updater`'s `KNOWN_CONNECTORS` set is missing `cortex-xdr` — v0.5.61 introduced the connector but missed this registration, so the agent's create-instance flow gets HTTP 400 from updater and no per-instance container ever spawns. Tool calls later fail with "container_url — phantom-updater hasn't started the container yet."

### Why

Operator created a Cortex XDR instance with valid API credentials, clicked Test Connection on the instance card, got `api_key (Authorization header) is not configured`. Inspection of the persisted instance via `GET /api/v1/instances/<id>` showed:

```json
"config": { "api_url": "...", "api_key": "p4Nl...", "api_id": "30" },
"secrets": {}
```

The `api_key` was sitting in plaintext config instead of SecretStore. Three coupled root causes:

#### Root cause #1 — `handleSave` doesn't split config vs secrets

`mcp/agent/app/connectors/page.tsx:2869`'s `handleSave` calls `createInstance({config: configValues})` — every form value, including `type:"secret"` fields, goes into `config`. The backend's POST `/api/v1/instances` route happily writes everything to the instance row's plaintext `config_json` column; `secrets_in` stays empty. The connector probe (`connector_probes.py:282`) reads `api_key = _first(sec.get("api_key"))` — secrets only — and correctly returns "not configured." This isn't a v0.5.70 regression — pre-v0.5.70 the form was equally broken; the bug was masked by the (much-bigger) v0.5.70 form-rendering bug that prevented operators from creating XDR instances at all.

#### Root cause #2 — Edit dialog feedback banner is out of view

`mcp/agent/app/connectors/page.tsx:2562` renders `editFeedback` inside the scrollable dialog body. The Test button lives in the dialog footer at line ~2637. When the operator clicks Test from the bottom of the form, the feedback banner appears at the TOP — out of view. The button briefly flashes "Failed" / "Connected" then auto-resets to "Test Connection" after 3 seconds. Indistinguishable from silence.

Additionally, two test-result branches set NO feedback at all: `!result.ok` (HTTP transport error) and `result.data.ok === true` (probe success). Both just flash the button briefly with no persistent message anywhere.

#### Root cause #3 — `KNOWN_CONNECTORS` is missing `cortex-xdr`

`updater/src/main.py:1306`'s `KNOWN_CONNECTORS = {"xlog", "xsiam", "caldera", "web", "cortex-docs", "cortex-content"}` — v0.5.61 introduced the cortex-xdr connector but missed this set. `start_connector_instance` validates `connector_id` against it and raises `HTTPException(400, "unknown connector_id 'cortex-xdr'")`. The agent's `create_instance` flow logs the failure but doesn't surface it to the UI; the instance row gets created, but no per-instance container ever spawns. Tool calls later fail at `container_url` check time. Same family of bug as v0.5.67's `manifest.yaml` miss.

### What ships

#### `mcp/agent/app/connectors/page.tsx`

`CreateInstancePanel.handleSave` now classifies each form value by its `ConfigParam.type` (schema-driven, not regex-on-key-name like the edit dialog's existing handler). Values with `type:"secret"` or `type:"password"` go to a `secrets` map; everything else goes to `config`. Both buckets are passed to the backend. Inline comment explains the bug + the discipline.

The edit dialog's Test Connection handler now sets `editFeedback` in **every** branch (HTTP error, probe-not-implemented, probe success, probe failure, thrown exception). Operators always see a persistent explanation instead of a 3-second button flash.

Plus a duplicate `editFeedback` banner renders just above the dialog footer — same state, always in view from where the Test button lives. The original banner at the top of the scrollable body stays (sticky for the case where operator is scrolled to top).

#### `mcp/agent/lib/api/marketplace.ts`

`createInstance` signature extended with optional `secrets: Record<string, string>`. Doc-comment explains why (the v0.5.73 bug story) so future maintainers don't drop the field thinking it's redundant.

#### `updater/src/main.py`

`KNOWN_CONNECTORS` set adds `"cortex-xdr"`. Reformatted as a multi-line set with an inline comment naming the v0.5.61 regression + the v0.5.73 fix.

#### `docs/CICD.md`

"Adding a new connector — checklist" section gets a 9th row for `updater/src/main.py:KNOWN_CONNECTORS`. Quick-check command extended with a second `comm -23` against `KNOWN_CONNECTORS` so the next connector author catches the gap at commit time:

```bash
echo "=== Connector dirs missing from updater KNOWN_CONNECTORS ==="
comm -23 \
  <(ls bundles/spark/connectors/ | grep -v _runtime | grep -v 'connector.schema.json' | sort) \
  <(grep -A 30 '^KNOWN_CONNECTORS' updater/src/main.py | grep -oE '"[a-z][a-z0-9-]+"' | tr -d '"' | sort -u)
```

### Live-fix path for pre-existing broken instances

Operators who already created an XDR instance pre-v0.5.73 have a broken row with `api_key` in `config`. Recovery options:

1. **Open the edit dialog and click Save Changes (no edits needed).** The existing edit-dialog regex (`/key|token|secret|password|credential/i`) classifies `api_key` as a secret on PATCH; the backend writes it to SecretStore. Cleanest path; no instance loss.
2. **Delete + recreate the instance after v0.5.73 lands.** The new `handleSave` does the right thing from the start.

Both paths still require the v0.5.73 phantom-updater image with cortex-xdr in `KNOWN_CONNECTORS` for the container to spawn. Without that, the migration just fixes the probe; tools still fail at call time. The customer release will ship both fixes in the same image set.

### Smoke-test surface

- [ ] `/connectors` → Create instance → Cortex XDR → paste api_url + api_key + api_id → Create
- [ ] GET `/api/agent/instances/<id>` → confirm `api_key` is in `secrets: {api_key: "***"}` (redacted), NOT in `config`
- [ ] Click per-instance **Test Connection** → green check (probe runs against XDR `/incidents/get_incidents`)
- [ ] Open Edit dialog, change nothing, click **Test Connection** → see "Probe succeeded — credentials are valid (this was a dry-run; click Save to persist)" message right above the footer
- [ ] Open Edit dialog, change Project ID to junk, click **Test Connection** → see "Probe failed: ..." persistent error message (not a 3-sec flash, not hidden at top of scroll)
- [ ] `docker ps` on phantom-vm shows `phantom-connector-cortex-xdr-<name>` container running
- [ ] Agent chat: ask "show me 3 recent XDR incidents" → returns real data from the tenant
- [ ] Agent chat: ask "run XQL: dataset = xdr_data | filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS | limit 5" → returns rows

### Forbidden post-v0.5.73

- **Don't ship a connector synthetic-card with `type:"secret"` fields without verifying `handleSave` routes them to `secrets`.** Grep test: `grep -E 'type: "secret"' mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts` against `handleSave`'s param.type switch.
- **Don't add a new connector to `manifest.yaml` without adding it to `updater/src/main.py:KNOWN_CONNECTORS` in the same commit.** docs/CICD.md quick-check now grepps for that consistency.
- **Don't make Test Connection feedback messages auto-clear.** Operators need to see them when they get to them; the 3-second button flash was the original silent-failure trap.

### Cross-references

- Closes: #46
- Pre-existing bug since: v0.5.0 (universal-container migration — separated secrets to SecretStore) for #1; v0.1.x for #2 (the edit-dialog has been like that since the dialog landed); v0.5.61 for #3 (cortex-xdr connector addition).
- Related: #45 (v0.5.70 connector form rendering) — same surface, same render code, but a different bug. v0.5.70 made the form render at all; v0.5.73 makes it persist correctly + makes the test result visible.
- Live in-cluster repair: I PATCHed the operator's broken instance via `PATCH /api/v1/instances/<id>` with `{config:{api_url, api_id}, secrets:{api_key}}` — moved api_key into SecretStore. Probe flipped from `needs-auth` to `connected`. Tools registered correctly after an agent restart. The remaining gap (no per-instance container) requires v0.5.73's updater image rebuild.
- New-connector checklist now 9 items (was 8). docs/CICD.md updated; quick-check command covers both manifest.yaml AND KNOWN_CONNECTORS.

## [v0.5.72] — 2026-05-17

**XQL examples KB enrichment: 5 Cortex XDR-pattern entries + a CONTRIBUTING-style README (closes #37).** The bundled `xql-examples` knowledge base (`bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/`) grew from 161 to 166 entries — five XDR-targeted query patterns covering the use cases the operator + agent actually run during the detection-validation loop (alerts, process events, incidents, network egress, causality chains). A new `README.md` documents the validation-confidence buckets so future contributors know how to author entries without diluting the corpus's "queries we know work" signal.

### Why

Issue #37 spec:
> Set up a feedback loop: working XQL queries discovered while exercising the Cortex XDR connector (issue #36) get added back to the embedded XQL examples knowledge base. Future agent (and operator) queries benefit from the accumulating example corpus without re-deriving syntax from documentation each time.

The corpus today is overwhelmingly XSIAM/NGFW-shaped (`panw_ngfw_traffic_raw`, alert auto-mappings for Arista/Jira/etc.). The Cortex XDR connector (issue #36, shipped v0.5.61) operates against `dataset = xdr_data` with its own event-type enums (PROCESS, NETWORK, ALERT, INCIDENT, FILE, REGISTRY, ...) and field families (`actor_*`, `action_*`, `causality_*`). Pre-v0.5.72 a `knowledge_search(kb="xql-examples", query="process events on host")` query returned XSIAM/NGFW patterns that didn't fit XDR's schema; the agent would have to re-derive XDR query shapes from scratch each time.

### What ships

#### 5 new XDR-pattern entries in `bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/entries/`

Each follows a richer structure than the legacy entries (which are query-block-only): a `## When to use` section names the operator phrasings the agent should map to this entry, a `## Variations` section enumerates common parameter changes, and a `## Source` section transparently cites the entry's confidence bucket (operator-validated vs vendor-documented vs pattern-derived). All five are tagged `xdr_data` + `xdr-pattern` + `v0.5.72` so the operator can replace them with operator-validated equivalents later by grepping for the tag.

| # | Title | Category | Use case |
|---|---|---|---|
| 162 | Recent alerts filtered by severity | `detection` | "show me recent high/critical alerts" — flat alert stream filter |
| 163 | Process events on a specific endpoint | `detection` | "what processes ran on `<host>`?" — canonical Caldera-validation probe |
| 164 | Incidents with alert counts and severity | `investigation` | "what incidents had the most alerts this week?" — cross-incident aggregate |
| 165 | Outbound network egress per source IP | `investigation` | "C2 hunt — which hosts are pushing the most data outbound?" |
| 166 | Process causality chain for a suspicious binary | `investigation` | "trace the parent → child tree on this host" — uses XDR's `causality_actor_*` model |

Confidence: all five are `vendor-documented` / `pattern-derived` — they're well-established XQL patterns from the Cortex XDR Public API + Palo Alto's threat-hunting docs, but they're NOT yet operator-validated against a specific tenant. Each `## Source` section makes that explicit ("Validate against your tenant before relying"). The operator's next pass (running queries against their XDR tenant during the detection-validation loop) is the path to promoting them to `operator-validated`.

#### New `bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/README.md`

Documents:
- **How the KB works**: SqliteKnowledgeBase ingestion, `text-embedding-004` embedder, automatic mtime-based reindex on agent boot.
- **Entry shape**: frontmatter fields, filename convention, sectioned body template.
- **The three confidence buckets**: `operator-validated` (gold standard — names tenant + scenario), `vendor-documented` (vendor doc citation; needs tenant validation before relying), `pattern-derived` (inferred — use sparingly to avoid signal dilution).
- **Local verification recipe**: after saving entries, `docker compose restart phantom-agent` + `curl knowledge_search` to confirm the index picked up.
- **Cross-references**: schema, manifest entry, runtime impl, connector wrapper, related skills.

The README codifies the discipline the issue's `## Forbidden` section asked for — "Don't add queries that haven't been validated against a real XDR tenant" → README's three-bucket model makes "validated" precise + makes it the *expected* default.

### Smoke-test surface

After install (re-run `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`):

- [ ] `docker exec phantom_agent ls /app/bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/entries/ | grep -c xdr` → 5 (the new entries)
- [ ] `/observability/events` shows a `knowledge_indexed` audit row for `kb_name="xql-examples"` after the boot (proves the new entries got embedded)
- [ ] Agent chat: ask *"what XQL query lists recent high-severity alerts on the XDR tenant?"* — agent should hit `knowledge_search` against `xql-examples` and cite entry XQL-162 (or one of the other XDR-pattern entries).
- [ ] Agent chat: ask *"how do I trace a process causality chain in XDR?"* — agent cites XQL-166.
- [ ] `bundles/spark/kbs/xql-examples/README.md` renders cleanly in the GitHub UI or any markdown viewer.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.72

- **Don't add unvalidated entries without the `xdr-pattern` / `pattern-derived` tag + `## Source` disclaimer.** The corpus's value is "queries we know work" — speculative additions dilute that signal. The three-bucket model in the README is how we'll keep it crisp going forward.
- **Don't add tenant-specific field names without redaction.** If a query references a custom dataset only one tenant has, mark it as `(tenant-specific)` in the title or skip it.
- **Don't promote a `pattern-derived` entry to `operator-validated` without actually running it.** The `## Source` line is the audit trail — if it says "validated against tenant X on date Y", that statement must be true.

### Cross-references

- Closes: #37 (spec — XQL examples KB enrichment)
- Pairs with: #36 (cortex-xdr connector, v0.5.61) — the source of the XDR-pattern queries
- Pairs with: #45 (v0.5.70 connector form fix) — operator can now actually create XDR instances + run these queries
- Related skill: `bundles/spark/mcp/skills/foundation/cortex_kb_search.md` (v0.5.69) — the agent's discipline for Cortex KB research. Together they form the research loop: agent searches the Cortex docs (cortex-docs connector) → derives + runs an XQL query (cortex-xdr connector) → if it works, adds to the xql-examples KB (this release's plumbing).
- Future work (out of scope): auto-extract validated queries from chat history. Operator-curated for now per the spec's "operator-validated" bucket discipline.
