## [v0.5.71] — 2026-05-17

**`/providers` Test Connection button: dirty-tracking + JSON-touched gate (closes #18).** Pre-v0.5.71 the button was active immediately after page load even when no field changed — clicking it sent the backend's masked-bullet sentinel as the service-account JSON, the `/providers/vertex/test` endpoint responded "no Vertex service-account JSON is configured yet — paste a real service-account JSON above and click Test", and the operator misread that as "my saved auth is broken" on a working install. The fix brings Test onto the same dirty-tracking model Save Changes already uses (per v0.1.34's untouched-secret round-trip), with one extra check: the JSON field specifically must differ from its loaded value, because the test endpoint receives the JSON from the form and can't probe with the masked sentinel.

### Why

Reported during the v0.2.1 upgrade smoke on `bupa-engine`. The operator's saved Vertex auth was still working in chat (v0.1.36 settings persisted across the upgrade via the named volume), but Test Connection on `/providers` returned a "no JSON configured" error that's wrong in both shape (auth IS configured at rest) and trigger (the button shouldn't have been clickable in the first place). Low-severity UX bug but high friction during operator-facing demos + customer onboarding — a misleading "NO JSON CONFIGURED" banner on a working install undermines trust in the platform's auth status.

### What ships

#### `mcp/agent/app/providers/page.tsx`

**New `canTestVertex()` helper** returns `{ ok: boolean; reason: string }`. The three checks, in order:

1. Project ID populated.
2. Region populated.
3. Service Account JSON populated.
4. JSON differs from the loaded value — i.e., the operator has re-pasted (or this is a fresh install with no prior JSON). Changing only Project or Region without re-pasting the JSON is NOT enough, because the test endpoint receives the JSON from the form and can't probe with the masked sentinel.

When the gate fails, the `reason` string becomes the disabled-button tooltip — the operator sees exactly which precondition isn't met ("Paste a Service Account JSON before testing", "Re-paste the Service Account JSON to test. The masked sentinel can't be probed; your saved creds are already in effect at runtime.", etc.) instead of an opaque grayed-out button.

**Test Connection button** now uses `disabled={!canTestVertex().ok}` and `title={canTestVertex().reason}` instead of the prior `disabled={!hasTestableApiKey("vertex")}` which only enforced the "all three populated" condition.

Save Changes button is unchanged — its prior `disabled={!hasTestableApiKey("vertex") || !isProviderDirty("vertex") || savingProvider === "vertex"}` already enforced dirty-tracking (Save is fine sending the masked sentinel for the JSON, because the backend treats the sentinel as "leave that slot alone"; Test isn't).

The anthropic / openai / ollama Test buttons are unconditionally `disabled` (those providers are WIP); only the Vertex button needed the fix.

### Smoke-test surface

After install (re-run `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`):

- [ ] Sign in to `/providers` with valid Vertex creds (Project + Region + paste JSON → Save). Re-load the page.
- [ ] Page renders: Project + Region in cleartext, JSON shown as masked bullets, Test Connection button **disabled** (hover → tooltip explains "Re-paste the Service Account JSON to test").
- [ ] Type a new Project ID → button STAYS disabled (Project changed but JSON wasn't re-pasted; tooltip still explains).
- [ ] Restore the original Project ID → button still disabled.
- [ ] Paste a real Service Account JSON over the masked field → button **enables** → click → JWT exchange → success/failure banner reflects the actual probe result.
- [ ] Fresh install (no saved Vertex creds): Project + Region + JSON all empty → button disabled with tooltip naming the first missing field. Fill all three → button enables.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.71

- **Don't make Test Connection accept the masked sentinel.** The backend deliberately rejects the sentinel because there's no way to probe it. Adding "if sentinel: skip the call" would silently turn Test into a no-op without telling the operator.
- **Don't apply the same `canTestVertex` pattern to Anthropic / OpenAI Test buttons yet.** Those providers are WIP / disabled at the card level; their Test buttons stay unconditionally disabled until the providers themselves ship. When they do ship, follow the same `canTest<Provider>()` pattern documented here.

### Cross-references

- Closes: #18 (spec)
- Bug reported on: v0.2.1 upgrade smoke (bupa-engine)
- Same-pattern dirty-tracking discipline: v0.1.34 (untouched-secret round-trip via `***` sentinel preservation on Save) — Test now joins Save on the same model.
- Out of scope: the error message itself when Test legitimately fails (already correct for the case where JSON IS pasted but is invalid). Other providers (anthropic / openai) are still WIP.

---

## [v0.5.70] — 2026-05-17

**Connector instance form: widget vocabulary + Advanced Settings hack removed (closes #45).** The instance-creation form on `/connectors → Create Instance` had three coupled bugs that made cortex-xdr + xsiam impossible to configure: API URL had no input element, the required API ID field was hidden behind a collapsible "Advanced Settings" disclosure regardless of meaning, and the Create button stayed disabled because the un-fillable URL field never satisfied `allRequiredFilled`. v0.5.70 expands the widget vocabulary, fixes the renderer to handle every type, and deletes the Advanced split — every field now lands in one unified Configuration section.

### Why

Operator hit this during v0.5.67 hands-on smoke (operator screenshot):

> When I try to create an XDR instance: API URL has no text input field, API Key has one, API ID went under Advanced Settings (which is weird), and the Create Instance button stays disabled. Same with XSIAM — Playground ID also went under Advanced. We need to support multiple field types based on the YAML file: text, radio, dropdown, long text, short text; encrypted (asterisks) for sensitive fields, clear for URL/ID. Remove the Advanced section.

Three coupled bugs in `mcp/agent/app/connectors/page.tsx`:

1. **`ConfigParam.type` union too narrow** — pre-v0.5.70 was `text | secret | boolean | select | array`. The synthetic-card config blocks in `route.ts` use `"url"` and `"string"` (12 fields across 4 connectors: xlog, caldera, xsiam, cortex-xdr, cortex-content). `renderConfigField()` switched on `param.type`, hit no matching case, returned undefined → labels rendered without inputs.
2. **`standardConfig`/`advancedConfig` slice() hack** — line 2794-2795 had `slice(0, -1)` for standard + `slice(-1)` for advanced. The last field landed in Advanced regardless of meaning. cortex-xdr's `[api_url, api_key, api_id]` array → api_id always behind the collapsed disclosure. Inline comment: `// Split config into standard and "advanced" (simulate hidden fields)` — "simulate" was the smoking gun.
3. **Create Instance button stayed disabled** — downstream of #1. With no URL input, `configValues.api_url` stayed undefined; `allRequiredFilled` checks required fields against `configValues[name]`; button stayed grayed.

### What ships

#### `mcp/agent/app/connectors/page.tsx` — widget vocabulary expanded + Advanced disclosure removed

**`ConfigParam.type` union** now includes the full widget taxonomy:

| Widget | Renders | Use when |
|---|---|---|
| `text` | Plain text input | Short free-form strings (default for unknown types) |
| `url` | Text input + `inputMode="url"` | API endpoints, callback URLs |
| `string` | Plain text input | Alias for text — JSON-Schema-style config (api_id, IDs) |
| `number` | Text input + `inputMode="numeric"` | Integer / numeric tuning knobs |
| `secret` / `password` | Masked input with eye-toggle reveal | API keys, tokens, passwords |
| `textarea` | Multiline `<textarea>` | JSON service-account blobs, PEM certs |
| `select` | Single-select dropdown | Discrete enum with 4+ options (requires `options[]`) |
| `radio` | Radio button group | 2-3 options where visibility beats compactness (requires `options[]`) |
| `multi_select` | Checkbox chip list | Pick multiple from known enum, serializes as JSON array (requires `options[]`) |
| `boolean` | Toggle switch | On/off flags |
| `array` | Free-form chip-list editor | List-of-string fields with no enum (e.g. web's `allowed_domains`) |

**`renderConfigField()` rewritten** to handle every type. text/url/string/number share the same `<input>` renderer with `inputMode` hints. secret/password share the masked renderer. textarea uses `<textarea>` with `font-mono resize-y`. radio renders a chip-style button group with proper a11y `<input type="radio">` underneath. multi_select renders checkbox-style chips where the selection serializes to a JSON-stringified array. A default-case branch catches future drift between the union and the renderer — falls back to plain text input + an error message telling the operator to fix the connector card (instead of label-only ghosts).

**Removed `standardConfig`/`advancedConfig` slice() hack** + the `showAdvanced` state + the entire Advanced Settings UI section (~36 lines of JSX). Every field now renders in one unified Configuration section in declaration order. Future "advanced" grouping needs a schema-level flag (e.g. `widget: "advanced"` or property-level `x-advanced: true`), not positional heuristics.

#### `bundles/spark/connectors/connector.schema.json`

The `configSchema` description now documents the optional `widget` keyword + the derivation rule when `widget` is omitted (string→text, integer/number→numeric text, boolean→switch, array→multi_select if enum present else array). Cross-references the help/architecture#connectors-design section for the full table. Not enforced yet — the synthetic-card config blocks in `route.ts` already drive the UI; the YAML widget keyword is forward-looking for the eventual single-source-of-truth refactor.

#### `mcp/agent/app/help/architecture/page.tsx`

New "Setup form widgets (v0.5.70+)" SubSection inside `#connectors-design` documents the 11-row widget table + the post-v0.5.70 unification rule (every field renders under Configuration; no Advanced split; future advanced-grouping needs a schema flag).

#### `mcp/agent/app/help/user/page.tsx`

New "The instance creation form (v0.5.70+)" SubSection at the top of the `#connectors` section describes the form's two sections (Instance Identity + Configuration), names the widget types in operator-friendly language, and explicitly calls out the v0.5.70 fix (label-only ghosts → working inputs; no more Advanced disclosure).

### Smoke-test surface

After install:

- [ ] `/connectors` → click **Create instance** → select Cortex XDR
  - API URL field renders as a text input (clear text, not masked)
  - API Key field renders as a masked input with eye-toggle
  - API ID field renders as a text input (clear text, in the **main Configuration section** — NOT under Advanced)
  - **No "Advanced Settings" disclosure visible** anywhere in the form
  - Type a value in each → **Create Instance** button enables (transitions from grayed to blue)
- [ ] Same for **XSIAM** connector — API URL + API Key + API ID + Playground ID all render in the main Configuration section (Playground ID no longer in Advanced)
- [ ] Same for **Caldera** connector — Caldera URL + API Key + Red user + Red password all render
- [ ] Same for **xlog** connector — xlog URL + API Token render
- [ ] Test caldera-content / cortex-docs / cortex-content (connectors with no secrets or with only one) — form still renders cleanly
- [ ] After Create Instance succeeds: instance card appears; click per-instance **Test Connection** → green (per v0.5.58 flow)
- [ ] Edit-instance dialog still works (separate render path, unaffected by v0.5.70)
- [ ] `/help/architecture#connectors-design` → new "Setup form widgets (v0.5.70+)" SubSection renders with the 11-row widget table
- [ ] `/help/user#connectors` → new "The instance creation form (v0.5.70+)" SubSection at the top of the section

### Forbidden post-v0.5.70

- **Don't reintroduce the `slice(-1)` Advanced split.** Any "advanced" classification must come from a schema-level signal (e.g. `widget: "advanced"` or `x-advanced: true` on the property), not positional heuristics. The whole bug here was a positional heuristic that produced unpredictable form layout for every new connector.
- **Don't add new field types to `ConfigParam.type` without updating `renderConfigField` in the same change.** The pre-v0.5.70 bug was a union that grew silently while the renderer didn't. The default-case branch in `renderConfigField` now warns the operator instead of failing silently, but the right fix is keeping the union + the switch in lockstep.
- **Don't ship a connector synthetic card with a widget type the renderer doesn't handle.** Grep test before adding a new connector or rewriting a config block:
  ```bash
  grep -oE 'type: "[a-z_]+"' mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts | sort -u
  ```
  Against the renderer's switch cases in `mcp/agent/app/connectors/page.tsx` `renderConfigField`. Two seconds, catches the bug immediately.

### Cross-references

- Closes: #45 (spec)
- Bug discovered via: v0.5.67 hands-on smoke (operator screenshot of broken XDR form)
- Companion to: v0.5.67 (cortex-xdr manifest catalog fix) + v0.5.68 (description refresh). v0.5.67 made the card installable; v0.5.68 made the description right; v0.5.70 makes the form actually usable. Together v0.5.67/68/70 complete the XDR connector's release polish.
- Future work (out of scope): wire `mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts` to read directly from `bundles/spark/connectors/*/connector.yaml` instead of maintaining a parallel synthetic-card list. Two parallel catalogs is a known v0.5.0+ debt — track separately.
- Edit-instance dialog (`mcp/agent/app/connectors/page.tsx` around line 2568) uses a SEPARATE simpler render path that classifies fields via regex on the key name. That path was unaffected by the v0.5.70 bugs but the regex-based password detection is a different drift — track separately.

---

## [v0.5.69] — 2026-05-17

**Cortex KB search becomes "fully working"** — three new foundation skills ported from the operator's personal `myworkassistant/cortex-assistant/cortex-docs-search` library (1024 lines of agent-guidance + query-shaping tables + raw API reference), plus a real bug fix to `cortex-docs/fetch_topic`'s child-descent logic that surfaces nested-deep content (multi-level descent, preserve-thin-content-on-descent-failure, lower threshold at recursion levels).

### Why

During v0.5.68 dogfooding the cortex-docs connector (the operator-as-agent loop's first real exercise), I noticed:

1. **The connector tools work** — `cortex-docs/search` returns the canonical `Cortex XDR API Reference` topic for "Cortex XDR API" queries. ✅
2. **But `fetch_topic` was single-level only** — when a topic is a thin TOC-stub container, it descends into direct children; when a child is ALSO a stub, descent stops. The operator's earlier dogfooding session against the XDR API Reference returned thin content because the actual per-endpoint docs aren't in this topic's direct children.
3. **And Phantom was missing the agent-guidance layer entirely** — the operator's personal `myworkassistant` library has a 300-line SKILL.md with the workflow + a 254-line search-patterns reference + a 470-line API-reference doc. The cortex-docs connector ships the scripts (already ported to Phantom in v0.5.55) but NOT the discipline for using them well.

v0.5.69 fixes both:

- **Port the agent-guidance** as three Phantom foundation skills with proper lazy-loading per Phantom's skill convention.
- **Fix the multi-level descent** in `fetch_topic_with_fallback` so deeper TOC structures are surfaced naturally.

### What ships

#### Three new foundation skills

`bundles/spark/mcp/skills/foundation/cortex_kb_search.md` (~370 lines):
- Main workflow skill: decompose → search → fetch → synthesize → cite
- Step-by-step guide on building queries (strip user language, use Palo Alto vocabulary)
- Use `suggest` whenever query contains an action verb ("deploy", "install", "configure")
- Common user→Palo Alto vocabulary mappings (deploy→set up, wipe→isolate, kill process→terminate process, scheduled task→time triggered job, etc.)
- Mandatory product-scope-by-default for XQL/XDR/XSIAM/XSOAR/Cloud/Xpanse
- Source citation discipline + constraints + cross-references to the two reference skills

`bundles/spark/mcp/skills/foundation/cortex_kb_search_patterns.md` (~250 lines):
- **Lazy-loaded** — only when search returns 0 results, question is vague, or quality-check is needed
- 6 query-by-intent tables (one per Cortex product) mapping common user phrasings to canonical search terms
- Terminology reference (DSPM/CSPM/CIEM → Palo Alto brand names, etc.)
- Fallback strategies + relevance-check + response-quality-checklist
- XQL stage quick-reference table (10 stages with correct search terms)

`bundles/spark/mcp/skills/foundation/cortex_kb_api_reference.md` (~470 lines):
- **Lazy-loaded** — only when crafting custom API calls beyond the connector tools
- Full Fluid Topics API spec: 9 endpoints with request/response shapes
- Metadata-filter patterns (by publication, cluster, product)
- Sort options, error handling, locale guidance (`en-US` is the only locale with full content)

All three skills use Phantom's standard frontmatter (name/displayName/category/description/icon/source/loadingMode/locked/attack), `loadingMode: on-demand` so they don't bloat the agent's context for non-Cortex questions, and cross-reference each other for the lazy-load pattern.

#### `fetch_topic_with_fallback` — multi-level descent + thin-content preservation

`bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-docs/src/fetch_topic.py`:

**New `max_depth` parameter (default 2)** — when a fetched child is itself a stub, recursively descend into ITS children, up to max_depth levels. Pre-v0.5.69 was single-level only.

**Dual-threshold logic** — at top level (_depth=0), use the operator-supplied `min_content_chars` (default 300). At recursion levels (_depth>0), use a tight 50-char threshold so thin-but-real content (200-char meaningful prose) gets preserved instead of aggressively descending and losing it to children that turn out to be even thinner.

**Preserve bare content on descent failure** — if descent yields no enrichable content but the bare fetch returned thin-but-real content, KEEP the bare content (instead of replacing it with a "[Container topic — children empty]" placeholder). Add a footnote pointing to `fetch_toc` for manual TOC navigation.

**TOC caching across recursion levels** — `_toc_data` is passed down through recursive calls so deeper levels don't re-fetch the same TOC.

**Verification (against live public docs)**:

| Case | Pre-fix | Post-fix | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| XDR API Reference (truly empty container, 1 child) | 0 chars bare → 732 chars after descent | 732 chars after descent | Single-level descent already sufficient; no regression |
| Stages with `min_content_chars=10000` (stress test) | 128 chars placeholder | 2755 chars (alter + arrayexpand combined) | Multi-level descent now preserves children content even when their own children are stubs |
| Stages with default threshold | 387 chars bare content | 387 chars bare content | No descent fires; substantive content is left alone |

#### connector.yaml + connector.py — expose max_depth

`bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-docs/connector.yaml`:
- Version bumped 0.3.1 → 0.3.2
- New `max_depth` arg in `fetch_topic` tool spec (`Max recursion depth when children are also stubs (default 2; set 1 to restore pre-v0.5.69 single-level descent)`)

`bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-docs/src/connector.py`:
- `cortex_fetch_topic()` wrapper accepts + forwards `max_depth`

### Smoke-test surface

After install:

- [ ] `/skills` → new "Cortex KB Search" skill card (loadingMode: on-demand). Click → full skill markdown renders. Two related skill cards: "Cortex KB Search — Query Patterns" + "Cortex KB Search — API Reference" (both loadingMode: on-demand).
- [ ] `cortex-docs/fetch_topic` called against the Cortex XDR API Reference topic (`map_id=GD6sG6FlxDWxAn13_eZuUQ`, `topic_id=w91IELc94ACm9EJ2tr5RrA`) — returns 732 chars + `descent_depth: 1` + `children_fetched: ["APIs Overview"]`.
- [ ] Agent chat: "find me docs on how to deploy Broker VM on Azure" — agent should: (1) call `cortex-docs/suggest("Broker VM Azure")` first to find the exact Palo Alto title, (2) call `cortex-docs/search` with `product=xdr` using that title, (3) cite the source. (Per the new skill's workflow.)
- [ ] Agent chat: "what does the XQL filter stage do?" — agent searches `"Cortex Query Language filter"` with `product=agentix` (per the XQL stage quick-reference in cortex_kb_search_patterns). Returns filter stage syntax + example.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.69

- **Don't bypass the connector to reach the public Cortex docs API.** That's the v0.5.69 skill's whole point — `cortex-docs/search` and `cortex-docs/fetch_topic` ARE the agent's path. Direct urllib calls in agent reasoning are the antipattern this skill exists to prevent.
- **Don't load the two reference skills eagerly.** They're `loadingMode: on-demand` for a reason — the search-patterns skill is only needed for ambiguous/failed searches, the API-reference skill only for custom-API-call construction. Loading them preemptively bloats the agent context for the 95% of queries that don't need them.
- **Don't ship a new agent-guidance skill without `loadingMode: on-demand`** unless the skill is truly relevant to most operator questions. The default should be lazy.

### Cross-references

- Source: operator's personal `~/Documents/myworkassistant/skills/cortex-assistant/cortex-docs-search/` (Feb-Mar 2026 authoring; v0.5.69 port to Phantom)
- Companion: v0.5.68 (XDR connector description rewrite for general-purpose use) — both this session's smoke-test feedback
- Bug discovered via: operator-as-agent dogfooding of the cortex-docs connector while building the XDR connector

## [v0.5.68] — 2026-05-17

**Rewrite Cortex XDR connector description + metadata for general-purpose use.** v0.5.61's framing was too narrow ("read-only counterpart to Caldera for detection-validation loop") — operators reading the marketplace card would conclude the connector only makes sense if they're driving Caldera attacks. In reality the XDR connector is just as useful for everyday SOC workflows: incident triage, threat hunting, investigation reporting. v0.5.68 broadens the description to reflect that. Also bumps the connector version 0.1.0 → 0.1.1 + replaces the narrow `detection/validation` tags with `siem/incidents/alerts/threat-hunting/investigation/incident-response`. No code change.

### Why

Operator (during v0.5.67 hands-on smoke) noted:
> "Lets also update the xdr connector description and metada to focus on the generic use of the connector, currently its focused on caldera simulation validation on xdr"

That's right. The XDR connector wraps the Cortex XDR Public API's read paths — incidents, alerts, XQL queries. Detection-validation against Caldera is ONE use case among many; the connector is just as useful for any incident-response / threat-hunting / investigation workflow. The original v0.5.61 framing reflected the EPIC #39 motivating use case rather than the connector's full surface area.

### What ships

#### `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-xdr/connector.yaml`

Description (was): `"Cortex XDR connector — list incidents + run XQL queries against the XDR data lake. Closes the agent-as-operator detection-validation loop with the Caldera connector."`

Description (now): `"Cortex XDR API wrapper — list incidents and alerts, drill into specific cases, and run XQL queries against the XDR data lake. General-purpose surface for incident response, threat hunting, investigation reporting, and detection coverage validation."`

Tags (was): `["cortex", "xdr", "edr", "incidents", "xql", "detection", "validation"]`

Tags (now): `["cortex", "xdr", "edr", "siem", "incidents", "alerts", "xql", "threat-hunting", "investigation", "incident-response"]`

Version bumped to 0.1.1.

#### `mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts`

Same description + tags rewrite. longDescription now leads with three operator-question examples (incident triage, alert drill-down, XQL hunt) before mentioning Caldera-validation as one use case among several. setupGuide step 7 broadened from "ask 'show me Cortex XDR incidents in the last 24 hours'" to three example natural-language queries spanning the connector's four tools.

#### Honest acknowledgment in the connector.yaml comments

Added a v0.5.68 comment noting the rewrite + WHY the original framing was narrow. Future operators reading the source will see the reasoning trail.

### Smoke-test surface

After install:

- [ ] `/connectors` → Cortex XDR card displays the new description (mentions "incident response, threat hunting, investigation reporting" not just Caldera)
- [ ] Card tags show `siem` + `threat-hunting` + `investigation` chips (no more `detection`/`validation`-only framing)
- [ ] Hover/click for longDescription — leads with three operator-question examples
- [ ] Version field shows `0.1.1` with both 0.1.0 + 0.1.1 entries in the version history

### Forbidden post-v0.5.68

- **Don't narrow connector descriptions to a single use case** without leading with the connector's broader surface. The XDR connector wraps a SIEM/EDR API — its description should lead with the API surface (incidents + XQL), not with one downstream use case (Caldera-validation). Same rule for future connectors: lead with what the API DOES, follow with example use cases.
- **Don't remove the Caldera-validation use case from the description.** It's still a valid + important use case — just shouldn't be the dominant framing.

### Cross-references

- Companion to: v0.5.67 (manifest fix) — same operator hands-on smoke that caught the v0.5.67 bug also surfaced this framing concern. Together v0.5.67 + v0.5.68 close out the XDR connector's release polish.
- Pattern source: each connector description should lead with the API surface (xlog "log generation engine", caldera "C2 framework integration", xsiam "XSIAM PAPI wrapper"). XDR now follows the same pattern.

## [v0.5.67] — 2026-05-17

**Fix Cortex XDR connector install failure ("connector 'cortex-xdr' not found in catalogue").** v0.5.61 shipped the connector code + the synthetic marketplace card, but missed the `bundles/spark/manifest.yaml:toolConnectors[]` entry that the MCP's install endpoint reads. Result: card visible in `/connectors`, install button returns HTTP 404. Plus a comprehensive **"Adding a new connector — checklist"** section in `docs/CICD.md` that codifies all 8 required edits to prevent recurrence.

### Why

Operator hit this on v0.5.66 hands-on smoke: clicking Install on the Cortex XDR card produced the error `connector 'cortex-xdr' not found in catalogue`. Tracing the call:

- UI's `/connectors` → renders from `mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts` synthetic-card list ✅ (cortex-xdr was added in v0.5.61)
- UI's "Install" button → `POST /api/v1/marketplace/{id}/install` → MCP's `_scan_catalogue()` reads `bundles/spark/manifest.yaml:toolConnectors[]` ❌ (cortex-xdr was MISSING here)

Phantom has two parallel connector catalogs:
1. **Bundle catalogue** in `manifest.yaml` — drives the install endpoint
2. **Synthetic card list** in `route.ts` — drives the UI's available-connectors display

v0.5.61 updated #2 but missed #1. The card rendered but install failed.

### What ships

#### `bundles/spark/manifest.yaml` — add cortex-xdr to toolConnectors

```yaml
- id: "cortex-xdr"
  path: "./connectors/cortex-xdr/"
  version: "0.1.0"
  required: false
```

Inserted after `cortex-content`'s entry. Marked `required: false` (agent functions without an XDR tenant; this connector enables detection-validation only).

#### `docs/CICD.md` — new "Adding a new connector — checklist" section

8-row table enumerating every load-bearing edit when adding a new connector:

| # | File | Failure if missing |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `connectors/<id>/connector.yaml` | Build fails |
| 2 | `connectors/<id>/src/connector.py` | Tool calls ImportError |
| 3 | `connectors/<id>/Dockerfile` | No image built |
| **4** | **`manifest.yaml:toolConnectors[]`** | **"not found in catalogue" install failure** |
| 5 | `.github/workflows/build-connectors.yml` job | No image pushed |
| 6 | `connector_probes.py` PROBE_IMPLEMENTED + probe block | Test Connection misleading |
| 7 | `marketplace/connectors/route.ts` synthetic card | Not visible in UI |
| 8 | `connector_loader.py` env-var aliases (if any) | First-time-setup translation breaks |

Plus 2 operator-doc edits (`/help/architecture` + `/help/user`) and a **quick-check command**:

```bash
comm -23 \
  <(ls bundles/spark/connectors/ | grep -v _runtime | grep -v 'connector.schema.json' | sort) \
  <(grep -oE '^  - id: "[a-z][a-z0-9-]+"' bundles/spark/manifest.yaml | grep -oE '"[a-z][a-z0-9-]+"' | tr -d '"' | sort -u)
```

Any output = a connector dir without a manifest entry. Empty output = consistent. Future connector PRs run this before commit.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.67

- **Don't ship a connector synthetic card (route.ts) without a manifest entry (manifest.yaml).** That's the exact failure v0.5.61 introduced + v0.5.67 fixed. The two catalogs MUST stay in sync.
- **Don't merge a connector PR without running the quick-check.** Two seconds. Catches the bug immediately.

### Smoke-test surface

After v0.5.67 install:

- [ ] `/connectors` → Cortex XDR card visible (already worked pre-v0.5.67)
- [ ] Click "Install" on the Cortex XDR card → succeeds; row added to marketplace_store
- [ ] "Create instance" → form shows API URL / API key / API ID fields → save → Test Connection green

### Cross-references

- Bug introduced by: v0.5.61 (missed manifest.yaml entry when adding the new connector)
- Followups: none — this is a complete fix + future-recurrence prevention. v0.5.66 entrypoint-hook ensures the Caldera-side plugin activation; v0.5.67 ensures the agent-side connector catalog activation. Pair makes the new-connector path complete.

## [v0.5.66] — 2026-05-17

**Entrypoint hook ensures phantom + emu plugins enabled across upgrades.** Companion to v0.5.65's plugin-pattern fix. Discovered during v0.5.65 post-install smoke: even with phantom plugin code correctly baked into `plugins/phantom/data/` at the right path, Caldera's entrypoint script reads `local.yml` from the **`caldera_conf` named volume** which was populated at first install — Docker doesn't re-copy named-volume content from new image builds. So v0.5.63/65's `default.yml` modifications were shadowed by the frozen volume content; restart → entrypoint regenerates `local.yml` from the volume's frozen `default.yml` → phantom + emu entries lost on every restart. Closes the v0.5.65 follow-up.

### Why

v0.5.65 fixed the data/ volume-shadow bug (#44) by moving Phantom content to `plugins/phantom/data/`. Verified by `docker exec caldera ls /usr/src/app/plugins/phantom/data/abilities/` — all 12 subdirs present in the image.

But the **plugin enable** step still didn't take effect at runtime. Investigation:

1. v0.5.65 workflow correctly inserted `- phantom` + `- emu` into the image's `/usr/src/app/conf/default.yml`
2. The customer compose mounts `caldera_conf:/usr/src/app/conf` — volume content shadows image content
3. The volume was populated at first install (pre-v0.5.65) with a default.yml that has NO phantom/emu
4. Image's modified default.yml is NEVER read at runtime — the volume's stale copy wins
5. Caldera's entrypoint regenerates `local.yml` from the volume's `default.yml` on every startup → phantom/emu absent

Manual workaround (`docker exec ... sed ... local.yml; docker restart`) **doesn't work** because the entrypoint OVERWRITES local.yml on every restart from default.yml.

### Root cause

Docker named volumes are populated from the image on FIRST CREATE only. Subsequent image rebuilds don't update the volume. This is a one-way ratchet: any config-file modification that should propagate via image rebuilds is shadowed forever.

The bug applies to BOTH volume mounts in the caldera compose:
- `caldera_data:/usr/src/app/data` — fixed in v0.5.65 by moving content to `plugins/<name>/` (outside mount)
- `caldera_conf:/usr/src/app/conf` — v0.5.66 fixes by extending the entrypoint to reconcile at startup (the OPPOSITE approach: accept the volume mount, do the merging in code)

### What ships

#### `installer/docker-compose.yml` — extended caldera entrypoint

After the existing `cfg["users"]["red"] = {red_user: red_password}` block, the entrypoint now appends:

```python
plugins = cfg.setdefault("plugins", [])
for required in ("phantom", "emu"):
    if required not in plugins:
        plugins.append(required)
        print(f"[caldera-init] enabled plugin: {required}")
```

Idempotent — re-runs are no-ops once entries exist. Logs the change so operators can see what happened in `docker logs caldera`. Runs on every container start, so:

- Fresh install: phantom + emu added during the first entrypoint pass; preserved in `local.yml` thereafter
- Upgrade from any v0.5.x: phantom + emu added on next restart; subsequent restarts no-op
- Operator deliberately disabled a plugin via removing from list: entries re-added on next restart (the correct way to disable a plugin is via its `hook.py` `enabled` attribute or the Caldera UI — not via list-removal)

#### Why NOT just modify default.yml

That's what v0.5.65 tried — and it works in the image, but the runtime sees the volume's frozen default.yml. The entrypoint approach catches the volume-shadow case AND the fresh-install case in one place.

#### Why this is Scenario 2 (installer change)

`installer/docker-compose.yml` is part of the customer installer template. Operators run the new installer to pick up the entrypoint change. No customer-side data migration; the entrypoint reconciles automatically.

### Smoke-test surface

After installing v0.5.66 (`sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`):

- [ ] `docker logs caldera | grep "caldera-init.*enabled plugin"` shows both `enabled plugin: phantom` and `enabled plugin: emu` on the first post-upgrade restart (no output on subsequent restarts — idempotent)
- [ ] `docker exec caldera grep -A 14 '^plugins:' /usr/src/app/conf/local.yml` shows phantom + emu in the plugins list
- [ ] `curl -sk -H 'KEY: ...' http://<vm>:8888/api/v2/health | jq '.plugins[] | select(.enabled and .name=="phantom")'` returns a non-empty object — phantom plugin loaded
- [ ] Caldera UI → Adversaries → Phantom phishing kill chain + Phantom Lookalike Validation visible with full atomic_ordering resolved (no "Missing ability" rows)
- [ ] CTID emu adversaries still empty (v0.5.63's walker bug remains; tracked for v0.5.67)

### Forbidden post-v0.5.66

- **Don't try to fix conf-volume issues by modifying default.yml in the image.** Volume shadows it. Use the entrypoint pattern.
- **Don't remove the entrypoint's plugin-enable block.** Operators upgrading from any pre-v0.5.66 release rely on this to pick up phantom + emu enable.

### Cross-references

- Discovered during: v0.5.65 post-install smoke (this session)
- Companion to: v0.5.65 (#44) — together they fix the volume-shadow problem in both directions (data/ via plugin layout + conf/ via entrypoint reconciliation)
- Pattern source: phantom-agent's v0.3.2 skills-bootstrap entrypoint (same marker-driven default-merge for `/app/skills/`)
- Followups: v0.5.67 — CTID emu walker bug + atomic-based Phantom chains (#42)

## [v0.5.65] — 2026-05-17

**Fix v0.5.57's "factory state" claim — Phantom Caldera content moved to plugin layout so it survives the `caldera_data` volume mount.** Discovered during smoke-test of v0.5.58-64: the running phantom-caldera container's `/usr/src/app/data/` directory contained NONE of the v0.5.57 kill-chain abilities, v0.5.63 CTID adversaries, or v0.5.64 lookalikes — because the customer compose mounts `caldera_data:/usr/src/app/data` as a named volume that shadows any image-baked content at runtime. v0.5.65 corrects the overlay strategy: Phantom content now lives at `plugins/phantom/data/` (a path NOT volume-mounted), and the phantom plugin is registered in Caldera's `default.yml` plugins list. Closes [#44](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/44).

### Why

The smoke test of v0.5.58-64 (operator-driven `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev` followed by direct `docker exec caldera ls /usr/src/app/data/abilities/`) revealed:

- Only Caldera's default empty `data/abilities/` subdirs visible (`collection/`, `discovery/`, etc.)
- **None** of the 11 Phantom subdirs (`00-bootstrap/`, `04-credential-access/`, ..., `14-lab-safe-lookalikes/`) present
- Caldera UI Adversaries view: no Phantom Lookalike Validation, no APT29/FIN6/OilRig from CTID
- CI logs SHOW the `cp -rv` succeeded — files made it into the build context
- But `installer/docker-compose.yml` mounts `caldera_data:/usr/src/app/data` as a Docker named volume → image-baked content is shadowed at runtime

In short: **v0.5.57's "factory state" claim was aspirational, never validated against a fresh install.** Earlier in this session the kill chain worked only because the YAMLs had been REST-uploaded — invisible to fresh customer installs. The smoke caught this for v0.5.63 (CTID emu — 0 adversaries) and v0.5.64 (lookalikes — directory missing), then traced it back to v0.5.57.

### Root cause

Caldera's volume-mount design is intentional — `caldera_data` and `caldera_conf` preserve operator-modified state (UI-created adversaries, edited config) across container recreates. For NEW content shipped with a release, the correct integration surface is the **plugin pattern**:

- Plugins live at `plugins/<name>/` — NOT volume-mounted, so image-baked content persists
- Each plugin has `plugins/<name>/data/{abilities,adversaries,planners,sources,payloads}/`
- Caldera's `data_svc.py` scans both `data/*` AND `plugins/*/data/*` at startup
- Plugin content loads with the plugin's access level (red/blue/etc.)

The stockpile plugin is the canonical example: 162 abilities baked into `plugins/stockpile/data/abilities/` survive every container recreate + render in Caldera UI immediately on every fresh install.

### What ships

#### A — New `phantom` plugin shell

`bundles/spark/caldera-content/plugin/`:

- `__init__.py` (empty, just for Python package recognition)
- `hook.py` — minimal `name = 'Phantom'`, `description = ...`, `address = ''` (no GUI tab), empty `async def enable(services)`. Pure-content plugin; the plugin loader registers metadata + triggers data_svc to scan `plugins/phantom/data/`.
- `VERSION.txt` — `0.1.0`

#### B — `build-caldera.yml` overlay step rewrite

Old: `bundles/spark/caldera-content/{abilities,adversaries}/` → `third_party/caldera/data/{abilities,adversaries}/` (shadowed by volume)

New: `bundles/spark/caldera-content/{abilities,adversaries,plugin/{__init__.py,hook.py,VERSION.txt},README.md}` → `third_party/caldera/plugins/phantom/{data/abilities,data/adversaries,__init__.py,hook.py,VERSION.txt,PHANTOM-KILL-CHAIN-README.md}` (survives volume mount)

Step renamed: `Overlay Phantom kill-chain content` → `Overlay Phantom plugin content`.

#### C — Enable phantom plugin in `default.yml`

New step `Enable phantom plugin in caldera default.yml` — idempotently inserts `- phantom` into the `plugins:` list in `third_party/caldera/conf/default.yml`. Insertion point: between `manx` and `response` (alphabetical).

**Critical**: target is `default.yml` not `local.yml`. The v0.5.63 step that tried `local.yml` failed (`WARN: local.yml not found`) because `local.yml` doesn't exist in the build context — it's generated at runtime by Caldera. `default.yml` IS in the image and works as expected for fresh installs.

#### D — v0.5.63 emu-enable step now targets `default.yml` too

The same path-correction applied to the emu-enable step (was `local.yml`, now `default.yml`). v0.5.63's CTID overlay step itself remains buggy (0/0 walker output — wrong directory layout assumption). That's tracked separately as future work; v0.5.65 unblocks the broader plugin pattern even if CTID-emu content stays empty until its walker is fixed.

### Upgrade-vs-fresh-install caveat

`installer/docker-compose.yml` also mounts `caldera_conf:/usr/src/app/conf` as a named volume — so existing operators with populated `caldera_conf` volumes have a `local.yml` that overrides our new `default.yml`. **For existing installs**, the operator action is:

```bash
# One-time, after running the v0.5.65 dev-installer
docker exec -it caldera sh -c \
  'grep -qE "^- phantom$" /usr/src/app/conf/local.yml || sed -i "/^- manx$/a- phantom" /usr/src/app/conf/local.yml'
docker restart caldera
```

For fresh installs (factory-reset path, never-installed-before VMs): no operator action needed — `default.yml` is loaded directly when `local.yml` doesn't exist yet, and phantom plugin auto-enables.

A follow-up release (v0.5.67+) will add an entrypoint hook that merges new default plugins into existing `local.yml` at startup, making the upgrade case truly hands-off too.

### Smoke-test surface

After v0.5.65 install:

- [ ] `docker exec caldera ls /usr/src/app/plugins/phantom/data/abilities/` — see all 11 Phantom subdirs
- [ ] `docker exec caldera ls /usr/src/app/plugins/phantom/data/adversaries/` — see `phantom-phishing-kill-chain.yml` + `phantom-lookalike-validation.yml`
- [ ] **Run the one-time operator action above** (since this is an upgrade, not a fresh install)
- [ ] Caldera UI → Adversaries → Phantom Lookalike Validation + Phantom phishing → ransomware kill chain both visible with full atomic_ordering resolved
- [ ] Caldera UI → Abilities → search "Lookalike" → 5 abilities (Powerkatz-lite, Fodhelper-trace, ETW-patch-marker, AMSI-bypass-marker, comsvcs-minidump-trace)

### Forbidden post-v0.5.65

- **Don't reintroduce the data/ overlay pattern** for content shipped with releases. Use `plugins/<name>/data/` always — that path is outside the volume mount and works through both fresh and upgrade installs.
- **Don't modify `local.yml` in the build context.** It doesn't exist there; the file is runtime-generated and lives on the caldera_conf volume. Always modify `default.yml`.

### Cross-references

- Bug introduced by: v0.5.57 (factory-state overlay claim, never validated)
- Bugs exposed: v0.5.63 (CTID emu — content present in build context but didn't reach image runtime due to data/ pattern), v0.5.64 (lookalikes — same issue)
- Related: v0.3.2 skills-bootstrap entrypoint pattern (phantom-agent's `entrypoint.sh` already implements the marker-driven default-merge for `/app/skills/`; v0.5.67+ phantom-caldera could adopt the same pattern for `conf/local.yml` merging)
- Cross-refs: EPIC [#39](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/39)

## [v0.5.64] — 2026-05-17

**Lab-safe lookalike abilities for techniques Cortex signature-blocks.** 5 new abilities under `bundles/spark/caldera-content/abilities/14-lab-safe-lookalikes/` that emit the same syscall / registry / file-write / process-create patterns Cortex XDR's signature engine matches against — without achieving the actual malicious effect. Detection events fire; sandcat agent survives the full chain. Plus a `Phantom Lookalike Validation` adversary that runs all 5 in sequence. Closes [#43](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/43).

### Why

The v0.5.57 phantom-killchain run + Alice 2.0 run exposed two distinct ways Cortex XDR interferes with adversary execution:

1. **Cortex kills the parent process** — e.g. when sandcat spawns a child that matches a Cortex signature (Fodhelper → cmd.exe → Child Process Protection code 80400057). The agent dies; the chain stalls.
2. **Cortex blocks the sensitive syscall** but lets the parent continue — Mimikatz's `OpenProcess(lsass.exe, ...)` returns "access denied" via `cyvrmtgn`; the PowerShell host runs to completion with exit 0 but credential extraction fails.

For detection-coverage testing, case 1 means "we can't test what happens AFTER the blocked technique" because the chain stops. Case 2 means "the technique partial-fires" — useful for detection-rule validation but not for narrative completeness.

The lookalike pattern: emit the syscall / registry / parent-child / command-line pattern Cortex signatures match against, then EXIT before the part that would kill the agent. Cortex fires its detection event (because the matched pattern occurred); the chain continues.

### What ships

#### 5 new ability YAMLs at `bundles/spark/caldera-content/abilities/14-lab-safe-lookalikes/`

| Ability | Real technique | What Cortex would block | What the lookalike emits | Lab-safety |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `powerkatz-lite.yml` | T1003.001 LSASS dump (Mimikatz) | LSA acquire syscall | P/Invoke `kernel32!OpenProcess(lsass.exe, PROCESS_VM_READ \| PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION)` → immediately `CloseHandle` + literal Mimikatz signature strings in PowerShell variable scope for AMSI scan match | No memory read; no credential extraction; handle immediately closed |
| `fodhelper-trace.yml` | T1548.002 Fodhelper UAC bypass | `fodhelper.exe` auto-elevation child spawn | `Set-ItemProperty HKCU:\Software\Classes\ms-settings\Shell\Open\command\(Default)` → 500ms wait → cleanup | Does NOT invoke fodhelper.exe; registry hierarchy removed after the SET |
| `etw-patch-marker.yml` | T1562.006 ETW patching | `VirtualProtect` write to ETW function pointer | `GetModuleHandle("ntdll.dll")` → `GetProcAddress(ntdll, "EtwEventWrite")` → query address only | No `VirtualProtect` call; ETW remains fully functional |
| `amsi-bypass-marker.yml` | T1562.001 AMSI bypass | Reflective `SetValue` on `AmsiUtils.amsiInitFailed` | Embeds literal `'System.Management.Automation.AmsiUtils'` + `'amsiInitFailed'` strings in variables for AMSI scan match | No reflective field set; AMSI active for subsequent commands |
| `comsvcs-minidump-trace.yml` | T1003.001 LSASS dump (rundll32) | rundll32 + comsvcs.dll + MiniDump → lsass.exe handle | `rundll32 comsvcs.dll, MiniDump -1 <path> full` (invalid PID -1) | rundll32 spawns + loads comsvcs.dll but MiniDump fails on bad PID; no lsass access |

Each ability:
- Deterministic UUID5 from a fixed Phantom namespace
- 60s timeout (lookalikes are quick — they emit the pattern + exit)
- Explicit `[+] CORTEX SHOULD FIRE` / similar markers in stdout so operators can grep the chain output to confirm intent
- Description names the safety mechanism + links #43 for traceability

#### 1 new adversary YAML `bundles/spark/caldera-content/adversaries/phantom-lookalike-validation.yml`

5-step chain running all lookalikes in narrative order: credential dumping (2 variants) → privilege escalation → defense evasion (2 variants). Expected total runtime ~3 minutes. Use to validate "do my Cortex rules fire on the patterns?" without any agent-killing or actual exploit.

### Smoke-test surface

After install (operator runs `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`):

- [ ] Caldera UI (https://localhost:8889) → Abilities → search "Lookalike" — see 5 new abilities tagged with their real ATT&CK technique
- [ ] Caldera UI → Adversaries → "Phantom Lookalike Validation" exists with 5 atomic_ordering entries; all UUIDs resolve cleanly
- [ ] Run the lookalike-validation adversary against `group=red` xdragent → all 5 steps complete (no sandcat death) in ~3 minutes
- [ ] Per-step output shows `[+]` markers + the specific pattern emitted (LSASS handle attempt, registry SET hierarchy, ETW GetProcAddress, AMSI signature strings, rundll32 comsvcs command line)
- [ ] Cortex XDR console (or via #36 XDR connector once v0.5.61 lands): detection events for each step within the run window — specifically:
  - Powerkatz-Lite: Sysmon EID 10 (handle to lsass.exe from PowerShell)
  - Fodhelper-Trace: Sysmon EID 13 on the registry value SET
  - ETW-Patch-Marker: Cortex BTP behavioral alert on the GetProcAddress sequence
  - AMSI-Bypass-Marker: Cortex AMSI integration alert on the literal-string match
  - Comsvcs-Minidump-Trace: Sysmon EID 1 with rundll32 + comsvcs.dll + MiniDump command line

### Forbidden post-v0.5.64

- **Don't ship a lookalike that actually achieves its malicious effect.** Powerkatz-lite MUST NOT extract credentials. Fodhelper-trace MUST NOT auto-elevate. AMSI-bypass-marker MUST NOT disable AMSI. The point is detection-pattern emission without operational compromise.
- **Don't bury lookalikes in the main adversary list.** They live in a dedicated `14-lab-safe-lookalikes/` directory + tagged `lookalike` + `lab-safe` so operators know which abilities are real vs. detection-only.
- **Don't lookalike-ify techniques Cortex doesn't block.** If the real technique runs through Cortex (like the v0.5.57 chain's T1070.001 `wevtutil cl Security`), there's no need for a lookalike. Lookalikes are ONLY for techniques where Cortex's prevention layer would stop the test.

### Cross-references

- EPIC: [#39](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/39)
- Source of need: [#36](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/36) (XDR connector for telemetry capture) + [#40](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/40) (battery matrix where Cortex blocks were documented)
- Pairs with: [#42](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/42) (atomic-based chains — DEFERRED to next session; atomic-author may substitute a lookalike in place of a real atomic test for Cortex-blocked chains)

---

## [v0.5.63] — 2026-05-17

**CTID Adversary Emulation Plans baked into the phantom-caldera image.** Extends the v0.5.57 build-overlay pattern with two new workflow steps: clone the CTID `adversary_emulation_library` from `center-for-threat-informed-defense/...` into the emu plugin's data tree, then enable the `emu` plugin in Caldera's `local.yml`. Operators get APT29 / FIN6 / OilRig / menuPass / Sandworm / Carbanak preloaded — real APT TTPs Caldera ships specifically for the kind of detection-validation work this whole workstream is about. Closes [#41](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/41).

### Why

Discovered in the v0.5.57 plugin audit: `mitre/emu` plugin shell shipped with Caldera 5.3 but had **0 abilities + 0 adversaries** in our build. The actual content lives in a separate repo (`center-for-threat-informed-defense/adversary_emulation_library`) that Caldera's emu plugin reads at runtime — we never cloned it.

CTID's library contains real APT TTPs operators want to test SOC detection rules against:
- **APT29** (Cozy Bear) — Microsoft / DoJ / FireEye attributed campaigns; 2-day chain with 50+ steps each
- **FIN6** — POS-malware criminal group; credential theft → lateral → exfil chain
- **OilRig** — Iranian APT; phishing → backdoor → persistence → exfil
- **menuPass** (APT10) — China-attributed; MSP supply-chain attacks
- **Sandworm** — Russian GRU; NotPetya emulation
- **Carbanak** — Financial APT; SWIFT-network targeted

Each is curated, ATT&CK-mapped, and well-documented — much higher quality than what we'd author from scratch.

### What ships

#### `.github/workflows/build-caldera.yml` — two new steps

**Step "Overlay CTID Adversary Emulation Plans into emu plugin"**:

- Shallow-clones `github.com/center-for-threat-informed-defense/adversary_emulation_library` to `/tmp/ctid-library` (no auth required — public repo)
- Walks both CTID directory layouts (newer `<adv>/Resources/caldera/`, older `<adv>/Emulation_Plan/CALDERA/`)
- Copies ability YAMLs to `third_party/caldera/plugins/emu/data/abilities/<adv-name>/`
- Copies adversary YAMLs to `third_party/caldera/plugins/emu/data/adversaries/<adv-name>/`
- **Graceful degrade**: if the CTID clone fails (network blip, repo moved), the step exits 0 with a warning rather than blocking the entire phantom-caldera image build. The emu plugin then reports empty content; stockpile + atomic + phantom adversaries still ship.

**Step "Enable emu plugin in Caldera config"**:

- Idempotent insertion of `- emu` into the `plugins:` list in `third_party/caldera/conf/local.yml`
- Insertion is alphabetical (between `debrief` and `fieldmanual`)
- Skip if already present (re-runs don't duplicate)
- Outputs the post-edit plugins block for build-log inspection

Both steps run after the existing v0.5.57 Phantom-content overlay so emu content doesn't trample our kill chain.

### Smoke-test surface

After install (operator runs `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`):

- [ ] `docker exec caldera ls /usr/src/app/plugins/emu/data/adversaries/` — see directories for each CTID-published adversary (APT29, FIN6, OilRig, etc.)
- [ ] `docker exec caldera ls /usr/src/app/plugins/emu/data/abilities/` — see ability YAMLs grouped by adversary
- [ ] Caldera UI (https://localhost:8889) → Adversaries → filter list — find ~10-15 new adversaries from CTID library alongside the 27 stockpile adversaries
- [ ] Pick an adversary (e.g. FIN6) → view its atomic_ordering → all UUIDs resolve to real ability records (no "Missing ability" rows)
- [ ] Create a one-step test operation from one of the CTID adversaries → completes (or surfaces specific step-level errors we can decode)
- [ ] Phantom kill chain + stockpile adversaries still appear correctly — overlay didn't trample existing content

### Forbidden post-v0.5.63

- **Don't pin the CTID library to a specific commit hash** unless we discover layout-instability. Their `main` branch is curated; shallow-cloning latest is appropriate for a content overlay (vs. pinning a code dependency where reproducibility matters more).
- **Don't enable additional plugins (e.g. `human`) without populating their data.** That produces an empty plugin pane in Caldera UI, which is operator-confusing. Enable + populate in the same release.
- **Don't ship CTID content in a separate phantom-emu image.** Keep it in phantom-caldera so the customer compose only changes one digest. The overlay-at-build pattern is the architectural fit.

### Cross-references

- EPIC: [#39](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/39)
- Pattern source: v0.5.57 overlay-at-build (already documented in `.github/workflows/build-caldera.yml`)
- Pairs with: [#40](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/40) (`docs/caldera-adversary-battery.md`) — once emu adversaries are running, the battery matrix expands with their rows
- Pairs with: [#42](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/42) (atomic-based Phantom adversaries — coming up in the next release)

## [v0.5.62] — 2026-05-17

**`docs/caldera-adversary-battery.md` — durable matrix tracking adversary runs against the Phantom + Cortex XDR lab.** Closes [#40](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/40).

### Why

Adversary runs accumulate findings that currently live only in session context. When the chat compacts or a new session starts, that knowledge vanishes — the next attempt to run e.g. Worm rediscovers the same broken-ability issues from scratch. v0.5.62 externalizes the findings as a living markdown matrix.

### What ships

`docs/caldera-adversary-battery.md` — initial population covers the 3 adversaries we've already run (Phantom kill chain v0.5.57, Alice 2.0, Defense Evasion) plus a queue of 13 remaining stockpile adversaries prioritized by expected Cortex-detection density. Schema captured: adversary metadata, Cortex preventions observed, known-broken steps with TODO/SKIP/fix-status, per-run protocol checklist. Plus a skip-list with reasons (Everything Bagel too long, Windows Worm variants domain-dependent, etc.).

Every adversary run going forward should append a row + per-adversary detailed-findings sub-section.

### Smoke-test bullets

- [ ] `docs/caldera-adversary-battery.md` renders cleanly in GitHub markdown view
- [ ] When next adversary fires (e.g. Ransack after agents are back up), append matrix row + detailed-findings sub-section in the same release as the next code change touching adversary content

### Forbidden post-v0.5.62

- **Don't keep adversary findings only in chat context** going forward. Every run produces a matrix update; that's the smoke test for whether the doc is paying off.
- **Don't replace per-adversary detailed findings with high-level summaries.** Some adversaries have 30+ steps; broken-step details matter (which step, what error, what fix). The detailed-findings sub-section is where that lives.

## [v0.5.61] — 2026-05-17

**Cortex XDR connector — read-only counterpart to the Caldera connector for closing the agent-as-operator detection-validation loop.** New per-instance connector with 4 tools (`xdr_get_cases_and_issues`, `xdr_get_incident_extra_data`, `xdr_run_xql_query`, `xdr_get_xql_results`). Same Cortex Public API auth pattern as XSIAM (Authorization + x-xdr-auth-id headers), unified `api_url` / `api_id` / `api_key` field names per v0.5.59 / [#35](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/35). First release on the now-fixed (v0.5.60 / [#38](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/38)) build-connectors pipeline. Closes [#36](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/36).

### Why

The Phantom + Cortex XDR detection-validation workflow (EPIC [#39](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/39)) needs a programmatic way for the agent to verify "did Cortex actually catch what we just fired via Caldera?" without manual operator inspection in the XSIAM/XDR console. v0.5.61 closes that loop:

```
operator: "Run the v0.5.57 phantom-killchain via Caldera, then check what Cortex caught"
   ↓
agent: caldera/create_operation(...) + poll to completion
   ↓
agent: xdr/run_xql_query("dataset=xdr_data | filter agent_hostname='xdragent'
                          and _time > to_timestamp(<op_start_ms>)")
   ↓
agent: xdr/get_cases_and_issues(endpoint="xdragent", from_time=<op_start>)
   ↓
agent: builds per-step coverage matrix → reports to operator
```

This is the workflow phantom-agent (Gemini model) will eventually run autonomously per [#39](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/39)'s Phase 3 roadmap. v0.5.61 ships the connector first; manual operator-driven dogfooding (via me, Claude) validates the API surface before phantom-agent skills are authored against it.

### What ships

#### New connector directory `bundles/spark/connectors/cortex-xdr/`

- **`connector.yaml`** — config schema with `api_url`, `api_id`, `api_key` (matches v0.5.59 / XSIAM unified naming + the future-proof slot for any additional Cortex products). Optional `pollIntervalSeconds` (default 3s) + `maxPollAttempts` (default 40 = ~2 min) for the synchronous XQL path.
- **`src/_xdr_client.py`** — minimal async httpx-based client with distinct exception types per failure mode (XDRAuthError / XDRRateLimitError / XDRServerError / XDRRequestError / XDRResponseError). Wrapped by the connector's `_wrap_xdr_call` decorator at the tool boundary so the agent gets uniform `{ok: false, error}` envelopes with `is_auth_error` / `retryable` flags for branching.
- **`src/connector.py`** — 4 tool functions, all `@_wrap_xdr_call`-decorated:
  - `xdr_get_cases_and_issues(from_time, to_time, endpoint, severity, status, limit)` — POST `/incidents/get_incidents` with filter list. Returns compact summary per incident (incident_id, name, severity, status, alert_count, host_count, timestamps).
  - `xdr_get_incident_extra_data(incident_id, alerts_limit)` — POST `/incidents/get_incident_extra_data`. Returns full alerts + network/file artifacts.
  - `xdr_run_xql_query(query, tenant_ids, timeframe_from, timeframe_to)` — synchronous path: POST `/xql/start_xql_query/` → poll `/xql/get_query_results/` with bounded retries. Returns `{status: SUCCESS, results, total_rows, fields}` when complete; `{status: PENDING, execution_id}` when the query exceeds the synchronous poll window.
  - `xdr_get_xql_results(execution_id, limit)` — async path: poll an in-flight query by id. Caller uses this after `run_xql_query` returns PENDING.
- **`Dockerfile`** — `FROM phantom-connector-runtime:dev`, copy in src/, no extra deps (httpx already in the base runtime).

#### Wiring

- **`.github/workflows/build-connectors.yml`** — new `build-cortex-xdr-connector` job. Same `pull-policy: never` pattern as the other per-connector builds (the v0.5.60 / #38 fix).
- **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/connector_probes.py`** — `cortex-xdr` added to `PROBE_IMPLEMENTED`. Probe POSTs `/incidents/get_incidents` with `search_to: 1` for a minimal-data-transfer auth check. 200 = healthy; 401/403 = creds rejected (surfaced as `is_auth_error=true`); other 4xx/5xx = server-side fault.
- **`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/connector_loader.py`** — `cortex-xdr` registered in the manifest-translation map (empty dict — greenfield connector with no legacy env-var aliases, no first-time-setup path since v0.4.0 deleted setup.json).
- **`mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts`** — synthetic-card entry rendering the Cortex XDR connector on `/connectors`. Logo: red-tinted Cortex glyph distinct from XSIAM's orange (visually pairs with caldera's red bullseye in the SOC-simulation family).

### Smoke-test surface

After fresh install (operator runs `sudo /home/ayman/phantom-installer-dev`):

- [ ] `/connectors` → **Cortex XDR** card visible with the red Cortex glyph, displayName "Cortex XDR", tags include `xdr` / `edr` / `xql` / `detection`.
- [ ] Click card → "Create instance" → form shows **API URL** / **API key** / **API ID** labels (same uniform schema as XSIAM post-v0.5.59).
- [ ] Operator pastes real XDR creds (Advanced API Key from Cortex XDR console → Settings → Configurations → API Keys), saves. Modal closes immediately (per v0.5.58 / #33).
- [ ] On the new instance card, click "Test Connection" → ✅ Connection successful (probe hits `/incidents/get_incidents` with minimal body; 200 confirms auth).
- [ ] In a phantom-agent chat (or via direct MCP curl): `xdr_get_cases_and_issues(endpoint='xdragent', from_time='2026-05-16T00:00:00Z')` → returns a list of incidents from the Caldera adversary runs we did yesterday.
- [ ] `xdr_run_xql_query(query='dataset=xdr_data | filter agent_hostname="xdragent" | limit 5')` → returns 5 rows from XDR's data lake.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.61

- **Don't invent new auth field names.** v0.5.59 standardized on `api_url` / `api_id` / `api_key` for cross-Cortex uniformity. Future Cortex-family connectors (XSOAR, Cortex Cloud, etc.) use these names too.
- **Don't add write/action endpoints to this connector** (isolate endpoint, terminate process, run remote script) without an explicit operator-approval gate. Read-only first; destructive surfaces gated by chat-confirmation contract similar to Caldera adversary execution.
- **Don't add a `secretSlots` field that holds a refresh token or OAuth client secret.** Cortex XDR's API keys are the canonical auth — operator-rotatable, console-revocable, audit-logged. Don't introduce a parallel auth shape.
- **Don't expand the XQL `run_xql_query` synchronous-poll window past 5 minutes.** Long queries should return execution_id for caller-driven async polling. Locking the agent in a 5-minute wait on one tool call breaks chat responsiveness.

### Cross-references

- EPIC: [#39](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/39)
- Depends on (now shipped): [#38](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/38) (CI fix for per-connector image builds) + [#35](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/35) (uniform api_* field names)
- Next: phantom-agent skill that USES this connector after Caldera runs to produce coverage matrices — Phase 3 of EPIC [#39](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/39)

## [v0.5.60] — 2026-05-17

**Fix chronic build-connectors workflow failure (silent since v0.5.11).** Every per-connector image build (xlog / xsiam / caldera / web / cortex-docs / cortex-content) was failing with HTTP 401 on `docker build --pull` against the freshly-pushed `phantom-connector-runtime:dev` base. The chronic failure went unnoticed for 5+ release cycles because customer compose pins specific image digests baked at `release.yml` time; only the dev-iteration path was broken. Surfaced as a release-blocker when v0.5.59's XSIAM cred-rename needed the connector image to actually rebuild. Closes [#38](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/38).

### Why

Reading the v0.5.59 build-connectors failure:

```
#2 [internal] load metadata for ghcr.io/kite-production/phantom-connector-runtime:dev
#2 ERROR: unexpected status from HEAD request to https://ghcr.io/v2/kite-production/phantom-connector-runtime/manifests/dev: 401 Unauthorized
```

The race condition: `build-runtime` job builds + pushes `phantom-connector-runtime:dev` to GHCR. Per-connector jobs follow with `needs: build-runtime` and try to `FROM ghcr.io/.../phantom-connector-runtime:dev`. The composite action ran `docker build --pull` which forces a re-fetch of the base image's manifest from GHCR. But the freshly-pushed `:dev` version is **private by default** — it becomes org-readable only when associated with a GitHub Release (the `dev-latest` prerelease), and that association happens later in `build-dev-installer.yml`'s cascade, AFTER all per-connector jobs already tried (and failed) to build. So `--pull` hits the just-pushed manifest, gets 401, fails.

Pre-v0.5.59 releases that only touched connector.yaml metadata (logos, displayName, tags — read by phantom-agent at boot, not packaged into the connector image) were unaffected. v0.5.59 was the first release in months to need a `src/connector.py` change shipped in the per-connector image — and that's when the silent CI break became loud.

### What ships

#### `.github/actions/build-and-push-dev-image/action.yml`

New input `pull-policy` (default `'always'`, accepts `'never'`). When `'never'`, the composite action omits the `--pull` flag from `docker build`. Default stays `'always'` so first-party base layers from Docker Hub etc. continue getting CVE-fresh pulls; the opt-out is narrowly scoped to "the base was just built locally by a prior job in the same workflow run."

#### `.github/workflows/build-connectors.yml`

Every per-connector job (`build-xlog-connector`, `build-xsiam-connector`, `build-caldera-connector`, `build-web-connector`, `build-cortex-docs-connector`, `build-cortex-content-connector`) sets `pull-policy: never`. They inherit the locally-cached `phantom-connector-runtime:dev` produced by their `needs: build-runtime` dependency seconds earlier. The `build-runtime` job KEEPS the default `'always'` because its base is `python:3.12-slim` from Docker Hub — first-party publicly-readable image where `--pull` is the right call.

#### `docs/CICD.md`

New entry **#12** in the CI/CD failure-modes catalog documenting the failure mode + remediation pattern. Quick-reference table extended.

### Why fixing this also rebuilds v0.5.59's XSIAM image

The build-connectors workflow won't trigger on a workflow-file-only change (the path filter is `bundles/spark/connectors/**` and `phantom-connector-runtime/**` — neither matches `.github/workflows/build-connectors.yml`). To exercise the fix, after the v0.5.60 commit lands:

```bash
gh workflow run build-connectors.yml --ref main
```

This force-triggers the workflow with the now-fixed composite action. All 6 per-connector images rebuild with fresh digests — including the v0.5.59-era XSIAM image carrying the dual-name `_get_fetcher()` code. The next `build-dev-installer.yml` cascade resolves the new digests and republishes `dev-latest` with both v0.5.58 + v0.5.59 + v0.5.60 changes baked in coherently.

### Smoke-test surface

After the fix lands + force-trigger:

- [ ] `gh run view <new-run-id>` shows all 7 jobs (build-runtime + 6 per-connector) ALL succeeded.
- [ ] Per-connector build step's log shows `docker build (pull-policy=never)` group header — no `--pull` flag in the actual command.
- [ ] `gh api orgs/kite-production/packages/container/phantom-connector-xsiam/versions --jq '.[0]'` shows a digest created post-v0.5.60 commit time.
- [ ] On phantom-vm post-install: `docker inspect phantom-connector-xsiam-<instance-id> | jq .[0].Config.Image` shows the new digest. `xsiam_get_datasets` tool call succeeds with the new dual-name `_get_fetcher()` code path.
- [ ] Future pushes that touch `bundles/spark/connectors/**` trigger `Build connectors` automatically and succeed without manual intervention.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.60

- **Don't remove `--pull` from any workflow's first-party base build.** Docker Hub bases (`python:3.12-slim`, `node:20-alpine`, `debian:bookworm-slim`) MUST stay `--pull` to catch upstream CVE updates. The opt-out is narrowly scoped to "Phantom-own image just built locally in the same run."
- **Don't auto-associate `:dev` package versions with `dev-latest` from build-runtime.** That was the alternate fix path (Path 2 in #38) — explicitly rejected because it broadens the partial-release window where `dev-latest` references images that haven't passed the rest of the pipeline. The `pull-policy: never` fix is narrower + preserves the release-association ordering invariant.
- **Don't expect build-connectors to auto-trigger on workflow-file edits.** Its path filter is narrow (`bundles/spark/connectors/**` + `phantom-connector-runtime/**`) — workflow edits piggyback on the next source change OR force-trigger with `gh workflow run build-connectors.yml`. This is intentional (per the v0.5.12 narrowing) — workflow-file edits otherwise produced spurious image rebuilds with new digests despite no real source change.

---

## [v0.5.59] — 2026-05-17

**XSIAM connector credentials renamed to `api_url` / `api_id` / `api_key`.** Standardization in preparation for the Cortex XDR connector (issue #36, landing in the next release) which uses the same auth model. Both Cortex products will now expose the same three field names instead of XSIAM's PAPI-specific `papiUrl` / `papiAuthId` / `papiAuthHeader`. Existing instances keep working through the rename via dual-name read at the connector + probe + coverage-tool layers. Closes [#35](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/35).

### Why

Cortex XSIAM and Cortex XDR are the same Palo Alto Networks platform with the same auth pattern: a base URL + an X-Auth-ID header + an Authorization header. Pre-v0.5.59, XSIAM's connector exposed these as `papiUrl` / `papiAuthId` / `papiAuthHeader` — names that reflect XSIAM's PAPI-specific terminology. The upcoming Cortex XDR connector would have had to invent yet another set of names, forcing operators (and the agent) to translate when moving between products.

v0.5.59 collapses both products' field names to a uniform `api_url` / `api_id` / `api_key` schema. The new Cortex XDR connector (issue #36) ships with these names from day 1; XSIAM is migrated in this release.

### What ships

#### Connector spec — uniform field names

`bundles/spark/connectors/xsiam/connector.yaml`:

- `configSchema.required`: `["api_url", "api_id"]` (was `["papiUrl", "papiAuthId"]`)
- `configSchema.properties`: `api_url`, `api_id` (was `papiUrl`, `papiAuthId`). Field descriptions updated; `playgroundId` and `webhookEndpoint` retain their XSIAM-specific names.
- `secretSlots`: `api_key` is now the required Authorization-header slot (was `papiAuthHeader`). `webhookKey` stays.

#### Connector code — dual-name read for backwards compat

`bundles/spark/connectors/xsiam/src/connector.py` `_get_fetcher()`:

Lookup chain accepts BOTH the new uniform names AND the legacy `papi*` names. New names win when both present (operator has re-saved the instance via the form post-upgrade); legacy falls through when only legacy is set (operator hasn't touched the instance since the v0.5.59 upgrade — instance auto-loaded from existing config). Same dual-name pattern in:

- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/connector_probes.py` (XSIAM probe)
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/builtin_components/coverage_tools.py` (coverage cycle XSIAM client)
- `bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/connector_loader.py` (manifest-to-settings key translation — both legacy AND new names map to the same env-var slots)

#### UI form labels

`mcp/agent/app/api/marketplace/connectors/route.ts`: the synthetic-card fields for the XSIAM connector now show **"API URL"** / **"API key"** / **"API ID"** labels with `name: "api_url"` / `"api_key"` / `"api_id"` form keys. New instances created via the marketplace form save the new names.

#### First-time setup wiring

`bundles/spark/manifest.yaml`: the `primary-xsiam` instance template maps its LHS keys to the new names (`api_url`, `api_id`, `api_key`). The setup-template variable names on the RHS (`${setup.xsiamPapiUrl}` etc.) stay as-is — those are setup.json variable references, not instance config keys.

#### Docs + journeys + tests

- `mcp/agent/lib/journeys.ts` — `ops-edit-xsiam-via-connectors-v0135` journey updated to reference the new field names; legacy-name backwards-compat noted in the summary.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/skills/foundation/cortex_xql_query_authoring.md` — troubleshooting matrix updated; both legacy and new error strings mentioned.
- `bundles/spark/mcp/tests/test_coverage_store.py` — happy-path test uses the new names; missing-config test accepts either legacy or new error wording.

### Smoke-test surface

After install:

- [ ] Fresh install: `/connectors` → install + create XSIAM instance → fields show **"API URL"** / **"API key"** / **"API ID"**, NOT "PAPI URL" / "Auth header" / "Auth ID". Test Connection succeeds (with valid creds).
- [ ] Upgrade from a pre-v0.5.59 install with an existing XSIAM instance carrying the legacy `papiUrl`/`papiAuthHeader`/`papiAuthId` config keys: instance continues to work, probe still succeeds, `xsiam_get_datasets` (or any other XSIAM tool) still returns data. No migration required.
- [ ] On the upgraded instance, click Edit in the instance card. The form re-renders the fields with the new "API URL" labels but the saved values come from whichever name pair was stored. If the operator saves without changing anything, the instance keeps the legacy keys (passive migration is opt-in via explicit form save).
- [ ] Tool error path: temporarily clear the api_key slot on an instance. Next XSIAM tool call surfaces `"xsiam instance has no api_key (Authorization header) configured"` — operator-actionable error wording.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.59

- **Don't remove the legacy `papi*` fallback reads** until a major version bump. Operators may have unmigrated instances in production volumes; the dual-name read is the only thing keeping them working.
- **Don't add yet another name pair** for the Cortex XDR connector. v0.5.60's XDR connector adopts the same `api_url` / `api_id` / `api_key` names from day 1; if a future Cortex product joins, it uses these names too.
- **Don't migrate stored instance config keys on boot.** The dual-name read pattern is intentionally lazy: instances get the new names ONLY when an operator clicks Edit + Save in the form, not when the agent boots. This avoids data-touch on every restart.

### Forward dependencies

v0.5.60 ships the Cortex XDR connector (issue #36) which adopts these field names from day 1. The connector will use cortex-docs (issue #34, shipped in v0.5.58) to research the exact Cortex XDR API endpoint specifications before implementation.

---

## [v0.5.58] — 2026-05-17

**Connector instance create + test feels honest.** Two issues compounded into a confusing operator experience when creating a new connector instance: (1) the modal stayed open after a successful Create — making operators think something went wrong — and (2) the test-connection panel routinely showed "Could not reach the service. Verify your credentials and try again." for connectors that have no wired probe (like `cortex-docs` and `cortex-content`, which talk to unauthenticated public APIs). v0.5.58 fixes both: the modal closes immediately on successful create, and the two cortex-docs/content connectors now have real probes that actually verify upstream reachability. Closes [#33](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/33) + [#34](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/34).

### Why

Operator reported while creating a `cortex-docs` instance:
> "I did create an instance of cortex kb connector, got this error 'Could not reach the service. Verify your credentials and try again.' Also when I click on Create Instance, the instance creation window persists while it should disappear once I click Create Instance."

Both halves of that feedback turn out to be a single coherent UX failure: the in-modal Test Connection affordance assumed every connector has a wired probe, but cortex-docs (and cortex-content) talk to **public, unauthenticated** upstream APIs that aren't in the original `PROBE_IMPLEMENTED` registry. The MCP correctly returned `probe_implemented: false` for those connectors, but the UI's failure-path message was misleadingly credential-focused for a connector that has zero `secretSlots`. Combined with the modal not closing, the operator believed Create had failed when it actually succeeded.

v0.5.58 treats the two fixes as one release because they're the same concept — make "create + verify a connector instance" feel honest end-to-end.

### What ships

#### Issue #33 — Create Instance modal closes on success (UI)

`mcp/agent/app/connectors/page.tsx` — `CreateInstancePanel`:

- `handleSave` now calls **`onClose()`** after `onCreated()` in the success branch. Modal dismisses immediately, the instance card renders in the parent list, operator clicks Test Connection there (where it already lives at the per-instance level).
- **Removed in-modal Test Connection section** entirely: the `testStatus`/`setTestStatus` state, `createdInstanceId`/`setCreatedInstanceId` state, `handleTestConnection` callback, and the ~60-line Test Connection UI block. The per-instance Test Connection button on `/connectors` is the canonical surface; the duplicate in-modal version was the source of the dual-action ambiguity.

#### Issue #34 — Real probes for cortex-docs + cortex-content

`bundles/spark/mcp/src/usecase/connector_probes.py`:

- `PROBE_IMPLEMENTED` set extended from `{xlog, caldera, xsiam}` to `{xlog, caldera, xsiam, cortex-docs, cortex-content}`.
- **`cortex-docs` probe**: `POST {baseUrl}/api/khub/suggest` with `{"inputText": "xql"}`. Lightweight, unauthenticated, returns 200 with a small JSON body when the Fluid Topics docs API is reachable. 4xx (e.g. malformed body) still surfaces as healthy because it proves the upstream is alive — only 5xx / connection errors are reported as failures.
- **`cortex-content` probe**: `GET https://api.github.com/repos/{githubRepo}` (defaults to `demisto/content`). Works unauthenticated (~60 req/hr GitHub rate limit) and benefits from the optional `githubToken` secret (5000 req/hr). Distinguishes auth errors (401/403/404) from genuine reachability failures (5xx, connection timeout).

### Smoke-test surface

After fresh install:

- [ ] `/connectors` → "Cortex docs (knowledge)" + "Cortex content (playbooks)" cards both show their logos + correct displayName/tags.
- [ ] Click either card → "Create instance" → fill instance name (other fields can default) → click "Create Instance".
- [ ] **Modal closes immediately.** The new instance appears in the parent list.
- [ ] On the new instance card, click "Test Connection" → status flips to ✅ "Connection successful" within ~5s.
- [ ] (If you block phantom-vm's egress to `docs-cortex.paloaltonetworks.com` or `api.github.com`) — Test Connection flips to ❌ "Connection failed" with a useful error.
- [ ] Existing instances (xsiam, caldera, xlog) — Test Connection still works exactly as before.
