  - **Scenario 2 (blue)**: pill changes PATCH → MAJOR. Versioning: `v5.29 → v6.0 (MAJOR bump)`. Customer: "Download NEW installer from release · Installer flag: WIPE_VOLUMES=false (default)". Footer: "VOLUMES PRESERVED (flag)".
  - **Scenario 3 (red)**: keeps MAJOR pill. Customer line rewritten: "Download NEW installer from release · Installer flag: WIPE_VOLUMES=true". Footer: "VOLUMES WIPED → DEFAULTS" (was "VOLUMES WIPED + BACKUP"). No more interactive `UPGRADE` confirmation prompt.
- **Section 5 (Rollback · Scenario 3) — simplified to fully manual**:
  - Removed the 5-step automated tar restore procedure.
  - New content: "Fully manual · NO automation" + "No automated content/data restore." + "Operator owns the entire flow" + bullets for customer-side backup decisions + manual data restoration.
  - Footer pill: "NO AUTOMATED RESTORE BY DESIGN".
- **Operator yank card** updated: removed "phantom-updater stops surfacing it as 'available'" reference; replaced with "hides from release listing / fresh installer downloads skip it."
- **Section 6 (Path-filter contract)** — emphasis added:
  - Subtitle changed from "no source change → no workflow trigger → no digest change → no container recreate" to "no source change → NO new image · NO new release tag · same digest retagged from prev release".
  - Path filters header changed to "PATH FILTERS — UNTOUCHED SERVICES NEVER REBUILD OR RETAG".

#### `docs/CICD.md`

- **§ Change scenarios** — fully rewritten:
  - S1: "MINOR bump within current major (v5.29 → v5.30)" + "Customer's existing installer at /opt/phantom/phantom-installer reads the latest minor manifest" + "No new installer binary published". Added "The 'no UI Update button' rule" subsection.
  - S2: "MAJOR bump (v5.29 → v6.0)" + "WIPE_VOLUMES=false flag (baked in at installer-build time for Scenario 2 releases)".
  - S3: "MAJOR bump (v5.29 → v6.0)" + "WIPE_VOLUMES=true flag" + "No interactive prompt — the major version bump + release notes are the customer's signal".
  - New subsection **The installer's `WIPE_VOLUMES` flag — Scenario 2 vs Scenario 3** explaining the build-time flag mechanic.
- **§ Decision tree** updated for the new versioning split (MINOR for S1, MAJOR for S2+S3).
- **§ Scenario implementation status** table rewritten — flag-based mechanic is the new future-work item.
- **§ Customer onboarding flow** — step 7 rewritten to remove "UI Update button" mention.
- **§ Rollback procedure → Customer-side rollback (Scenario 3)** — rewritten as fully manual: "No automated content/data restore. Operator owns the entire flow." Removed the 5-step tarball restore procedure.
- **§ Operator-side: yanking a bad release** — removed phantom-updater UI Update reference.
- **§ phantom-updater in the release loop** — fully reframed:
  - New header "Primary role: per-instance connector container lifecycle" (phantom-updater's actual production role today).
  - v0.5.20 model correction note explaining the upgrade-detection endpoints are vestigial.
  - "Legacy upgrade endpoints (vestigial)" subsection acknowledging the endpoints still exist in source but aren't consumed.
  - Forbidden list: added "Re-introducing a customer-facing UI Update button."
- **§ Per-service path-filter contract** — new "The 'untouched services' invariant — load-bearing rule" subsection explicitly stating: NO new image build, NO new release tag, same content digest retagged, customer compose doesn't recreate container.

#### `CLAUDE.md`

- Three-scenario summary table updated: S1 = Minor (v5.29 → v5.30), S2/S3 = MAJOR (v5.29 → v6.0) with `WIPE_VOLUMES` flag.
- phantom-updater cross-reference updated to reflect the v0.5.20 reframing (primary role is connector container lifecycle; UI Update button removed).

### Forbidden post-v0.5.20

- **Re-introducing "UI Update button" in customer-facing docs.** It's an upgrade path we don't operate.
- **Mixing the new scenario model with the old PATCH-S2 versioning.** Once this lands, S2 = major bump everywhere; no "S2 as patch bump" leftover anywhere.
- **Documenting automated S3 rollback steps.** S3 rollback is operator-manual by design; auto-restore risks corrupting customer data on mismatched schemas.
- **Building an interactive `UPGRADE` confirmation prompt for S3.** The mechanic is a build-time installer flag, not a runtime prompt.

### Files

- `docs/cicd-pipeline.svg` (5 visual fixes)
- `docs/CICD.md` (Change scenarios + decision tree + impl status + Customer onboarding step 7 + Rollback Scenario 3 + Operator yank + phantom-updater + path-filter contract)
- `CLAUDE.md` (three-scenario summary table + phantom-updater cross-reference)
- `CHANGELOG.md` — this entry
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts` — v0.5.20 entry prepended

Third end-to-end exercise of v0.5.17 discipline.

1. Operator chat described 6 revisions to the CI/CD model.
2. Agent opened #21 with full template body capturing all 6 changes; applied `status:spec`, `component:docs`, `component:installer` (per the WIPE_VOLUMES future implementation), `scenario:docs-only`.
3. Operator's directing chat message functioned as authorization (prompt-and-apply path).
4. Agent transitioned `status:spec` → `status:spec-approved`; posted comment logging verbal authorization.
5. Implementation iteratively with before/after screenshots per operator request.
6. This commit; after push → `status:in-progress`; after CI → `status:dev-built` + smoke matrix to chat + #21 comment.

## [v0.5.19] — 2026-05-15

**Update the CI/CD diagram + docs to reflect the v0.5.17/v0.5.18 spec-driven workflow.** v0.5.16's SVG showed the build/test/release pipeline as if it stood alone. v0.5.17 added an upstream step (the spec issue with 7-stage label lifecycle) and v0.5.18 added the area dimension; the diagram was out of date. v0.5.19 makes the visual whole again.

**Closes #20.** Second issue exercised end-to-end under the v0.5.17 spec-driven discipline (after #19).

### What ships

#### `docs/cicd-pipeline.svg` — new top section + viewBox extension

A new **Section 0 "Spec-driven workflow lifecycle"** added at the top, showing the 7-stage state machine that wraps the existing pipeline:

```
spec → spec-approved → in-progress → dev-built → testing-complete → release-approved → released
```

Each stage rendered as a colored box:
- Color matches the actual GitHub label color (gray `#C5CAD3` → blue `#5B9BD5` → yellow `#FBCA04` → cyan `#1D76DB` → green `#0E8A16` → orange `#F4B400` → purple `#5319E7`)
- Stage number + status label name in the header bar
- One-line description of when the transition fires
- "AGENT" or "OPERATOR" ownership pill at the bottom

Below the 7 stages, an **anchor panel** ("WHERE EACH STAGE ANCHORS IN THE PIPELINE BELOW") shows which downstream section materializes each lifecycle event:

| Stage | Anchors at |
|---|---|
| 3 · `in-progress` | Dev cycle § git push |
| 4 · `dev-built` | Dev cycle § staged binary + `dev-latest` |
| 6 · `release-approved` | Release cycle § `git tag vX.Y.Z` |
| 7 · `released` | Release cycle § GitHub Release publish · closes #N |

**viewBox change**: `1200×1740` → `1200×1980` (+240px tall). All existing 6 sections + summary bar shifted down by 240px; layout otherwise unchanged.

#### `docs/CICD.md` — Visual overview section-to-diagram map updated

Added a new top row to the section-to-diagram map: "Spec-driven workflow lifecycle (top — NEW in v0.5.19)" → `docs/CICD.md § Spec-driven workflow` + `CLAUDE.md § Spec-driven workflow`.

Added a closing paragraph clarifying the mental model: the lifecycle is the OUTER state machine; the pipeline sections are the INNER mechanics that fire on lifecycle transitions. Diagram reads top-to-bottom as **lifecycle → pipeline**.

#### `CLAUDE.md` — cross-reference audit, no changes needed

Audited all 17 `docs/CICD.md#…` anchors used in CLAUDE.md. Cross-checked against the 132 headings (## and ###) in docs/CICD.md using GitHub's slug derivation rules. **All 17 anchors resolve cleanly** — no broken links, no changes needed to CLAUDE.md.

The audit method is documented in this entry for future use: when in doubt about link integrity, run a slug-derivation script against both files and diff the sets.

### Design choices

- **New section at the TOP**, not the bottom or a sidebar. The lifecycle precedes everything else temporally (issue opens before any code is written); placing it at the top mirrors the reading order with the temporal order.
- **Color-match to actual label colors**, not pipeline-section colors. The visual ↔ tracker correspondence is exact — when an operator sees a `status:dev-built` label in cyan on a GitHub issue, the same cyan appears on stage 4 of the diagram.
- **Anchor panel between lifecycle and pipeline**, not arrows running down through the existing sections. Long anchor arrows would visually cut through Sections 1-6 and add noise; the panel summarizes the same information without drawing across the canvas.
- **No animation on the lifecycle stages.** The state machine is reference material; transitions happen at human scale (hours to days between stages), not at animation cadence.

### What v0.5.19 explicitly does NOT do

- Replace the existing 6 sections with the lifecycle. The lifecycle ADDS context; the existing sections still carry the operational detail.
- Generate the diagram from a programmatic source (Mermaid, etc.). Hand-authored SVG with semantic color choices remains more maintainable than a transpilation layer.
- Add automation that updates the SVG when label colors change in `labels.json`. Manual editing is fine at this cadence; if label colors churn, a sync script becomes warranted.
- Animate the lifecycle as a sequence (stage 1 → stage 2 → stage 3 …). The diagram is a state machine reference, not a time-lapse.
- Animate the connector arrows in the new top section. Static is sufficient; animated arrows there would compete with the existing animated streams in the Dev cycle section below.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.19

- **Adding new sections to the SVG without updating the section-to-diagram map** in docs/CICD.md. The map is the navigation aid; an orphaned section in the diagram is a UX gap.
- **Letting the lifecycle stages drift from the actual GitHub label set.** If a label name changes in `.github/labels.json`, the diagram's stage header must change too. Same lockstep discipline as CLAUDE.md ↔ release-notes.ts ↔ help-pages.
- **Reducing the lifecycle to "before / during / after"** in the visual. The 7 distinct stages each represent an actual transition operators (and the agent) make explicit decisions about. Compressing them defeats the discipline.

### Files

- `docs/cicd-pipeline.svg` — viewBox `1200×1740` → `1200×1980`, new Section 0 (~240px), 7 existing sections shifted down 240px
- `docs/CICD.md` — Visual overview map updated; mental-model paragraph added
- `CHANGELOG.md` — this entry
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts` — v0.5.19 entry prepended

### Spec-driven flow exercise log

This is the **second issue end-to-end under v0.5.17**. Lifecycle so far:

1. Operator chat: "lets update our ci/cd svg diagram..."
2. Agent opened [#20](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/20) with template body (Summary / Why / What ships / Smoke / Forbidden / Cross-references); applied `status:spec`, `component:docs`, `scenario:docs-only`
3. Operator chat: "go ahead"
4. Agent transitioned `status:spec` → `status:spec-approved` per prompt-and-apply path; posted comment logging verbal authorization
5. Implementation in this commit
6. After push: agent will transition to `status:in-progress`
7. After build-dev-installer.yml succeeds: `status:dev-built` + smoke matrix posted to issue + chat
8. Operator runs matrix: applies `status:testing-complete`
9. Operator approves tag (chat or label): `status:release-approved`
10. After release.yml: `status:released` + #20 closes

## [v0.5.18] — 2026-05-15

**Add `area:*` label dimension for feature-aligned classification.** v0.5.17 shipped a 3-dimension label taxonomy (`status:*`, `component:*`, `scenario:*`). v0.5.18 adds a 4th orthogonal dimension — `area:*` — for fine-grained, feature-aligned filtering. An issue can now carry one of each: `component:agent` + `area:chat` + `scenario:1` for a chat UI tweak.

First release exercised under the v0.5.17 spec-driven discipline. **Closes #19.**

### Why a separate dimension

The existing `component:*` labels are tied to **build-pipeline path filters** — they predict which `build-<svc>.yml` workflow will fire. `component:agent` covers ~80% of issues since most surfaces live in `mcp/agent/**`. That's great for predicting CI behavior but terrible for filtering "all chat changes" vs "all auth changes" vs "all observability changes."

The new `area:*` dimension is **feature-aligned**. Where `component:agent` answers "the agent workflow rebuilds," `area:auth` answers "this affects the operator-facing login flow."

The two dimensions are orthogonal: a change to `mcp/agent/lib/auth.ts` carries BOTH `component:agent` (build target) AND `area:auth` (feature surface). Combining them gives both "which workflow fires" + "which feature is affected" without overloading either dimension.

### What ships

**19 new `area:*` labels** (color `#14B8A6` teal — distinct from `component:*` blue):

| Label | Surface |
|---|---|
| `area:chat` | `/` chat UI, streaming, conversation surface |
| `area:skills` | `/skills` page, skills library, runtime |
| `area:memory` | agent memory store, context handling |
| `area:knowledge` | knowledge base, RAG, vector index |
| `area:jobs` | `/jobs` page, scheduler, definitions, runs |
| `area:instances` | `/instances` page, connector instance lifecycle |
| `area:approvals` | approval queue, bypass-approvals semantics |
| `area:api-keys` | `/settings/api-keys`, key create/rotate/revoke |
| `area:observability` | `/observability/*` (all 8 sub-pages) |
| `area:settings` | `/settings` + sub-pages |
| `area:models` | model selection, per-job override |
| `area:providers` | `/providers`, Vertex/Gemini provider config |
| `area:personality` | `/settings/personality` |
| `area:backup-restore` | `/settings/backup-restore`, export/import |
| `area:rest-api` | `/api/agent/*` REST surface |
| `area:profile` | `/profile`, password change flow |
| `area:factory-reset` | `phantom-factory-reset` script + flow |
| `area:password-reset` | `phantom-reset-admin-password` + in-container CLI |
| `area:auth` | login, session, `phantom_auth` cookie, SecretStore |

**Files**:
- `.github/labels.json` — 19 new entries under a new `area:*` block with `_comment` header explaining the orthogonal-to-component design
- `docs/CICD.md § Spec-driven workflow` — new "Area" subsection with the full 19-label table, common label combinations table (showing how the four dimensions compose: e.g., "Chat UI bug fix" → `component:agent + area:chat + scenario:1`), and the rationale for keeping the dimensions orthogonal
- `CLAUDE.md § Spec-driven workflow` — expanded responsibility #3 (mechanical labels) to mention that I apply ALL relevant classification labels at issue creation time across all four dimensions
- Sync script run against `kite-production/phantom` — all 40 labels (21 existing + 19 new) confirmed live

### Items from operator's list already covered (NOT recreated)

| Operator said | Already covered by |
|---|---|
| connectors | `component:connectors` |
| installer | `component:installer` |
| help documents | `component:help-pages` |
| user journeys | `component:journeys` |

These remain in `component:*` because they're build-pipeline boundaries, not feature areas within the agent.

### Pre-existing `area: ui` label

The repo has an older `area: ui` label (with a space after the colon — different format from the v0.5.17 taxonomy). **Left alone** — coexists but isn't actively used in the spec-driven flow. Migration to the new no-space format deferred (would require updating any issues that historically carried it). Mentioned in docs/CICD.md § Spec-driven workflow → Area dimension as a known relic.

### What v0.5.18 explicitly does NOT do

- Migrate the pre-existing `area: ui` label to the new format. Coexists; future spec issue if anyone wants to clean up.
- Add labels for features not in the operator's list (e.g., no `area:notifications` even though `/notifications` exists). New `area:*` labels follow the same spec-driven discipline — open a `scenario:docs-only` issue and run `sync-labels.sh`.
- Make any `area:*` label required. They're zero-or-more per issue; an issue with no `area:*` is fine (e.g., a pure CI/workflow change might have only `component:workflows`).
- Add automation that auto-applies `area:*` labels based on changed paths. Path-to-area mapping is too ambiguous to automate today (most `mcp/agent/**` changes could plausibly be 2-3 area labels). Manual application keeps the labels meaningful.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.18

- **Conflating `component:*` and `area:*` dimensions.** They answer different questions; an issue affecting an agent feature should have BOTH (e.g., `component:agent` + `area:jobs`), not one or the other.
- **Creating `area:agent` or similar** that duplicates `component:*`. The agent isn't a feature area; chat / skills / jobs / providers etc. are features WITHIN the agent.
- **Creating `component:chat` or similar** that duplicates `area:*`. Chat isn't a build target; it's a feature surface within `component:agent`'s build scope.
- **Adding new `area:*` labels without a spec issue.** Same discipline as any other change — open `scenario:docs-only` issue + update `.github/labels.json` + run sync script.

### Files

- `.github/labels.json` — 19 new entries in `area:*` block
- `docs/CICD.md` — new "Area" subsection in § Spec-driven workflow
- `CLAUDE.md` — responsibility #3 expanded
- `CHANGELOG.md` — this entry
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts` — v0.5.18 entry prepended

### Spec-driven flow at v0.5.18

This is the **first release end-to-end under the v0.5.17 spec-driven discipline**:

1. Operator described the change in chat
2. Agent opened issue [#19](https://github.com/kite-production/phantom/issues/19) with full spec (Summary / Why / What ships / Smoke-test bullets / Forbidden / Cross-references)
3. Agent applied mechanical labels: `status:spec`, `component:workflows`, `component:docs`, `scenario:docs-only`
4. Operator authorized via chat ("lets go")
5. Agent transitioned to `status:spec-approved` per operator's verbal authorization (prompt-and-apply path)
6. Agent implemented + this commit references `Closes #19` in the footer
7. After push, agent will transition issue to `status:in-progress`
8. After `build-dev-installer.yml` succeeds: `status:dev-built`
9. Operator runs smoke matrix, applies `status:testing-complete`
10. Operator authorizes tag (chat OR `status:release-approved` label)
11. After `release.yml` succeeds: `status:released` applies; issue closes

If any step doesn't work as designed, that's a v0.5.17 regression to fix.

## [v0.5.17] — 2026-05-14

**Spec-driven workflow Phase 1: every non-trivial change now starts as a GitHub Issue.** v0.5.16's diagram visualized the existing pipeline; v0.5.17 adds one upstream step — an issue that documents the spec BEFORE the work, with labels tracking lifecycle, classified by component + scenario, and closed automatically at release time. The pipeline mechanics that follow (build, install, smoke, approval, tag, release) are unchanged.

### Why spec-driven

Pre-v0.5.17 the workflow shipped releases directly from "operator noticed something" → commits → CHANGELOG entry post-fact. The motivation lived in chat (ephemeral) or in the operator's head (not searchable). v0.5.17 flips that: the issue body documents the motivation BEFORE work begins, and becomes the CHANGELOG entry at release time. Audit trail moves from "what shipped" (CHANGELOG, post-fact) to "what we're shipping + why" (issue, pre-fact + durable in GitHub's tracker).

For a single-operator project this is overkill on small changes (hence the `scenario:trivial` escape hatch) but pays off the first time you ask "why did we do v0.5.X?" six months later and don't remember.

### What ships

- **`.github/labels.json`** (NEW) — canonical label taxonomy. 21 labels in 3 dimensions:
  - **Status** (7, exactly one at a time): `spec` → `spec-approved` → `in-progress` → `dev-built` → `testing-complete` → `release-approved` → `released`. Color gradient (gray → green → purple) makes lifecycle visible in the issue list.
  - **Component** (9, zero or more): mirrors per-service path-filter scopes (`agent` / `xlog` / `caldera` / `connectors` / `installer` / `workflows` / `docs` / `help-pages` / `journeys`).
  - **Scenario** (5, exactly one): mirrors docs/CICD.md § Change scenarios (`1` / `2` / `3` / `docs-only` / `trivial`). Colors match the SVG diagram (green/blue/red for 1/2/3).
- **`.github/scripts/sync-labels.sh`** (NEW) — idempotently applies `labels.json` to the repo via `gh label create --force`. Runs once at bootstrap + whenever the taxonomy changes. Already applied to `kite-production/phantom`; all 21 labels live.
- **`.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/release.md`** (NEW) — spec-driven issue template. Pre-applies `status:spec`. Body sections (Summary / Why / What ships / Smoke-test bullets / Forbidden / Cross-references) mirror the CHANGELOG entry structure 1:1, so the issue body becomes the CHANGELOG entry at release time with minor wording edits.
- **`docs/CICD.md` § Spec-driven workflow (NEW top-level section)** — full mechanics: lifecycle states, label taxonomy with rationale per dimension, trivial-change escape hatch criteria, auto-promotion design decision (Option A: label is metadata, chat approval stays; Options B + C deferred to v0.6.0+), cross-reference patterns between issues + commits + release.yml, backfill policy (none — start fresh from v0.5.17), forbidden patterns.
- **CLAUDE.md updates**:
  - New `### Spec-driven workflow (MANDATORY for non-trivial changes)` subsection under "Build & release workflow contracts" codifying the 6 agent responsibilities (verify/open issue before work, `Refs #N`/`Closes #N` in commits, mechanical-status labels, smoke matrix to chat AND issue, trivial escape hatch, `release-approved` as metadata-only).
  - Updated "Agent-behavior contracts that touch CI/CD" to include the spec-driven workflow as contract #1 (alongside pre-deploy gate, smoke-test bullet contract, approval phrasing, post-tag closure deliverable, documentation discipline).
- **TOC update** in docs/CICD.md adds the new section.

### Auto-promotion: Option A for v0.5.17

The proposal included "operator adds `status:release-approved` to auto-move to release." v0.5.17 adopts **Option A** — label is metadata-only, NOT a trigger. The actual tag push still requires explicit chat approval per CLAUDE.md § Approval phrasing.

Why Option A first: zero new automation to maintain, zero misclick risk (customer releases are not reversible), preserves the chat-approval gate that v0.5.x has demonstrated works. Future-work Option C (label → confirmation PR → operator merges → tag) deferred to v0.6.0+ when enough release cycles accumulate to know whether the friction is real.

### What v0.5.17 explicitly does NOT do

- Backfill issues for v0.5.6-v0.5.16. CHANGELOG entries already describe them well; forcing retroactive issues creates ceremony without traceability benefit.
- Build auto-labeling workflow (commit → label transition). Phase 2 — wait until manual practice stabilizes the flow.
- Build auto-close on release (release.yml → close referenced issues). Phase 3 — depends on auto-labeling.
- Build Option B (label auto-triggers tag) or Option C (label → confirmation PR → tag). Option B is too risky; Option C deferred to v0.6.0+.
- Convert the issue template to `.yml` form. Markdown is sufficient for v1; forms add maintenance cost.
- Apply spec-driven discipline retroactively to the 11 unreleased patches. Those ship under the existing flow; the new flow starts with the NEXT change after v0.5.17.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.17

- **Pushing non-trivial code to main without an open issue.** "Non-trivial" = anything that would generate a CHANGELOG entry beyond a one-liner.
- **Closing an issue without a release.** Issues close when `status:released` applies. Pivots that abandon scope: revert to `status:spec` with a comment, or explicit close as "won't fix" / "duplicate" / etc.
- **Mixing `status:` labels** (e.g., having both `status:in-progress` AND `status:dev-built` at once). Lifecycle is single-state; transitions remove the prior state.
- **Skipping spec for "I'll write it later."** The pre-work audit trail is what the spec-driven discipline buys; skip-it-and-fill-in-later just reverts to the pre-v0.5.17 workflow.
- **Building auto-tagging off `status:release-approved` (Option B)** before v0.6.0+. Customer releases are not reversible; misclick risk is too high.
- **Granting agents permission to apply operator-owned status labels** (`spec-approved`, `testing-complete`, `release-approved`). Agent owns mechanical labels (`in-progress`, `dev-built`, `released`); human decisions stay human.

### Files

- `.github/labels.json` (NEW)
- `.github/scripts/sync-labels.sh` (NEW, executable)
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/release.md` (NEW)
- `docs/CICD.md` — new "Spec-driven workflow" section + TOC update
- `CLAUDE.md` — new "Spec-driven workflow" subsection under Build & release contracts; "Agent-behavior contracts" list updated to 6 items
- `CHANGELOG.md` — this entry
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts` — v0.5.17 entry prepended

### Repo state at v0.5.17

- 21 labels live on kite-production/phantom (verified via `gh label list`)
- Issue template wired via `?template=release.md` URL parameter
- Spec discipline documented in both authoritative locations (docs/CICD.md for mechanics, CLAUDE.md for agent behavior)
- Next non-trivial change opens [Issue #1 (or higher) — Phase 2 auto-labeling] under the new flow

## [v0.5.16] — 2026-05-14

**Add visual CI/CD pipeline diagram to docs/CICD.md.** v0.5.13-v0.5.15 documented the pipeline + scenarios + mechanics in 1684 lines of prose. v0.5.16 adds a single SVG diagram (`docs/cicd-pipeline.svg`) as a scannable visual overview that complements the text. The diagram is the scaffold; the doc has the detail.

### What ships

- **`docs/cicd-pipeline.svg`** (NEW, 41 KB, 707 lines of SVG) — six-section comprehensive diagram covering:
  1. **Dev cycle** (top): swim-lane layout with OPERATOR lane (local edit → push → install → smoke → ASK approval) over CI/CD lane (build-<svc>.yml → build-dev-installer.yml → dev-latest prerelease → staged binary). Animated flow arrows show pipeline direction.
  2. **Release cycle**: git tag → release.yml → GitHub Release → customer (A: UI Update button via phantom-updater, B: re-download installer). Includes monorepo invariant callout.
  3. **Three change scenarios — decision tree**: central diamond ("What did the change touch?") with three color-coded outcome cards (Scenario 1 = green/code-only, Scenario 2 = blue/installer-change, Scenario 3 = red/breaking). Each card shows trigger, versioning, customer experience, volume policy.
  4. **GHCR per-version access matrix**: 3×2 grid mapping PAT type (customer / operator / no-association) against tag type (customer release / dev). Each cell shows the HTTP response. Includes "LOAD-BEARING MECHANIC" callout flagging build-dev-installer.yml's prerelease step as the v0.5.9 fix.
  5. **Rollback & recovery**: four side-by-side paths (Scenario 1+2 rollback via older installer, Scenario 3 manual backup restore, operator yank via `gh release edit --draft`, factory reset as cross-cutting).
  6. **Path-filter contract + REBUILT/UNCHANGED diagnostic**: workflow → path-filter table on the left, color-coded diagnostic status legend on the right (🆕 FIRST-BUILD, ✓ UNCHANGED, 🔨 REBUILT, 📦 STABLE-ADVANCED).
- Summary bar at the bottom reinforcing the core invariants.
- **`docs/CICD.md`** — new "Visual overview" section at the top (just after Contents) that embeds the SVG + provides a section-to-diagram map so readers can correlate visual region with the corresponding text section.

### Design choices

- **Color semantics**: blue = controlled (operator + dev cycle), indigo = integration/core (CI workflows), green = success (Scenario 1, customer pull working), amber = autonomous/cross-cutting (factory reset, dev-latest prerelease), red = danger (Scenario 3 breaking change).
- **Animated flow arrows** (`stream-r` / `stream-d` CSS classes) on key paths show pipeline direction without requiring reader to parse text labels first.
- **Compact + dense**: 1200×1740 viewBox, ~195 text elements + 76 rectangles in 6 sections. Designed to be scannable from 4 feet (color + shape carry the gist) AND zoomable for word-level reading.
- **Standalone SVG**: no HTML wrapper, no external dependencies. Renders in any modern browser or markdown viewer. Easy to embed elsewhere (GitHub README, customer-facing portal in the future).

### Maintenance contract

When the pipeline changes substantively (new workflow file, scenario boundary moves, GHCR access model shifts), update `cicd-pipeline.svg` in the same release that updates the prose in `docs/CICD.md`. Symmetric with the CLAUDE.md ↔ release-notes.ts ↔ help-pages discipline: visual + textual descriptions stay in lockstep.

### What v0.5.16 explicitly does NOT do

- Animate the diagram as a GIF or video. Static SVG is sufficient for reference purposes; animation would distract from the structural argument the diagram is making.
- Generate per-section SVGs as separate files. One comprehensive diagram is easier to maintain in lockstep with the doc; if scope demands per-section diagrams later, they're additive.
- Replace any text in docs/CICD.md with the diagram. The diagram is a scaffold; words carry the detail.
- Add the SVG to the architecture page (`/help/architecture`). docs/CICD.md is operator/maintainer-facing; the customer-facing architecture page describes runtime topology, not CI/CD. Different audience, different doc.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.16

- **Letting cicd-pipeline.svg drift from the text** when CI/CD changes. Stale diagrams are worse than no diagrams — they actively mislead future readers.
- **Embedding the SVG in CLAUDE.md.** CLAUDE.md is the agent-behavior contract; visual reference belongs in docs/CICD.md where the mechanics live. CLAUDE.md cross-references docs/CICD.md.
- **Generating the SVG via a build script** (e.g., from a Mermaid file). Hand-authored SVG with semantic colors + animations is more controllable and doesn't add a build dependency.

### Files

- `docs/cicd-pipeline.svg` (NEW)
- `docs/CICD.md` — new "Visual overview" section + TOC entry
- `CHANGELOG.md` — this entry
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts` — v0.5.16 entry prepended

---

## [v0.5.15] — 2026-05-14

**Close the remaining 7 documentation gaps (9-15) in `docs/CICD.md`.** v0.5.14 closed the high-priority gaps; v0.5.15 finishes the audit by adding the remaining sections — some are immediately actionable (build cache observability with concrete commands, runner capacity thresholds), some are future-work notes (image signing, beta channel), some are policy codification (deprecation lifecycle).

### What ships — 7 new sections in docs/CICD.md

1. **Deprecation policy** — new top-level section, after Change scenarios. Codifies the 3-stage deprecation lifecycle (announce → warn → remove) with minimum windows (≥30 days announce → warn, ≥90 days announce → remove). Where announcements live (CHANGELOG + release-notes + /help/architecture + /help/user + architecture-page Implementation gap). Stage 2 warning mechanisms per surface type (UI banners, MCP tool docstrings, API `Deprecation` headers, installer config-key warnings). Stage 3 removal as MAJOR version bump per Scenario 3 discipline. Distinct from breaking-change emergency releases. Forbidden: removing without lifecycle, shortening windows under "urgency," announcing without naming the replacement path.

2. **Tag immutability + accidental tag deletion recovery** — expanded subsection inside Rollback procedure. Codifies tag-immutability rules (never force-push a different commit to a published tag, never delete a published tag), accidental-tag-deletion recovery procedure (recover commit SHA from GitHub Release / workflow run / git reflog → recreate tag → push → verify customer flow), pointer to PREV_V race for back-to-back tagging.

3. **Pre-release / beta channel (future)** — new top-level section after phantom-updater. Status today: not implemented. Future-work design sketch: separate `vX.Y.Z-beta.N` tag pattern, prerelease + NOT-latest marking, mechanic for phantom-updater channel-awareness. When this becomes relevant + implementation work needed + forbidden patterns (marking dev-latest as latest, obscuring beta status, granting beta customers repo scope).

4. **Build cache observability** — new top-level section after Base-image digest pinning. Where caches live on the self-hosted runner (Docker layer cache under /var/lib/docker/overlay2/, buildx cache, npm cache per-job ephemeral). Inspection commands (`docker system df`, `docker system df -v`, `docker ps --format`, `docker images "ghcr.io/.../*"`, `docker buildx du`). Symptom-to-cause table for cache problems. Cleanup recipes (safe daily prune, aggressive old-version-tag removal, build-cache-only prune). Monitoring thresholds (>80% urgent, >90% urgent + investigate retention). When cache helps vs hurts. Forbidden: `--volumes=false` omission, direct filesystem manipulation, disabling `--pull`.

5. **Self-hosted runner capacity** — new subsection inside phantom-vm runner prerequisites. Current state (one runner on phantom-vm). When to add a second (queue depth >3 jobs for >5 min, recurrent operator-reported delays). Topology options (same-VM vs separate-VM). Adding-a-second-runner procedure. Runner failover plan if phantom-vm goes down (currently no failover; mitigation steps documented).

6. **Image signing, SBOM, provenance (future)** — new top-level section after Image digest pinning contract. Status today: not implemented. Design sketch for when enterprise customer demand triggers implementation: cosign keyless signing via OIDC, syft for SBOM generation per image, slsa-github-generator for SLSA Level 3 provenance. Operational impact (~30-60s added to release.yml runtime, optional install-time verification gate). Forbidden: signing with repo-secret keypair, install-time SBOM generation (defeats provenance), jumping to SLSA L3 without intermediate levels.

7. **CI/CD pipeline observability** — new top-level section after CI/CD failure modes. Status today: no automated alerting (workflow logs + `gh run list` are the manual interface). What we have today (`$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`, `gh run` family, GHCR/release inspection, install-time output). What's missing (failed-build alerting, build-success-rate dashboards, build-time regression tracking, Actions/GHCR quota tracking, dev-latest aging alerts, opt-in customer install telemetry). Suggested implementation paths with effort estimates. When-to-implement triggers. Forbidden: telemetry without opt-out, centralized log forwarding from customer installs, routing CI/CD alerts to customer channels.

### Audit complete

After v0.5.15, all 15 audit gaps from the v0.5.13 retrospective are closed (v0.5.14 closed 1-8; v0.5.15 closes 9-15). docs/CICD.md is now 1684 lines (up from 770 at v0.5.13, +118%). The doc is reference-quality for both humans + agent operations.

| File | At v0.5.13 | At v0.5.14 | At v0.5.15 | Total delta |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `docs/CICD.md` | 770 | 1272 | 1684 | +914 lines (+119%) |
| `CLAUDE.md` | 717 | 730 | 730 | unchanged this release |

### Doc-vs-implementation status (where the gaps stood before, where we are now)

| # | Gap | Before v0.5.14/15 | Now (after v0.5.15) | Implementation work needed? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Customer onboarding flow | Not documented | ✅ Full section | No — process documentation only |
| 2 | PAT recipes | Black box | ✅ Concrete recipes per type | No |
| 3 | Rollback procedure | Not documented | ✅ Full procedure incl. tag immutability | No |
| 4 | CI/CD failure modes | Scattered | ✅ 11-entry catalog with structure | No |
| 5 | phantom-updater architectural role | Underspecified | ✅ Full architectural section | Future: scenario-awareness for Scenarios 2 + 3 |
| 6 | Composite action | Name-dropped | ✅ Inputs/outputs/steps documented | No |
| 7 | Monorepo release invariant | Implicit | ✅ Explicitly codified | No |
| 8 | PR cycle vs main-push cycle | Implicit | ✅ Table + rules | No |
| 9 | Build cache observability | Not documented | ✅ Full section with commands | No |
| 10 | Image signing / SBOM / provenance | Not documented | ✅ Future-work section with design sketch | Yes — when enterprise customer needs |
| 11 | Pre-release beta channel | Not documented | ✅ Future-work section with design sketch | Yes — when beta-program triggers |
| 12 | Deprecation policy | Not codified | ✅ Lifecycle + windows + surfaces | No (process only; enforcement on first deprecation) |
| 13 | CI/CD observability + alerting | Not documented | ✅ Today's surfaces + future-work | Yes — when first overnight failure goes unnoticed |
| 14 | Self-hosted runner capacity | Not documented | ✅ Thresholds + failover plan | No today; add 2nd runner when triggered |
| 15 | Tag conflicts / yanked releases | Partial | ✅ Full procedure + immutability rules | No |

### What v0.5.15 explicitly does NOT do

- Implement any of the future-work items in gaps 10, 11, 13 (image signing, beta channel, alerting). Documentation is the contract for "when this becomes relevant, this is what we do."
- Implement Scenario 3 installer logic (still deferred to first triggering release).
- Add CI for the docs themselves (e.g., link-checking docs/CICD.md anchors). Could be future work; not yet a real friction point.
- Move any agent-behavior contract out of CLAUDE.md.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.15

- **Removing any of the new sections to "trim the doc."** Each section codifies a discipline or anticipates a real-world need. Trimming creates the same gap we just closed.
- **Adding future-work implementations without the corresponding doc updates** (e.g., implementing cosign signing without updating docs/CICD.md § Image signing to reflect the new operational reality).
- **Letting CHANGELOG entries accumulate without referencing the codified policies.** When we deprecate a feature, the CHANGELOG entry MUST cross-reference docs/CICD.md § Deprecation policy.
- **Routing customer CI/CD observability data into our infrastructure without opt-out + privacy review.** The forbidden patterns in observability + telemetry are non-negotiable.

### Files

- `docs/CICD.md` — 7 new sections + TOC update (~412 lines added)
- `CHANGELOG.md` — this entry
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts` — v0.5.15 entry prepended

## [v0.5.14] — 2026-05-14

**Close the 8 highest-priority documentation gaps in `docs/CICD.md`.** v0.5.13 extracted the CI/CD pipeline mechanics to a dedicated guide; v0.5.14 fills the gaps that an audit revealed afterward — areas where the doc described what we have today but missed material that an operator (or future me) would need when something nontrivial happens.

### What ships — 8 new sections in docs/CICD.md (and CLAUDE.md pointers)

1. **Customer onboarding flow (first-time install)** — new top-level section. Covers prerequisites the customer brings (Linux VM, Docker version, RAM, disk, outbound HTTPS), the end-to-end download → install → first-login → password-change → provider-configure path, what customers DON'T need (no GitHub Actions access, no `gh` CLI), and the operator-side delivery responsibilities (PAT generation, secure delivery, post-install check-in). Closes the gap where the doc described the upgrade path but treated first-install as implicit.

2. **PAT recipes (customer + operator)** — new subsection under GHCR per-version access. Concrete generation steps for both PAT types (classic + fine-grained), exact scopes (`read:packages` only for customers; `read:packages + repo` for operators), rotation procedure, and "what wrong scope looks like" so a future operator can recognize misconfigured tokens at a glance.

3. **Rollback procedure** — new top-level section. Customer-side rollback for Scenario 1+2 (re-run older installer, volumes preserved), Scenario 3 (manual backup-restore — operationally expensive by design), operator-side yank procedure (`gh release edit --draft`), recovery from `release.yml` partial failure (re-dispatch or delete+retag). Closes the "we shipped a bad release, what now?" gap.

4. **phantom-updater in the release loop** — new top-level section. Architectural piece previously underspecified. Covers source location, MCP_TOKEN auth + PAT auth for GHCR pulls, the 5 API endpoints (`/healthz`, `/api/v1/version/current`, `/version/check`, `/update/status`, `/update`), update detection mechanics (GitHub Releases API polling + manifest fetch + per-service digest comparison), update-apply SSE flow, V1 scope constraints (no rollback, no volume snapshots, no self-update), which 3 services updater manages, and scenario-aware behavior (today: Scenario 1 fully automated; Scenarios 2 + 3 need operator-driven installer download — Scenario 3 enforcement requires the `INCOMPATIBLE_FROM` mechanism that's documented but not yet implemented).

5. **`build-and-push-dev-image` composite action** — new subsection under Workflow file layout. Documents the inputs (8), outputs (`digest`, `ref`), 4 steps (GHCR login, build with `--pull`, push-with-retry, capture-digest from RepoDigests), and why it's a composite action vs a reusable workflow. Closes the gap where the action was name-dropped in the workflow file layout but its actual behavior was opaque.

6. **Monorepo release invariant** — new subsection under The two installers. Codifies the rule: all 11 images (5 stack + 6 connector) ship in lockstep at the same `vX.Y.Z`. Mixed-version manifests not supported by phantom-installer or phantom-updater. Closes the implicit-invariant gap that would otherwise be discoverable only by attempting to violate it.

7. **PR cycle (vs main-push cycle)** — new subsection under Build & release mechanics. Table comparing what fires on `pull_request` events vs `push` to main events. PR builds: docker build + tests + lint, NO `:dev` push, NO `dev-latest` republish, NO installer staging. Push-to-main: full pipeline. When to use each.

8. **CI/CD failure modes + recovery playbook** — new top-level section near the end. 11 actual failure modes we've hit (`docker login` timeout, Cloud NAT throttle, workflow_run cascade serialization, PREV_V race, `gh` not on self-hosted runner, OCI-index Accept-header mismatch, missing `dev-latest` prerelease, runner offline, partial `release.yml`, disk full, stale `:dev` digest) plus a quick-reference table. Each entry: symptom → cause → remediation → prevention. Closes the gap where each failure mode took 30-90 minutes to diagnose first time — future encounters should resolve in <5 minutes.

### CLAUDE.md changes

- New "When CI/CD breaks — failure-mode catalog" subsection inside the CI/CD pipeline section, with explicit instruction: "check [docs/CICD.md § CI/CD failure modes + recovery playbook] FIRST" when something goes wrong.
- New "Other operator-facing CI/CD topics" subsection with crisp pointers to the 7 other new sections in docs/CICD.md (customer onboarding, PAT recipes, rollback, phantom-updater, monorepo invariant, PR cycle, composite action).
- TOC at top of docs/CICD.md updated with all new sections + anchors.

### Doc size delta

| File | Before v0.5.14 | After v0.5.14 | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| `docs/CICD.md` | 770 lines | 1272 lines | +502 lines (+65%) |
| `CLAUDE.md` | 717 lines | ~730 lines | +13 lines (cross-references only) |

The bulk of v0.5.14 is in docs/CICD.md (where it belongs per the v0.5.13 split — CI/CD knowledge in the CI/CD guide).

### What v0.5.14 explicitly does NOT do

- Implement Scenario 3's installer code. Still documented as deferred to first triggering release.
- Implement phantom-updater's scenario-awareness for Scenarios 2 + 3. The contract is documented; the implementation is TBD when first needed.
- Build base-image auto-update workflow. Documented as a future helper; cadence remains manual.
- Add CI/CD-level observability / alerting (gap #13 from the audit). Lower priority; deferred until repeated build failures actually hide.
- Add SBOM / image signing / provenance attestation (gap #10). Deferred to enterprise-customer demand.
- Document deprecation policy (gap #12). Not yet relevant; no features deprecated.
- Build-cache observability (gap #9). Deferred until repeated cache-hit-rate questions arise.

### Forbidden post-v0.5.14

- **Removing the failure-mode catalog from docs/CICD.md.** The catalog's value depends on accumulating real failures + their resolutions. Empty catalog = useless catalog.
- **Adding failure-mode entries without the symptom → cause → remediation → prevention shape.** Pattern matters; freeform entries don't help future-me debug at 3am.
- **Re-implementing customer onboarding flow steps in CLAUDE.md.** CLAUDE.md points at docs/CICD.md; the source-of-truth is one place.
- **Treating the rollback procedure as a customer-facing UX.** Currently it's an operator-side runbook. If we want customer-driven self-service rollback in the UI, that's a separate product decision + design conversation.
- **Granting customers `repo` scope on their PATs** "for convenience." The PAT recipes section is explicit: scopes are minimum-viable for each role. Don't broaden customer PATs.

### Files

- `docs/CICD.md` — 8 new sections + TOC update
- `CLAUDE.md` — cross-reference subsections added under "CI/CD pipeline → see docs/CICD.md"
- `CHANGELOG.md` — this entry
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts` — v0.5.14 entry prepended

## [v0.5.13] — 2026-05-14

**CI/CD pipeline documentation extracted to [`docs/CICD.md`](docs/CICD.md), with the three change scenarios codified.** Operator-driven discipline correction: previously CLAUDE.md mixed agent-behavior contracts (when I MUST ask, what I MUST share) with pipeline mechanics (workflow files, image tags, GHCR access). The mix made CLAUDE.md hard to navigate and made the CI/CD model implicit instead of explicit. v0.5.13 separates the concerns:

- **`docs/CICD.md`** (NEW, ~770 lines) — pipeline mechanics, workflow files, change scenarios, customer upgrade flows. The authoritative CI/CD reference for both humans and the agent.
- **CLAUDE.md** (trimmed from 1157 → 717 lines, −38%) — agent-behavior contracts only, with pointers into docs/CICD.md for the mechanics that the behavioral contracts enforce.

### The three change scenarios (NEW in docs/CICD.md)

Every release classifies into one of three scenarios. Identical for dev prereleases and customer releases — the dev cycle mirrors the customer cycle modulo the digest content.

| Scenario | Trigger | Versioning | Customer experience | Volumes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | Code-only, installer unchanged | Patch (vX.Y.Z+1) | UI Update button OR re-download installer (byte-identical) | Preserved |
| **2** | Code + installer change (backwards-compatible storage) | Patch (vX.Y.Z+1) | Download new installer, run, auto-detects upgrade | Preserved |
| **3** | Backwards-incompatible storage schema | **MAJOR (v(X+1).0.0)** | Download new installer, types `UPGRADE` to confirm volume backup + wipe | Wiped (backed up to disk first) |

Each scenario in docs/CICD.md gets: trigger conditions, versioning rule, what artifacts we ship, customer experience walkthrough, volume policy, customer confirmation prompt (if any), examples from history, release-time discipline. Plus a top-level decision tree: "what did the change touch?" → "which scenario?".

**Factory reset** (cross-cutting) is documented as the manual operator path available across all three scenarios — wipes volumes, preserves `.env`, re-runs the installer, gated behind the `FACTORY RESET` confirmation.

### What ships

- **`docs/CICD.md`** (NEW) — full pipeline guide. 18 sections covering: overview, three change scenarios with decision tree + implementation status table, the two installers, image tag convention, GHCR per-version access, per-service path-filter contract, `docker compose pull` semantics (NOT a fresh build), REBUILT/UNCHANGED diagnostic, base-image digest pinning (+ caldera carve-out), install location + volumes + recovery utilities, workflow file layout, build & release mechanics, phantom-vm runner prerequisites, code → VM contract, smoke-test commands, installer build env-var contract, image digest pinning contract for the customer compose, consolidated forbidden list.
- **`CLAUDE.md`** trimmed:
  - **Removed** (now in docs/CICD.md): full `## Local-mirrors-customer deploy flow` section (270 lines), full `## Remote-first workflow` section (110 lines), full `## Build & release workflow` mechanics (130+ lines of mechanics; kept the agent-behavior subsections), full `## Image digest pinning contract` (50 lines), CI/CD smoke-test commands from `## Command reference` (60 lines).
  - **Replaced with pointers**:
    - New `## CI/CD pipeline → see docs/CICD.md` section with the three-scenario summary + the agent-behavior list + the approval-gates summary.
    - New `## phantom-vm operator environment` section (keeping VM coordinates + credentials + IAP access pattern, which are agent-environment-specific not CI/CD).
    - New `## Build & release workflow contracts` section with just the BEHAVIORAL contracts: pre-deploy gate, smoke-test bullet contract, approval phrasing, pointer to the post-tag closure deliverable template in Documentation discipline.
    - New `## Image digest pinning → see docs/CICD.md` pointer.
    - Renamed `## Command reference` to `## Stack runtime behavior` (skills bootstrap is product behavior, not CI/CD).
  - **Kept verbatim** (agent-behavior or product knowledge): Operator communication context, Contained-release discipline, Agent credential guardrail, What Phantom is, Pre-build context refresh, Architecture page is the spec, Documentation discipline (including the Release closure report template), Canonical-state discipline, Stack topology, Architecture that isn't obvious from the tree, Configuration, Conventions to preserve.
- **All 6 workflow file headers** updated: `CLAUDE.md § Per-service path-filter contract` → `docs/CICD.md § Per-service path-filter contract`. Cross-references between workflows + CLAUDE.md still work; future maintainers see the actual reference file.

### Mental model the split enforces

- **CLAUDE.md** = "instructions to the AI agent about how to be a good collaborator on this project." Agent-facing contracts, behavior, project-internal disciplines.
- **`docs/CICD.md`** = "instructions to humans (including the AI) about how the CI/CD pipeline actually works." Pipeline mechanics, change scenarios, workflow file structure, customer upgrade flows.

When pipeline mechanics change (a new build workflow, a new path filter, a Dockerfile pin update, a new release scenario), update docs/CICD.md. When agent-behavior changes (a new smoke-test contract, a new approval gate), update CLAUDE.md. Most changes update both (mechanics that have a behavioral implication), but the split makes it visible which side of the boundary each change lives on.

### Scenario 3 implementation status: documented, NOT implemented

The contract for Scenario 3 (backwards-incompatible storage schema, volume reset required) is fully documented in docs/CICD.md including:
- `INCOMPATIBLE_FROM` flag baked into the installer at build time
- Backup-then-wipe logic gated behind `UPGRADE` confirmation
- Backup destination `/opt/phantom/backups/<release>-pre-upgrade-<timestamp>/`
- Release-time discipline for breaking-change documentation
- No bypass flag — confirmation is non-negotiable

**The implementation isn't in v0.5.13** because no v0.5.x change has triggered Scenario 3 yet. The contract is documented now so when the first backwards-incompatible release ships (likely v1.0.0 or v2.0.0), the implementation has a clear target. docs/CICD.md § Scenario implementation status lists exactly what's needed in installer/phantom-installer.template.sh + installer/build-phantom-installer.sh to make Scenario 3 work.

### What v0.5.13 explicitly does NOT do

- Implement Scenario 3's installer logic. Documented; deferred to first triggering release.
- Touch the installer template or workflow logic. Pure docs reorganization + workflow header reference updates.
- Move agent-behavior contracts (smoke-test bullets, post-tag closure report, approval phrasing) out of CLAUDE.md. Those are intentionally kept where the agent reads them.
- Move the Release closure report template out of CLAUDE.md's Documentation discipline section. It's an agent-behavior deliverable; CLAUDE.md is where I look for the template shape.

### Files

- `docs/CICD.md` — NEW comprehensive CI/CD guide
- `CLAUDE.md` — trimmed + restructured (38% smaller, mechanics moved to docs/CICD.md)
- `.github/workflows/{build-xlog,build-agent,build-caldera,build-connectors,build-dev-installer,release}.yml` — cross-reference updates (CLAUDE.md § → docs/CICD.md §)
- `CHANGELOG.md` — this entry
- `mcp/agent/lib/release-notes.ts` — v0.5.13 entry prepended
